Boutique 2-room by Talpiot Market — magazine-grade renovation, long kitchen counter, big dining table PURSUE
Haifa · ₪3,700/mo rent · 2 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪3,700/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
The standout of the Haifa cluster. A fully renovated 2-room boutique flat beside Talpiot Market: tadelakt-style walls, high ceilings, rattan pendants, plants, tons of natural light — genuinely the nicest place to BE in this batch. The kitchen is a long (~2.5m) uninterrupted counter with sink, cooktop and a countertop oven, plus a large solid-wood dining table that seats 6 and doubles as a workspace for two. Bedroom is the mamad (safe room), AC throughout, and the shuk's fresh produce is a 3-minute walk.
+
  • Genuinely beautiful, bright and inviting — the best-presented unit in the cluster by a wide margin
  • Long unbroken kitchen counter with real prep space, cooktop + countertop oven (kitchen = their #1 criterion, met)
  • Big 6-seat solid wood dining table = two comfortable work spots; sofa + armchairs as a second zone
  • Talpiot Market (Sirkin 35) a few minutes' walk — fresh fruit/veg/spices, ideal for vegan + gluten-free cooking
  • Fully furnished to a high standard ('ברמת בית מלון'): TV, fridge, bed, wardrobe, sofa, dining set
  • Bedroom is a mamad (protected room) — safety plus in the building
  • Hadar/Talpiot: dense bus + Metronit network, Haifa Center–HaShmona train ~15 min by bus for the weekly Tel Aviv trips
  • No broker fee ('ללא דמי תיווך'); building fully renovated including water/electrical infrastructure, coded entry
  • Building occupied by young people — sociable and lively area (cafés, bars, flea market)
–
  • ₪3,700 + est. ₪650 utilities ≈ ₪4,350/mo all-in — under the cap but only by ₪150, and that rests on the ESTIMATE since the ad states no utility figures
  • Only 2 rooms — the two work spots share one open space, no closable second room
  • No built-in oven, only the countertop oven visible in the photos
  • Talpiot/flea-market area can be noisy and scruffy at street level; listed by an agency (Enriq Group), likely a commission
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — Yad2 listing, short_term unknown
  • Ask for the actual arnona + electricity/water figures; the ₪4,500 cap has almost no headroom at this rent
  • Ask whether the price is negotiable for a 2-month furnished stay, and whether there is a broker fee
  • Ask which floor and how noisy the street is at night (flea market / bars nearby)
  • Confirm there is a working cooktop (photo suggests one at the counter's right edge) and whether an oven can be added

The kitchen is the best in the cluster: white cabinets with a freestanding gas range (oven + 4-burner cooktop), a dishwasher, fridge, a long run of prep counter and a dining table — a genuine cook's kitchen for two. The rest matches: a bright, fully furnished 3-room flat with three sofas and a sweeping Haifa-bay view, two bathrooms (tub + separate toilet), and a bedroom with wardrobe + desk. It is a 5-minute walk to the Technion (Nesher gate) with a bus stop to Haifa opposite the door and a minimarket nearby, the ad explicitly says it suits a couple and allows a longer stay, and entry is 1 Oct 2026 — within the Sep–Nov window. Several photos (staged rooms, a cat, other balconies) are scraper contamination; the genuine unit is the terrazzo-floored one in photos 00–07.
+
  • Excellent kitchen: oven + 4-burner gas + dishwasher + fridge + long counter + dining table
  • Fully furnished ('קומפלט') and move-in ready
  • Bright, spacious living room with a wide Haifa-bay view — room for two work desks
  • Explicitly 'suitable for a couple'; 5-min walk to the Technion, bus to Haifa at the door, minimarket nearby
  • Entry 1 Oct 2026 fits the Sep–Nov window; two bathrooms (tub + separate toilet)
–

  • Arnona ₪550/2mo; no parking; no balcony
  • Photo set is partly contaminated with other apartments — verify the real unit
  • Listing is framed annual; short-term not explicitly stated
?

  • Confirm which photos are this exact unit and that the kitchen shown (oven + dishwasher) is included
  • Ask about quiet on Havatzelet St and whether 1 Oct can flex earlier into September

The kitchen is the standout: a full-size freestanding gas range (four burners + oven), a generous grey countertop, full cabinetry, a full-size fridge and a microwave, plus a separate dining table — genuinely good for a couple cooking most meals. At 62 m² it is large and bright for the price, furnished with a bed, wardrobe and balcony, and the listing explicitly offers flexibility on all rental terms (flagged short-term). The bathroom is older but clean; it is a 1-room open layout on the top (3rd) floor.

Afula, Keren HaYesod — the dream kitchen, a ready office room, ₪2500 ALL-IN including bills PURSUE
Afula · ₪2,500/mo rent · 3 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16

rent ₪2,500 · Ad: 'מחיר כולל חשבונות' — price stated as including bills; ₪2500 treated as all-in.
confirm short-term
A dated-but-immaculate 3-room flat on a quiet central Afula street whose big U-shaped kitchen is exactly what this couple needs: two long work surfaces, gas hob AND electric hob AND a built-in oven, plus washer and fridge. The third room is already set up as an office with a computer desk and chair, and there is a double bed and two wardrobes. The ad states the price includes bills, so ₪2500 is the true all-in — a full ₪2000/mo under cap, which is car-and-coworking money. The decor is cherry-wood-and-cream-tile 1990s rather than bright and modern, but it is clean, well-equipped and the agent explicitly pitches it 'מצוינת לזוג צעיר'.
+
  • Best kitchen in the cluster: large U-shape, generous counter on 2+ surfaces, gas hob + electric hob + built-in oven (all stated in the ad and visible in photos)
  • ₪2500 ALL-IN (ad: 'מחיר כולל חשבונות') — ~₪2000/mo under cap, enough to fund a car for the whole stay
  • 3 rooms: bedroom + living + a room already furnished as an office with a computer desk (workspace for two, one behind a closable door)
  • Well furnished: double bed, 2 wardrobes, computer desk, washer, fridge, dining table
  • Ad says quiet street in the city centre — walkable to Afula's shops, shuk and BIG Afula centre
  • Afula centre = direct Egged 825 to Tel Aviv Central (~1h45–1h50, hourly) from the central bus station; Afula Emek train station ~2.2 km (bus 3, ~9 min) for Haifa in ~35–50 min
  • ~20–25 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea — family is genuinely close
–
  • Dated interior (cherry-wood kitchen, old cream floor tiles); functional and clean but not the bright modern 'nice place to be'
  • Light looks average — blinds down in most photos, no big open living shot
  • 3rd-floor walk-up implied (קומה שלישית), no lift mentioned
  • Agency (מגדלים נדל"ן) is investment-focused; likely a broker fee
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — ad says short_term unknown, this is the single biggest open question on the best-fit flat here
  • Confirm 'כולל חשבונות' really covers electricity + water + arnona + vaad with no cap for two people running ACs all September
  • Ask whether the computer desk and office room stay for the tenancy
  • Verify AC in every room (only some rooms visible)
  • Ask which floor exactly and whether there is a lift

Explicitly listed as a sublet (short_term listed_ok) — the only listing in the cluster that already matches the 2-month need. 'A window to the bay': a big glazed balcony with a genuine panoramic view of Haifa Bay, the port and the sea, with a glass table set up on it as a workspace. The kitchen is a real L-shaped cooking kitchen with a freestanding 4-burner gas range + oven and corner counter space. Lived-in and dated rather than styled, but warm, spacious and furnished throughout.
+
  • SUBLET, explicitly short-term-friendly — no need to negotiate an exit from a 12-month lease
  • Real cookable kitchen: freestanding gas range with oven + 4 burners, L-shaped counter with corner prep space
  • 4.5 rooms — enormous for two people; easily a closable work room each
  • Spectacular panoramic bay/port/sea view from a large glazed balcony; a glass desk is already set up there
  • Two bedrooms with double beds, full furniture, washing machine + dryer, internet, AC in the main rooms
  • Renovated bathroom + separate toilet
  • ₪3,300 + est. ₪650 ≈ ₪3,950 all-in — comfortable headroom under the ₪4,500 cap
  • Tel Amal is a quiet older residential neighbourhood on the Neve Sha'anan slopes with good bus links to central Haifa
–
  • Dated finishes and visibly cluttered with the owner's belongings — it will need to be cleared for a 2-month stay
  • The half-room has no AC (stated in the ad)
  • No train station within walking distance — Tel Aviv trips mean a bus down to Haifa Center–HaShmona or Hof HaCarmel first
  • The ad half-markets the half-room to a flatmate ('החצי חדר יכול להיות מוקצה לדירת שותפים... שאר התשתיות נחלקות') — clarify the whole flat is on offer
  • Utilities not stated — the ₪3,950 all-in figure uses the ₪650 estimate
?
  • Confirm the sublet dates line up with Sept–Nov 2026 and that the WHOLE flat (not a room-share) is available
  • Ask how much of the owner's furniture/belongings stay and whether the flat is cleared
  • Ask for actual arnona + bills figures
  • Check bus frequency from Pe'er St down to Haifa Center–HaShmona train for the weekly Tel Aviv commute
  • Ask about the nearest supermarket — no walkable shuk in this neighbourhood

Masada St sublet, ₪3,000 all bills included — homey, work-ready, Carmelit at the door; best fit in Haifa PURSUE
Haifa · מסדה · ₪3,000/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪3,000/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪3,000 + arnona n/a · מרוהט וכלל את כל החשבונות... מרוהט כולל הכול · missing arnona not stated
no photos
A genuinely lived-in, warm 2.5-room (60sqm) sublet on Masada — Haifa's bohemian cafe street in Hadar. Photos show a real home: plant-filled living room with big sofa and book shelves, double bed, decorated bathroom with tub, a kitchen with a long counter run and full-size fridge, and a bright enclosed sun-balcony that makes a natural second workspace. Ad explicitly offers a sublet with ALL bills included, a work corner and fast fiber. Carmelit Masada station is on the street (ride down to Paris Sq, ~10 min walk to Merkaz HaShmona train for Tel Aviv), 24/7 supermarket across the road, Talpiot produce market ~8 min walk. The main risks are calendar fit (posted for 22.7 entry) and that no photo shows the cooktop/oven.
+
  • Sublet — short-term is the listing's whole premise; no landlord-agreement gamble
  • ₪3,000 with all bills included → ~₪1,500/mo under budget
  • Fully furnished and genuinely inviting: plants, real sofa, equipped kitchen with generous counter, bathtub
  • Two workable work-spots (stated work corner + enclosed sun balcony) and fast fiber internet — ideal for two remote workers
  • Carmelit station on the street, 24/7 super opposite, cafes downstairs, Talpiot market walkable — car-free living
  • Kibbutz Hazorea ~30–35 min drive / direct-ish bus via Yokneam — easy family visits
–
  • Availability posted from 22.7 — the sublet window may not stretch into a Sept–Nov 2-month stay
  • Cooktop/oven not visible in any photo (ad says 'equipped kitchen' only)
  • Masada is a nightlife/cafe street — evening noise likely, and it's floor 1
  • Vintage building — charming but expect old-building quirks (no elevator, older windows)
?
  • FIRST question: does the sublet cover ~2 months inside Sept–Nov 2026? (posted entry 22.7)
  • Verify stove + oven exist and work (they cook most meals, vegan/GF)
  • Ask whether bedroom faces the street or the back — bar noise on weekends
  • Confirm 'all bills' covers arnona + electricity + internet explicitly

Massada 29 — tastefully furnished 45sqm literally at the Carmelit station, on Haifa's café street, tenant paying ₪200/mo toward rent PURSUE
Haifa · מסדה · ₪2,500/mo rent · 2 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A lease-transfer from a tenant who has already moved out and needs a replacement fast — she offers to pay ₪200/mo toward the rent until the contract ends, making it effectively ₪2,300. Small (45sqm) but genuinely well put together: grey sofa, plants, rattan chair, gateleg dining table, double bed, wardrobe + clothes rack, and — per the ad but not the photos — a LARGE computer desk. Kitchen has a ~2m counter with a ceramic cooktop and drawers; no oven. Massada 29 is the Carmelit's Massada station address, on the street nicknamed 'Haifa's Sheinkin'.
+
  • Bright, clean and genuinely tastefully furnished — a nice place to be, not a landlord's beige box
  • Real ~2m kitchen counter with a ceramic cooktop and drawer storage = actual prep space
  • Ad explicitly lists a 'שולחן מחשב גדול' (large computer desk) not shown in photos, plus a dining table + 2 chairs
  • Literally at the Carmelit 'Massada' station (the station's address is Massada 29) — funicular straight down to the Lower City / Haifa Center–HaShmona train
  • Massada St: 'the Haifa Sheinkin' — cafés, bars, sushi, falafel, bookshops, grocery, a lively Jewish-Arab scene, all on foot; Talpiot Market close by
  • Cheap: ₪2,500 nominal, effectively ₪2,300 with the ₪200/mo the tenant pays; ~₪3,150 all-in leaves big budget headroom for a car or co-working
  • Tenant is motivated and moving fast — leverage on terms
  • Fully equipped: fridge, washing machine, TV, rug, nightstand, mattress included
–
  • 45sqm and 2 rooms is tight for two people working from home all day
  • No oven — cooktop only (negotiable given the real counter space, but they'd need a countertop oven)
  • Massada is a nightlife street — bar/café noise at night is a genuine risk; the flat faces it
  • It's a lease assignment, so the end date is fixed by the existing contract — may not stretch to a clean Sept–Nov window
  • Utilities not stated; ₪3,150 all-in uses the ₪650 estimate
?
  • Ask when the existing contract ends — this is the make-or-break question for a Sept–Nov 2026 window
  • Confirm the ₪200/mo subsidy applies to them and for the whole period
  • Ask which floor and how loud the street is on a Thursday night
  • Confirm the large computer desk is still there (it's not in the photos)
  • Ask for arnona + bills figures

Afula centre, HaMagen — the most inviting unit in the cluster: real kitchen with oven, a desk already there, arnona + water included PURSUE
Afula · מרכז העיר · ₪2,900/mo rent · 2 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,550/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪2,900 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'מחיר כולל ארנונה ומים !!!' — arnona and water stated included; electricity not mentioned, so bills fallback applied for electricity only.
confirm short-term
A properly designed, freshly renovated 2-room unit and the best-looking place in this cluster by a distance: bright open living/kitchen with a grey sofa, a yellow armchair, track lighting and — already in the photo — a desk and chair by the window. The kitchen is real: a long counter with a black glass hob, a built-in oven, fridge and marble splashback, with genuine prep space either side. The ad states the price includes arnona and water, so realistically only electricity is extra (~₪3050 all-in). Only 2 rooms, so the second work spot is the dining/desk corner rather than a closable room.
+
  • Genuinely lovely to be in: bright, clean, well-lit, quality furniture (sofa, armchair, bed, wardrobe) — meets the 'nice place to BE' bar better than anything else here
  • Real cookable kitchen: long counter with usable prep space, ceramic/induction hob, BUILT-IN OVEN, fridge
  • A desk and chair are already in the living room
  • Price includes arnona AND water (ad: 'מחיר כולל ארנונה ומים') — all-in likely ~₪3050 with only electricity extra
  • Ad describes 'ריהוט איכותי' and 'מוקפדות עד הפרט האחרון' — the photos back it up
  • Afula centre: walkable groceries/shuk/BIG, direct 825 bus to TLV Central ~1h45 from the central bus station, ~20–25 min drive to Hazorea
–
  • Only 2 rooms — one desk plus the dining corner; no separate closable room for the second worker
  • Central Afula street, so less quiet than the Migdal HaEmek / Afula-Illit options; judge the actual street noise on a visit
  • Ad markets 'מבחר יחידות מגורים' (a portfolio of units) — the photos may be of a sister unit rather than this exact one
  • Small footprint overall (2 rooms, no sqm given)
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability (short_term unknown)
  • Confirm THESE photos are this exact unit — the agency advertises several near-identical units
  • Confirm what 'כולל ארנונה ומים' leaves out — assume electricity is on you, ask for a typical summer figure
  • Check AC coverage in the bedroom as well as the living room
  • Ask what's downstairs / how noisy HaMagen is in the evening

Be'er Yaakov · המושבה · ₪3,800/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪3,800/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included

The single best fit in this cluster. Photos show a bright, genuinely inviting unit: glossy white floors, a modern grey sofa, framed art, and — remarkably — a corridor already furnished with TWO desks and two office chairs, which is exactly the workspace-for-two requirement. The kitchen is a proper galley with a long continuous counter, upper cabinets and a full-size fridge. Price includes everything except electricity, entry 1.8.26 fits a Sept–Nov stay.
+
  • Two desks + two office chairs already in place — workspace for two, no improvisation needed
  • Long galley counter with real prep space and upper cabinets; fully equipped per the ad ('רק להגיע עם מזוודות')
  • Bright, clean, tastefully furnished — a genuinely nice place to spend hot Sept–Oct days indoors
  • HaHermon St is ~14 min walk to Be'er Yaakov train station → direct line to Tel Aviv
  • HaMoshava is the quiet old-village core of Be'er Yaakov, not a main road
  • ~20 min drive to family in Gedera
  • Price stated as all-inclusive except electricity — arnona, water and vaad covered
  • Entry 1.8.26 means it is free and waiting for a Sept start
–
  • No oven or stovetop is visible in any kitchen photo — the ad says 'fully furnished' but never names cooking appliances
  • Be'er Yaakov is a small town: groceries and services are limited compared to Rishon/Rehovot
  • ₪3,800 is the top of the sensible range here; with the ₪650 fallback estimate the all-in lands at ~₪4,450, just under the cap
?
  • CRITICAL: ask for a photo of the cooking surface — is there a hob and/or oven? This is the one thing that could sink an otherwise ideal listing
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (listed short_term unknown; entry 1.8.26 suggests it may be sitting empty and open to a short let)
  • Ask for the average monthly electricity bill — it is the only excluded cost
  • Verify the walk to Be'er Yaakov station and the TLV timetable frequency

landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
A well-renovated, fully furnished 3-room flat on the quiet stretch of Tel Hai in Hadar, with the price stated as including arnona, water and electricity — which is what keeps it under the ₪4,500 cap. The kitchen is a long (~2.5m) dark counter with sink, a cooktop and a countertop oven plus wooden uppers: real prep space. Living room is bright with a sofa, dining table and a wood-slat TV wall; one bedroom already has a desk with an office chair, the other a double bed and wardrobe.
+
  • ₪4,000 stated ALL-IN (ארנונה, מים וחשמל included) — no utility surprises, and it fits the cap on STATED figures
  • Long kitchen counter with genuine prep space + cooktop + countertop oven
  • 3 rooms: a closable second room means each of them can have a real work spot
  • A desk + office chair already installed in one bedroom; dining table for the second spot
  • Freshly renovated, fully furnished ('משופצת חדשה', 'מרוהטת קומפלט'), bright, tasteful
  • Floor 1 — no long climb, which matters in Hadar
  • Ad specifically says the quiet part of Tel Hai ('רחוב תל חי 24 השקט')
  • Close to Metronit, buses, supermarkets; Talpiot Market and the Massada café strip within walking distance
  • No broker fee ('ללא עמלת תיווך')
–
  • At ₪4,000 all-in it eats almost the whole budget — nothing left over for a car or co-working, and any extra charge breaks the cap
  • No built-in oven, just a countertop unit
  • Hadar generally is scruffy at street level; 'the quiet part' is the agency's claim, unverified
  • Managed by an agency (ספיר פרימיום) using hard-sell copy ('מהרו לחטוף') — verify the all-in claim in writing
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — listed as immediate entry, short_term unknown
  • Get the 'includes everything' claim in writing, including any ועד בית
  • Ask whether the rent drops if utilities are billed separately, or whether a shorter stay costs more
  • Verify the street noise on Tel Hai at the actual house number

KITCHEN is the standout: a full run of taupe cabinets with a long white countertop, a proper 4-burner gas range WITH a built-in oven, a large fridge, and a stacked washer/dryer — easily the best cooking setup in this cluster for a couple who cooks most meals. The rest of the unit matches: modern grey-porcelain floors, a wood-slat feature wall, a big L-shaped sofa, and a fully renovated grey-marble bathroom with a glass rain shower. At 60m² it is also the largest and most move-in-ready of the five, though it sits at the top of the budget.
+
  • Excellent full kitchen: 4-burner gas stovetop AND oven, generous counter/prep space, large fridge, plus washer+dryer
  • Fully renovated and tastefully furnished (sectional sofa, TV, feature wall) — truly move-in ready
  • Largest unit at 60m², room for two to work from laptops
  • Flexible entry date (כניסה: גמיש) — encouraging for a short-term arrangement
  • 5-min walk to the market = walkable fresh produce (good for vegan/GF cooking); train to Tel Aviv ~25 min

  • ₪4,500 — top of budget, and arnona (~₪200/2mo) is NOT included, so effective cost is a bit higher
  • Ramla is the least quiet of the cluster (near the market) and a bit further from the Gedera family than Rehovot/Kiryat Ekron
  • No dedicated desk shown; two remote workers would share the living-room surfaces
  • Currently occupied/lived-in — availability timing needs checking
?

  • Ask whether the oven is gas or electric and confirm the range works
  • Ask about street noise/quiet given proximity to the market, and about parking if they end up with a car
  • Confirm arnona amount and what 'some items stay' means for the furniture

no photos
The kitchen is the best in this cluster: a modern fitted kitchen with dark-gray cabinets, a long white quartz counter with real prep space, a 4-burner gas cooktop, microwave, and (per the listing) an oven and full fridge — exactly what two people cooking most meals need. The rest matches: a brand-new boutique building, ~80 m² fully furnished (sofa, dining table+4, double bed, wardrobes), a 12 m² balcony with a bay/Carmel view, mini-central AC and private covered parking. Note the photo set is a broker montage — several images (older apartments, a shabby shower, recycled 'desk' shots) are clearly NOT this unit; judge it on photos 00-07 and 18.
+
  • Modern full kitchen: quartz counter + prep space, gas cooktop, microwave, oven and fridge per description — top choice for cooking
  • Brand-new, bright, high-spec finish; genuinely move-in clean
  • ~80 m² with 3 rooms — easily room for two to work on laptops (dining table + a second surface)
  • Balcony with view, private covered parking, in-apartment safe room, mini-central AC
  • Fully furnished including 65" TV, seating, beds and wardrobes
–
  • ₪4,500 is the top of budget and arnona adds ~₪300/mo, so effective cost is over ₪4,500/mo
  • Carmel/Ahuza slope: bus-served but not on the Metronit — a car-free TA commute means a bus/connection down to Hof HaCarmel station
  • Photo montage mixes in other apartments, so the exact unit needs in-person confirmation
  • On a fairly busy commercial street (kiosks/shops at street level) — verify quiet
?
  • Confirm the landlord will do a ~2-month short-term let (listing is annual, 12 payments)
  • Confirm the oven is present and working (photos clearly show cooktop+microwave; oven only stated in text)
  • Ask the true monthly all-in (rent + arnona + va'ad bayit) and whether parking is included
  • Check street noise from the front and the real walk/bus time down to Hof HaCarmel train
80
Cheapest and most spacious — real oven+cooktop kitchen, explicit 6-month lease PURSUE
Kiryat Ata · יוספטל · ₪2,250/mo rent · 3.5 rooms · 85.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪2,900/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,250 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is a genuine, usable galley: white cabinets top and bottom, a black granite counter, sink, microwave and a freestanding gas stove with oven — real prep space plus both oven AND cooktop, the couple's top need. At ₪2,250 it is by far the cheapest option, a spacious 3.5-room / 85 m² apartment that is actually lived-in and furnished (sofas, beds, TV, dining table, and a desk for laptop work), in a neighborhood the owner describes as quiet and pleasant and 'really close to the Metronit.' It is the only listing here offering an explicit short lease (6 months), a strong plus for a short-term couple. Note the listing pads in several staged/stock photos that do not match the real unit, so the actual condition should be verified.
+
  • Cheapest by far (₪2,250, well under the ₪4,500 budget)
  • Real kitchen with oven + gas cooktop + black granite counter + ample cabinets (the #1 priority)
  • 3.5 rooms / 85 m² — spacious for two remote workers; a desk/workspace is visible in photos
  • Explicit 6-month lease — the only short-term-friendly term in the cluster
  • Quiet neighborhood (Sderot Yoseftal), owner notes it is 'really close to the Metronit'
  • Already furnished and lived-in: sofas, beds, TV, dining table, closet, fridge
–
  • Described as only 'partially furnished' — owner may remove some items before handover
  • 6-month contract is longer than their ~2-month need; may require negotiating a shorter term or overpaying
  • Listing mixes in staged/stock photos also seen in the other Kiryat Ata listings, lowering photo confidence
  • Floor 3 of 4 — elevator presence uncertain in an older block
  • Bathroom is tight (washing machine crammed beside the shower stall)
  • Kiryat Ata has no train; Tel Aviv trips are ~1.5h+ by Metronit+train (minor, since both work remotely)
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month stay is possible, or whether they can sub-let within the 6-month term
  • Confirm exactly which furniture stays vs is removed
  • Verify the gas cooktop and oven work and are included
  • Ask about elevator access and street/neighbor noise
  • Check car-free transit/route to Kibbutz Hazorea (~35-50 min by car; longer without one)

Bright, fully-equipped 3-month Lod sublet near Ganei Aviv train — huge budget headroom, but cooking is on portables PURSUE
Lod · גני אביב · ₪2,750/mo rent · 2 rooms · 52 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪3,560/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,750 + arnona ₪160 + est. utilities ₪650
no photos
Explicit 3-month sublet (from Aug 7) in Ganei Aviv, Lod's self-contained northern neighborhood — dates fit Sept-Oct perfectly. Photos show a clean, light-filled 2-room flat on floor 11 (2 elevators, floor mamad): sofa+TV+dining table, a desk in the second room, and a kitchen with real counter runs and full cabinetry — but cooking is a portable 2-burner plus a toaster-oven on the fridge, no built-in stove/oven. All-in ≈ ₪3,300 (2,750 + 160 arnona stated + bills), leaving ~₪1,200/mo headroom. Ganei Aviv rail station is ~1.3km (18-min walk or short bus), ~20-25 min to TLV; Carrefour Market, Holmes Place and clinics ~5 min walk.
+
  • True sublet, 3 months from Aug 7 — exactly their window, no landlord-agreement gamble
  • All-in ≈ ₪3,300 — well under budget; headroom funds co-working or a car
  • Real kitchen counters + full cabinets; fridge, washer, kettle present ('arrive with clothes only')
  • Bright living room; clean renovated condition; floor 11 with 2 elevators and floor-level mamad
  • Workspace: desk in second room + dining table = two laptop spots
  • Ganei Aviv train station ~18-min walk (or bus), ~20-25 min to Tel Aviv; Lod hub nearby for Jerusalem/south lines
  • Carrefour Market, kupot holim, country club within ~5 min walk; quiet residential-towers street
  • Reasonable drive (~25-30 min) to Gedera-side family
–
  • No built-in stovetop or oven — cooking on portable burners + toaster oven (counters are good, so workable, but a real minus for daily vegan+GF cooking)
  • Washer lives in the kitchen, eating some counter length
  • No bedroom/bed photo among the 5 pictures — bed unverified
  • Ganei Aviv is pleasant but isolated from Lod center; Lod outside the enclave is rough around the edges
?
  • Confirm exact sublet window and whether a ~2-month (Sept-Oct) stay within the 3 months is acceptable
  • Ask for a bedroom photo / confirm double bed and mattress condition
  • Confirm cooking setup: gas/electric portable burners included? OK to add an oven/burner they buy?
  • Ask typical monthly electricity+water+vaad to firm up the ~₪3,300 all-in estimate
  • Verify train frequency from Lod Ganei Aviv station at commute hours (some services skip it; Lod main station is the fallback, 4km bus ride)

Karmiel Gamla/Hadar: bright 55 m² ground-floor unit with a real cooking kitchen, a desk already in place, and ₪2,650 incl. arnona+water PURSUE
Karmiel · הדר · ₪2,650/mo rent · 2.0 rooms · 55.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,300/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪2,650 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'שכר הדירה כולל ארנונה ומים. חשמל על פי צריכה.' — arnona + water included in ₪2,650; electricity metered separately (no figure given).
car recommendedconfirm short-term
The strongest all-round fit in the cluster. Photos show a genuinely cookable kitchen — L-shaped black granite counter, freestanding gas range with a full oven, large fridge, generous prep run — plus a big, white, bright living room that already contains a proper desk with an office chair and two sofas, a 6-seat dining table, and a garden sitting corner. Private landlord (not an agency), explicitly quiet-and-clean neighbourhood, aimed at a single/couple. Rent includes arnona and water; only electricity is metered, so realistic all-in is roughly ₪2,950–3,050 — leaving a large share of the ₪4,500 budget free for a rental car.
+
  • Real kitchen: gas range WITH oven, L-shaped granite counter with a generous prep run, full-size fridge — the best kitchen/price ratio in the cluster
  • A desk + office chair already in the flat, in a large living room with room for their own transportable desk = two workable spots
  • ₪2,650 includes arnona and water; all-in likely ≈₪3,000 — ~₪1,500/mo of budget left over for a car and co-working
  • Bright, clean, white, spacious for 55 m²; ground floor with a private garden sitting corner
  • Ad explicitly says quiet, clean neighbourhood, suited to a single/couple; private landlord means a short-term ask can be negotiated directly
  • Karmiel has a train station (northern entrance, beside the central bus station) — ~2h to Tel Aviv Center via Ahihud/Kav HaHof
  • Kibbutz Hazorea ~50 min drive
–
  • Décor is plain/sparse — functional rather than inviting; no styling, bare walls
  • Electricity is on consumption and Sept–Oct AC in Karmiel will add ~₪300–400/mo
  • Ad is strict: no pets, no smokers, 'quiet clean people' (repeated) — landlord may be picky about a 2-month let
  • Homeless.co.il photo sets are padded with a 'similar ads' carousel: photos 6–9 (the small white kitchen, the phone-screenshot garden) look like they belong to other ads — only the first ~6 are reliably this unit
  • Karmiel→Tel Aviv is ~2h each way by train and the station is not walkable from most neighbourhoods
  • 12 payments/year listed — landlord is thinking annual lease
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — listing says immediate entry / 12 payments a year, so short-term is unconfirmed
  • Verify which photos are actually this unit (ask for a fresh set / video walkthrough) given the homeless carousel padding
  • Ask what electricity typically runs per month with AC in September
  • Check the exact walk/bus from Gamla-Hadar to Karmiel train station and to a supermarket
  • Confirm the oven and hob in the photos stay with the flat

A 30 sqm studio that punches far above its price. The kitchen is the star: a long counter run with a stainless 4-burner hob, a toaster oven, a full-size fridge and real prep space — better than most 3-room flats in this cluster. Travertine floors, a leather sofa, a dining table, wall lighting and a ceiling fan make it feel like somewhere to actually live, and it opens onto a covered patio with a garden and swing seat. Entry 01.10 fits an Oct–Nov window.

  • ₪3,000 including arnona and water — leaves ~₪1,200/mo of budget for a car or co-working
  • Covered patio + garden with seating and a swing — outdoor space for a hot September
  • Separate entrance; ad explicitly says 'רחוב שקט במיוחד' (particularly quiet street)
  • Fully furnished: bed, sofa, dining table, wall lights, ceiling fan

  • Entry date 01.10 lines up with an Oct–Nov stay

It is fully furnished and very spacious (120 m2, 4 rooms) with a dedicated office room already set up with a desk and an ergonomic chair, ideal for two people working from laptops. It sits on Derech Akko, directly on the Metronit BRT spine (excellent car-free transit) and near the train - but that main road raises a noise question, the listing gives no short-term info, and 3,900 is the top of budget.

A 75 sqm 3-room flat renovated 'as new' in Ramat Balfour, and the kitchen alone justifies a call: a long uninterrupted white counter with a 4-burner gas hob, a built-in oven, sink and deep cabinetry — the most generous single work surface in the cluster. The living room is furnished with a 6-seat dining table, a large corner sofa, a glass display cabinet and AC, and it reads bright and spacious. Ramat Balfour is a quiet, young-family neighbourhood and it is the closest of the towns here to Kibbutz Hazorea after Ramat Yishai. Only 3 photos, and the ad never actually says 'furnished', so both need confirming.
+
  • Excellent kitchen: very long counter, 4-burner gas hob, built-in oven, lots of storage — cooking two meals a day here would be a pleasure
  • 3 rooms / ~75 sqm — room for a closable second workspace
  • Photos show it furnished: big sofa, dining table for 6, display cabinet, AC
  • Renovated 'משופצת כחדשה'; bright and clean
  • Ramat Balfour is a quiet residential neighbourhood of young families — good on the 'quiet street' criterion
  • ~15–20 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea — the best family proximity of any pursue-grade option here
  • ₪2800 + est. bills ≈ ₪3450 all-in, comfortably under cap
–
  • Only 3 photos — no bedroom, no bathroom, no view of the street or building
  • The ad text never states the flat is furnished ('רק להיכנס ולגור' only); the furniture may be the current tenant's
  • Ad pushes 'אפשרות לשכירות לטווח ארוך' — long-term is what they want to sign
  • No stated utilities — ₪650/mo fallback assumed
  • Migdal HaEmek to TLV is ~1h45 by Egged bus or ~1h51 by train changing at Haifa; the Migdal HaEmek–Kfar Baruch station is out of town, not walkable
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability (short_term unknown) — the ad leans long-term
  • ASK FOR MORE PHOTOS, especially bedrooms — and confirm the furniture stays
  • Confirm arnona + bills figures for Migdal HaEmek (no numbers stated)

  • Check the walk to a supermarket from Keren HaYesod

Kitchen is the standout: an L-shaped fitted kitchen with a gas cooktop, a built-in oven, a dishwasher, a large side-by-side fridge, and a breakfast-bar island that adds genuine second-surface prep space — confirmed from photos, not just the ad text. It's a renovated, bright 80 m2 / 4-room flat with 3-way airflow, two full bathrooms, and an existing desk workspace in the bedroom, so both partners can work remotely without fighting for space. The catch is price (₪4,500 is the ceiling) and that the furniture belongs to the outgoing tenant and is offered only for a 'symbolic fee', so furnished status must be nailed down.
+
  • Best kitchen by far: gas cooktop + oven + dishwasher + big fridge + island/breakfast-bar counter — ideal for a couple cooking most meals
  • Spacious 80 m2 / 4 rooms — easily fits two laptop workspaces; a desk is already set up in the bedroom
  • Renovated and bright, 3-way airflow, two bathrooms/showers
  • Central location near the BIG Caesarea mall for groceries; ready-to-move entry date 25/7/2026 (before their Sept start)
  • Family in Kibbutz Hazorea is only ~30-40 min away
–

  • Furniture is the previous tenant's and only available 'for a symbolic fee' — furnished status is not guaranteed
  • Or Akiva has no train station; nearest is Binyamina (~40-55 min to TA) but reaching it needs a bus/ride — awkward with no car

  • With no car, check Or Akiva -> Binyamina bus frequency for the TA commute

rent ₪1,200 + arnona ₪750 + est. utilities ₪650
no photos
Warm, arty private house on Bialik St in Kadima-Tzoran, subletting Aug 15 - Oct 14 (possibly earlier entry). Photos show a genuine cook's kitchen (5-burner gas hob in long stone counters, Delft-tile backsplash, island + full dining), a double-height living room with fireplace and an antique writing desk, bright bedrooms, and a landscaped 'magical' garden with patio and pergola. Sublet-board price ₪1,200/mo + stated arnona ₪750 → all-in ≈ ₪2,700-2,900 even with big-house bills — deep under budget IF the price is real (symbolic house-minding rents do happen; a dog bowl is visible, so duties are likely). Weakness: transport — no train in town; buses 55/56 to Beit Yehoshua station (~25 min) then ~20 min train → ~1h15 to TLV; car recommended.
+
  • Real, generous kitchen: 5-burner gas hob, long counters, island — built for people who cook
  • Inviting character home: double-height living space, fireplace, antique desk (ready workspace), bright bedrooms

  • Big landscaped garden with shaded seating — great for heat-season living

  • 6 rooms — two separate work rooms trivially
–
  • Sublet window ends Oct 14 and requires ~Aug 15 start — their 2 months would be Aug 15-Oct 14, only partly inside Sept-Nov (OK only with their stated flexibility)
  • Transport is the weak leg: bus-to-train commute ~1h15 to TLV; car strongly recommended for Roy's weekly trips
  • ₪1,200/mo is suspiciously low — likely house-minding duties (dog bowl visible: possible pet care), or a data/typo risk
  • Stated arnona ₪750/mo is high (big house) and big-house electricity in Sept heat adds up
  • Lived-in family home: they'd live among the owners' belongings
  • Far from Gedera (~50 min) and Hazorea (~45 min)
?
  • VERIFY the ₪1,200/mo price is real and monthly, and exactly what it covers (arnona ₪750 — who pays? bills?)
  • Ask about duties: dog/cat care, garden watering, pool upkeep — what's expected
  • Confirm entry/exit flexibility: can they start earlier/later, and is Aug 15-Oct 14 acceptable vs their Sept-Nov preference
  • Confirm there's a working oven (hob clearly seen; oven not in frame; ad says 'all appliances')
  • Check bus 55/56 first/last times vs Roy's TLV days; price a 2-month rental car from the savings
  • Confirm internet quality (remote work) — suburban house, likely fine but ask
75
Koresh St Afula: designer-grade renovation, huge counter, ₪2,500 stated ALL-IN — best value-for-condition in the cluster; no oven PURSUE
Afula · ₪2,000/mo rent · 2 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪2,500/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,000 + arnona ₪200 + utilities ₪300 (stated in ad) · 2000 שכירות+ 150 חשמל+ 200 ארנונה+ 100 מים+ 50 ועד בית=2500 כולל חשבונות
confirm short-term
Agency (Nadlan Afula Yazamut) 2-room, 50sqm on the 4th floor, renovated to a genuinely high standard: long black kitchen with marble-look worktop giving the most prep space of any listing here, huge corner sofa, double bed + wardrobe + mirror + TV, luxury bathroom. The ad itemizes the full cost: 2,000 + 150 electricity + 200 arnona + 100 water + 50 va'ad = ₪2,500 including bills. East-central location is also the closest of the Afula listings to the train station (~10-15 min walk). Cooking is hob + microwave — no oven listed; with this much counter, a bought portable oven solves it.
+
  • Stated ₪2,500/mo ALL-IN — ₪2,000 headroom; cheapest fully-priced renovated option
  • Exceptional condition: brand-new renovation, modern bathroom, 'huge' rooms for 2-rm layout
  • Kitchen counter is generous — best prep surface in the cluster; fridge, microwave, hob, washing machine, 2 ACs
  • Closest Afula listing to the rail station (~1km): hourly Valley-line trains, plus central bus station and direct 825 bus to TLV
  • Bright: white walls, large windows, spotless floors; pets allowed
–
  • No oven stated or visible (only כיריים) — they'd buy a portable/toaster oven
  • No dining table or desk in photos/inventory — their own desk covers one workstation, second needs improvising
  • 4th floor, no elevator mentioned
  • East-of-center address (~15-20 min walk to the center's shops), quieter but less walkable to the shuk
  • Agency ad — commission question on a 2-month stay
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (not stated)
  • Verify what furniture is actually included beyond photos (dining table? desk allowed?)
  • Ask if landlord adds an oven or agrees to them installing a portable one
  • Confirm agency fee treatment for short lets
  • Verify exact address/floor and elevator

Dated but fully-equipped 2.5-room on HaHagana - real oven + gas hob at 2,400 PURSUE
Kiryat Bialik · ₪2,400/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 40.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪3,050/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,400 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is the real draw here: a freestanding gas range (oven plus gas cooktop), a microwave and a tall fridge in dated blue cabinetry - the only clearly-shown unit with both an oven and a hob, exactly the cooking capability this couple needs, though the counter is a single modest run rather than a big prep surface. The apartment is fully furnished (beds, wardrobes, sofa, dining table, washer, two AC units) and described as renovated on a quiet street, but it looks dated - blue kitchen, old terrazzo floors, patterned couch. At 40 m2 / 2.5 rooms and 2,400 it is the best-value genuine cook's kitchen in this cluster.
+
  • Real oven + gas cooktop + microwave - full cooking capability
  • Fully furnished
  • Cheap: 2,400/mo
  • 40 m2, 2.5 rooms - a bit more room than the other cheap units
  • Quiet street, renovated per listing, shelter 80 m away
  • Kiryat Bialik - Metronit + train access

  • Dated look: blue cabinets, old terrazzo, worn patterned furnishings
  • Kitchen counter / prep space is modest (single run)
  • Low floor in an older building
  • Photos 06-17 are 'similar listings' contamination; only 00-05 are this unit
?
  • Confirm ~2-month short-term availability (listed as annual/12 payments)
  • Confirm the gas range + microwave stay with the unit
  • Check walking distance from HaHagana St to the nearest Metronit stop and a supermarket
  • Ask whether a prep table can be added for more counter space

Herzliya Neve Amal unit, 6.9–5.11 — exact 2-month window, has a workspace PURSUE
Herzliya · Neve Amal · ?/mo rent · facebook · added 2026-07-15
no photos
Furnished housing unit in Neve Amal, a quiet 'kibbutz-vibe' Herzliya neighborhood, offered exactly 6.9–5.11 (the couple's 2-month window) while the owners travel. Photos show a desk + office chair, so there's a real workspace for two remote workers, and it's 15 min from Gaash beach/Herzliya marina. Price is DM-only ('reasonable price'); a suburban Herzliya unit plausibly fits budget, but Neve Amal is not walking-distance to Herzliya's train, so TA access is bus-based.
+
  • Exact 6.9–5.11 window — no date negotiation needed
  • Real workspace (desk + chair) for two remote workers
  • Quiet, green, kibbutz-vibe neighborhood — matches 'quiet' priority
  • Herzliya is well-located toward TA

  • No price in post ('reasonable, DM') — could still be over budget
  • Neve Amal → Herzliya train is a few km (bus, not walkable) — a factor if no car
  • Kitchen/size not shown

Afula center, Giora Yoseftal 18 — top-grade new renovation, 3 rooms (real office room), ~3,200 all-in, quiet boutique building PURSUE
Afula · מרכז העיר · ₪3,000/mo rent · 3 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪3,000/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪3,000 + arnona n/a · המחיר כולל כל החשבונות חוץ מחשמל — price includes all bills except electricity · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
The strongest listing in this cluster: a high-end fresh renovation (marble-look floors, designer bathroom) on a quiet street in the center, in a 5-unit boutique building. 3 rooms at 60sqm gives a genuine second room for the two-laptop office; brand-new furniture (double bed, wardrobe, dining set; ad adds sofa + washing machine). Rent 3,000 includes ALL bills except electricity → all-in ~3,150-3,250. Best transport in the cluster: ~13 min walk to Afula train station and ~6 min to the CBS (825 direct to TLV). Gap to close: the new kitchen has a clear ~2m counter but no visible cooktop or oven — with this prep space a portable solves it, but verify.
+
  • Highest renovation standard seen in the cluster — bright, new, genuinely inviting
  • 3 rooms: dedicated closable office room for two remote workers — rare at this price
  • 3,000 incl. all bills except electricity → ~3,150-3,250 all-in, well under budget
  • Quiet street in the center (5-resident boutique building); private ad, no broker fee
  • Best transit access: ~13 min walk to the train, ~6 min to CBS, direct 825 bus to Tel Aviv every ~30 min
  • Brand-new furniture already in place (bed, wardrobe, dining set); parking
  • ~22-25 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea
–
  • No cooktop or oven visible in the new kitchen — cooking hardware currently unproven (prep counter is good)
  • Kitchen is a single ~2m run, compact for heavy daily cooking
  • Electricity on top — Sept AC use adds ~150-250/mo
  • 'First come wins' + available mid-July: real risk it is gone before September
  • Train to TLV still needs a Haifa transfer (~2h); the direct bus is the practical link
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability — ad pushes immediate entry, so ask early
  • Verify cooking setup: is a cooktop/oven being installed, or is a portable acceptable?
  • Confirm the sofa and remaining furniture from the ad text actually arrive
  • Confirm 'all bills except electricity' explicitly covers arnona, water AND va'ad

Wolfson 19 Afula: ₪1,640 fully-lived-in 2.5rm with real counter space and a ready desk+monitor workspace — cheapest solid option in the cluster PURSUE
Afula · דרום עפולה · ₪1,640/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪2,500/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪1,640 + arnona ₪210 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
no photos
Private ad, 50sqm on a quiet, leafy south-Afula street two blocks off the main drag. Photos show a complete, modest home: dining table for four, sofa + rocking chair, double bed, wardrobe, bookshelf, and crucially a desk WITH monitor and office chair already in place. Kitchen is dated but has a long granite counter (real prep space); cooking is on a double portable hotplate plus what looks like a countertop oven in the utility room, and the owner explicitly offers to complete missing equipment. All-in lands around ₪2,300 — huge budget headroom.
+
  • All-in ≈ ₪2,300/mo (1,640 rent + 210 stated arnona + est. bills) — ₪2,200 of monthly headroom vs budget
  • Kitchen passes the gate: long granite counter, full utensils/dish setup; owner says missing equipment will be added on request
  • Real workspace already installed: desk + monitor + ergonomic chair, plus dining table as a second work spot
  • Genuinely furnished throughout (bed, wardrobe, sofa, dining set, fan, washing machine); AC listed
  • Available 2026-08-23 — perfect for a Sept 1 start
  • Quiet green residential street per ad, yet 2 min from Afula's main street; mercolet, clinic and schools nearby; ~15 min walk to the central bus station and direct 825 bus to Tel Aviv
–
  • Dated finishes: old blue-tile bathroom, worn kitchen fronts — functional, not pretty
  • No built-in stovetop/oven — cooking on portable double hotplate + countertop oven
  • 4th floor, no elevator mentioned — daily stair climb in end-of-summer heat
  • Train station is ~2.2km (not walkable daily); Tel Aviv by rail needs a transfer (~2h), bus 825 ~1h45
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (short-term not stated; available_from 23.8 suggests a standard year lease)

  • Verify elevator absence and bills ballpark (electricity with AC season)
  • Confirm the desk/monitor/office chair stay for the rental

Central Rishon 3.5 rooms at ₪3,300 with a full oven+hob kitchen and a built-in desk wall — but the building is mid-TAMA 38 PURSUE
Rishon LeZion · אברמוביץ' · ₪3,300/mo rent · 3.5 rooms · 90 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,950/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,300 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
Outstanding value: 3.5 rooms in central Rishon for ₪3,300, with the best all-round layout in the cluster. The kitchen is a real one — white L-shape with a built-in oven, a hob, a dishwasher and a window. One bedroom has a long built-in desk plus an office chair; another has a second desk. Spacious living room with sofas and a dining area. The big caveat is that the ad states the building is in the TAMA 38 process, which for two people working from home all day is a serious noise risk.
+
  • 3.5 rooms for ₪3,300 — by far the best space-per-shekel here; a closable separate work room is possible
  • Genuine kitchen: built-in oven + hob + dishwasher + decent counter and a window
  • A long built-in desk with an office chair already installed, plus a second desk in another room — workspace for two solved
  • Spacious, light living room with sofas and a dining table
  • Abramovich is between Herzl, Rothschild and Zhitomirsky — near the centre yet described as quiet and set back from the nightlife
  • Sherut taxis run along Herzl to the train every few minutes; Rishonim station ~29 min walk
  • All-in lands around ₪4,300 with the fallback estimate — comfortably inside the cap
  • ~25 min to Gedera
–
  • 'הבניין בתהליך תמ"א 38' — active structural renovation means drilling, scaffolding and dust, potentially all day, every day. For two remote workers this is the single biggest risk
  • 'ריהוט חלקי' — the ad refuses to list the furniture ('פירוט ימסר בטלפון'), so the desks and sofas in the photos may belong to the current tenant and leave with them
  • Floor 2 with no lift mentioned
  • Dated 1980s finish — functional and clean but not charming
  • Agency listing, so a broker fee is likely on a standard lease
?
  • FIRST QUESTION: what stage is the TAMA 38 work at, and will there be active construction during Sept–Nov? If yes, this drops to a skip
  • Get the exact furniture list in writing — specifically whether the built-in desk, chairs, sofas and bed stay
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability and whether the agency will do a short let (and what fee)
  • Ask for arnona and average bills — neither is stated
74
Best kitchen in the cluster + 7-min walk to Kiryat Motzkin train — but all-in lands right on the ₪4,500 line PURSUE
Kiryat Motzkin · מוצקין הותיקה · ₪3,500/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 55 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,450/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,500 + arnona ₪300 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
Freshly renovated 2.5-room on Sderot Weizmann 46, listed today. The kitchen is the standout of the whole Krayot batch: a ~3m gray shaker run with a generous white countertop, a built-in oven, a ceramic/induction hob, sink and space to actually work — exactly the 'two work surfaces' dream. The rooms are bright and white with a sofa; the ad lists sofa, wardrobe and double bed, so furnishing is real but sparse. Transport is the other big win: the ad claims 5 min to the Metronit and 7 min to Kiryat Motzkin station, from which trains reach Tel Aviv Center in ~1h20-1h30 every 30 minutes.
+
  • Genuinely cookable kitchen: built-in oven + hob + long countertop with real prep space
  • Walking distance to Kiryat Motzkin train station (direct TLV trains ~1h25, every 30 min) and to the Metronit
  • Renovated throughout — new bathroom, clean bright rooms, AC in each room
  • Listing posted 2026-07-16, so it is live right now
  • Arnona is low for the size (₪300/mo stated)
–
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,450 (₪3,500 + ₪300 arnona + ₪650 estimated bills) — essentially at the hard cap, with no headroom if bills run high
  • Furnishing looks thin: rooms photograph nearly empty apart from one sofa
  • Sderot Weizmann is Kiryat Motzkin's main boulevard — the ad claims a quiet environment, but the street itself carries traffic and the Metronit
  • 2.5 rooms means the second workspace is a corner, not a closable room
  • Photos 6-7 (dark corridor) look like a different set than the lived-in kitchen shot — the set may be mixed
?
  • Confirm ~2-month availability Sept-Nov 2026 (Yad2 listing, short-term not stated)
  • Ask for the actual monthly electricity/water/va'ad figure — if it exceeds ~₪700 this breaks the ₪4,500 cap
  • Confirm what furniture is actually included and whether a bed/mattress is in place
  • Verify the 7-min walk to the station and how much traffic noise reaches the flat (which floor, which side)
  • Ask whether the kitchen in the photos is this unit's

Afula · מרכז עפולה עלית · ₪2,500/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,150/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪2,500 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'מחיר כולל ארנונה !!' — arnona stated included; electricity/water not mentioned.
confirm short-term
A 2.5-room ground-floor unit with its own separate entrance and a covered wooden deck/yard, in a quiet part of Afula Illit. The kitchen is real and L-shaped: a freestanding cooker with oven and 4 gas burners plus counter on two runs, bright windows over the sink. Inside it is white, clean and furnished with two large sofas and a sideboard. The private entrance and shaded outdoor deck are a genuine asset for a hot September, and at ₪2500 including arnona it leaves a lot of budget over. The deck itself is rough-and-ready (corrugated roof, the boiler on show) rather than charming.
+
  • Real kitchen: L-shaped with two work surfaces, freestanding oven + 4 gas burners, good light
  • Private separate entrance + covered deck/yard — rare here, and valuable in the September heat
  • ₪2500 including arnona (ad: 'מחיר כולל ארנונה !!') — all-in likely ~₪2900–3000
  • Bright, clean and furnished (two large sofas, sideboard, ACs seen in two rooms)
  • Ad states quiet location ('במיקום שקט') and a 'מטבח איכותי'
  • Ground floor = no stairs; Afula = 825 direct bus to TLV ~1h45, ~20–25 min to Hazorea
–
  • 2.5 rooms — workspace is one desk spot plus the second sofa area, no separate closable office
  • The deck is utilitarian: corrugated roof, boiler on the wall, weathered decking
  • No bed visible in any of the 4 photos — the 'ריהוט מלא' claim is unverified for the bedroom
  • Ground floor + separate entrance can mean less privacy/security; also more street noise if the street is busy
  • Afula Illit is further from the centre than the other Afula options — check the walk to a supermarket
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability (short_term unknown)
  • Ask for bedroom photos and confirm a bed + wardrobe are included
  • Confirm electricity/water are extra on top of the arnona-inclusive ₪2500
  • Check whether the deck gets usable shade and whether it's private
  • Check walking distance to groceries and to the 825 bus stop

Karmiel · ₪2,850/mo rent · 3 rooms · 86 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16

car recommendedborder areaconfirm short-term
A 3-room flat, one flight up, furnished komplet, at ₪2,850. The kitchen is the real thing: 4-burner gas hob, built-in oven, microwave, granite L-counter with a workable run. The standout extra is a glazed closed balcony looking into treetops — a natural second work spot, which matters for two people on laptops all day. A third room has a built-in desk/counter under a window with a hill view. The furniture is dated 'grandma' style (green velour sofa, brown veneer units) but everything is clean, light and complete.
+
  • 3 rooms = a genuine closable second workspace, plus a glazed sun-balcony with tree views as a third spot
  • Proper kitchen: gas hob + built-in oven + microwave + granite L-counter with real prep space
  • 'מרוהטת קומפלט' — fully furnished, plus an in-flat storage room; they'd only bring the desk
  • Bright throughout; green/tree outlook from the balcony and the study room = calm, pleasant to be in

  • Karmiel: train to Tel Aviv (~2h), local buses, malls and supermarkets in town; Hazorea ~50 min drive
–

  • Neither arnona nor bills are stated, so the all-in is an estimate (~₪850/mo assumed on top)
  • One flight of stairs, no lift mentioned
  • Agency listing (Keller Williams) — no direct landlord contact, agent fee likely on a long lease
  • Street/neighbourhood quiet level not verifiable from the photos; HaBroshim is residential but unconfirmed
  • Karmiel→TLV ~2h each way and the station is at the northern entrance, not walkable from most of town

  • Check the possible duplicate 02805312bd394dfb (Ilanot 20, Tene, 170 m away, same price/rooms) — no photos available on that one to compare
  • Ask whether the closed balcony is usable in September heat (glazed balconies bake without AC)

rent ₪2,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is genuinely good and confirmed in the photos: a full white gas range (oven + 4-burner cooktop), a dark-granite counter with real prep space, a fridge, glass-front cabinets and a breakfast-bar peninsula — proper for a couple who cooks most meals. The flat is a spacious, bright 3.5-room with terrazzo floors, reasonably furnished (sofas, bed, dining table, TV console) though listed 'partial' (extra furniture on request), with two bathrooms. Downsides: Tel Hanan is the older, more working-class part of Nesher (less green/quiet than Givat Amos), it is larger than a couple needs, and several photos (a staged living room, a modern gray kitchen, two phone screenshots) are a DIFFERENT unit — the real apartment is the older-but-functional one in photos 00–04. Close to the Technion and the new Metronit line; already vacant (entry Jan 2026).

  • Already vacant (entry Jan 2026) — flexible move-in

  • Tel Hanan is the older, less-green, more urban part of Nesher
  • Only 'partial' furnishing — extra furniture 'on request'; confirm what is actually included
  • Bigger (more rooms to heat/clean) than a couple needs

  • No parking noted; framed as annual/long-term

Gorgeous renovated 2.5-rm in Yafo D with the best kitchen in the cluster, ~₪4,100 all-in — but the building is mid-TAMA-38 construction PURSUE
Tel Aviv · יפו ד', גבעת התמרים · ₪3,400/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 52 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪4,250/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,400 + arnona ₪200 + est. utilities ₪650 · Yad2 field: ארנונה לחודשיים: 400 (= ₪200/mo); bills not otherwise stated
confirm short-term
no photos
Beautifully styled, bright, fully furnished 52sqm 2.5-room at Hinanit 7 (Yafo D / Givat HaTmarim) for ₪3,400 + ₪200 stated arnona. The kitchen is genuinely excellent for cooks: built-in cooktop, oven, long counters PLUS a big island/bar — best cooking setup of all 9 TLV listings. Red Line stops on Jerusalem Blvd (Mahrozet/HaBesht) are ~10-12 min walk with a direct ride to central TLV and Bat Yam. The catch: the building itself is undergoing TAMA-38 works (due to finish in ~1.5 years), so weekday construction noise is a real risk for two at-home remote workers, and the landlord asks a 3-month-rent bank guarantee.
+
  • Real cook's kitchen: cooktop + oven + island + generous counters (photo-verified) — top of cluster
  • Renovated, bright, tastefully furnished; genuinely a nice place to BE
  • 2.5 rooms / 52sqm — the half-room + daybed nook give two workable laptop spots (plus room for their own desk)
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,050-4,150 (3,400 + 200 arnona + ~₪450-550 est. bills) — comfortable headroom under ₪4,500
  • ~10-12 min walk to Red Line (Mahrozet/HaBesht) on Jerusalem Blvd; quiet small residential street
  • Washing machine, oven, fridge, microwave, Tami4, sofa, TV all included
–
  • Building is mid-TAMA-38 construction (~1.5y to completion) — likely daytime drilling/noise, the main threat for two remote workers home all day
  • Requires 3 months' rent bank guarantee — heavy friction for a 2-month stay
  • Bed has no mattress ('צריך לקנות מזרן') — they'd need to buy one for a short stay
  • Yafo D is a modest working-class pocket; fewer cafes/amenities than central TLV
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability and that the landlord accepts a short let (short_term only 'likely')
  • Ask directly: is TAMA construction active on the building now? What works are scheduled Sept-Nov? Visit on a weekday morning to hear it
  • Will the landlord waive/reduce the 3-month bank guarantee for a short stay, and provide a mattress (or deduct its cost)?
  • What do bills run (electricity, water, va'ad)? Confirm arnona ₪200/mo as listed
  • Nearest supermarket walk (Yafo D / Wolfson area) for vegan+GF staples
72

Karmiel · ארבל - יחיעם · ₪2,400/mo rent · 3 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪3,050/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,400 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated

Renovated 70 sqm on Yehiam St in Karmiel's quiet southern quarter: a long modern kitchen counter (3m+, large countertop oven; full cooktop not visible — verify), renovated bathroom, double bed, comfy big-sofa living room, and — rare in this cluster — a dedicated office room with desk and office chair, ideal for two remote workers. Floor 4 with open hill views over a calm residential street, and Karmiel's station (~3km) gives an hourly ~2h train to Tel Aviv. Currently tenant-occupied and tagged 'partial furniture' — nail down exactly what stays.
+
  • Dedicated office room with desk + office chair — best two-person workspace in the cluster
  • Renovated, bright and genuinely inviting (photo-verified, lived-in)
  • Huge kitchen counter/prep space plus a large countertop oven
  • Quiet residential street with open views from floor 4
  • Train town: roughly hourly ~2h to Tel Aviv; parking included; pets OK
  • All-in ≈ ₪3,100 (est.) — comfortable headroom

  • 'Partial furniture' tag + moving-out signs: the office desk, sofa or beds may leave with the tenant
  • Floor 4, elevator unknown — a walk-up is a real minus in September heat
  • Agency ad, short-term unknown — landlord agreement needed

?

  • Exactly which furniture stays (office desk/chair, sofa, beds) — ad is tagged 'partial furniture'
  • Confirm the cooktop/oven situation in the kitchen
  • Is there an elevator for floor 4?

Best in batch: bright 2-room 60sqm with a real kitchen counter, separate bedroom and two balconies at 3,400 PURSUE
Bat Yam · הנביאים · ₪3,400/mo rent · 2 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,400/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,400 + arnona ₪350 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A 60sqm 2-room flat on HaHashmonaim 67 in the Nevi'im neighbourhood, 1st floor, with three air directions and genuinely the best liveability here: a bright living room with a sofa and a wide glazed enclosed balcony, plus a small sun balcony, and a separate bedroom with a proper double bed. The kitchen is a long galley with a full run of counter and cabinets — real prep space, though no hob or oven is in the frame. All-in is 3,400 + 350 stated arnona + est. bills ≈ 4,400, under the cap, and central Bat Yam is a grid of quiet one-way residential streets rather than a main road.
+
  • Real kitchen: a long counter run with full cabinets — the generous prep space they want (2m+)
  • Two separate spaces — living room and a closable bedroom — so two people can work apart; the enclosed balcony is a third spot
  • Bright, calm and inviting; three air directions (W/N/S) matters for a hot September
  • 60sqm for 3,400 — much more room than the studios here at the same or higher price
  • Properly furnished: sofa, coffee table, double bed, wardrobe, washing machine
  • Renovated bathroom/shower; AC
  • Quiet residential street in the Nevi'im area, not a main road
  • All-in ~4,400 stays under the cap
–
  • No stovetop or oven visible in the kitchen photo — must be confirmed (the counter is there, so a portable hob is a workable fallback)
  • Dated building fabric — old terrazzo floors, cracked plaster around the bedroom window, old shutters
  • Arnona is the highest stated in the batch at 350/mo, eating most of the headroom under the cap
  • Not on the light-rail street — a walk or a bus to the nearest Red Line stop on Sderot HaAtzmaut
  • Broker listing (רי/מקס אקסטרה) — fee likely
  • A pinui-binui redevelopment scheme is in planning for the HaHashmonaim area — worth knowing, though irrelevant over 2 months
?
  • Confirm the kitchen has a hob and/or oven — the single decisive question
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (short_term unknown)
  • Check the walk time to the nearest Red Line stop and which buses serve HaHashmonaim to Tel Aviv
  • Ask what electricity/water run per month

The most livable-feeling place in the batch for the money. A large open living/kitchen space with a stone accent wall, a ~3m wood-brown kitchen run with a real counter, a hob under a hood and a dishwasher, plus a proper bedroom with wardrobe, double bed, ceiling fan and AC. Bright and genuinely inviting rather than staged-empty. Sderot Yerushalayim is a main artery in Kiryat Yam ד' with Metronit access and the C mall within walking distance; no train, so Tel Aviv means a Metronit-plus-train combination via Motzkin or Hof HaCarmel.
+
  • Real cookable kitchen: long counter run, hob with hood, dishwasher, fridge — plenty of prep space
  • Large open living area that reads bright and inviting, not cramped
  • Furnished with what matters: double bed, wardrobe, sofa, table

  • Good bus/Metronit access and walkable shopping (C mall) per the ad

confirm short-term
The most practically-equipped flat in the cluster: 4 rooms on the 4th floor with a lift, parking and a storage room, fully furnished. The kitchen is spacious and real — a long white run with a dark counter, a freestanding cooker with oven, plus a second counter surface by the door. Crucially two separate rooms already contain a desk and chair, which solves 'workspace for two' outright. Everything is 1980s: terrazzo floors, tired bathroom vanity, old wooden bedsteads. It works beautifully on paper and only moderately on the 'bright, inviting place to be' test.

Afula · מרכז העיר · ₪2,900/mo rent · 2 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,550/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A renovated, fully furnished 2-room unit right on Afula's Independence Square. The kitchen is proper: a long white counter with sink, a freestanding gas cooker with oven, fridge and upper cabinets, with a table and chair alongside. Bedroom has an iron bed and mirrored wardrobe, the living room a leather sofa, armchair and dining table, all on white marble-look floors and bright throughout. Location is the whole story: this is the centre of Afula, a short walk to the shuk, shops and the central bus station where the direct 825 to Tel Aviv leaves hourly. It is also the busiest square in the city, so quiet it is not.
+
  • Real kitchen: long white counter, freestanding gas cooker WITH oven, fridge, decent prep space
  • Fully furnished and immediate: bed, wardrobe, leather sofa, armchair, dining table + chairs
  • Renovated, bright, clean, marble-look floors, AC in bedroom and living room
  • Unbeatable walkability: on Kikar HaAtzmaut, minutes from the shuk, BIG Afula and the central bus station
  • Direct Egged 825 to TLV Central ~1h45–1h50 hourly from the adjacent central station — the best realistic TLV link in this cluster
  • ~20–25 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea

  • Only 2 rooms — one desk spot plus a dining table, no closable second workspace

  • ₪2900 + est. ₪650 bills ≈ ₪3550 all-in — no stated utilities to check it against
  • Ad is 90% agency boilerplate; almost no actual detail about the unit

63sqm, 3.5 rooms, fully furnished, directly opposite the beach — the kitchen window looks straight onto the sand (kitesurfers in the listing photo). The kitchen is genuinely cookable: freestanding cooker with oven, fridge, two long counter runs, cabinets, plus a dining table by the sea-view window. The catch: decor is deeply dated (floral wallpaper, heavy ornate sofas, old dressers) — clean and complete, not shabby, but far from the bright-modern ideal. 3.5 rooms mean real space for two separate work spots (small side room + living room, plus room for their own desk). Floor 4 with no elevator. All-in ≈ ₪3,125 (2,500 + stated arnona 125 + est. bills ~₪500). Quiet seaside street; Kiryat Haim rail ~15-20 min walk (or Metronit 3 / buses), TLV by train ~70-80 min; Hazorea family ~35km.

All-in lands around ₪2,950, which is ₪1,500/mo under cap.

landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
Posted this month and just renovated to a high standard: bright white rooms, a big glazed balcony with an open view over treetops, a new bathroom, new wardrobe and bed, and full furnishing. The ad states the price includes all bills except electricity and, crucially, entry on 30.9 — which lines up with a Sept–Nov stay better than anything else here. Balfour is a strong address: its own Red Line stop, Shufersal Deal at number 25, Balfour Market at 97, and a short walk to the beach. The kitchen is the open question — a clean new ~1.5m counter with sink, washer and small fridge, but no hob or oven in frame.
+
  • Entry date 30.9 matches the Sept–Nov window almost exactly
  • Just renovated (photos from July 2026) — bright, clean and genuinely inviting
  • Large windows and a balcony with an open green view; lots of daylight in every room
  • Ad states the price includes all bills except electricity → all-in ≈ 4,100, under the cap
  • Balfour has its own Red Line light-rail stop — direct to Tel Aviv
  • Shufersal Deal at Balfour 25 and Balfour Market at 97 — groceries on the street
  • Fully furnished: bed, wardrobe, white goods, new AC; new bathroom
  • 2 rooms — a second, separate space to work in
–
  • No hob or oven visible in the kitchen — the counter is modest (~1.5m) and shared with a sink and washing machine; prep space is adequate at best, not the 'dream' two-surface kitchen
  • Only a small under-counter fridge
  • An entry date of 30.9 usually signals the landlord wants a 12-month lease, not 2 months — the biggest risk here
  • Balfour is a busy through-street with the light rail on it — check which side the flat faces (the photos suggest it looks over trees, not the road)
  • Broker listing (מרכז הנדלן) — fee likely
?
  • Ask whether a hob/oven exists or can be added — decisive
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability; the 30.9 entry is promising but the term is unstated
  • Ask what electricity typically runs per month
  • Confirm which way the flat faces relative to Balfour Street and the light-rail track
68
Nesher (Ofakim) 2-room, ₪2,495 fully furnished — best value; real counter + gas cooktop but only a toaster-oven MAYBE
Nesher · ₪2,495/mo rent · 2.0 rooms · 60.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪3,145/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,495 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is a functional service-porch galley: a long dark-granite counter with good prep space, a tabletop 2-burner gas hob, a toaster-oven, microwave, fridge and washing machine by a bright window — workable for cooking, though there is no full built-in oven, which matters for a couple who bakes and roasts (and cooks vegan/GF). It is the cheapest listing and fully furnished (two sofas, bed, dining table), a right-sized 2-room (separate bedroom + living), bright with terrazzo floors, low running costs (arnona ₪0), and a 10-minute walk to the Technion. Finishes are older and it is the top floor (4/4). Photos look coherent to one apartment apart from one possibly-different bathroom.
+
  • Best value: ₪2,495, fully furnished, arnona ₪0 (low running cost)
  • Real prep space: long granite counter + gas cooktop + toaster-oven + fridge + washing machine
  • Couple-sized 2-room (separate bedroom + furnished living room)
  • Bright; 10-minute walk to the Technion
  • Move-in ready (immediate entry)
–
  • No full oven — only a tabletop 2-burner gas hob plus a toaster-oven
  • Top floor (4/4) — can be hot in September; older cabinets/finish
  • One bathroom photo looks like a different unit — verify
  • Framed as annual/long-term
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month furnished short-term let is OK (ad is annual-only)
  • Confirm the cooking setup: is the gas hob fixed, and is there any real oven?
  • Ask about summer heat/AC on the top floor and quiet on Ofakim St
68
Dream-kitchen kibbutz house with perfect Sept-Oct dates — everything hinges on the unlisted price MAYBE
גבעת חיים איחוד · גבעת חיים איחוד · ₪100/mo rent · 6 rooms · 350 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪750/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪100 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
no photos
Nearly-new 160sqm (built) family house in Kibbutz Givat Haim Ihud, sublet Aug 5 - Nov 2 with partial-period entry allowed — the best date fit in the cluster. Photos show a spectacular cook's kitchen (full gas cooker + large oven, dishwasher, very long counters, walls of teal cabinetry, garden view), two warm living areas, 4 bedrooms (workspace solved), and field views; washer+dryer and AC in every room. The ₪100 'price' is a placeholder — actual rent unknown, and a house like this could easily exceed the ₪4,500 all-in cap. Transport is the other catch: no walkable train (Hadera West ~5-6km), Route-4 corridor buses (e.g., 852/703) are workable but slow — realistically car life, ~1h to TLV.
+
  • Best kitchen seen in this cluster: big oven + gas hob, dishwasher, massive counter and storage — ideal for daily vegan+GF cooking
  • Beautiful, new, warm and bright house — an exceptional place to spend two indoor months
  • Dates fit perfectly: Aug 5 - Nov 2, and 'part of the period' explicitly OK
  • 4 bedrooms + two living areas = effortless double remote-work setup
  • Kibbutz quiet, fields view, parking; ~40 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea family
  • Fully equipped: washer + dryer, TVs, ceiling fans, AC everywhere
–
  • Price unknown (₪100 placeholder) — market rent for such a house could be far over budget
  • Car essentially required: train ~5-6km (Hadera West), buses to TLV slow/infrequent — Roy's weekly TLV trips ~1h+ each way
  • Groceries = kibbutz minimarket + drives to Hadera; no walkable shuk/supermarket variety
  • Far from Gedera-side family (~1h+)
  • House size means higher electricity (AC) and possibly high arnona — ask what subletter pays
?
  • FIRST QUESTION: asking price, and what it includes (arnona? water/electricity? gardening?) — if ≤ ~₪4,000-4,500 all-in this becomes a top candidate
  • Confirm a Sept-Oct (or Sept-Nov 2) 2-month slice is fine (ad says partial OK — reconfirm)
  • Any house-minding duties (garden, pets, plants)?
  • Check Route-4 bus reality at commute hours (Givat Haim junction stop) and whether they'd budget a rental car from the savings
  • Kibbutz guest/renter arrangements (gate access, use of kibbutz pool/dining if any)
68
Modern renovated 3-rooms in a quiet 2-story building (Tel Amal) — desk, dining table, built-in oven; missing only a cooktop and a stronger neighborhood MAYBE
Haifa · תל עמל · ₪2,800/mo rent · 3 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪3,575/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,800 + arnona ₪125 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
no photos
50sqm 3 small rooms at HaYarden 32, Tel Amal, private landlord, ₪2,800 + ₪125 stated arnona (~₪3,350 est. all-in). Photos show a clean modern renovation: gray corner kitchen with genuine two-run prep counter and built-in stainless oven, fridge and microwave; simple fresh bedroom; a laundry room with green tree views; glossy floors throughout. The ad's furniture list is unusually work-friendly — writing desk, expandable dining table + chairs, 2 wardrobes, AC in every room, sofa, TV. Notably absent from both list and photos: a stovetop. Building is small (2 floors) on a quiet green street — but Tel Amal belongs to the weak Halisa–Tel Amal–Neve Paz cluster and everything runs on buses.
+
  • Real kitchen prep space (corner L, two runs) + built-in oven + fridge + microwave
  • Work-ready furnishing: writing desk AND expandable dining table — two workspaces solvable in 3 rooms
  • Modern, clean, bright renovation — pleasant to spend indoor months in; AC in every room
  • Quiet: small 2-story building, trees outside, described as accessible-but-quiet area
  • ~₪3,350 est. all-in leaves ~₪1,100 headroom; arnona stated at only ₪125/mo
–
  • No stovetop listed or visible — they'd need to buy a portable hob (negotiable per their rules since prep space is real)
  • Tel Amal: socio-economically weak cluster; groceries = local minimarkets, no walkable shuk
  • Bus-dependent — no walkable train/Carmelit; Tel Aviv trips mean bus + train via downtown or Lev HaMifratz
  • 3 rooms inside 50sqm = each room is small
  • Short-term unknown — needs landlord agreement (score capped accordingly)
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (not stated)
  • Confirm there is (or may be added) a cooktop; ask if landlord would supply one
  • Verify utilities ~₪400 est. (electricity/water/vaad) on top of rent+arnona
  • Check bus frequency from Yad LaBanim corridor toward Merkaz HaShmona train for the TLV commute
