Yokneam · רמת יקנעם · ₪3,400/mo rent · 3 rooms · 74 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪4,050/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,400 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
car recommendedconfirm short-term
Agency 3-room, 74sqm, top floor (3/3, walkup) renovated 'from the foundation', facing the Jezreel Valley — the view photo is gorgeous and the flat is clean and simply but adequately furnished: corner sofa + recliner + TV console, wooden dining table + chairs, galley kitchen with gas hob, freestanding oven, new fridge, microwave, washer, plus a fold-out wall table. Its trump card for this couple: Yokneam is ~10 min drive from Kibbutz Hazorea (family), in a green, quiet town next to a shopping center. But at 3,400 rent + est. arnona ~₪350 + bills, all-in lands ≈ ₪4,150-4,250 — inside budget with almost no headroom, and Tel Aviv is a ~2h direct 826 bus (no train in town).
+
  • Family proximity: ~10 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea — best in cluster by far
  • Real kitchen: gas hob + oven + moderate counter + fold-out prep/eat table; new fridge
  • 3 rooms — dedicated office room for two remote workers, plus dining table
  • Renovated from scratch, bright, with open Jezreel Valley views; quiet green residential area near shopping center
  • Immediate entrance; pets allowed; building shelter
–
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,150-4,250 — nearly exhausts the ₪4,500 cap (arnona/bills estimated, not stated)
  • Tel Aviv access is the weakest here: no train in town (Kfar Yehoshua station ~12km), 826 bus ~2h; car strongly helps
  • Top-floor walkup under the roof — heat in Sept + 3 flights of stairs
  • Furnishing is functional-plain (no desk; simple pieces), less charming than the photos' view suggests
  • Agency ad; commission on a short let would break the budget
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (not stated)
  • Get exact arnona + typical bills to nail the all-in number vs the ₪4,500 cap
  • Ask the agency about fee on 2 months — potentially decisive
  • Check roof insulation/AC coverage for the top floor in September
  • Verify Roy's realistic TLV commute (826 schedule) vs staying near Afula's train+bus hub

A spacious 1970s-era 4-room on Sderot Weizmann: a long living room, a separate dining room, an enclosed sun porch, several bedrooms with beds and wardrobes, and one room already set up with a built-in desk. The kitchen is large and eat-in with a ~3m counter run under a window — dated orange cabinets, but generous prep space, which is what matters here. Everything is tired but clean and light. Central Motzkin means the Metronit and Kiryat Motzkin station (direct TLV trains ~1h25) are both close.
+
  • Large eat-in kitchen with a long counter run — real prep space for two cooks
  • 4 rooms: a closable work room (one already has a desk) plus a second spot, ideal for two remote workers
  • Spacious and bright with a sun porch; separate dining room
  • Central Kiryat Motzkin — Metronit plus Kiryat Motzkin train station (TLV ~1h25, every 30 min)

The kitchen has a ~2m dark counter with sink, microwave and fridge — real prep space, but no hob and no oven visible. The headline feature is the cost: the ad states ₪3,100 covering rent, arnona, water, electricity, va'ad and internet, which is ₪1,400 under the cap with zero bill risk.

  • Brand new and spotless: new bathroom, new mattresses, new AC

  • Real (if modest) counter run, fridge and microwave already in place

  • No hob and no oven visible — cooking would need a portable hob purchase (budget headroom covers it, but confirm)

  • Kiryat Ata has no train station — Tel Aviv means bus/Metronit to Motzkin or Merkazit HaKrayot, then train

Renovated kitchen: real counter + 2 burners, no oven SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,187/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,837/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,187 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen clears their bar as a GOOD (not perfect) fit: a clean, renovated white kitchen with a genuine full-length countertop split into two usable work zones by the sink, plus a working two-burner portable hotplate already provided — real cookable prep space, exactly the "portable stovetop" they said they'd accept. The only gap is the oven (none present), which they explicitly called negotiable, and there is counter room to add a countertop oven — though the fridge looks like a small under-counter unit, a real limit for a couple cooking most meals. The rest is a compact, fully furnished but dated 1-bedroom in Wadi Salib / downtown Haifa: livable for two months bringing only a desk, with an enclosed-balcony dining nook as a second work spot. Location is the standout for a car-free couple — flat downtown, a short walk to the Haifa Center–HaShmona train (coastal line to Tel Aviv) and Metronit BRT lines 1 & 2, with the Talpiot produce/vegan shuk reachable uphill.
+
  • Renovated kitchen with a genuine full-length counter and two work zones (left + right of the sink) — real prep space, not a token ledge
  • A working two-burner portable hotplate is already provided (the exact 'portable stovetop' they said they'd accept)
  • Under budget at ~₪4,187/mo (cap ₪4,500)
  • Excellent transit for a no-car couple: short flat walk to Haifa Center–HaShmona train (coastal line to Tel Aviv) + Metronit BRT lines 1 & 2; Carmelit funicular nearby
  • Fully furnished — sofa bed, second couch, wardrobe, plus a dining nook that can double as a 2nd work spot; they only need to bring the desk
  • Talpiot Market (fresh-produce shuk with a vegan food shop) reachable; convenient to Kibbutz Hazorea family (~30–40 min)
  • Kitchen and bathroom are clean and bright
–
  • No oven at all — they'd need to buy a countertop oven (there is counter room for one)
  • Fridge appears to be a small under-counter unit — limited cold storage for a couple cooking most of their food
  • Dated, tired living areas: yellowed/aged curtains, old cork/terrazzo floors, older AC and light fixtures
  • Small apartment; the two-person work setup is cramped and the second spot is a frosted-glass enclosed-balcony nook
  • Groceries and the shuk are uphill — Haifa is steep, so it's a climb or a short Metronit/Carmelit hop from the door
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,837/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month stay in the Sept–Nov window is bookable and ask for the monthly/long-stay rate (availability listed fully open 61/61, min-nights up to 10, but a 2-month booking is unconfirmed)
  • Ask the host the fridge/freezer size and whether adding a countertop oven is OK
  • Confirm the sleeping setup — separate bedroom with a real bed, or is the sofa bed the main bed?
  • Verify street-level quiet: Wadi Salib is a redeveloping neighborhood and some blocks near the port/downtown can be noisier than the 'quiet and safe' description
  • Check GF-specific grocery availability (vegan is well covered in Haifa; gluten-free is less certain)

confirm short-term
no photos
Agency ad for a newly built yehidat diur near the city center and the college exit. Photos show everything new: freestanding gas stove WITH oven, single counter run with sink + microwave, gray sofa, small white dining table with two chairs, double bed, sliding wardrobe, stacked washer+dryer, wall TV. Rent includes arnona, va'ad and gas, plus capped water (₪100/2mo) and electricity (₪200/2mo) — realistic total ≈ ₪2,700–2,850 once two people working from home with AC blow past the tiny electricity cap. Clean and bright but genuinely small for a couple spending workdays inside.
+
  • Full real cooking: new freestanding gas hob + oven, new fridge, microwave — rare at this price
  • Effectively all-in pricing: arnona/va'ad/gas on the owner, utilities capped-then-metered; ≈ ₪2,700-2,850 realistic
  • Everything brand new and clean (first tenants were recent); Tadiran AC, bars, parking, storage
  • Central: walkable to city center shops and supermarkets; claimed quiet location
  • Immediate entrance; agency used to fast turnarounds
–
  • 38sqm / 2 small rooms — cramped for two simultaneous laptop workers; only one real work spot (small dining table)
  • Counter/prep space is moderate: one run, part occupied by microwave and kettle
  • Electricity cap of ₪200 per TWO months is unrealistically low for Sept AC use — overage on tenant, budget +₪150-250/mo
  • No pets, emphatic 'serious only' tone; agency commission likely
  • Sterile look — little warmth, curtains-closed photos make brightness unverifiable
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (not stated)
  • Ask agency about fee on a 2-month let — can kill the value
  • Verify room for their own desk in the bedroom or living space
  • Ask what floor 'ק-1' means here (ground vs first) and window orientation/natural light
66
Rothschild 75, Petah Tikva — the strongest apartment here (3.5 rooms, real desk, Sept sublet) but all-in ₪4,550, ₪50 over on the estimate alone SKIP
Petah Tikva · לב המושבה / מרכז העיר · ₪3,800/mo rent · 3.5 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,550/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,800 + arnona ₪100 + est. utilities ₪650
A genuine Sept-onward sublet of a fully furnished 3.5-room apartment in central Petah Tikva, from a tenant relocating. It is the only listing in this batch that matches the actual use case: it already has a dedicated office room with a desk chair and printer, a double bed, a single bed, a sofa, a washing machine, storage and a closable extra room — two people could work here without fighting over a table. The kitchen is a real lived-in one: a proper counter run either side of the sink with a freestanding 2-burner hob (no built-in oven). Rear-facing and quiet, pets welcome, 7 min walk to the light rail, shops and the shuk downstairs. It is dated — old terrazzo floors, scuffed walls, messy photos — and it lands ₪50 over the cap.
+
  • 3.5 rooms fully furnished, including a real work room with a desk and printer plus a second closable room — the best workspace-for-two in the batch by far
  • Explicit sublet with a Sept 1 start ('סאבלט מספטמבר עד דצמבר') — the only listing here that actually matches the dates, no need to talk a landlord into a short lease
  • Real kitchen: continuous counter with prep space on both sides of the sink, 2-burner hob, upper and open storage
  • Central PT: the ad's '7 min walk from the light rail' is credible — the Red Line runs from the PT terminus straight to Tel Aviv centre, plus buses everywhere. Strong for Roy's weekly Tel Aviv trips
  • Groceries are excellent: the PT shuk, Super Tal and Super-Pharm are all downstairs
  • Rear-facing and stated quiet ('דירה עורפית שקטה ונעימה'), spacious rooms, pets welcome, arnona only ₪100/mo per Yad2
  • Furnished right down to a work desk, printer, washing machine, storage and a hive shelf — they'd need to bring almost nothing
–
  • All-in ~₪4,550 (₪3,800 + ₪100 stated arnona + ₪650 EST. bills) — ₪50 over the ₪4,500 cap. The overage rests ENTIRELY on the ₪650 estimate: the ad states no bills figure, and at actual bills of ₪600 or less this lands inside the cap. This is the one listing in the batch where the cap decision is an artefact of an unverified number
  • Dated: old terrazzo floors, scuffed and marked walls, tired fittings — the photos are messy/lived-in, not a bright inviting showpiece
  • No oven (2-burner hob only), though the counter space makes a portable one viable
  • The sublet runs 1.9.26 → 1.1.27 with NO extension option — that's 4 months, and they want ~2. She may need the whole window covered
  • Rothschild is PT's main commercial street; the quiet claim depends on the flat being rear-facing as stated
  • Only 4 photos, none of the 3rd/4th room or the bathroom
?
  • GET THE ACTUAL BILLS — this is the whole decision. Ask for the average monthly electricity + water + vaad. Under ~₪600 and this becomes the batch's clear pursue
  • Ask whether a ~2-month Sept–Nov slice works, or whether she needs all 4 months covered — 4 months at this price may still be worth it
  • Ask for photos of the remaining rooms and the bathroom, and to confirm which room is the office
  • Verify the ₪100/mo arnona (suspiciously low for 3.5 rooms in PT — a realistic figure is ₪300–400/mo, which would push it further over)
  • Confirm the flat is rear-facing off Rothschild and check street noise
  • Clarify whether it's a sublet or a contract assignment ('החלפה בחוזה') and whether the owners must approve
66
Renovated furnished 3-room in Akko, 3 min from the sea, ₪3,000 — solid granite kitchen with gas hob PURSUE
Akko · ₪3,000/mo rent · 3 rooms · 67 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,650/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
border areaconfirm short-term
A 1st-floor 3-room in Akko renovated about five years ago, 190m from the beach. The kitchen is a real one: wood-look cabinets with a dark granite counter running an L, a gas hob, sink and a toaster oven — good prep space. Furnished with beds (a double plus two singles), sofas, table and fridge; the living room is plain but tidy with a stone feature wall and a new-ish bathroom. Akko Central has direct hourly trains to Tel Aviv in ~1h35.
+
  • Real cookable kitchen: granite counter with a proper run, gas hob, sink
  • Genuinely furnished — beds, sofas, table, fridge — and renovated ~5 years ago
  • 190m / 3 min walk from the sea; Sept-Oct swimming is a real perk
  • All-in ≈ ₪3,650, well under cap
  • Akko station has direct trains to Tel Aviv (~1h35, hourly)
–
  • Photos read gloomy — artificial light, plain brown covered sofas, no natural-light shot of the living room
  • One bedroom is set up with two single beds, so the layout suits a couple only after rearranging
  • Akko station is ~2km from Netiv Hativat Golani — not a walk, so a bus or bike is needed for train trips
  • 1st floor near a through-road: street noise and privacy worth checking
  • No oven visible beyond a counter-top toaster oven
?
  • Confirm ~2-month availability Sept-Nov 2026 (short-term not stated)
  • Ask for daylight photos and confirm which rooms get sun
  • Confirm the bed configuration — can the two singles be swapped for a double?
  • Check the walk/bus time from Golani 8 to Akko railway station
  • Check the walkable grocery options (vegan/GF) in this part of Akko
65
Airbnb studio by Talpiot Market — built-in oven + hotplate, TWO desks already set up, but tired walls and a single bed MAYBE
Haifa · ₪3,070/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,070/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪3,070 + arnona n/a · Airbnb monthly booking — utilities are included in the nightly/monthly rate by platform convention; no separate arnona or bills · missing arnona not stated
A 38sqm Airbnb studio steps from Talpiot Market, priced at ₪3,070/mo with utilities inherently included. Unusually for this batch it has a built-in oven plus a 2-plate electric hotplate on a ~1.5-2m counter, and — the rare find — a big desk with an office chair AND a second small desk, i.e. a work spot each. Also a jacuzzi tub and rain shower. The catch: the photos show cream walls that are visibly scuffed and dingy, and only a single bed.
+
  • Built-in oven + 2-plate hotplate + ~1.5-2m counter — a real if modest cooking setup
  • Two desks and an office chair already in place: workspace for two, solved out of the box
  • Airbnb monthly booking = utilities included and a 2-month stay is the native use case; no lease, no guarantor, no checks
  • ₪3,070/mo all-in — well under the cap, leaves ~₪1,400/mo of budget for a car or co-working
  • Talpiot Market on the doorstep: fresh produce for vegan/GF cooking, restaurants, bars
  • Jacuzzi tub + separate rain shower; strong AC; floor plan published by the host (38sqm total, 22sqm main room)
–
  • Walls are scuffed, patchy and cream-yellow in the photos — reads tired and dim, not the 'modern and bright' the listing claims
  • Only a single bed visible in the photos — a real problem for a couple
  • One 22sqm room for two people working all day, with no separation
  • Talpiot Market street noise; Airbnb monthly rates can jump for a Sept–Nov window
?
  • Confirm there is a DOUBLE bed — the photos only show a single
  • Get the actual Sept–Nov 2026 monthly quote including the Airbnb service fee and any cleaning fee
  • Ask whether the host will repaint / freshen the walls for a 2-month booking
  • Ask whether a proper cooktop can replace the 2-plate hotplate
65
Lod Ganei Aviv studio + 30m sun terrace, ₪2,200 + ₪125 arnona — walkable train, but the kitchenette has no cooking surface PURSUE
Lod · גני אביב · ₪2,200/mo rent · 1 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪2,975/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,200 + arnona ₪125 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
Bright, renovated, white-walled studio with a big bed, a large wardrobe, AC and a 30 sqm private roof terrace, at an all-in of roughly ₪2,975 — the cheapest serious option in the cluster. Ganei Aviv has its own train station on the Tel Aviv–Rishon line, a real advantage for weekly TLV trips. The catch is the kitchen: a ~2m counter run with sink and cabinets, but no hob and no oven visible.
+
  • All-in ~₪2,975 — roughly ₪1,500/mo under budget, which funds a car, co-working, or a proper portable induction hob and countertop oven
  • Lod Ganei Aviv train station is in the neighbourhood — a genuinely walkable station on the TLV–Rishon line
  • Bright, clean, freshly renovated, big windows; not shabby
  • A ~2m counter run with cabinets — the prep space is real even if the appliances are not
  • 30 sqm private sun terrace
  • Ganei Aviv is a large planned residential neighbourhood with local services, not a rough street
  • Close to Gedera (~30 min)
–
  • No stovetop and no oven visible — the ad only says 'מטבחון' (kitchenette). Buying both is affordable at this price, but confirm the counter can host them and the electrics can take it
  • Furnished only with a bed and wardrobe — no sofa, no dining table, no armchair. Nowhere comfortable to sit that isn't the bed
  • One open room: no separate workspace, and only their own portable desk to work at
  • Photos date from Dec 2025 — the listing may be stale or the unit re-let
  • Lod has weaker walkable grocery/vegan options than Rishon or Rehovot
?
  • Ask whether a hob and oven can be added, or whether the landlord will install them
  • Ask if any seating/table furniture can be provided — this is the difference between liveable and bare
  • Confirm the listing is still live (photos are ~7 months old) and that ~2 months Sept–Nov works
  • Time the actual walk from Aharon Lublin to Lod–Ganei Aviv station and check TLV frequency
  • Ask the average electricity bill
64
Cheap 3-room in Kiryat Eliezer near the sea — decent kitchen, only partly furnished MAYBENO LONGER AVAILABLEno longer found on source
Haifa · קרית אליעזר · ₪3,200/mo rent · 3.0 rooms · 65.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14

rent ₪3,200 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated

The kitchen looks workable: dark wood cabinets with a real counter run and upper storage, and the montage also shows a freestanding white oven/cooktop unit, so there is genuine prep space and likely both oven and cooktop — confirm in person. The flat is a lived-in 65 m² 3-room with a spacious living room and two balconies, but the listing says furniture is only PARTIAL (some chairs, table, wardrobes, a chest) — you may need to add a proper bed and seating. Finishes are dated (a wood-panel accent wall) and, again, the photos mix several apartments.
+
  • Cheapest 3-room here at ₪3,200, well under budget
  • Kitchen has real cabinets + counter and likely oven + cooktop
  • Spacious living room, two balconies, near the sea; ~13-min walk to Bat Galim station (train to TA) and ~5 min to Metronit
  • 65 m² / 3 rooms gives room for two to work
–
  • Only PARTIALLY furnished — bed/seating adequacy for a 2-month stay is uncertain
  • Dated decor; montage photos obscure the exact unit and its appliances
  • Kitchen oven presence not certain from the real-unit photos
?
  • Confirm the landlord will do a ~2-month short-term let (listing is annual, entry 17/7/2026)
  • Pin down exactly what furniture is included — is there a real bed, sofa, and dining setup?
  • Confirm oven + cooktop and counter space with a video/visit
  • Ask about parking (listed on-street) and street quiet

Bright, fully-furnished 4-room with the best kitchen in the cluster — priciest, same border risk SKIPNO LONGER AVAILABLEno longer found on source
Nahariya · מרכז · ₪3,500/mo rent · 4.0 rooms · 90.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪4,550/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,500 + arnona ₪400 + est. utilities ₪650
border areaconfirm short-term
The kitchen is excellent and exactly this couple's kind of space: a modern wood-and-white run with a built-in stainless oven, a 4-burner gas cooktop, range hood, large fridge, a decent counter and open shelving — well equipped for cooking most meals. The apartment is the brightest and most move-in-ready of the three: 90 m2, an airy furnished living/dining, furnished bedrooms, two bathrooms (one ensuite), elevator and shelter in the building, and a green park outlook a short walk from the sea, the train and the central bus station. The downsides are price (3,500, the cluster's highest, though under budget) and the same Nahariya border location, which saw rocket sirens and injuries in 2026.
+
  • Best kitchen of the three: built-in oven + 4-burner gas cooktop + range hood + large fridge + counter and open shelving
  • Fully furnished, bright and genuinely move-in ready for a 2-month stay
  • 90 m2, 3 air directions, two bathrooms (one ensuite)
  • Elevator and shelter in the building
  • Walkable to the sea, the train station and the central bus station — strong for a car-free couple
  • Green / park view, described as airy and renovated
–
  • Most expensive in the cluster at 3,500/mo (still under budget, but ~1,100/mo more than the cheapest option here)
  • Kitchen counter is a single-wall run — no large second prep surface
  • Still a border-town setting despite being walkable and central
  • Ad padded with the same shared 'promo gallery' photos as the other two Nahariya listings, so some images may not be this exact unit
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,550/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month SHORT-TERM let is possible — the listing is an annual lease (title says 8, features say 11 payments/yr)
  • Border/siren caveat: Nahariya is near the Lebanon border with rocket sirens and impacts recorded in 2026 — verify current security for Sept-Nov and comfort with siren risk before pursuing
  • Confirm which furnished photos reflect the actual unit versus the shared promo gallery
64
Nesher: nicest-condition cheap 2-room, real vintage kitchen — but no train in town MAYBE
Nesher · גבעת נשר · ₪2,500/mo rent · 2 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪3,320/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,500 + arnona ₪170 + est. utilities ₪650 · ארנונה 340 ש"ח לחודשים (₪170/month, stated in ad)
confirm short-term
no photos
Staged, clean and genuinely inviting 45sqm 2-room on HaMeyasdim 4 (Givat Nesher), floor 1 of a small 4-tenant building, ₪2,500 + stated ₪170/mo arnona → all-in ≈ ₪3,000. The retro green-tiled kitchen is real: built-in oven, counters plus an in-kitchen dining table for extra prep space. The structural weakness is Nesher itself: no train, so Tel Aviv means bus down HaMeyasdim to Merkazit HaMifratz then train (~1h50m door-to-door), and HaMeyasdim is the neighborhood's main street — floor-1 traffic noise needs checking. Closest listing in the cluster to Kibbutz Hazorea (~20-25 min drive).
+
  • Brightest, most inviting interior in the cluster — staged, tidy, feels like a home
  • Real cookable kitchen: built-in oven, counter space, kitchen dining table as second work surface
  • All-in ≈ ₪3,000 — big budget headroom
  • Ad says 'partial furniture' but photos show bed, dining table + 4 chairs, TV unit
  • Small quiet building (4 tenants), pets allowed, immediate entry
  • Walkable groceries along HaMeyasdim; closest cluster listing to Hazorea family (~20-25 min drive)
–
  • No train in Nesher — TLV commute is bus + train, ~1h50m+ each way (worst commute pattern for Roy)
  • HaMeyasdim is Givat Nesher's main street and this is floor 1 — traffic noise risk
  • 45sqm 2 rooms — two simultaneous workspaces will be tight
  • Stovetop/burners not clearly visible in the kitchen photo (oven yes)
  • 'Partial furniture' — no sofa visible; verify what stays
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short_term only 'likely')
  • Is there a stovetop/gas hob? Which furniture exactly stays ('ריהוט חלקי')?
  • Visit at rush hour — how loud is HaMeyasdim from inside on floor 1?
  • Homeless 'duplicate' 55cffbb0a48d975e is a DIFFERENT apartment (floor 4, different kitchen) — ignore

Renovated lake-view 3-room in Tiberias' Migdalei HaMelachim vacation tower, ~₪3,600 all-in — cluster's best transport, but verify the kitchen has a stove/oven MAYBE
Tiberias · ₪2,200/mo rent · 3 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪2,920/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,200 + arnona n/a + utilities ₪720 (stated in ad) · בנוסף למחיר השכירות יש תשלום ועד בניין 720 שח לחודש הכולל בתוכו כבלים ואינטרנט, נקיון שטחי ציבור, שימוש במעליות, שמירה 24/7 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
Freshly renovated 8th-floor apartment with a direct Kinneret-view balcony in Migdalei HaMelachim (Gdud Barak 17), which is an 18-unit vacation-apartment building on the lakefront with 24/7 guard and parking. New furniture (some still wrapped), elegant bathroom, and a genuinely generous L-shaped kitchen counter — the best prep space in the Tiberias listings — but NO cooktop or oven is visible in any photo, and for a couple that cooks everything this must be verified before anything else. ₪2,200 rent + ₪720 vaad (includes internet/cables, cleaning, elevators, 24/7 security) + ~₪215 arnona + electricity/water ≈ ₪3,600 all-in. A 12-min walk to Tiberias CBS puts the hourly direct 836 to Tel Aviv (~2h10m) in reach — as good as this cluster gets, though still a long haul.
+
  • Generous renovated kitchen counters — big L-shaped run, lots of cabinets — excellent prep space IF cooking appliances exist
  • All-new furniture and finishes; bright, clean, genuinely nice to be in
  • Kinneret-view balcony with seating; 8th floor lifts it above street noise
  • Vacation-apartment building = landlord accustomed to short stays — 2-month Sept-Nov deal is structurally plausible
  • Vaad ₪720 includes internet/cables, cleaning, elevator, 24/7 security and free parking; all-in ~₪3,600 leaves budget headroom
  • Best transport in the cluster: ~12 min walk to CBS, hourly direct 836 to Tel Aviv (~2h10m); city center/shuk ~10 min walk
  • 3 rooms — small second bedroom can be a closable office for their desk
–
  • No cooktop or oven visible in any photo — if the kitchen is appliance-less, that's a fail for this couple unless the landlord equips it (portable induction covers stovetop, but oven too = both missing)
  • Vacation building on the hotel/promenade strip: Sept-Oct is Tiberias' Tishrei-holiday high season — vacationer noise/turnover likely, and the cheap monthly rate through Sukkot needs explicit confirmation
  • Gdud Barak is the main lakefront road — traffic and promenade activity below (mitigated by floor 8)
  • Tel Aviv is still 2.5h+ door-to-door each way; no train anywhere in the region
  • Tiberias at -200m is brutally hot in September — they'll live on the AC (present throughout)
  • Agency listing; no dining table visible in photos — workspace surfaces need checking
?
  • FIRST: verify the kitchen has a working cooktop and oven (photos show neither) — dealbreaker question
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability at the monthly rate, explicitly including the Rosh Hashanah/Sukkot weeks (vacation building — nightly-rate temptation is real)
  • Ask about vacationer noise in the building during Tishrei holidays
  • Confirm arnona amount and who pays it, and typical electricity bills with AC
  • Confirm a dining/work table is (or can be) included — only a small console visible
  • Check which minimarket/supermarket is closest for their vegan/GF staples (center has minimarkets; larger supermarkets up the hill)
64
3-room fully furnished at ₪3,200 right at the Netanya shuk — best structure and groceries, but only 2 tired photos PURSUE
Netanya · צפון מזרח מרכז העיר · ₪3,200/mo rent · 3 rooms · 52 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,075/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,200 + arnona ₪225 + est. utilities ₪650 · Yad2 field: ארנונה לחודשיים 450 → ₪225/mo. Utilities not stated in ad.
confirm short-term
Structurally the strongest fit in the cluster: 3 rooms, fully furnished (fridge, sofa, table, chairs, bed, wardrobes, washing machine), air-conditioned, at ₪3,200 with arnona stated at ₪225/mo — all-in lands near ₪3,875. Sitting at the entrance to the Netanya covered market (~170 stalls of fresh produce) is a genuine advantage for a vegan + gluten-free couple who cook daily, and the central bus station next door is the transport hub. The catch is evidence: only two blurry video-frame photos, no kitchen shot at all, and the interior looks dated and currently lived-in by an older household.
+
  • 3 rooms — a separate closable room for one worker plus a spacious living/dining area for the other
  • ₪3,200 with arnona stated at ₪225/mo; all-in ~₪3,875 leaves real budget headroom
  • Fully furnished per ad: fridge, sofa, table, chairs, bed, wardrobes, washing machine — they only bring the desk
  • At the entrance to the Netanya shuk (Shoham 10, ~170 stalls) — fresh produce for vegan/GF cooking is a 2-minute walk
  • Next to the central bus station: direct buses to Tel Aviv and bus 25 down Herzl to the train station
  • Ad confirms air conditioning and calls the living room spacious
–
  • Only 2 photos, both low-quality video frames — condition is poorly evidenced and the furniture looks dated and worn
  • NO kitchen photo whatsoever — the #1 non-negotiable is completely unverified
  • Shuk entrance + central bus station area is the noisiest, grimiest part of Netanya; market deliveries start before dawn
  • Photos suggest the flat is currently occupied, so what's actually left in it come September is unclear
  • Netanya train station is ~2.5–3 km away, bus-only from here
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
  • Demand kitchen photos before anything else — oven? stovetop? counter space?
  • Ask which floor, whether there's an elevator, and which way the windows face (shuk side vs. back)
  • Ask about morning market noise and confirm the furniture stays

Cheapest central 4-room with a genuinely cook-ready kitchen — but on the Lebanon border MAYBE

rent ₪2,400 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
border areaconfirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is a real asset for a couple who cooks most meals: full white cabinetry, a long stone countertop with sink, a freestanding oven plus a 4-burner gas cooktop, and extra old cabinets kept in the back for heavy storage. The rest is a spacious, quiet 80 m2 older-style 4-room (terrazzo floors, big windows, 4 air directions) with a renovated bathroom and an enclosed balcony that has been used as an extra room / work corner. At 2,400 it is the cheapest and best-value unit in the cluster. The catch is the location: Nahariya sits ~10 km from Lebanon and took rocket sirens and impacts through 2026.
+
  • Cheapest in the cluster at 2,400/mo, far under the 4,500 budget
  • Good, genuinely usable kitchen: oven + 4-burner gas cooktop, long stone counter, sink, and lots of cabinet storage
  • Spacious 80 m2, 4 air directions, described as quiet and central, close to the sea and the Ga'aton
  • Enclosed balcony used as an extra room + work corner — helpful for two laptops
  • Public bomb shelter 15 m from the door (relevant given the border-siren risk)
  • Private covered parking under the building
  • Landlord furnishes to tenant needs (fridge, oven+gas cooktop, sofa, leather armchair, table, computer chair, double mattress, single bed, wardrobes)
–
  • Top floor (3 of 3), older-style finish — kitchen and bath are renovated but floors/feel are dated
  • Photos show a mostly empty apartment; actual furniture depends on the landlord delivering what is promised
  • No in-apartment safe room (mamad) — only the nearby public shelter
  • Ad's photo set is padded with a shared 'promo gallery' (sea view, terrazzo living rooms, artificial-grass balcony) that also appears in the other two Nahariya ads, so several images likely are NOT this unit
?
  • Confirm the landlord will do a ~2-month SHORT-TERM let — the listing is an annual lease (12 payments/yr)
  • Border/siren caveat: Nahariya is ~10 km from Lebanon and had rocket sirens and impacts in 2026 (rockets off the beach, injuries in town) — verify the security posture for Sept-Nov and the couple's comfort with siren risk before pursuing
  • Confirm exactly which furniture/appliances will be supplied, and whether the building has a working elevator

Renovated, fully-furnished Rishon 3-rooms with a real oven+stove — but ~₪4,850 all-in busts the budget SKIP
Rishon LeZion · רמת אליהו · ₪4,200/mo rent · 3 rooms · 65 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪4,850/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,200 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
Freshly renovated 65sqm 3-rooms on a quiet side street in Ramat Eliyahu, west Rishon. Photos show the best real kitchen of the apartment candidates here: full freestanding oven+4-burner stove, compact but genuine counters (main run + corner section), plus washer AND dryer, two sofas, dining table, two bedrooms (one with a small desk), AC in every room. Transit is solid: direct TLV buses (19/163/264) nearby and Moshe Dayan station ~2.7km (9-18 min train to TLV). The killer: ₪4,200 rent + arnona (est. ~₪250, not stated) + bills ≈ ₪4,850 all-in — over the ₪4,500 cap — and it's a standard rental needing landlord agreement for ~2 months.
+
  • Real cookable kitchen: full oven + stovetop, decent prep space for its size
  • Renovated and fully furnished incl. washer + dryer — rare at this level
  • 3 rooms = separate workspace options (desk in bedroom 2 + dining table)
  • Quiet small residential street (3-story buildings), parking, shelter next door, pets OK
  • Direct buses to Tel Aviv + Moshe Dayan station (9-18 min train) — good TLV commute
  • Ramat Eliyahu commercial center + supermarkets walkable; ~20-25 min drive to Gedera family
–
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,850 (4,200 + ~250 arnona est. + ~400 bills) — exceeds ₪4,500 budget unless negotiated
  • Standard rental: ~2-month Sept-Nov stay requires explicit landlord agreement ('likely' only)
  • All photos taken at night — daylight brightness unverified; windows look modest
  • No elevator (floor 2 of 3 — minor)
  • Ramat Eliyahu is a working-class area mid-renewal; fine but not charming
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,850/mo all-in exceeds the ₪4,500 cap (auto-downgraded to skip)
?
  • REQUIRED: confirm landlord agrees to a ~2-month furnished stay inside Sept-Nov 2026
  • Negotiate: at ₪3,800-3,900 rent the all-in fits the ₪4,500 cap — the 'partners OK' ad suggests flexibility
  • Ask actual arnona (not stated; ~₪250/mo estimated from town+65sqm) and typical bills
  • Visit in daylight to judge brightness; check what's downstairs/street noise
  • Confirm bed sizes and that the desk stays

The nicest surprise in the cluster: freshly renovated 54 sqm 3-room on a quiet street in Canaan — real shaker-style kitchen with a full oven + gas range and ~2.5m of counter, bright living room with dining table, sofas and hill views, two clean furnished bedrooms. All-in ≈ ₪2,575 (arnona stated ₪125/mo), leaving ~₪1,900/mo of headroom that could fund co-working or occasional car days. The catch is Safed itself: ~3h to Tel Aviv (direct 846 bus only ~2x/day, otherwise bus 361 to Karmiel + train) and Canaan is a steep hill neighborhood.
+
  • Real kitchen: full oven + gas range + generous counter — best cooking setup in the cluster after Karmiel/Yehiam
  • Renovated, clean, bright — genuinely inviting for people home all day
  • Cheapest all-in in the cluster (≈ ₪2,575) — ~₪1,900/mo budget headroom
  • Quiet building on a residential street; AC; dining table works for two laptops
  • Safed's mountain climate is a real plus for an end-of-summer stay
–

Best kitchen-per-shekel in this batch: 3,200 incl. arnona+water+internet, long granite counter, hotplates — but deep SE Tel Aviv with bus-only transport PURSUE
Tel Aviv · כפיר, נווה כפיר · ₪3,200/mo rent · 1 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,200/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪3,200 · Ad text: 'המחיר כולל ארנונה מים ואינטרנט חשמל בנפרד' — 3,200 includes arnona, water and internet; electricity is billed separately (not stated, so no figure invented).
confirm short-term
A renovated ground-level studio unit on Mahal 38 in the Kfir / Neve Kfir pocket of Kfar Shalem, south-east Tel Aviv. The photos show the strongest kitchen in the batch: a full wall of cabinets, a long granite worktop with sink, upper cabinets and a real fridge — plus the ad states electric hotplates and a microwave. Clean, decent natural light, own entrance onto a small yard, and a bar counter with stools that could serve as a work spot. The catch is the location: Kfar Shalem is bus-only for reaching central Tel Aviv, and the furnishing is plain and dated with no bed mentioned or shown.
+
  • Real cookable kitchen: full cabinet run with a long granite countertop, a second bar-counter work surface, upper storage and a proper fridge — this is the 'two work surfaces' case, not a token kitchenette
  • Electric hotplates and microwave stated; only an oven is missing (they can buy a countertop oven and still have prep space)
  • 3,200 stated as including arnona, water AND internet — electricity separate (~250-350/mo for a couple in Sept), so all-in ≈ 3,500. Roughly 1,000/mo under the cap = real money left for a car or co-working
  • Clean and reasonably bright; renovated 'like new' per the ad, AC stated
  • Own entrance with a small private outdoor area; free street parking (a genuine rarity in Tel Aviv)
  • Ad explicitly claims a quiet, pleasant street — Mahal is a residential edge street of the Kfir neighbourhood, not a main artery
  • Still inside Tel Aviv municipal limits, so Roy's weekly TLV interests are a bus ride, not an intercity trip
  • Reasonable road access south to Gedera via Derech Bar-Lev / Ayalon
–
  • Transport is the weak point: no light rail nearby (Red Line's Karlibach/Shalma stations are far); it's buses on Derech Bar-Lev / Moshe Dayan, ~25-35 min to central TLV
  • Kfar Shalem / Kfir is a rough, under-invested south-east neighbourhood under an urban-renewal plan — verify the immediate street rather than trusting the ad's 'quiet and pleasant'
  • No bed is listed in the ad or visible in any photo — the ad lists fridge, wardrobe, microwave, hotplates and a storage cabinet, but no bed. For a couple with no furniture this is a real gap
  • No oven
  • Furniture is sparse and dated: a bar table with folding stools is the only seating/eating surface — no sofa, no desk, no proper table for two laptops
  • Aesthetically plain (dark brown cabinetry, beige floor tiles, old-style bathroom) — functional rather than inviting
  • 'No pets' and immediate entry stated
  • Walkable groceries unverified — the ad only says 'close to shopping centres'
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short_term unknown; ad says immediate entry)
  • Ask directly whether a double bed is included, or whether the couple must supply one
  • Ask for the exact electricity arrangement (separate meter? estimated monthly?)
  • Ask whether an oven can be added or if a countertop oven is permitted
  • Ask what the street is like at night and where the nearest supermarket is
  • Check the walk/bus time to the nearest Red Line station in practice

Living room is tired (worn sofas under throw covers); bedrooms plain with a new-in-nylon mattress. Quiet street, parking to the door, shelter adjacent. confirm short-term
no photos
33 sqm 2-room apartment on Agrippas directly opposite Mahane Yehuda shuk (West Jerusalem), floor 6 with elevator, renovated ~5 years ago and it shows in the single collage photo: long black-cabinet kitchen counter with generous prep space, bright dining corner with city-view windows, fresh modern bathroom, twin beds, washer and dryer. Entry exactly Sept 1 matches their window, and two rooms give real dual-workspace options. The math is the problem: ₪4,500 rent + stated bills of ₪250-350 (+₪25 stated arnona) lands ~₪4,775-4,875 all-in, over the cap, and this is a J-m agency ad, so a broker fee would sting badly on a 2-month stay. No cooktop or oven is visible in the photo (real counter means a portable is acceptable, but verify). Shuk-facing Agrippas means daytime market bustle and night bar noise — floor 6 and window orientation are the mitigation; light rail (Mahane Yehuda stop) is ~3 min away with Navon station ~2 stops for the 35-40 min Tel Aviv train.
+
  • Best kitchen of the cluster by photo: long counter, ample cabinets — real prep space (cooktop/oven pending verification)
  • 2 rooms: separate bedroom enables two simultaneous work calls; dining table plus room for their own desk
  • Bright, renovated ~5 years ago, modern bathroom, washer AND dryer, AC, elevator to floor 6
  • Directly opposite the shuk: unbeatable vegan/GF produce access, health-food stores on Agrippas
  • Light rail ~3 min walk, ~2 stops (or ~15 min walk) to Navon station; 35-40 min trains to TLV every 30 min
  • Entry date exactly Sept 1 — aligns with their window
–
  • Over budget all-in: 4,500 rent + stated ₪250-350 bills (+₪25 arnona) ≈ ₪4,775-4,875/mo
  • Agency listing (J-m תיווך) — a typical one-month broker fee would be brutal amortized over a 2-month stay
  • Opposite the shuk on Agrippas: early-morning deliveries, buses, and Machneyuda-area nightlife noise; floor 6 helps but doesn't eliminate it
  • Only 1 photo (collage): cooktop/oven not visible, overall condition only partly verifiable
  • Short-term not explicit — a ~2-month Sept–Nov stay needs landlord/agency agreement
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,825/mo all-in exceeds the ₪4,500 cap (auto-downgraded to skip)
?

  • Verify a stovetop and oven exist (not visible in the photo) and check fridge size
  • Try negotiating rent or an all-in package ≤ ₪4,500 given the over-cap total
  • Ask whether windows face the shuk or the back, and what floor-6 night noise is like Thu-Fri

  • ₪3,100 stated all-in (arnona, water, electricity) — no bill surprises, ~₪1,400/mo of budget left over

Haifa · ₪2,300/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪2,300/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪2,300 · Yad2 ad: 'שכירות: 2,300 בחודש כולל חשבונות (חשמל, מים וארנונה)'
confirm short-term
The best kitchen-per-shekel in the cluster: a modern L-shaped kitchen with a genuine built-in oven AND a ceramic/induction cooktop, with usable corner counter space — at ₪2,300/mo stated to include electricity, water and arnona. Clean, recent black-and-wood renovation, fully furnished with a real bed and headboard, sofa, TV, washing machine and fridge, and a bomb shelter in the building. The problem is where it is: HaHashmal St in Neve Paz, an eastern bay-facing neighbourhood known for industrial air pollution.
+
  • Genuine built-in oven + cooktop + L-shaped counter — the full cooking setup, rare at this price
  • ₪2,300/mo stated all-in (electricity, water, arnona) — the cheapest real all-in in the cluster; ~₪2,200/mo of budget left over for a car and co-working
  • Recently and properly renovated ('משופצת עד היסוד'), clean modern finishes
  • Genuinely furnished: real double bed with headboard, sofa, TV, wardrobe, fridge, washing machine
  • Bomb shelter in the building; bus and grocery within walking distance per the ad
  • No broker fee ('ללא תיווך')
–
  • HaHashmal St in Neve Paz is specifically known for suffering the Haifa Bay industrial air pollution — a real problem for two people who are indoors all day with the windows open in Sept–Oct heat
  • Neve Paz is a 1970s neighbourhood-rehabilitation project, characterised by steep slopes and lots of stairs; modest and not an inviting place to walk around
  • No desk, dining table or obvious work surface anywhere in the photos — they'd be working off the sofa plus their own desk
  • 2 rooms, small; living room is cramped
  • No train within reach; buses only, and it's far from the German Colony / Massada scene
?
  • Check the live air-quality readings for the Neve Paz / Haifa Bay area before committing (govmap air-pollution layer)
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — short_term unknown
  • Get the 'includes everything' claim in writing
  • Ask whether a dining table can be added — there is nowhere to put two laptops
  • Ask how many stairs to the flat

confirm short-term
A renovated 1.5-room unit in Tzur Shalom, northern Kiryat Bialik, at ₪2,100 with bills explicitly included. The kitchen is better than the room count suggests: a ~2.5-3m white run with sink and genuinely generous counter space, plus fridge and washer. Furnished with a double bed, wardrobe, a small black leatherette sofa and coffee table. Clean, white and reasonably light — but small, and the washing machine sits in the living space. Metronit line 1 is close per the ad.
+
  • Bills included at ₪2,100 — the cheapest true all-in in the cluster and no bill risk
  • Long white counter run: real prep space despite the small unit
  • Renovated and clean; double bed, wardrobe, sofa, fridge, washer all in place
  • Close to Metronit line 1 per the ad, and the Nahariya-Be'er Sheva rail line serves the area via Kiryat Motzkin
  • Leaves ~₪2,400/mo of budget for a car and co-working
–
  • 1.5 rooms is tight for two people working from home all day — no second closable space
  • Washing machine sits in the living area, which eats the small footprint
  • Tzur Shalom borders a light-industry zone to the north — air quality and surroundings worth checking
  • No hob or oven visible in the photos (the ad claims an oven)
  • The unit is functional-white rather than warm; limited places to 'be' inside
?
  • Confirm ~2-month availability Sept-Nov 2026 (short-term not stated)
  • Confirm whether 'כולל חשבונות' also covers arnona, or only electricity/water
  • Confirm the oven/hob claim with a photo
  • Ask how close the industrial zone actually is and whether there is noise/smell
  • Check for a library or co-working within walking distance, given only one work spot
62
Karmiel Morad HaGay: small 40 m² 2-room, cheap and quiet with a green valley view, dated but complete MAYBE
Karmiel · אשכול · ₪2,500/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪2,650/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪2,500 + arnona ₪150 · Ad: 'ארנונה: כ-300 ש"ח לחודשיים' = ₪150/mo. No figure given for electricity/water.
car recommendedborder areaconfirm short-term
40 m² on the first floor at ₪2,500 + a stated ₪150/mo arnona — one of the cheapest workable options here, and the only one already flagged short_term 'likely'. The kitchen is small but real: 4-burner gas hob, small built-in oven, microwave, an L-counter plus a bar ledge with stools — modest prep space, not generous. Views from the windows are green hills and treetops and the street looks calm. Furniture is complete but dated (dark veneer wall units, an old-style bedroom set). Washer and dryer included.
+
  • All-in ≈₪3,300 (₪2,500 + ₪150 arnona + est. bills) — cheap, leaves ~₪1,200/mo for a car
  • Kitchen has both a hob and a small oven, plus an L-counter and a bar ledge — workable, if tight
  • short_term already assessed as 'likely' — the best short-term odds in the cluster
  • Green hill/tree view from the windows; quiet-looking street with a garden below
  • Fully furnished incl. washer AND dryer; first floor only
  • Karmiel: train to TLV (~2h), buses, in-town shopping; Hazorea ~50 min
–
  • 40 m², 2 rooms — genuinely small for two people working from home all day; one workspace at best
  • Dated, brown, uninviting furniture; 'renovated' is a stretch given the photos
  • Kitchen counter is modest — fine for prep, not the 'big countertop' dream
  • Bills not stated (only arnona) — electricity in Sept AC season is on them
  • Agency listing (RE/MAX) with boilerplate text; the flat's specifics come from the amenity list, not the description
  • Karmiel→TLV ~2h each way; station not walkable from Eshkol
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability and whether the ₪2,500 holds for a short let
  • Ask for the electricity/water estimate; arnona is stated at ₪300/2 months
  • Ask whether the second room can hold a desk, or if the bar ledge is the only work surface
  • Check possible duplicates a91cda2586b3c747 and c3e07c44b1d5d49c (same town/price/rooms) — photos differ from both, so they look like distinct units

Real-but-modest Hadar kitchen: no oven, tight for two to work SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,478/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪5,128/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,478 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen is genuine but modest: a full-size Electra fridge/freezer, a real granite countertop split into two short prep runs, good cabinet storage, and a provided 2-burner portable hotplate — but no oven of any kind. That clears the "token kitchenette" bar (there's real prep space and a proper fridge), yet counter room is tight for a couple who cook most meals, and they'd have to add a countertop oven that would compete for that space. Location is a strength for cooking and car-free living: lower Hadar / Wadi Nisnas in Haifa, with the fresh-produce shuk and supermarkets walkable and Metronit/Carmelit/train links to Tel Aviv. The big drawback for these two is workspace — it's effectively one combined living/sleeping room with only a tiny table beyond the single desk they bring.
+
  • Genuine granite counter with two short prep runs + full-size fridge/freezer + a 2-burner portable hotplate already provided — real cooking is possible
  • Excellent for vegan/GF home cooking: Wadi Nisnas fresh-produce market and Hadar supermarkets within walking distance
  • Central Hadar location is car-free friendly — Metronit BRT, Carmelit funicular, and downtown train station reachable, with frequent trains/buses to Tel Aviv
  • Fully furnished for a 2-month stay (bed, dining set, TV, clothes rack, washing machine); ground floor with a small planted garden entrance
  • Price 4,478/mo within budget; Airbnb with 1-night min and full availability across the 61-day window (short-term friendly)
–
  • No oven at all — only a portable double hotplate; adding a countertop oven would eat into the already-modest prep space
  • Counter/prep space is modest (two short runs) — tight for a couple who cook most of their food
  • Weak workspace for two remote workers: just a tiny dining table plus the one desk they bring, in what looks like a single combined living/sleeping room with no separate spot
  • Basic, small and a bit dated; bathroom is worn (chipped door) and finishes are simple
  • Hadar/Wadi Nisnas is a dense, lively urban area and this is a ground-floor unit, so quiet and privacy are only moderate
  • Over budget: ≈₪5,128/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Confirm the exact ~2-month dates within Sept-Nov are open before committing (calendar shows full availability now, but re-check)
  • Ask the host whether a small countertop oven could be added, and whether there's room for it without losing prep space
  • Verify real street-noise level (ground floor on an urban street) and how much daytime light the main room gets for working
  • Ask whether both can comfortably work/take video calls at once — is there any second usable surface?
  • Clarify whether the '2 rooms' is a true separate bedroom or one combined space (photos suggest combined)

Kiryat Ekron Keren HaYesod — cheapest, closest to Gedera family, fully furnished (cooktop, no full oven) MAYBE

rent ₪3,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
border areaconfirm short-term
no photos
KITCHEN per the listing is a gas cooktop plus a toaster oven (no full built-in oven) and a fridge — workable for a couple who cooks, but the no-oven limitation matters for vegan/GF baking and roasting, and no reliable photo actually shows this unit's kitchen (the modern white kitchen shot is a recycled cross-listing image, and a black rooftop kitchen shot is a different unit entirely). What is genuinely shown is a recently renovated 37m² unit with fresh (if garish) marble-panel walls, glossy floors, a bedroom with a 4-door wardrobe, and a furnished living room. Its real appeal is price and location: ₪3,000 all-inclusive is the cheapest here and Kiryat Ekron is a quiet small town ~10 min from the Gedera family.
+
  • ₪3,000 including arnona — by far the cheapest in the cluster (cheaper is better for this couple)
  • Very close to the Gedera family (~10 min) and adjacent to Rehovot (~10 min) — quiet small-town setting they'd value
  • Fully furnished (sofa, table + chairs, bed + 4-door closet, fridge) and recently renovated
  • Has a real gas cooktop for daily cooking
–
  • No full oven — only a toaster oven; a real limitation for a couple who cooks most meals and may bake
  • This unit's actual kitchen is not reliably shown in any photo (several photos are recycled/other-unit carousel images), so counter/prep space is unknown
  • Smallest unit (37m²); tight for two people working from laptops, no desk shown
  • Kiryat Ekron has no train of its own — transit runs via Rehovot; more car-dependent with fewer specialty (vegan/GF) grocery options in town
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month (Sept–Nov 2026) short-term let is possible — listing is annual-oriented
  • Ask for real photos of THIS kitchen and confirm counter space, the cooktop, and whether any oven is available
  • Given a possible no-car scenario, check bus/train connections to Rehovot and grocery options within walking distance
  • Confirm what furniture actually stays and the condition (photo set mixes several different units)

₪2,200 ALL-IN renovated Wadi Nisnas studio with loft — unbeatable cost/location, but tiny for two remote workers MAYBE
Haifa · ואדי נסנאס · ₪2,200/mo rent · 1 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪2,200/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪2,200 · המחיר 2200 ש"ח כולל מיסים מים וחשמל
confirm short-term
no photos
Renovated ~30sqm one-room + sleeping gallery on Shivat Tzion, on the edge of Wadi Nisnas. Photos show a clean white studio: gray sofa, small round table, wardrobe, loft mattress, and a kitchenette with sink, portable electric double hob, small under-counter oven, mini-fridge and microwave. Location is the star: Wadi Nisnas market (superb produce/legumes/tahini for vegan-GF cooking, open Saturdays) is steps away and Merkaz HaShmona train station is ~11 min on foot — the best train access in this cluster for Roy's Tel Aviv weeks. But one small counter section and a mini-fridge make heavy daily cooking for two a squeeze, and two laptop workstations in 30sqm is tight.
+
  • ₪2,200 includes arnona, water AND electricity — cheapest real all-in in the cluster, ~₪2,300/mo headroom (funds co-working, which fixes the workspace problem)
  • ~11 min walk to Merkaz HaShmona train station (direct trains to Tel Aviv ~1h) plus Metronit and many bus lines nearby
  • Wadi Nisnas market around the corner — outstanding fresh produce for vegan/GF cooking
  • Renovated, clean, bright white finish; furnished (sofa, wardrobe, small table, loft bed); AC
  • Ad says long-term possible — flexible landlord, short stint looks feasible (available since 1.7)
–
  • 30sqm studio for a couple who are both home-working and home-cooking most meals — very tight
  • Kitchenette: one short counter run, portable hob and small oven, mini-fridge — cookable but cramped for 'most meals' cooking
  • Sleeping is a loft mattress up a ladder — fine for some, tiresome for two months
  • Shivat Tzion is a through-street with bus traffic — expect street noise on floor 1
  • Listing photos appear to mix two similar kitchen setups — verify what the actual unit contains
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (ad implies flexibility but doesn't state it)
  • Confirm fridge size and whether the under-counter oven seen in photos is actually in this unit (ad lists only electric hob, fridge, microwave)
  • Ask which floor/side the window faces — street side vs. back matters a lot for noise here
  • Check ceiling height under/over the gallery for comfortable desk placement

Spacious, clean studio on HaShomer 30 (Yalag, lower Hadar), 3rd floor, two aspects and a green/bay-glimpse view. The kitchen alcove (windowed ex-balcony) has an L-shaped tiled counter — genuine prep space — plus tall fridge-freezer, microwave and washer; cooking is on a stated electric hotplate, no oven, no built-in hob. By their own rule that's negotiable since prep space is real. Furniture includes a work desk + chair (stated) and a small round table → two work spots feasible. Talpiot market is 2-3 minutes away (produce paradise for vegan/GF), Metronit 3/5 stops nearby, train ~25 min downhill walk. The block is gritty market-adjacent Hadar. All-in ≈ ₪2,400-2,500 (1,800 + est. arnona ~₪150-180 + bills ~₪450). Not a duplicate of the Shapira studio (photo-checked).

  • Real prep counter in a windowed kitchen alcove
  • Work desk + chair stated, plus side table → both can work
  • Talpiot market at the doorstep; Metronit 3/5 minutes away
  • Bright, airy, two aspects, sea-glimpse; clean and tidy
  • 40sqm — roomier than the other budget studios

  • Gritty Yalag/market block — daytime bustle, tired surroundings
  • 1.5 rooms: both working all day in one space
  • Checks required; private landlord, short-term unknown
  • Train is a 25-min downhill walk (uphill home)

Photos: clean gray-tile living room with sofa, ~2m kitchen counter with mosaic backsplash and a black portable 2-burner hob (no oven), renovated bathroom, bedroom with double bed + large wardrobe + ceiling fan. Ad states price includes bills ('המחיר כולל הכל'). Transit is bus-dependent: buses down to downtown/Lev HaMifratz (~10-15 min); no walkable train. All-in ≈ ₪2,200-2,300.   • Real ~2m counter + working 2-burner hob (oven addable with headroom)

Radak 12, German Colony — great location, oven + cooktop + 6-seat table per the ad, but ₪4,650 all-in breaks the cap SKIP
Haifa · המושבה הגרמנית · ₪4,000/mo rent · 3.0 rooms · 60.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,650/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A 60sqm 3-room on the top floor of a beautiful Templer-era stone building in the German Colony — arguably the best neighbourhood in Haifa for this couple. The ad lists exactly the right things: fridge, oven, electric cooktop, central AC, electric shutters, a big table with six upholstered chairs, TV unit, large bedroom wardrobe and a double bed. The blocker is money: ₪4,000 rent plus the ₪650 utilities estimate is ₪4,650 all-in, over the hard cap.
+
  • German Colony — Haifa's most walkable and pleasant neighbourhood: cafés, restaurants, the Bahai Gardens, and ~15 min walk to the Haifa Center–HaShmona train for Tel Aviv trips
  • Ad lists an oven AND an electric cooktop — a real cooking kitchen
  • Big table with six upholstered chairs — two comfortable work spots
  • 3 rooms, furnished: double bed, large wardrobe, partial living room, TV unit
  • Central AC, electric shutters, solar water heater; the building is a handsome Templer stone house
  • Top floor (3/3), quiet per the ad ('אזור הדירה נהדר בשקט ונוחות שלה')
–
  • ₪4,000 + est. ₪650 utilities = ₪4,650 all-in — OVER the hard ₪4,500 cap. Note the overage rests only on the ₪650 ESTIMATE; the ad states no utility figures (ארנונה לחודשיים: 0 = unstated)
  • No photo of the actual kitchen — the homeless photo set is padded with the 'similar ads' carousel (different bathrooms, different buildings) and only the first two exterior shots and one bedroom are clearly this unit
  • Only a 'double youth bed' ('מיטת נוער כפולה') — likely not a proper double for a couple
  • 'סלון חלקי' — partial living room furniture
  • Top floor with no lift, and 60sqm is modest for 3 rooms
  • German Colony is the most tourist-heavy part of Haifa — busy at weekends
?
  • Ask for actual arnona + electricity/water figures — if utilities come in under ₪500/mo this clears the cap and becomes a strong pursue; the ₪4,650 is estimate-driven, not stated
  • Better still, ask whether ₪3,800 is acceptable for a 2-month furnished let — that would clear the cap outright
  • Request real photos of the kitchen and the living room (the ad photos are largely carousel padding)
  • Ask whether the 'double youth bed' can be swapped for a proper double
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability

?

landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
A 1.5-room accessory unit in Kiryat Haim West with the single nicest outdoor space in the cluster: a private pergola-shaded terrace with potted plants and a bistro table. Inside is a clean bright studio with bed, wardrobe and TV, and the kitchen has a genuinely long white counter with a desk-height extension — real prep space. But there is no cooktop and no oven anywhere in the photos, just a sink. Kiryat Haim has its own railway station and the Metronit, plus the beach.
+
  • Long white kitchen counter with a run of usable prep space plus a desk-height extension — the counter half of the kitchen criterion is genuinely met
  • ₪2,700/mo stated all-in INCLUDING internet — ~₪1,800/mo of budget left over
  • Private pergola terrace with plants and a bistro table — a lovely place to sit through a hot September evening
  • Kiryat Haim railway station is in the neighbourhood — direct trains to Tel Aviv; Metronit and the beach both close ('קרוב לים ולמטרונית')
  • Clean, bright, well kept; screens and bars on the windows; furnished with bed, wardrobe, TV
  • Quiet suburban Kiryat Haim streets rather than a Hadar main road
–
  • NO cooktop and NO oven visible anywhere — a sink and cabinets only; they'd have to supply both
  • 1.5 rooms / one room — two people working all day in a single studio with no separation
  • No second desk or dining table indoors
  • Kiryat Haim is far from the Carmel and from Haifa's café/culture life — a commute for everything social
  • Kiryat Haim also sits in the Haifa Bay belt — check air quality
?
  • Ask whether a cooktop and oven are included or can be installed — with this counter it would become a strong option
  • Confirm the walking time to Kiryat Haim train station and to the Metronit stop
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — short_term unknown
  • Ask where the second person would work — is the desk-height counter extension usable?
  • Check the exact street and air-quality readings
60
Elifelet: the best kitchen in the cluster and a big open living space — but ₪4,300 in a moshav where a car is mandatory MAYBE
Elifelet · ₪4,300/mo rent · 2 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪4,300/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
A genuinely impressive 2-room unit: a large L-shaped granite kitchen with a gas hob, built-in oven, extractor hood, microwave and a big fridge — easily the most cookable kitchen here, with real counter on two runs. The living space is huge and open (dining table, leather sofa, TV), the bedroom is a proper double with AC, and there's a garden and parking. Ad says 'the price includes almost all the bills' and that it's flexible for serious tenants. The problem is arithmetic and geography: at ₪4,300 the budget is exhausted, and Elifelet is a moshav of ~800 on the Korazim plateau near Rosh Pina — a car isn't optional there, and there's no money left to rent one.
+
  • Best kitchen of the 16: large L-shaped granite counter on two runs, gas hob, built-in oven, hood, microwave, big fridge — the 'two work surfaces' dream
  • Big open living/dining space, fully furnished ('להגיע רק עם מזוודות'), garden and parking
  • Bills essentially included per the ad, so the ₪4,300 is close to true all-in (under the cap)
  • Price explicitly flexible for serious tenants — a 2-month prepaid let is exactly the kind of leverage that moves it
  • Deeply quiet rural setting; Hazorea ~1h15 drive
  • Sits south of the 33.0N border-exclusion strip (32.96N) — in scope
–
  • ₪4,300 eats the entire budget in the one location where a car is genuinely mandatory — no leftover to fund it
  • Elifelet is a moshav of ~800 near Rosh Pina: no walkable supermarket, no meaningful public transport, Tel Aviv is ~3h+ each way by bus
  • Low ceilings and shuttered/curtained windows in the photos — the space reads enclosed and artificially lit rather than bright
  • 'כמעט את כל החשבונות' — 'almost all' is doing a lot of work; if arnona or electricity is actually excluded, all-in breaches the ₪4,500 cap
  • 'מתאימה לשותפים ומשפחות צעירות' — marketed to sharers/young families, so a 2-month couple may not be the target
  • Agency listing (Vered Nadlan)
?
  • Pin down exactly which bills are included ('almost all') — arnona? electricity? water? This decides whether it's under or over the cap
  • Push the stated price flexibility hard: at ~₪3,600–3,800 all-in this becomes a serious contender despite the car requirement
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
  • Ask how bright the unit really is by day and whether it's a ground/garden level
  • Price the reality: nearest groceries, and the true drive times to Rosh Pina, Karmiel and Hazorea
60
South Afula — ₪2200 for absolutely everything, clean and bright, real counter space but NO hob and NO oven MAYBE
Afula · דרום עפולה · ₪2,200/mo rent · 2 rooms · 35 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪2,200/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
The cheapest genuinely liveable option here: a tidy, bright 2-room unit (bedroom + living-kitchen) in a quiet, well-maintained building, with the ad stating the price includes all bills — ₪2200 all-in, less than half the cap. The kitchen has what matters most: about 2.5 m of clean white counter with real free prep space either side of the sink, plus fridge and microwave. What it does NOT have is any cooking appliance — no stovetop and no oven appear in the photo or the furniture list. That is normally fatal, but here the prep space is real and ₪2300/mo of leftover budget buys a very good induction hob and countertop oven twice over.
+
  • ₪2200 ALL-IN including every bill (ad: 'מחיר כולל הוצאות!!', 'כולל כל החשבונות') — ~₪2300/mo under cap
  • Real prep space: ~2.5 m of clean counter with usable surface on both sides of the sink
  • Clean, bright, tidy and modern-ish: white kitchen, good bathroom with glass shower, wardrobe + double bed + AC in the bedroom
  • Ad states a quiet, well-maintained building in a good location ('בניין מתוחזק ושקט במיקום מעולה')
  • Partial furniture listed: 3-door wardrobe, bed + mattress, washing machine, fridge, sofa
  • South Afula residential; 825 to TLV from town centre, ~20–25 min to Hazorea
–
  • NO stovetop and NO oven — neither in the photos nor in the ad's furniture list; the #1 criterion depends on them buying both
  • Small: bedroom + a combined living-kitchen; only 4 photos and no proper living-room shot
  • 2 rooms — one work spot plus their own desk somewhere in the living-kitchen; no separate closable room
  • 'ריהוט חלקי' — partial furniture only; no dining table or desk mentioned
  • One of the 4 photos is just the agency logo, so really only 3 photos of the unit
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability (short_term unknown)
  • ASK FIRST: is there a hob/oven at all, and would the landlord allow a portable induction hob + countertop oven? This decides the listing
  • Ask for photos of the whole living-kitchen space and its dimensions — can two desks fit?
  • Confirm 'כולל כל החשבונות' has no consumption cap (they'll run AC hard in September)
  • Confirm which floor and whether the quiet claim holds

Furnished ground-floor couple's unit with private patio — but no visible stove/oven SKIPNO LONGER AVAILABLEno longer found on source
Kiryat Ata · קרית בנימין · ₪4,000/mo rent · 3.0 rooms · 70.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14

rent ₪4,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
The kitchen is clean and newish with an L-shaped white-cabinet layout, a good black marble counter and a double sink (decent prep space) — but no cooktop or oven is visible (there is an empty appliance niche), a real gap for a couple who cook most meals. Otherwise it is an appealing ground-floor unit explicitly aimed at a couple: a private separate entrance onto a patio, quiet, bright and genuinely furnished (sofa, dining table with chairs, a made-up bed, fridge). The main downsides are the ₪4,000 price (priciest of the four) and an annual-only (12-payment) term. Several photos are staged/stock shots shared with other listings, so the real setup should be confirmed.
+
  • Furnished and move-in ready: sofa, dining set with chairs, made-up bed, fridge
  • Private separate entrance + patio, ground floor — quiet and explicitly couple-oriented ('for a married couple')
  • Bright and freshly renovated
  • Good black-marble counter run and a double sink
  • Within budget (₪4,000 ≤ ₪4,500)
–
  • No visible cooktop or oven (empty appliance niche) — cooking capability unclear, and cooking is the couple's #1 priority
  • Most expensive of the four at ₪4,000
  • Annual-only lease (12 payments) — a poor fit for a ~2-month stay
  • Ground floor can bring privacy/security/noise trade-offs
  • Listing padded with staged/stock photos reused across the other listings
  • Kiryat Ata has no train; Tel Aviv ~1.5h+ (minor for remote workers)
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,650/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month short-term stay is possible despite the annual framing
  • CRUCIAL: confirm whether a stove/oven is provided or must be brought — the current kitchen shows none
  • Confirm which furniture stays
  • Ask about the patio, privacy, noise and neighbors on the ground floor
  • Check car-free access to groceries and to Kibbutz Hazorea

Kfar Saba (Eli Cohen) dated 2.5-room — real galley kitchen, under budget SKIPNO LONGER AVAILABLEno longer found on source
Kfar Saba · אלי כהן · ₪4,000/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 63.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪4,894/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona ₪244 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
The kitchen is a dated but genuine galley: a good long run of dark stone counter, a real sink, and ample white upper/lower cabinets — meaningfully more true prep/cooking space than a kitchenette, which suits a couple who cooks most meals. However no built-in cooktop or oven is clearly visible in the photos (only a countertop toaster-oven sits on the washing machine), and the ad says most furnishings/appliances are 'negotiable with the current tenant' — so what stays is a real question. The rest of the flat is lived-in and worn (older bathroom, some ceiling staining, cluttered) but it is roomier (63 m², 2.5 rooms), cheapest at 4,000, and the living room already has a desk-with-monitor workspace.
+
  • Real galley kitchen with a proper counter run, sink and lots of cabinets — best cooking base in this cluster
  • Cheapest option: 4,000/mo, 500 under budget; low arnona (~244/mo)
  • Roomier: 63 m², 2.5 rooms — space for two remote workers (desk + monitor already in living room)
  • Quiet, leafy central Kfar Saba (Geysot HaShiryon); good supermarkets nearby for vegan/GF shopping, walkable
  • Kfar Saba–Nordau train to Tel Aviv (~35–45 min); flexible entry date
–
  • No built-in cooktop or oven clearly visible; kitchen appliances 'negotiable with current tenant' — cooking setup is uncertain
  • Dated and worn — old bathroom with staining, cluttered/occupied, only partially furnished (2 closets, dresser, table)
  • No parking spot (fine without a car, hard if they get one); 3rd of 4 floors, elevator unconfirmed
  • Far from family in both Gedera (south) and Hazorea (north) — long drive/train either way
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,894/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • CONFIRM ~2-month short-term availability — listed as annual (12 payments); current tenant is still in, which may or may not enable a short sublet
  • Ask exactly which appliances stay: is there a working cooktop/hob and an oven, or only the countertop toaster-oven? This is the deciding question for this couple
  • Confirm elevator vs 3rd-floor walk-up, and quietness of Eli Cohen St specifically
  • Photos are heavily padded — 06, 07, 09, 14 etc. are other units; judged on the lived-in set 00–05

Tel Aviv · ניר אביב · ₪4,500/mo rent · 2 rooms · 55 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
₪4,500/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
55sqm 2-room at Herzfeld St (Nir Aviv, SE Tel Aviv near Derech HaTayasim and Ramat Gan National Park), renovated to a genuinely inviting standard: big bright living room, two sofas, good bedroom. Contract price ₪4,500 including everything except electricity. In-unit kitchen is only a kitchenette (small counter, mini-fridge, countertop oven, no proper cooktop visible), but the ad offers a 'huge additional kitchen' for +₪100/mo — the photo shows a full real kitchen (built-in oven, long counters, full fridge). With electricity (~₪250-350, two ACs in Sept heat) + the kitchen option, all-in lands ≈ ₪4,850-4,950 — ~8-10% over the ₪4,500 cap, which weighs the score down.
+
  • Most spacious and among the nicest-condition listings in the cluster — bright, fully furnished, 'hotel-level' renovation reads true in photos
  • ₪4,500 stated to include rent + arnona + water + va'ad (all but electricity) — cost certainty
  • +₪100/mo option unlocks a genuinely huge real kitchen (photo-verified: oven, long counters, full fridge)
  • 55sqm 2-room = easy two workspaces (their desk fits)
  • Quiet residential pocket; Ramat Gan National Park ~5 min away; building shelter
–
  • All-in plausibly exceeds ₪4,500 (≈₪4,850-4,950 with electricity + kitchen option) — over budget
  • In-unit kitchen alone fails their cooking bar: kitchenette with countertop oven, no real cooktop or prep space seen
  • 3rd floor, no elevator
  • No train/light-rail nearby — bus-dependent (~20-30 min to central TLV); Purple Line civil works around Moshe Dayan Rd run through ~Oct 2026 (street disruption, line NOT open during their stay)
  • Agency listing (Torgi) — extra party in a short-term negotiation; sink units visible in bedroom photos suggest a subdivided building
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short_term only 'likely'; agency ad)
  • The +₪100 'huge additional kitchen': private or shared? Where is it relative to the unit? Lockable? — this decides the listing
  • Confirm what exactly 'כולל הכל מלבד חשמל' covers (arnona, water, va'ad, internet?) and typical electricity bills with AC
  • Any agency fee for a 2-month let?
  • Check street works status on nearby Moshe Dayan Rd (Purple Line) for noise/access

The kitchen is the weak point for heavy cooks: a modest dark counter run under a bright 4th-floor window — what may be a small portable hob sits on the counter, and there's no oven. confirm short-term

Brand-new, genuinely beautiful wood-ceiling unit on a quiet Golan moshav 8 min from Katzrin: fully furnished (corner sofa, dining table, quality double bedroom), AC throughout, and rent includes arnona, water, internet and electricity up to ₪500/2mo — roughly ₪3,450-3,650 all-in with moshav fees. Kitchen has an induction cooktop, hood, fridge and a real counter run but no oven visible or mentioned. The dealbreakers for this couple: transport to Tel Aviv is effectively car-only (~2.5h drive; bus = Katzrin 843 once a day ~3h15m or via Tiberias ~4h), and it sits ~12 km from the Israel-Syria ceasefire line — mid-2026 is calm with US-brokered security talks ongoing, but the front is unresolved (IDF active in the buffer zone; nearby Katzrin took direct rocket hits in 2024), which collides with their 'not near borders' preference.
+
  • Condition is exceptional: new, bright, inviting — easily the nicest interior in this cluster; explicitly pitched at a couple
  • Near all-in pricing: arnona, water, internet included + electricity up to ₪500/2 months — ~₪3,450-3,650/mo total, under budget
  • Fully furnished: corner sofa + TV, dining table, double bed + wardrobe, outdoor dining porch, AC in every room
  • Very quiet moshav setting; Golan elevation (~400m) is meaningfully cooler than Tiberias in Sept heat
  • Aniam has an artists' village with cafes and a small makolet; Katzrin's commercial center + supermarket 8 min drive
  • Available Aug 5 — fits a Sept start
–
  • Golan Heights, ~12 km from the Israel-Syria ceasefire line: calm in mid-2026 and de-escalation talks are advancing, but the situation is unresolved (IDF operating in the buffer zone, Feb 2026 Quneitra incursion) — conflicts with the couple's 'not near borders' preference, so capped at maybe
  • Transport to Tel Aviv is very poor: no direct service; Katzrin's single daily 843 bus (~04:50, ~3h15m) or ~4h via Tiberias — Roy's weekly TLV routine essentially requires a car
  • No oven visible or mentioned (induction cooktop only); counter is decent but the kitchen is compact
  • Only one closable room — two-laptop workspace means dining table + porch, no separate office
  • Moshav fees (מיסי מושב) explicitly NOT included and amount unstated
  • No pets allowed (not relevant unless they get one); electricity beyond ₪500/2mo on tenant
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short_term is 'likely', not explicit)
  • Ask the moshav fee amount per month (מיסי מושב)
  • Ask if an oven exists or if the landlord would allow/add a portable convection oven; confirm counter/prep space in person
  • Ask typical electricity use vs the ₪500/2mo cap (Sept AC)
  • Reality-check the car question: without a car this location doesn't work for weekly TLV trips

Private 2-room, 45sqm, 1st floor in the HaVered quarter. Photos show a genuinely cookable kitchen — built-in gas hob AND built-in oven, L-shaped wood-cabinet counter with fair prep space, retro fridge — plus a bright green-view living room with sofa and armchairs, double-ish bed with large sliding wardrobe (somewhat cluttered/lived-in), separate shower and WC (dated but fine). The blocker is explicit: 'מיועדת לשכירות טווח ארוך' — the owner wants long-term, so a 2-month Sept–Nov window is a long shot. Costs ≈ ₪2,900 all-in (2,300 + est. arnona ~₪170 + est. bills). Migdal HaEmek has the direct 826 bus to Tel Aviv and a rail station 4km south (Superbus 6/9 shuttle); Hazorea is ~20 min by car.
+
  • Only listing here with a fully built-in oven + gas hob — real cooking out of the box
  • ≈ ₪2,900/mo all-in estimate — comfortable headroom
  • Bright living room with garden/green views; complete furnishing (sofa, armchairs, bed, big wardrobe)
  • Quiet location with good neighbors per ad; bus stops and commercial center close by
  • Private landlord, no agency fee; ~20 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea (family)
