  • Lived-in and a bit worn/cluttered (fabric drawers, older bathroom) — functional, below the renovated options in feel
  • 45sqm/2 rooms: workable but no dedicated office room; one work spot + room for their desk
  • No train in town (station 4km, shuttle bus); TLV ~2h by 826 bus or bus+rail combo
  • Arnona and bills not stated — estimated from town+size
?
  • Ask anyway about a ~2-month Sept–Nov let — the ad says long-term, so get an explicit yes/no before investing more
  • Verify bed size (photo shows a narrow-ish metal frame)
  • Confirm arnona (₪170/mo is an estimate for 45sqm Migdal HaEmek)
  • Check exact bus options to Tel Aviv and to the rail station from HaVered
58
HaHarish St Afula: top-grade fresh renovation at ₪3,400 stated all-in — but photographed empty and the promised furniture list has no sofa or table MAYBE
Afula · שכון עובדים · ₪2,850/mo rent · 3 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪3,400/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,850 + arnona ₪250 + utilities ₪300 (stated in ad) · (2850+ 150חשמל+ 250 ארנונה+ 100מים+50ועד-בית = 3400 מיסים וריהוט)
confirm short-term
Agency 3-room, 70sqm, 3rd floor in Shikun Ovdim (quiet residential quarter NW of the center). March photos show a just-finished, genuinely high-end renovation — black kitchen with white quartz counter, new bathroom with double shower — but the flat is completely empty. Ad text promises: fridge, washer, microwave, hob, double bed + mattress, wardrobe, 3 ACs, and states 2,850+150+250+100+50 = ₪3,400 including bills. No oven, and pointedly no sofa, dining table or desk in the list — for two homeworkers that means buying/bringing the living layer. Not a duplicate of the Wolfson 3-room or Yoseftal 18 (different units verified by photos).
+
  • Stated ₪3,400/mo all-in including arnona and bills — clear, inside budget
  • Immaculate new renovation: quality tiling, new bathroom, new kitchen with quartz counter (decent prep space both sides of sink)
  • 3 rooms / 70sqm — separate office room possible
  • Quiet residential neighborhood, ~15 min walk to center; 3 ACs promised
  • Immediate entrance

  • No oven (only כיריים) — portable oven needed
  • Listing dates to March 2026 — state and availability may have changed
  • Agency ad; commission question
  • ~1.7km to the train station; TLV rail needs transfer
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (not stated; ad is stale from March)
  • Ask for CURRENT photos — what furniture is actually in place now, especially sofa/table
  • Confirm the ₪3,400 all-in figure still holds and covers everything
  • Ask about adding an oven

Over cap at ~₪4,650 all-in — a genuinely lovely Kiryat Bialik home with a cook's kitchen and a ready-made office SKIP
Kiryat Bialik · ביאליק דרום · ₪3,500/mo rent · 3.0 rooms · 75.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,650/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,500 + arnona ₪500 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
On quality this would be a top pick: a warm, well-decorated 75sqm 3-room in Bialik Darom with pale sofas, art, plants and a big sun-porch window; a U-shaped red-and-black kitchen with granite counters on both sides, a 4-burner gas hob, a built-in oven and a dishwasher; and a second room already set up as an office with a monitor, ergonomic chair and whiteboard. Renovated, with an elevator, shelter, storage and Metronit plus road 22 nearby. The cost is what kills it.
+
  • Best all-round kitchen in the cluster: U-shape, granite counters both sides, gas hob, built-in oven, dishwasher
  • A room already furnished as a home office (desk, monitor, ergonomic chair) plus a second bedroom
  • Warm, decorated and genuinely inviting — the nicest place to spend a day in this batch
  • Renovated, ACs in every room, elevator, shelter, storage, solar water heater; pets allowed
  • Close to the Metronit and road 22
–
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,650 (₪3,500 + ₪500 stated arnona + ₪650 est. bills) — over the ₪4,500 hard cap, forcing a skip
  • ₪3,500 for 75sqm is at the top of the Bialik market
  • The current tenants' furniture is what makes it lovely — much of it may leave
  • Kiryat Bialik has no station of its own; the train means a hop to Kiryat Motzkin
?
  • The overage rests on the ₪650 ESTIMATE — rent + stated arnona is ₪4,000, so if real bills are ≤ ₪500/mo it fits. Given the quality, this is the single listing here most worth one call to get the true bills figure
  • Confirm which furniture stays — especially the desk and office chair
  • Confirm ~2-month availability Sept-Nov 2026 (short-term not stated)
  • homeless.co.il set: verify the later small-format photos belong to this unit and are not carousel padding

25 sqm basement studio, Meirovitch 5, at ₪2,800 truly all-inclusive — cheapest certain cost, but tiny MAYBE
Netanya · מרכז העיר דרום · ₪2,800/mo rent · 1 rooms · 25 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪2,800/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪2,800 · Ad: 'המחיר כולל ארנונה, וועד הבית, אינטרנט, כבלים, חשמל ומים' — everything included in the ₪2,800.
confirm short-term
A 25 sqm basement studio (with windows) in south-central Netanya where ₪2,800 covers absolutely everything — arnona, vaad, electricity, water, internet and cable. That's ~₪1,700/mo of leftover budget, the biggest surplus here by far, and total cost certainty. The kitchen is a modest but real white run with counter and sink, and there's a double bed and a small dining table. The problem is scale and light: 25 sqm below ground is small for two people who are home most of the day in September heat.
+
  • ₪2,800 all-in stated explicitly (arnona, vaad, internet, cable, electricity, water) — total cost certainty, ~₪1,700/mo left over
  • Fully furnished including appliances; double bed and a dining table already there
  • Kitchen is a genuine cabinet run with counter and sink, not a token nook
  • Central Netanya (מרכז העיר דרום) — walkable to Herzl, the centre and the beach
  • Immediate entry and month-by-month payment — landlord is already flexible on term
  • Ad says the basement has windows, so it isn't a sealed box
–
  • 25 sqm is genuinely small for two people working from home all day
  • Basement floor — light will be limited whatever the ad says, and Sept–Oct heat plus a low unit is a real comfort risk
  • No stovetop or oven visible in any photo — only a kettle on the counter
  • Counter space is modest and the only table is a small two-seater — no second work spot
  • Bed and mattress look worn in the photos
  • Netanya train station ~2.5–3 km away, bus-only
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability (month-by-month payment suggests yes)
  • Ask what cooking appliances exist — hob? oven? — and whether a portable induction can be used
  • Ask how much daylight the windows actually give and how the unit copes in September heat
  • Visit in person before committing; 25 sqm basements photograph larger than they live
58
On the merits the best flat in the batch — real kitchen, TWO desks, park outside — but ₪3,900 + est. bills lands ₪50 over the cap SKIP
Ramat Gan · רמת עמידר · ₪3,900/mo rent · 2 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,550/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A renovated 2-room unit on HaTfutsot in Ramat Amidar, bright with two air directions, backing onto Wadi Kufr park. This is the only listing in the batch that satisfies both top criteria at once: a proper white kitchen with a long counter run, upper cabinets, a gas hob, a countertop oven, sink and a washer+dryer; and TWO real work positions — a desk and chair in the living room and a second desk with an office chair in the separate bedroom. Bright, clean, inviting, green outlook. It fails only on arithmetic: ₪3,900 with nothing stated about bills means an estimated ₪4,550 all-in, ₪50 over the hard cap — and on a furniture caveat buried in the ad.
+
  • Real, cookable kitchen: long counter run with genuine prep space, gas hob, countertop oven, upper + lower cabinets, sink — the #1 criterion properly met
  • TWO working desks already in place (living room + bedroom), one with a proper office chair — the workspace-for-two requirement met outright, which almost nothing at this budget does
  • Separate closable bedroom — one can take a call while the other works
  • Washing machine AND dryer in-unit
  • Renovated, bright, two air directions, well-kept; a genuinely nice place to be through a hot Sept-Oct
  • Wadi Kufr park literally outside the door (photo 06) — green, calm, and a quiet residential street rather than an artery
  • Well served by buses (116, 205, 472, 64, 68, 70); close to Bar Ilan University and Ramat Gan College — a campus library is a walkable work fallback
  • Plenty of parking beside the building; solar water heater; AC
–
  • All-in ₪4,550 vs the ₪4,500 cap. NOTE: the ₪50 overage rests ENTIRELY on the ₪650 fallback estimate — the ad states nothing about arnona or bills. Arnona on ~45sqm in Ramat Amidar is low, so actual all-in may well come in under ₪4,500
  • FURNITURE IS NOT INCLUDED as shown: the ad says 'ניתן לקנות/לקבל ריהוט חלקי' — the furniture (including both desks) may be bought from the outgoing tenant or may leave. For a couple who own no furniture this is a material risk, and the two-desk advantage could evaporate
  • No light rail within walking distance — the Red Line's Aharonovich stop is ~15-20 min walk or a short bus; weaker than the Bursa/Florentin options for Roy's weekly Tel Aviv trips
  • Ramat Amidar is a lower-income neighbourhood in the middle of an approved pinui-binui renewal programme — demolition/construction activity is a real risk for a Sept-Nov stay
  • Photos are agent-watermarked with a phone number and one is pure agency branding — only ~6 are of the actual unit
  • Agency listing (עומר רייבי) — broker fee likely; agent does not work Saturdays
  • 'יחידת דיור' — a dwelling unit, so worth checking permit/metering status
?
  • GET THE ACTUAL ARNONA + BILLS. This is the only thing standing between this flat and a pursue — if arnona+utilities come in at ≤₪600/mo, all-in is under the cap and this becomes the strongest lead in the batch. Worth the phone call before anything else
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (Yad2 regular rental, short_term unknown)
  • Establish exactly what furniture stays, at what price, and specifically whether BOTH desks and the bed remain — the whole case rests on this
  • Possible duplicate of 1408dc2f4e825b6a (Ramat Gan/Bnei Brak border, 2 rooms, 45sqm, floor 2, ₪3,800) — that entry has no photos to compare against, so it could not be confirmed. Worth an eyeball if photos appear
  • Check current pinui-binui construction activity on HaTfutsot before viewing
58

~₪3,900/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,000 + arnona ₪250 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A freshly renovated and properly furnished 2-room flat on HeHalutz in central Hadar: double bed with wardrobe, a large black L-shaped sofa, TV console, modern grey bathroom, and the ad advertises 'a cool garden'. It presents well and the furniture is real furniture rather than landlord-grade filler. The disqualifying gap is that not a single one of the five photos shows the kitchen — the #1 criterion is entirely unverified.

  • Has a garden ('גינה מגניבה') — outdoor space for a hot September
  • HeHalutz is a central Hadar spine: Metronit, dense buses, supermarkets; Talpiot Market and Massada café strip within walking distance
  • ₪3,000 + ₪250 stated arnona + est. bills ≈ ₪3,650 all-in — comfortable headroom
  • Arnona figure is stated (₪500/2mo), so the cost picture is partly grounded

  • HeHalutz is a busy commercial main street — traffic and shop noise are a real risk
  • 2 rooms only; no desk or dining table visible for two people working
  • Rooms read a bit dim and boxy in the photos; no shot of the advertised garden either
?
  • Request kitchen photos before anything else — counter length, cooktop, oven
  • Request a photo of the garden
  • Ask which side the flat faces — HeHalutz frontage would be noisy
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — short_term unknown
  • Ask what electricity/water run to (arnona stated at ₪250/mo)

Bright, freshly renovated and genuinely inviting Kiryat Ata 2-room — but the kitchen has no hob and little counter MAYBE

≥₪3,450/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,800 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
The nicest place to simply *be* in this batch: white marble-look floors, a good sofa under pendant lights, a new bed, a sun balcony with table and chairs, and a new bathroom — all fresh and light in central Kiryat Ata. The problem is the #1 priority: the kitchen is a short white L with a sink, a wall unit and a fridge, no hob and no oven, and only a modest amount of counter. For a couple cooking most meals it is the weak point in an otherwise lovely flat.
+
  • Bright, clean and genuinely inviting — the best 'nice place to be' in the cluster
  • Freshly renovated with new furniture: sofa, new bed, new bathroom
  • Sun balcony with seating — a real plus for Sept-Oct
  • Central Kiryat Ata: walkable shops and services, buses close
  • All-in ≈ ₪3,450 (₪2,800 + ₪650 est.), comfortable headroom

  • 2 rooms: one bedroom plus a living room, so the second workspace is the sofa or the balcony

Sirkin 8, by Talpiot Market — sunny 3-room at ₪2,600 all-in, superb location, but a token kitchenette and thin furniture MAYBE
Haifa · שוק תלפיות · ₪2,600/mo rent · 3 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪2,600/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪2,600 · Yad2 ad: 'כולל כול החשבונות וכולל כל הריהוט'
confirm short-term
Three rooms right by Talpiot Market at ₪2,600 including all bills and all furniture — on paper an excellent deal, and the main room in the photos is genuinely lovely: warm sunlight across a wood floor, high ceiling, airy. But the kitchen is a small stone-topped kitchenette of maybe 1.2m with a microwave and no visible cooktop or oven, and the 'full furniture' amounts to a black futon and a bare mattress on a pallet.
+
  • ₪2,600/mo stated all-in including bills AND furniture — ~₪1,900/mo of budget left over for a car or co-working
  • 3 rooms — space to separate two workers
  • Talpiot Market (Sirkin 35) essentially next door: fresh fruit, veg, spices — ideal for vegan + gluten-free cooking; restaurants, bars, the hipster-art scene
  • Lovely natural light in the main room, wood floor, high ceilings, clean renovated bathroom
  • Floor 2, secure building with a gate, ad says quiet good neighbours; bus stop and grocery close
  • Explicitly welcomes couples ('מתאים לסטודנטים יחידים זוגות ומשפחות'); no broker fee
–
  • Kitchenette is small (~1.2m counter) with a microwave and no visible cooktop or oven — fails the #1 criterion on the photo evidence
  • 'All the furniture' is a futon and a bare mattress on a pallet — nowhere comfortable to sit, no desk, no dining table
  • Only 4 photos, and none of them shows the kitchen properly
  • Requires cheques ('חייב צ'קים') — awkward for a 2-month stay
  • Sirkin is in the market precinct: deliveries from early morning, bar noise at night
?
  • Request proper kitchen photos — is there a cooktop or oven at all, and how long is the counter really?
  • Ask what furniture actually comes with it — the photos and the 'כל הריהוט' claim don't match
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability and whether cheques are really required
  • Ask which floor/side and how early the market deliveries start
  • Get the all-in claim in writing
58
Leib Shimoni, Neve Sha'anan — big bright 4-room with a dream kitchen and bay views, but ₪4,700 all-in on STATED figures SKIP
Haifa · נווה שאנן · ₪4,300/mo rent · 4 rooms · 115 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪5,350/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,300 + arnona ₪400 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A large, airy, renovated 4-room in Neve Sha'anan with panoramic Carmel and bay views from two balconies. The kitchen is the best in the cluster: gas cooktop plus built-in oven, generous runs of counter on two sides, masses of storage, and a curved breakfast-bar peninsula — the 'two work surfaces' dream, literally. Big mahogany dining table with six chairs, sofas, beds, wardrobes, new AC. But ₪4,300 rent plus ₪400 stated arnona is already ₪4,700 before electricity and water.
+
  • The best kitchen in the cluster: gas cooktop + built-in oven, counter runs on two walls, a breakfast-bar peninsula, huge storage
  • Large mahogany dining table with six chairs — two comfortable work spots; 4 rooms means a closable work room each
  • Big, bright and airy; panoramic Carmel and bay views from two balconies
  • Fully furnished: sofas, beds, wardrobes, fridge, oven, new AC units
  • Two toilets and a renovated shower room; parking, proper bomb shelter, new solar water heater
  • Neve Sha'anan: quiet residential area near the Technion with dense bus service
–
  • ₪4,300 + ₪400 STATED arnona = ₪4,700/mo before electricity and water — over the hard ₪4,500 cap on stated figures alone, not on the estimate. With bills it's ~₪5,150
  • Floor 4 of 4 with NO lift — a serious daily grind
  • Ad targets flatmates ('מתאימה לשותפים') and long-term tenants — hostile to a 2-month let
  • Dated terrazzo floors and 1980s finishes despite the renovation
  • No train within reach; Tel Aviv trips need a bus down to Hof HaCarmel or Lev HaMifratz first
?
  • The only route in is a rent cut to ~₪3,600 to clear the cap once arnona and bills are counted — unlikely at 4 rooms, but the kitchen is good enough to justify asking
  • If the landlord would consider a 2-month furnished let at a lower rate, revisit
58
Mevaseret Zion garden unit — the most charming place in the cluster, but the estimate pushes all-in over the cap MAYBE
Mevaseret Zion · מבשרת ירושלים · ₪4,000/mo rent · 2 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,000/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪4,000 · Ad: 'ארנונה כלולה במחיר' — arnona included. Electricity/water extra, amount NOT stated in the ad; no figure invented here, so the ₪650 fallback applies and tips the total over the cap.
car recommendedconfirm short-term
By far the most inviting interior here: warm, styled, plant-filled, with rattan papasan chairs, built-in shelving, a wooden bench, art, a mirror and a separate entrance onto a private garden and yard. The kitchen is real — a granite counter with sink, a double electric hob and cabinets — though there is no oven. Two rooms, a mamad, AC, arnona included in the ₪4,000. The problem is purely arithmetic: with the ₪650 fallback bills estimate the all-in reaches ~₪4,650.
+
  • The nicest place to simply BE in this entire cluster — bright, warm, styled, personal, genuinely inviting
  • Granite counter with sink and a double electric hob; a real (if compact) cookable kitchen
  • Separate entrance, private garden and yard — outdoor space for a hot September
  • Arnona explicitly included in the price
  • Furnished: wardrobe, two armchairs, dining table with two chairs, coffee table, fridge, sofa-bed
  • Mamad (safe room) and AC
  • Pets allowed; ad explicitly targets a couple wanting quiet
  • Mevaseret is leafy and quiet, ~15 min to Jerusalem
–
  • OVER THE CAP ON THE ESTIMATE: ₪4,000 + arnona (included) + the ₪650 fallback bills estimate = ~₪4,650. The overage rests ENTIRELY on the estimate — the ad states arnona is included and only electricity/water are extra, which for a small unit realistically runs ~₪250–350/mo, putting the true all-in near ₪4,300
  • No oven (double electric hob only) — a countertop oven would be needed
  • Counter space is one modest run with the sink in the middle — adequate, not the 'dream' two-surface kitchen
  • No obvious workspace: the small dining table seats two and there is no desk or second work spot
  • Mevaseret has no train station — Jerusalem Yitzhak Navon (fast train to TLV) needs a bus or drive first; a car is close to necessary
  • Furthest listing from family in Gedera
?
  • ASK THE ACTUAL ELECTRICITY + WATER AVERAGE — if it comes in near ₪300, the true all-in is ~₪4,300 and this becomes a strong pursue. This one number decides the listing
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability
  • Ask whether a small oven can be added and whether there is room for a work desk
  • Check the bus connection from Brosh St to Jerusalem Navon station for the TLV run
58
Afula, Giora Yoseftal — high-spec renovation, ₪3500 all-in fully itemised, but every photo is of an empty flat MAYBE
Afula · ₪2,950/mo rent · 3 rooms · 75 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,500/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,950 + arnona ₪250 + utilities ₪300 (stated in ad) · Ad itemises: '2950 שכירות + 150 חשמל + 100 מים + 250 ארנונה + 50 ועד = 3500 ש"ח כולל חשבונות'. bills_stated_month 300 = electricity 150 + water 100 + vaad 50.
confirm short-term
A 3-room 2nd-floor flat renovated to a high standard: crisp white walls, big grey porcelain tiles, two smart bathrooms with rain showers, ACs fitted. The ad itemises the all-in cost honestly and attractively — ₪2950 + ₪150 electricity + ₪100 water + ₪250 arnona + ₪50 vaad = ₪3500 — and lists furniture (fridge, washer, microwave, hob, double bed + mattress, wardrobe, 3 ACs). The problem is that all seven photos show a completely empty flat, and the kitchen is a single black run tucked into a corridor-like space with moderate counter and no oven. Worth a call, but the furniture and kitchen claims need proving.
+
  • Genuinely high-standard renovation — bright, spotless, two good bathrooms with rain showers and glass screens
  • All-in cost fully itemised at ₪3500 (ad: '2950 שכירות+ 150 חשמל+ 100 מים+ 250 ארנונה+ 50 ועד = 3500 כולל חשבונות') — ₪1000 under cap
  • 3 rooms + spacious living room = space for a closable second workspace
  • 3 ACs stated, and one is visible in each room photographed
  • Ad lists a hob, fridge, washer, microwave, double bed + mattress and wardrobe
  • Central Afula: 825 direct to TLV ~1h45, walkable shops, ~20–25 min to Hazorea
–
  • EVERY photo shows an empty flat — none of the promised furniture is visible, and there's no sofa or dining table in the list either way
  • Kitchen is a single black run in a corridor-ish space: moderate counter, no oven mentioned or visible (only 'כיריים')
  • Kitchen placement means the 'spacious living room' may not actually be an eat-in space
  • Bare white empty rooms — the flat needs its furniture to pass the 'nice place to BE' test
  • Giora Yoseftal is central Afula; street noise unverified
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability (short_term unknown)
  • DEMAND CURRENT PHOTOS with the furniture actually in place — this is the deciding question
  • Confirm whether there's an oven, or only a hob, and measure the kitchen counter
  • Ask whether a sofa and dining/work table are included (neither is in the ad's list)
  • Check what's downstairs on Yoseftal and how noisy the street is
  • Note: a same-street unit (Giora Yoseftal 18) is separately listed by the same agency — different unit, but compare them side by side

rent ₪4,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is the weak spot to verify: the real unit shows a fridge and a dining area (photo 00) and the listing lists a cooktop but NO oven, and no clear photo of the actual kitchen counter/prep is provided (the polished kitchen shots are recycled from other listings). Otherwise it's an appealing modern unit — a new, well-kept high-rise, floor 14 with a sea view, clean furnished living/dining and bedrooms, near the train and Metronit and a strip of cafes/restaurants. Priced at the ₪4,500 ceiling plus ~₪250/mo arnona.
+
  • Modern, clean, new building; quiet high floor with a sea view
  • Good transit: near lower-city train stations and Metronit, lots of eateries nearby
  • Furnished with dining table+4, sofa, two beds, wardrobes; lobby laundry in building
–
  • Cooktop but NO oven, and the actual kitchen/prep space isn't shown — real cooking capacity unproven
  • At the ₪4,500 budget ceiling AND ~₪500/2-months arnona → effective cost over budget
  • No parking; only 58 m²
  • Montage photos make the true finish level hard to judge
?
  • Confirm the landlord will do a ~2-month short-term let (listing is annual, immediate entry)
  • Get clear photos/video of the actual kitchen — counter space and whether an oven can be added
  • Confirm all-in monthly cost with arnona and va'ad bayit (high-rise fees can be steep)
  • Ask whether the sea-view unit shown is truly the one for rent
57

Kitchen is a real L-shaped fitted counter with a sink, wooden base cabinets, and an electric cooktop — genuine prep space and a working stovetop, but no oven, which matters for a couple who cook most meals. It's a compact 30 m2 furnished studio (double bed, wardrobe, wicker sitting corner, fridge) ~100 m from the sea on a quiet street with a private yard. Location is the strongest asset: Givat Olga sits near Hadera-West station for a 45-55 min TA train.

  • Kitchen and finishes look somewhat dated; some listing photos appear to be of other units

The upside is space and outdoors: 68 m2 on the ground floor with a private yard, in a newer-built pocket of Kiryat Bialik the listing calls central and transit-convenient. But finishes are tired (pink dated bathtub, worn floors) and at 2,700 it is pricier than the better-kept cheaper units.

Best kitchen and condition of the cluster — but essentially unfurnished and annual-only MAYBENO LONGER AVAILABLEno longer found on source
Kiryat Ata · קרית בנימינה · ₪3,300/mo rent · 3.0 rooms · 70.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪4,210/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,300 + arnona ₪260 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
The kitchen is the strongest in the cluster: a long gray galley with a freestanding gas stove/oven, a generous counter run and lots of cabinets — excellent for a couple who cook most meals. The apartment is beautifully renovated (high-end marble bathroom, gray-marble floors, bright) in the quiet Kiryat Binyamin neighborhood. The problem for a 2-month stay is fit: the real photos show it largely empty (the listed furniture is only fridge/oven/closet — no bed, sofa or dining set), and it is annual-only with entry 1/1/2026 (12 payments). The listing also reuses staged/stock photos from other units.
+
  • Excellent kitchen — oven + gas cooktop + long counter + ample cabinets (best of the four)
  • High-end renovation: marble bathroom, gray-marble floors, bright throughout
  • Quiet residential Kiryat Binyamin neighborhood (near Kiryat Binyamin Park)
  • Within budget (₪3,300)
–
  • Essentially unfurnished (only fridge/oven/closet listed; real photos show empty rooms) — needs a bed, sofa and table for a 2-month stay
  • Annual-only lease, entry 1/1/2026 — both term and timing clash with a Sept-Nov ~2-month need
  • Floor 4 of 4 — elevator presence uncertain
  • Listing padded with stock/other-unit photos (sea view, wooden-island kitchen, etc. do not match)
  • Kiryat Ata has no train; Tel Aviv ~1.5h+ (minor for remote workers)
?
  • Confirm whether any short-term (~2-month) option exists at all given the annual framing
  • Confirm what furniture, if any, is actually included
  • Verify move-in timing (listing says 1/1/2026) against their Sept-Nov window
  • Ask about elevator access on the top floor
  • Check car-free groceries and family access to Kibbutz Hazorea

Quirky multi-level Nachlaot studio with a real (small) kitchen, steps from the shuk SKIPNO LONGER AVAILABLEno longer found on source
Jerusalem · נחלאות · ₪4,000/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 22.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪4,650/mo all-inincl. arnona · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-termvery small
The kitchen is the best in this cluster and cooking here is realistically possible: a genuine compact L-shaped kitchen with a sink, two runs of counter, upper and lower cabinets, a cooktop and a mini-fridge (no oven). It's still small and oven-less, so gluten-free baking/roasting is out and two people prepping at once will bump elbows, but daily vegan cooking is workable. The catch is the unit itself: 22m2 spread over three levels connected by internal stone stairs, partly below ground (dim, small/glass-block windows, a portable dehumidifier in shot), and the owner explicitly says it suits a single person. Location is excellent for a car-free couple - Shabazi St in the heart of Nachlaot, a short walk to Mahane Yehuda shuk (ideal for vegan/GF fresh produce), Sacker Park, the light rail and city center.
+
  • Best kitchen of the cluster: real cabinets, L-shaped counter, cooktop, mini-fridge
  • Fully furnished incl. sofa-bed, dining table, wardrobe, washer AND dryer, AC
  • Prime walkable Nachlaot location, minutes from Mahane Yehuda shuk (vegan/GF friendly)
  • Price ₪4,000 includes arnona (municipal tax) and water; under budget
  • Multi-level layout gives some separation between living, kitchen and sleeping nooks
–
  • No oven - a real limit for a GF/vegan couple who cook most meals
  • Only 22m2 and owner states it 'suits a single person' - tight for two
  • Partly underground, small/glass-block windows, likely dim; dehumidifier suggests humidity
  • Three levels with lots of internal stairs; no proper desk - two laptops would be cramped
  • Older, worn finishes (dated bathroom) vs. the renovated look of the other listing
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,650/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Confirm ~2-month short-term availability - listing says immediate entry / 12 payments a year, so short-term is unconfirmed
  • Ask about natural light, damp/humidity and whether the AC/dehumidifier runs constantly
  • Ask whether two people can realistically live and both work remotely here
  • Check for any oven or countertop oven, and total usable counter space

On paper it's an appealing option — 59 m2 / 3 rooms, quiet and central, ₪3,000, and notably advertised on 6-month (not annual) terms, which is the friendliest short-term signal in the cluster.   • Ad lists only minimal furniture (AC, closet, table); likely NOT furnished enough for a 2-month stay without adding items
  • Or Akiva has no train station; Binyamina (~40-55 min to TA) needs a bus/ride — awkward with no car
  • Kitchen not described in the listing text at all
?

  • Clarify what furniture is actually included; a furnished 2-month stay likely needs more than AC/closet/table

Cheap furnished 2-room SUBLET in central Hadar — right profile on paper, but zero photos to judge the kitchen MAYBE

≥₪2,800/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,150 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
no photos
Sublet-board listing (short-term native) at ₪2,150/mo for a furnished, 'new, bright, ventilated' 2-room unit on Amos St, Hadar Merkaz. Location is genuinely convenient: Carmelit HaNevi'im ~7 min, Talpiot market ~8 min, Merkaz HaShmona train ~15 min downhill, Metronit 5 through lower Hadar. But there are NO photos, so the couple's #1 criterion (a real cookable kitchen) is unverifiable, and the ad's '5 minutes from Technion' line contradicts the Hadar address — template/repost noise. Estimated all-in ≈ ₪2,750-2,850 (rent + est. arnona ~₪130-200 + bills ~₪450).
+
  • Sublet — short-term is native, no landlord-agreement gamble
  • ₪2,150 rent → est. all-in ~₪2.8k, big headroom under the ₪4.5k cap
  • Central Hadar transit: Carmelit + Metronit + walkable train (Merkaz HaShmona ~1km)
  • Talpiot market walkable — cheap produce for vegan/GF cooking
  • Claimed new, furnished, bright, with AC/balcony/parking flags set
–
  • No photos at all — kitchen, counters, condition, workspace all unknown
  • '5 min from Technion' doesn't match Amos 1, Hadar — sloppy or recycled ad
  • Available from June; Sept-Nov window not stated
  • Floor 1 with bars in dense Hadar core — street noise/privacy unknown
  • 70sqm claim for a 2-room yehidat diur looks inflated
?
  • Ask for photos or an early visit — kitchen (oven/stove/counter) first, then workspace spots
  • Confirm the unit still runs as a sublet for ~2 months inside Sept-Nov 2026 (ad posted for June)
  • Are bills/arnona included in the ₪2,150? Get the real all-in number
  • Clarify the actual address (Amos 1 vs the Technion claim) before traveling to view

12-min walk to Nahariya's train terminus and a real kitchen — capped by the Lebanon-border security picture MAYBE
Nahariya · ₪2,499/mo rent · 3 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪3,374/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,499 + arnona ₪225 + est. utilities ₪650
border areaconfirm short-term
Clean, freshly painted 70 sqm 3-room on Kibbutz Galuyot St: real kitchen (gas range visible, ~2m stone counter), double bed and wardrobes, glass dining table, and a spare empty room that could take their desk. Nahariya is the cluster's transit king — ~1.1km walk to the terminus with direct Tel Aviv trains ~1h40 — and shops are walkable. But Nahariya sits ~9km from the Lebanon border: the March–June 2026 war put rocket barrages over the city (hospital moved underground, beaches closed) and the June 26 framework agreement remains fragile, so per the standing rule this is capped at 'maybe' pending a security re-check near September.
+
  • Best transport in the cluster: ~12 min walk to Nahariya station, direct TLV trains ~1h40
  • Real kitchen: gas range visible plus a decent stone counter
  • Clean, freshly painted, immediate entry
  • Walkable supermarkets, shops and services (city center ~700m)
  • Spare empty room = easy second workspace for their own desk
  • All-in ≈ ₪3,225 (arnona stated ₪225/mo)
–
  • Security cap: ~9km from the Lebanon border; rocket barrages on Nahariya as recently as May–June 2026, ceasefire fragile and rejected by Hezbollah
  • Only partially furnished: living room sparse, one room empty, no sofa visible
  • Floor contradiction: ad text says floor 2, listing field says floor 4; elevator unknown
  • Agency ad, short-term unknown
  • Kitchen counter modest for heavy two-person cooking
?
  • Re-verify the northern security situation before committing — this is the gating item for Nahariya
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability with the landlord
  • Clarify floor (2 vs 4) and whether there is an elevator
  • Exact furniture list — is there a sofa, and can a desk fit in the empty room?
  • Homeless ad 3ba205c32528b2d8 shows a very similar flat (same street/specs/price) with a different kitchen finish — possibly the same building or this unit pre-renovation; cross-check when contacting

HaMaccabim 10: grandma-style but ₪2,500 INCLUDING arnona, water and vaad — cheapest all-in MAYBE
Kiryat Ata · גבעת הרקפות, גבעת תל חי · ₪2,500/mo rent · 3 rooms · 65 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪2,500/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪2,500 · המחיר 2500 כולל ארנונה ומים וועד בית למעט החשמל — rent includes arnona, water and vaad; electricity excluded
confirm short-term
Time-capsule 3-room (65sqm) in Givat HaRakafot, Kiryat Ata: ₪2,500 including arnona, water and vaad bayit — only electricity on top, so all-in ≈ ₪2,700-2,750, the cheapest in the cluster by far. Fully furnished (oven, gas, fridge, dining set, sofa) with a real retro-pink kitchen with decent counters, entrance is just 4 steps up, quiet surroundings near a commercial center and bus stop, and the dining nook has an open view toward the bay. The catch: heavy grandma decor and photos taken mid-occupancy with clutter everywhere — 'a nice place to BE' is doubtful until seen empty.
+
  • Cheapest all-in in cluster: ≈ ₪2,700-2,750 (rent includes arnona + water + vaad; only electricity extra)
  • Real kitchen: oven, gas, fridge stated; retro but decent counter run and storage
  • Fully furnished (dining table, sofa, beds, dresser) — and can even be emptied if preferred
  • Quiet surroundings (stated), commercial center + bus stop + abundant parking nearby
  • Practically ground floor (4 steps) with shelter behind the building
  • Open view over the bay from the dining nook
–
  • Very dated ornate interior (chandeliers, floral sofas) — far from the bright/clean vibe they want
  • Photos show the current occupant's heavy clutter — hard to judge condition underneath
  • No train in Kiryat Ata — TLV ~1h45m-2h; workspace ergonomics unclear (vanity/dining tables only)
  • Religious landlord ('not on Shabbat') may mean stricter house norms
?
  • Confirm landlord agrees to ~2-month Sept-Nov stay (short_term unknown)
  • Ask to view AFTER current tenant clears out — condition and light without the clutter
  • Confirm floor situation (ad says first floor/4 steps; listing metadata says floor 2)
  • Where do two laptop workstations go? Check the dining table + a desk spot
  • Do not call on Shabbat (stated in ad)

confirm short-term
65sqm 3-rooms one building up from the Masada cafe strip, private landlord, ₪2,700 + ₪350 stated arnona (~₪3,500 est. all-in with utilities). The ad's inventory is exactly what they need — fridge, oven (photos show a freestanding 4-burner electric stove+oven), washing machine, kitchen table, double bed, wardrobe, dresser and a writing desk — and 3 rooms means two real workspaces. The catch is condition: photos show badly peeling paint in the bedroom and second room, a very old kitchen with minimal counter, and vintage-worn floors. Location is genuinely excellent (Carmelit Masada station, Talpiot market, cafes below); the flat itself is the weak link.

  • REAL cooking setup confirmed: freestanding stove + oven, fridge, washing machine, kitchen table

  • Kitchen counter/prep space is minimal (sink counter + a side cabinet) despite the real stove
  • Only 3 photos — much of the flat unseen
  • Ad targets partners/roommates — furniture is a mixed bag (double + single beds)
  • Short-term unknown — needs landlord agreement
?

  • Ask for more photos or a video call tour — 3 photos hide a lot; check whether peeling walls extend to living areas
  • Verify utilities beyond arnona (est. ~₪450 for 65sqm) and vaad bayit

₪2,000 Shikun Dalet flat with a real stove+oven kitchen, next to Big Tiberias and Yesh Hesed — cheap and cookable, but faces the main road with partial furniture MAYBE
טבריה · שיכון ד · ₪2,000/mo rent · 3 rooms · 68 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪2,650/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
Honest private ad in upper Tiberias (Jabotinsky 2, Shikun Dalet): partially renovated ('the renovation is partial' per the seller), tidy kitchen with a freestanding gas cooker (stovetop + real oven), a workable single-run counter, microwave and fridge — a genuinely cookable kitchen at ~₪2,850/mo all-in. It's steps from the Yesh Hesed supermarket and the Big Tiberias center, with a heavily-served bus stop nearby. The catches: the flat fronts HaNasi Weizmann, Tiberias' main upper artery (ad claims new noise-insulating doors), the ad promises only partial furniture (two single beds + wardrobe + table and chairs — sofa and the photographed desk may leave with the tenant), and the WC shows patched, unpainted wall damage.
+
  • Real cooking kitchen confirmed in photos: gas stovetop + oven, decent counter run, microwave, fridge — rare certainty in this cluster
  • ~₪2,850/mo all-in (₪2,000 + ~₪240 arnona + bills) — big budget headroom for co-working or a car
  • Groceries as good as Tiberias gets: Yesh Hesed and the Big center supermarkets within a short walk — vegan/GF-friendly stocking
  • Bus stop with many lines nearby (ad) for the hop to the CBS and the hourly 836 to Tel Aviv
  • 3 rooms — a closable second room for their desk (a desk+chair setup is even pictured, though possibly the tenant's)
  • Private landlord (no agency fee); new noise-insulated doors per ad
–
  • Faces HaNasi Weizmann — the main upper-Tiberias road; street noise is a real risk despite the insulated doors
  • Furniture is explicitly partial: two SINGLE beds + wardrobe + table/chairs — no couple's double bed, and the sofa/desk in photos may not stay
  • Condition is mixed: partial renovation, dated wardrobe, visible patched wall damage in the WC — livable but not 'inviting'
  • Tel Aviv still 2.5h+ door-to-door (local bus + 836); no train
  • Tiberias September heat (-200m); AC present but bills will show it
  • Short-term status unknown — standard rental needing landlord agreement
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability with the landlord (not stated)
  • Ask exactly which furniture stays — specifically a double bed (or joining the two singles) and a sofa
  • Ask which side the bedroom faces (courtyard vs HaNasi road) and visit at rush hour for noise
  • Verify the oven works and see the kitchen counter in person
  • Confirm arnona amount and typical bills (nothing stated)

Rehovot Marmorek basement unit, ₪2,500 + ₪350 arnona — a fully-kitted kitchen and a desk, in a gloomy cellar MAYBE
Rehovot · מרמורק · ₪2,500/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 55.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,500/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,500 + arnona ₪350 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
Very strong on paper: all-in ~₪3,500, a real kitchen with a freestanding oven and 4-burner electric hob, and an explicit inventory that includes a work desk, washer/dryer, microwave, toaster oven and kitchenware. But the photos undercut it — this is a basement (קומה מרתף) with drop ceilings, fluorescent light, a dim corridor and heavy dated furniture. It fails the 'bright, inviting place to be' test that matters most for two people indoors all day.
+
  • All-in ~₪3,500 — about ₪1,000/mo under budget
  • Genuinely equipped kitchen: freestanding oven + 4-burner electric hob, fridge, microwave, toaster oven, kitchenware, decent counter
  • A work desk and shelving unit are explicitly included in the furniture list
  • Washer AND dryer in the unit
  • 2.5 rooms → a possible second work spot
  • Separate entrance, back yard, plenty of parking, faces an open field — quiet edge-of-town location
  • Rehovot is close to Gedera (~15 min) and has a real train station to TLV
–
  • It is a basement: drop ceilings, fluorescent lighting, dim corridor, minimal natural light. In a two-month stay where they are inside most of the day, this is a real quality-of-life problem
  • Furniture is old and gloomy — dark curtains, a heavy wooden wardrobe, worn sofa-beds
  • Sha'ar HaGai 6 is at the far edge of Marmorek: quiet, but isolated — a long way from Rehovot station and the centre, so a car or a bus dependency
  • Shared shelter with the whole house; no balcony
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability
  • Ask for daytime photos showing the actual natural light and window sizes — this decides it
  • Check the real distance/bus to Rehovot train station
  • A basement may actually be an advantage against September heat — worth weighing against the darkness

A 2-room flat in central Netanya, fully furnished and clearly well-kept, at ₪3,500 that includes everything except water and electricity — so all-in lands around ₪3,950–4,000. The bedroom has a double wooden bed, a wardrobe and a small built-in desk; the living room has seating and a coffee table. It reads clean and cared-for rather than shabby, just dated. The disqualifying gap is that four photos cover the living room, bedroom and toilet and none show the kitchen — the couple's number-one criterion is a blank.

  • ₪3,500 includes arnona and vaad; all-in ~₪3,950–4,000 on a water+electricity estimate keeps it under the cap
  • Clean and well-kept in the photos, decent natural light from the window wall
  • Central Netanya (מרכז העיר דרום), walkable to Herzl, shops and the beach
  • Named licensed agent with a listed licence number — a traceable, legitimate counterparty

  • NO kitchen photo in any of the 4 images — oven, stovetop and counter space are entirely unknown on the #1 non-negotiable
  • Furniture is dated (vintage slipcovered sofas, heavy wooden pieces) — functional but not inviting
  • Floor 2 with no elevator
  • Water and electricity are on top of the ₪3,500 — a hot September with heavy AC could push all-in toward ₪4,100+
  • Netanya train station ~2.5–3 km east, bus-only
  • Agent-listed, so expect a brokerage fee on top unless waived for a short let
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
  • Get kitchen photos first — this decides it
  • Ask what water + electricity typically run per month
  • Ask whether the brokerage fee applies for a 2-month let

Clean renovated ground-floor studio in Jaffa's Noga/American Colony — but the kitchen has no cooking appliances at all MAYBE
Tel Aviv · צפון יפו, המושבה האמריקאית-גרמנית · ₪3,000/mo rent · 1 rooms · 20 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪3,000/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-termvery small
A small, freshly renovated rear-facing ground-floor studio on Eilat St near Nitzana, in the Noga / American-German Colony pocket of Jaffa. Photos show a genuinely clean white unit: a grey kitchen run with sink, ~1.5-2m of counter and an upper cabinet, a bed base with mattress against a pine feature wall, a wardrobe, and a small bistro table with two chairs. The kitchen counter is real prep space, but there is no stovetop, no oven and no fridge visible anywhere — everything cooking-related would have to be bought. Location is the strongest card: a walkable, characterful part of Jaffa with the flea market, Shuk Levinsky and Red Line access.
+
  • Genuinely clean and freshly renovated — white walls, new bathroom, new kitchen cabinetry, not shabby
  • Real counter space: a continuous ~1.5-2m worktop plus a second surface, more than the usual token kitchenette at this price
  • Great location for Roy's weekly Tel Aviv interests: Noga compound / American-German Colony, walkable to Jaffa flea market, Jerusalem Blvd buses, and ~15 min walk to the Red Line Elifelet terminus (Neve Tzedek end)
  • Shuk Levinsky (excellent for vegan/GF staples) is roughly a 15 min walk north
  • Stated all-in is around 3,600-3,900 incl. arnona/vaad — leaves ~600-900/mo of budget free
  • Rear-facing (עורפית) off the street — likely quiet despite Eilat St being a through-road
  • Comes with bed+mattress, wardrobe, table and two chairs; small shared yard
–
  • No stovetop AND no oven in any photo — both would need buying, not just one (the ad lists no cooking appliance either)
  • No fridge visible in any photo
  • Small: a single room; the bistro table is the only surface, so it is one work spot, not two
  • Ground floor with barred windows and modest daylight; the shared yard in the photos is scruffy and cluttered
  • The ad's numbers are inconsistent — headline price 3,000 but the text says 3,400 rent / 3,600 'including everything except electricity'
  • Agency listing (Homerun) — expects checks, a guarantor and a month's deposit, which is awkward for a 2-month stay
  • Immediate entry (כניסה מיידית) advertised — likely gone or re-let long before September
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short_term unknown; ad pushes immediate entry)
  • Pin down the actual rent: 3,000 vs 3,400 vs 3,600 all-in — and what 'everything' covers
  • Ask whether a fridge and any cooktop are provided, or whether the unit is genuinely applianceless
  • Ask if the landlord will accept a 2-month term without a guarantor/checks
  • AC unlisted in the text but a split unit is visible in the photos — confirm it works
55
Kaplan 2, Holon — the best-equipped kitchen in the batch, killed by an all-in ~₪4,750 SKIP
Holon · גרין ועם · ₪4,100/mo rent · 3 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,750/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,100 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A renovated, fully furnished 3-room 60sqm on a quiet Green/Am side street. The kitchen is the real thing: a long white counter run, freestanding gas range with an oven, microwave, fridge, and a dining table — the strongest cooking setup in this batch. Living room has a sofa and sideboard, one bedroom has a double bed and closet, the second room is empty with a closet (room for their desk), AC in every room, washing machine on the service balcony. The problem is purely money.
+
  • Real cookable kitchen: gas stovetop + oven, generous continuous counter, fridge, microwave, dining table
  • Fully furnished and recently renovated (plumbing, electrics, kitchen tiling); clean and bright
  • 3 rooms = a closable second room for a workspace, plus a sofa/living area — good for two remote workers
  • AC in every room, electric shutters, washing machine
  • Kaplan is a quiet residential street in Green/Am, walkable to central Holon shops; frequent buses to Tel Aviv
  • Ad says flexible entry ('אפשרי לכניסה גמישה')
–
  • All-in ~₪4,750 (₪4,100 rent + ₪650 est. bills, arnona not even stated on top) — over the ₪4,500 cap. Note the overage does NOT rest on the estimate: rent alone is only ₪400 under the cap, and Holon arnona on 60sqm would add roughly ₪250/mo on its own
  • 60sqm across 3 rooms means the rooms are small; bedroom photo shows a bare mattress and scuffed walls
  • No train within walking distance (Holon Junction is a bus ride away); no light rail until the Green Line opens
  • Listed at ₪4,100 since April — sitting unrented, so likely priced above market
?
  • If the parent wants one price negotiation to attempt in this batch, this is it: at ₪3,700–3,800 rent it lands under the cap and becomes a strong pursue
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (short_term unknown)
  • Ask for the actual arnona and average electricity bill
55
₪2,400 Kiryat Ata 3-room with a brand-new kitchen and bathroom, dragged down by dim, heavy old furniture MAYBE
Kiryat Ata · גבעת הרקפות, גבעת תל חי · ₪2,400/mo rent · 3 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,050/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,400 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A mixed bag at Ben Ami 27. The kitchen is new — white cabinets with a real ~2m countertop run, sink, fridge — and the bathroom is freshly renovated with a glass shower. But the living and bedrooms are furnished with heavy 1980s brown sofas and ornate bedroom sets and photograph dim and gloomy. No hob or oven is visible; only a counter-top toaster oven. Cheap at ~₪3,050 all-in, but the landlord explicitly wants serious long-term tenants.
+
  • New kitchen with a genuine counter run; new renovated bathroom with glass shower
  • 3 rooms at ₪2,400 — all-in ≈ ₪3,050, lots of headroom
  • Furnished including fridge, washing machine, new ACs; shelter in the building; bus stop right by the door per the ad
  • No agency fee (direct from owner)
–
  • Landlord states he wants 'serious long-term tenants' — a 2-month stay is a hard sell
  • Furniture is dated and heavy; the rooms photograph dim and uninviting
  • No hob or oven visible — only a small counter-top toaster oven
  • Givat Rakafot, Kiryat Ata: no train, bus-dependent, hillside residential
  • No arnona or bills stated
?
  • Ask directly whether a ~2-month Sept-Nov let is possible — the ad asks for long-term
  • Confirm whether a hob/oven exists in the new kitchen
  • Ask for the arnona figure and daylight photos of the living room
  • Check bus frequency from Ben Ami to Merkazit HaKrayot / Kiryat Motzkin station
55
Vitkin, Ahuza — big furnished 3-room with a full oven+range and a 3m counter, but ₪4,650 all-in breaks the cap SKIP
Haifa · אחוזה · ₪4,000/mo rent · 3 rooms · 100 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,650/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A large, light, front-facing 3-room in Ahuza — an upscale, quiet, leafy Carmel neighbourhood — in a small building with only four tenants. The kitchen is a genuine cooking kitchen: a freestanding gas oven-and-stove range with a 3m+ counter run, a countertop oven and plenty of cabinets. Furnished with sofas, armchairs, a bed, a dining table and a chest; two balconies plus a glazed veranda; green views on all sides. The finishes are 1970s-tired, and ₪4,000 plus the ₪650 estimate puts it over the cap.
+
  • Real cooking kitchen: freestanding gas oven + stovetop range, 3m+ counter run with real prep space, countertop oven, lots of cabinets
  • Large and light 3-room with two balconies (one off the living room, one service balcony off the kitchen) plus a glazed veranda
  • Furnished: sofas, armchairs, bed, dining table, chest of drawers
  • Ahuza is an upscale, quiet, leafy Carmel neighbourhood with good local amenities — a pleasant place to walk and to be
  • Only four tenants in the building; green leafy views from every window; front-facing and bright
–
  • ₪4,000 + est. ₪650 utilities = ₪4,650 all-in — OVER the hard ₪4,500 cap. The overage rests only on the ₪650 ESTIMATE; the ad states no utility figures
  • Dated and tired: 1970s patterned kitchen tiles, worn floor tiles, aging bathroom — clean but a long way from 'bright and inviting'
  • Ahuza is up on the Carmel — every Tel Aviv trip means a bus down to Hof HaCarmel train first (~20 min)
  • Agency listing (RELOCATION) with a wall of self-promotional boilerplate — likely a broker fee on top
  • No desk; the dining table is the only work surface
?
  • Ask for actual arnona + bills figures — if utilities come in under ₪500/mo this clears the cap; the overage is estimate-driven, not stated
  • Ask whether ₪3,800 is acceptable for a 2-month furnished let — that clears the cap outright and this becomes a real contender on the kitchen alone
  • Ask about the broker fee
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
55
Plain but spacious 3-room in Kiryat Yam ד' with a real eat-in kitchen — ₪3,500 feels steep for what it is MAYBE
Kiryat Yam · קרית ים ד' · ₪3,500/mo rent · 3 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A clean, roomy, partially furnished 3-room: a big living room with a sectional sofa and TV, a white kitchen with a ~2.5m counter run, a separate gas hob unit and an eat-in table, and a bedroom with a bare mattress. Nothing is wrong with it and the kitchen works, but it is plain and institutional-feeling (wall-mounted heater, blank walls), and at ₪3,500 you pay Motzkin-boulevard money for a Kiryat Yam location with no train.
+
  • Real kitchen: counter run with sink, separate gas hob, room for a dining table
  • Spacious and clean 3 rooms — space for a closable work room
  • Central, accessible location with shops and public transport per the ad
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,150, still under cap
–
  • ₪3,500 is expensive for a plain, partially furnished flat in Kiryat Yam ד'
  • Only 'מרוהטת חלקית' — the bedroom shows a bare mattress and little else
  • Plain and slightly institutional; not somewhere you would enjoy being all day
  • No train in Kiryat Yam; Metronit + train to reach Tel Aviv
  • Managed by a property-management company (RENT360) — short-term flexibility is doubtful
?
  • Confirm ~2-month availability Sept-Nov 2026 (short-term not stated; management companies usually want 12 months)
  • Ask exactly what furniture stays — 'partially furnished' is doing a lot of work here
  • Ask for the arnona figure (not stated) — it could push all-in near the cap
  • Check whether an oven exists
55
Migdal HaEmek, 4 rooms / 90 sqm by Balfour forest — quiet, big, real kitchen, but ₪4150 all-in and firmly long-term MAYBE
Migdal HaEmek · ₪3,500/mo rent · 4 rooms · 90 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
car recommendedconfirm short-term
A 90 sqm 4-room flat on a quiet street next to Balfour forest, with 'most of the furniture' included. The kitchen is a long dated wood-and-dark-stone run with a freestanding oven/stove, microwave and a real dining table — properly cookable with good counter length. One bedroom already has a desk and chair. The living room is large but the photos catch it mid-move with paintings in boxes, and the whole flat is 1990s tiled and tired rather than bright. At ₪3500 + estimated bills it lands around ₪4150 all-in — the priciest thing in a cluster whose entire appeal is cheapness.
+
  • Real cookable kitchen: long counter, freestanding oven + stove, microwave, dining table in-kitchen
  • 4 rooms / ~90 sqm — plenty of space for two people working from home; one room already has a desk
  • Ad emphasises a quiet street next to Balfour forest ('רחוב שקט ובקרבת יער בלפור') — good for the quiet criterion and for walks
  • 'כולל רוב הריהוט' — most furniture included; ACs and window bars fitted
  • ~15–20 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea
–
  • ₪3500 + est. ₪650 bills ≈ ₪4150 all-in — only ₪350 under the hard cap, and no utilities are stated to verify it
  • Ad says 'שכירות לטווח ארוך' / 'מתאימה למשפחה או לזוג המחפשים בית נוח לטווח ארוך' — a 2-month let contradicts the pitch
  • Dated and tired: old floor tiles, 1990s fittings, living room photographed mid-move with boxes
  • Only 3 photos; no bathroom, no proper living-room-in-use shot
  • 'רוב הריהוט' is vague — which pieces actually stay?
  • Migdal HaEmek has no walkable train station; TLV ~1h45 by bus
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — the ad is explicitly long-term
  • Get real arnona + bills numbers before anything else: at ₪3500 rent the cap is close, and a high arnona on 90 sqm could push it over ₪4500 (auto-skip)
  • Ask exactly which furniture stays and get photos of the rooms as they'll be handed over
  • Ask how far the walk is to a supermarket from this street
  • Confirm AC in all 4 rooms
55
Tiberias HaShoftim: the best kitchen and a Kinneret view — but all-in lands ~₪4,550, over the hard cap SKIP
Tiberias · ₪3,500/mo rent · 4.5 rooms · 118 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,550/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,500 + arnona ₪400 + est. utilities ₪650 · Yad2 field: 'ארנונה לחודשיים: 800' = ₪400/mo. No electricity/water figures stated — ₪650 fallback applied, which puts all-in at ~₪4,550, over cap.
confirm short-term
Objectively the finest apartment in the cluster: 120 m², 4.5 rooms, gut-renovated, with a long black granite kitchen counter running the full wall, gas hob, built-in oven, dishwasher, two sinks, extractor and a large fridge — exactly the 'generous counter / two work surfaces' the couple want. Bright living room with a Kinneret view, a 6-seat dining table, a walk-in wardrobe room, a proper master double. It fails on arithmetic: ₪3,500 rent + a stated ₪400/mo arnona + ~₪650 estimated bills ≈ ₪4,550, over the hard ₪4,500 all-in cap. Tiberias's September heat is a second strike.
+
  • Best kitchen of the 16: full-wall black granite counter, gas hob, built-in oven, dishwasher, two sinks, hood, large fridge
  • 4.5 rooms (3 bedrooms) — a closable office plus a spare; the most workspace flexibility here
  • Partial Kinneret view from the living room and bedroom; bright, gut-renovated, genuinely inviting
  • Master bedroom has a real double bed; furnishing available; ample street parking
  • Near public transport, shops and groceries; direct bus to TLV (~2h10)
  • Hazorea ~1h drive
–
  • All-in ≈₪4,550 (₪3,500 + ₪400 stated arnona + ~₪650 est. bills) — over the hard ₪4,500 cap
  • Tiberias in September is extremely hot and humid; a 120 m² flat on floor 2/3 will run AC constantly, pushing bills ABOVE the ₪650 estimate, not below
  • Furnishing is optional ('יש אפשרות להשכיר עם ריהוט ויש אפשרות גם בלי') — the furnished price may be higher than ₪3,500
  • Currently occupied; the second bedroom has only a single bed
  • No train in Tiberias; bus or car only
  • Agency listing (S.M. Nadlan)
?
  • The one listing here worth a cap re-check: if the rent negotiates to ~₪3,300 for a 2-month prepaid let, or if bills come in stated and low, this jumps to a pursue on kitchen quality alone
  • Ask for the furnished vs unfurnished price — the ₪3,500 may be the unfurnished figure, which would make it worse, not better
  • Ask for actual summer electricity bills before assuming ₪650
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
  • Be honest about the September heat before spending budget here
54
Freshly-renovated Hadar studio, very cheap and clean — but a single hotplate and one room for everything MAYBE
Modiin · הדר עליון · ₪2,200/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 28.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪2,850/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,200 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is a clean white kitchenette with a modest counter run, sink, microwave and a full-size fridge, plus (per the listing) a portable electric hotplate — no oven and no built-in cooktop. There's some real prep space, so light cooking works, but a single hotplate is marginal for a couple cooking most meals (they'd want a second/portable burner). The plus side: it's genuinely freshly renovated, bright and spotless (gray marble-look tile, new bathroom with shower + washing machine), furnished with a double bed, wardrobe and dining table, and only ₪2,200. The catch is it's a 28 m² studio — sleeping, cooking and two laptops all share one room.
+
  • Very cheap (₪2,200) and genuinely renovated — clean, bright, move-in ready
  • Real (if modest) kitchen counter + full fridge + microwave + hotplate
  • Central Hadar Elyon: Metronit-served, walkable shops and Talpiot market nearby
  • New bathroom with shower and in-unit washing machine
–
  • Single portable hotplate, no oven, no built-in cooktop — limited for cooking most meals
  • 28 m² studio: one room for sleep + kitchen + two remote workers is tight
  • Ground floor with window bars — check light, privacy and quiet
  • Photo set mixes in other apartments
?
  • Confirm the landlord will do a ~2-month short-term let (listing is annual, immediate entry)
  • Ask whether a second burner / small oven can be added (or negotiate one in)
  • Check ground-floor noise/privacy and how the single room works for two laptops
  • Confirm the renovated studio shown is the actual unit

Renovated micro-studio steps from Rabin Square, months-long sublet, ~₪4,200 all-in — tiny, semi-basement light, compact kitchen MAYBE
Tel Aviv · הצפון הישן - צפון · ₪4,000/mo rent · 1 rooms · 23 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪4,000/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪4,000 + arnona n/a · המחיר כולל אינטרנט, ועד בית וחשבון מים (Yad2 ארנונה לחודשיים: 1 — likely a data quirk, verify) · missing arnona not stated
very small
no photos
23sqm studio sublet at Rabbi Moshe Ben Ezra 10, a genuinely quiet side street in the Old North next to Rabin Square. Freshly renovated, clean, with a real work desk + office chair; price ₪4,000 including internet, va'ad and water, arnona listed as ~zero, so all-in ≈ ₪4,150-4,250 (only electricity on top). Kitchen is compact-but-cookable: electric cooktop, small oven, fridge, microwave — though prep counter looks minimal (a dark pass-through bar is the main surface). Ground floor with high frosted windows and bars = limited natural light, and 23sqm is very tight for two people working from home.
+
  • Unbeatable everyday location: quiet street off Rabin Square — supermarkets, cafes, Ibn Gvirol/Dizengoff bus corridors all on foot
  • Sublet 'possible for a few months' — clean short-term fit, enter with a suitcase
  • ₪4,000 includes internet + va'ad + water; arnona stated ≈0 → all-in ≈ ₪4,150-4,250, under cap
  • Renovated and clean; includes a proper desk + office chair (one ready workspace)
  • Cooking possible: electric cooktop + small oven + fridge + microwave
–
  • 23sqm for a couple + two remote jobs = very cramped; only one workspace, little room for their own desk
  • Ground floor ('קומה 0') with high frosted windows + bars — semi-basement feel, limited daylight for people home all day
  • Kitchen prep space is minimal — cookable but frustrating for daily vegan+GF cooking
  • No light-rail nearby; Savidor/Arlosoroff train + Red Line ~1.7km (buses excellent though)
?
  • Confirm Sept-Nov ~2-month window (ad says 'a few months' — align dates)
  • Arnona listed as '₪1 per 2 months' — almost certainly a data quirk; verify who pays arnona and how much
  • Ask for more photos/video of the kitchen counter + daylight at midday (windows look high and frosted)
  • Would the owner allow adding their own desk (space check) and is there a second sittable work spot?
  • Boxes of new appliances in photos — confirm everything (kettle, toaster) is actually set up

Furnished garden unit on quiet Kibbutz Parod — rural car-country, and the photos are from 2022 MAYBE
פרוד · פרוד · ₪2,750/mo rent · 2 rooms · 150 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪3,400/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,750 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
Tidy 2-room unit in Parod's expansion with a large landscaped yard and deck, furnished (sofa, TV armchair, wardrobe, fridge, writing desk) with AC and parking. One-wall kitchen with a modest counter and what looks like a freestanding stove — cookable but not generous. Rural quiet is superb and the ad's 'long-term possible' wording implies short-term flexibility, but transit is the weak point: bus 361 from the Parod junction to Karmiel then train, ~3h door-to-door to Tel Aviv, and without a car groceries are a project. All photos are 2022 phone screenshots — current condition unverified.
+
  • Very quiet rural setting with a big private garden and deck
  • Furnished including a writing desk; AC, parking, available Aug 1 — fits Sept start
  • Short-term friendly: 'long-term possible' phrasing implies monthly flexibility
  • Intercity bus 361 (Haifa–Karmiel–Safed) stops at the Parod junction on Rt. 866
  • All-in ≈ ₪3,350 (est. arnona+bills) — inside budget
–
  • Car country: ~3h to Tel Aviv by bus+train — hard for Roy's weekly commute; headroom doesn't fund a rental car
  • No walkable supermarket (kibbutz minimarket at best) for daily vegan/GF cooking
  • One-wall kitchen with limited counter; oven not confirmed
  • Listing photos are 2022 screenshots — real current state unknown
  • Upper Galilee: 2026 Israel–Hezbollah war saw Galilee-wide sirens; June 26 ceasefire framework is fragile
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability explicitly
  • Ask for CURRENT photos — the ad reuses 2022 phone screenshots
  • Confirm oven/cooking setup and the actual unit size (150 sqm field is implausible; likely includes the yard)
  • Ask how bills are charged (garden units are often flat-rate) and whether arnona is included
  • Check walk time from the unit to the 361 stop at Parod junction
  • Re-verify the northern security situation closer to September
54
Tiberias: renovated, nicely furnished 2-room + balcony at ₪2,500 all-in except electricity — but a weak kitchen and September heat MAYBE
Tiberias · ₪2,500/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪2,500/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
The most pleasant-looking cheap flat here: renovated, fully furnished (sofa bed, 4-seat dining table, TV, double bed, dresser, coat stand), handsome patterned floor tiles, and a balcony looking onto palms and the lakefront hotels — so it sits close to the promenade/Old City. Price includes everything except electricity, so all-in lands near ₪2,900. The kitchen is the weak point: a single short white run with a sink, a microwave and a small hob, no oven, and only modest counter. And Tiberias in September is genuinely punishing — routinely mid-to-high 30s°C, in a basin, with humidity off the lake.
+
  • ₪2,500 covers everything but electricity — all-in ≈₪2,850–2,950, the cheapest properly furnished option here
  • Renovated, bright, actually inviting; decorative tiles and a coherent furnishing set
  • Balcony with a palm/lake-area outlook; walkable to the promenade, Old City shops and groceries
  • Fully furnished incl. a double bed and a dining table that could serve as a work spot
  • Direct Egged bus Tiberias→Tel Aviv, ~2h10–2h15, roughly hourly from ~05:00 to ~22:20 — better than the rural options
  • Hazorea ~1h drive
–
  • Kitchen is the weak link: short single counter run, no oven, small hob — usable but nowhere near 'generous prep space' for two people cooking every meal
  • Tiberias in September is brutally hot (lake basin, high humidity) — the single worst climate in this cluster for people who are indoors all day
  • 2 rooms only; the dining table is the only realistic second workspace
  • Near the promenade/Old City = tourist traffic and noise; the street's quiet level is unverified
  • Electricity on top, and AC will run hard in September — budget ₪400+/mo, not ₪250
  • No train in Tiberias at all; bus or car only
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
  • Ask for the exact address and check street noise — the balcony photos suggest a busy hotel-area road
  • Ask whether a real hob/oven can be added, or budget for a portable oven+induction plate (counter space is the harder constraint)
  • Confirm how many AC units and in which rooms, and ask the landlord what electricity runs in summer
  • Not a duplicate of fe65e2e6065664b9 despite '0m away' — photos show a completely different, occupied flat (different tiles, kitchen, layout)
53
New, fully furnished studio — but only an electric 2-burner kitchenette, no oven MAYBE
Kiryat Ata · מרכז · ₪3,000/mo rent · 2.0 rooms · 40.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪3,650/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is a compact kitchenette: a two-burner electric cooktop, a microwave and a small fridge, with minimal counter and no oven — usable for simple cooking but tight for a couple who cook most meals and want real prep space. On the plus side it is brand-new, clean and fully furnished (double bed, sofas, washing machine, dining table), a bright open studio with a pleasant open view, at a reasonable ₪3,000 including arnona and water. It is only 40 m² / 2 rooms, though, so two people on laptops share one small table, and it sits on Zevulun in the busier town center. Some photos show other units, so confirm the actual kitchenette is what is on offer.
+
  • Brand-new, clean and fully furnished, move-in ready (double bed, sofas, washing machine, microwave, dining table)
  • ₪3,000 including arnona and water
  • Central location (Zevulun 39) — walkable to shops in the town center
  • Bright open-plan studio with a pleasant open view
–
  • Kitchenette only — electric 2-burner, no oven, very little counter/prep space (weak for the couple's #1 priority)
  • Small 40 m² / 2-room studio — cramped shared workspace for two laptops
  • Town-center street (Zevulun) is likely busier and less quiet
  • Short-term availability unknown
  • Listing mixes in photos of other units (an L-shaped empty kitchen, a staged designer bedroom, an occupied gas-stove kitchen) that are not this studio
?
  • Confirm ~2-month short-term availability
  • Confirm the real kitchen is the electric 2-burner kitchenette (no oven) and whether that is enough for their cooking
  • Inspect actual counter/prep space in person
  • Assess whether two people can comfortably work on laptops in 40 m²
  • Ask about street noise on Zevulun in the center

rent ₪2,600 + arnona ₪100 + est. utilities ₪650

Among its genuine (terrazzo-floored) photos the kitchen looks better than the 'fridge only' screening: a white kitchen with a 4-burner gas cooktop, counter and cabinets, a fridge and a dining table (photo 06) — but no oven is visible and a second glossy galley shot is inconsistent, so the real setup needs checking. It is a cheap, renovated ground-floor unit with a garden exit and a wardrobe, but small (~40 m²) and only partly furnished (the description lists little beyond fridge + wardrobe). Heavy photo contamination: at least six images (a studio kitchenette with smiley fridge, a black-leather-sofa kitchen, a bathroom, a sunroom) are other ads, not this flat. Quiet residential Ramot Yitzhak; immediate entry.
+
  • Cheapest option at ₪2,600; renovated ground floor with a garden exit
  • A genuine photo shows a gas cooktop + counter + cabinets (better than 'fridge only')
  • Quiet residential Ramot Yitzhak; immediate entry
  • Wardrobe included
–
  • No oven visible; kitchen photos are inconsistent (two different kitchens shown)
  • Only 'partial' furnishing — description lists little beyond fridge + wardrobe (may lack sofa/bed)
  • Small (~40 m², effectively a 1-room unit) — tight for two people working from home
  • Very contaminated photo set — most images are other apartments
  • Ground floor can mean less light / more street noise; framed as annual
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month furnished short-term let is possible (ad is annual-only)
  • Get current real photos and confirm the kitchen (cooktop + any oven) and exactly what furniture is included
  • Check that two laptops can work comfortably in ~40 m², plus street quiet and walkable groceries without a car

Polished all-inclusive Ben Yehuda studio, monthly-basis-only at ₪3,650 — but a microwave-only kitchenette with no cooktop or oven MAYBE
Jerusalem · מרכז העיר · ₪3,650/mo rent · 1 rooms · 20 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪3,650/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪3,650 · המחיר כולל הכל ארנונה חשמל מים אינטרנט wifi, כבלים, ועד בית
confirm short-termvery small
no photos
Hotel-style renovated 20 sqm studio on the Ben Yehuda pedestrian mall (city center, West Jerusalem), 4th floor with elevator, rented on a monthly basis only with everything included — arnona, electricity, water, wifi, cable and va'ad — so all-in is ₪3,650, ~₪850/mo under the cap, and the structure fits a Sept–Nov stay perfectly. The blocker is the kitchen: a small niche with sink, microwave, kettle and coffee machine, no stovetop or oven, and the modest counter is mostly occupied by appliances — marginal-to-failing for a couple cooking vegan/GF meals daily for two months. Location is superb for Roy's commute (Jaffa Center light rail ~2 min walk, ~3 stops to Yitzhak Navon, 35-40 min trains to Tel Aviv every 30 min) and Mahane Yehuda shuk is ~10 min on foot; evening busker/nightlife noise from the midrachov is the other open question.
+
  • ₪3,650 truly all-in (arnona, electricity, water, internet, cable, va'ad stated included) — well under the ₪4,500 cap
  • "Monthly basis only" — exactly the short-term structure they need
  • New condition, tasteful full furnishing (double bed, sofa, bar table + stools, TV), modern bathroom, AC, elevator
  • Prime transit: light rail on Jaffa Rd ~2 min walk, ~3 stops to Navon station; 35-40 min trains to TLV every 30 min
  • Mahane Yehuda shuk ~10 min walk plus city-center supermarkets — strong vegan/GF shopping
–
  • Kitchen: microwave-only kitchenette nook — no stovetop, no oven, small appliance-crowded counter; daily cooking for two would be a struggle
  • 20 sqm for two remote workers: a bar table and a small round table, no room for a proper second workstation or their desk
  • Ben Yehuda midrachov below — buskers and nightlife into the night, especially weekends; floor 4 only partly helps
  • Fridge not visible in photos — likely small/under-counter
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month Sept–Nov window is actually available (monthly-only is stated, the dates aren't)
  • Ask whether a portable induction hob may be added and how much clear counter + fridge capacity there really is — this decides the listing
  • Ask which way the windows face and how loud the midrachov gets at night
  • Confirm the all-inclusive price holds unchanged for a 2-month autumn stay
52
Freshly renovated 4-room in Shikun Bet at ~₪3,200 all-in, walkable to the CBS — but the sparkling kitchen shows no cooktop or oven and the furnishing is workers'-flat sparse MAYBE
Tiberias · שיכון ב · ₪2,300/mo rent · 4 rooms · 80 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪2,950/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,300 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
The renovation is real: new marble-look floors, new white kitchen cabinetry with a good counter run and fridge, AC, and it's ~10 min on foot from Tiberias CBS (hourly 836 to Tel Aviv). But the photos show zero cooking appliances — no hob, no oven, just sink and fridge — and the furnishing pattern (double bed + rooms of single beds, 'suitable for partners') reads as basic worker/partner housing: beds on plain frames, one sofa, a dining table, nothing cozy. For a couple whose #1 criterion is a cookable kitchen, this is a verify-first listing; if a cooktop/oven exist (or the landlord adds them), the price, space and CBS walkability make it a reasonable value play, with the third bedroom as a closable office.
+
  • Genuinely renovated and clean — new floors, new kitchen units, bright rooms
  • ~₪3,215/mo all-in (₪2,300 + ~₪295 arnona + bills) — comfortable headroom
  • ~10 min walk to Tiberias CBS → hourly direct 836 to Tel Aviv (~2h10m); buses + makolet by the building per ad
  • 4 rooms / 80 sqm: dining table for two laptops plus a spare closable room for their desk
  • Kitchen counter/prep space is decent IF appliances are added; ad claims a quiet street
–
  • No cooktop or oven visible anywhere in the kitchen photos — as shown, it fails the couple's non-negotiable unless equipped
  • Sparse, institutional furnishing (bare beds, minimal living gear) — functional, not the warm 'place to BE' they want
  • Derech HaGvura is a through-road for the neighborhood — 'quiet street' claim needs an on-site check
  • Agency ad aimed at partners/workers; short-term status unknown
  • Tel Aviv 2.5h+ door-to-door; Tiberias Sept heat
?
  • FIRST: does the kitchen have (or will the landlord install) a cooktop and oven? Dealbreaker question
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (not stated)
  • Confirm the double-bed bedroom is included as photographed and whether a washing machine exists
  • Visit for street noise on Derech HaGvura (buses pass nearby)
  • Ask what vaad bayit, if any, applies (nothing stated)
