Brand-new Bursa micro-unit, ~₪3,700 all-in, great counter and Red Line on the doorstep — but one tiny room and no cooking appliances at all MAYBE
Ramat Gan · בורסה · ₪3,400/mo rent · 1 rooms · 12 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪3,400/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-termvery small
A freshly renovated 1-room unit in a subdivided flat on Harkon in Ramat Gan's Bursa complex, ₪3,400 including everything except electricity — roughly ₪3,700 all-in, the cheapest real all-in figure in this batch and ~₪800/mo under cap. The finish is genuinely new and bright (quartz counter, oak-look floor, barn-door bathroom, new furniture, washer AND dryer) and the counter is a surprisingly generous unbroken quartz run — real prep space. Two things hold it back hard: there is no hob and no oven anywhere in the photos, and it is a single small room where the bed physically abuts the kitchen counter.
+
  • All-in ~₪3,700 (₪3,400 covers arnona, water and vaad; only electricity extra) — ~₪800/mo of headroom for a co-working desk or a car, which directly answers the two-workspace gap
  • Genuinely good prep space: a long, unbroken quartz counter — better work surface than most of this batch, including places costing ₪800 more
  • Brand-new renovation throughout, bright and clean; all furniture is new per the ad
  • Washing machine AND dryer in-unit, plus AC
  • Bursa complex: walkable to the Aba Hillel Red Line station — direct light rail into Tel Aviv, this cluster's core advantage. Harkon itself is being rebuilt as a pedestrian midrahov
  • First floor over columns, rear-facing, with a lift; the ad calls it quiet
  • Bursa has supermarkets and services at street level
–
  • NO stovetop and NO oven visible in any of the 8 photos — only sink, counter and fridge. Missing one of the two is negotiable given the counter; missing both means buying an induction hob and a countertop oven (affordable from the ₪800/mo headroom, but confirm there's a dedicated circuit and that the landlord permits it)
  • It is one small room: the bed is directly against the kitchen counter (photos 06, 07). For two people living and both working here for two months, that is cramped
  • No workspace at all — no desk, no table, nowhere for a second person to sit and work; they'd be leaning on the kitchen counter or paying for co-working
  • 'דירה מחולקת' — a subdivided flat. Legality, metering and neighbour arrangements are worth checking, and electricity is often sub-metered at a markup
  • Bursa is an office district — lively on weekdays, dead in the evenings and at weekends; not much neighbourhood life
  • Agency listing (ג.נ. נכסים) — broker fee likely
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (Yad2 regular rental, short_term unknown)
  • ASK: is there a hob/oven not shown in the photos, or a spot to install a portable induction + countertop oven? This single answer decides it
  • Get the electricity arrangement in writing — sub-metered subdivided units sometimes bill above tariff
  • Confirm exact walking time to the Aba Hillel Red Line stop and whether the Harkon midrahov works are active (construction noise through Sept-Nov)
  • Worth weighing against a co-working membership funded by the ₪800/mo saving — that would resolve the workspace problem but not the cramped-living one
52
Cozy, genuinely homey ₪1,900 Motzkin unit with a real kitchen — but the landlord wants ONE long-term tenant MAYBE
Kiryat Motzkin · מוצקין הותיקה · ₪1,900/mo rent · 1.5 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪2,640/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪1,900 + arnona ₪90 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A warm, lived-in 1.5-room at Netiv HaAliya 7: sofa, double bed, wardrobe, art on the walls, and an L-shaped kitchen with a gas hob, toaster oven, microwave, dishwasher, washing machine and fridge with a workable counter. It has more personality than anything else in this batch and is dirt cheap (arnona is only ₪90/mo). The blockers are in the ad text: the landlord says it suits a single tenant, prefers long-term, and requires three payslips plus a guarantor.
+
  • Cozy and characterful — an actual home, not a staged empty box
  • Real kitchen: gas hob, toaster oven, microwave, dishwasher, fridge, washer, usable counter
  • Very cheap: all-in ≈ ₪2,640 (₪1,900 + ₪90 arnona + ₪650 est.) — the lowest true cost here
  • Fully furnished 'just walk in and live' including bed, wardrobe, sofa, tables
  • Motzkin Vatika location — near the Metronit and reasonably close to Kiryat Motzkin station
–
  • Ad says it suits ONE tenant ('מתאימה לדייר/ת אחד/ת') — a couple may be refused outright
  • Landlord prefers long-term renters, which conflicts with a 2-month stay
  • Requires three payslips plus a guarantor — awkward for remote freelancers
  • 1.5 rooms: cramped for two people working from home; no second work spot
  • No pets; the flat is small and the kitchen is dated
?
  • Ask up front whether two people for ~2 months is acceptable — the ad says otherwise on both counts
  • Ask whether the payslip/guarantor requirement can be replaced by paying the full period upfront
  • Confirm exact walking distance to Kiryat Motzkin station
  • Confirm the flat is available at all for Sept-Nov 2026

A private (non-agency-sounding) listing for a 69sqm 2.5-room flat in mid-Hadar with two big rooms, an enclosed balcony and a ~30sqm private yard. The ad lists two wardrobes, two beds and kitchen cabinets, and states arnona at ₪300/mo. There are no photos and no mention of a fridge, cooker, oven, sofa or table — so both the kitchen and the furnishing level are unverified.
+
  • 69sqm with two large rooms plus an enclosed balcony — genuinely spacious, room for two to work apart
  • ~30sqm private yard — outdoor space, rare at this price
  • Arnona stated (₪600/2mo = ₪300/mo), so the cost picture is partly grounded; ~₪3,200 all-in leaves good headroom
  • Mid-Hadar with public transport close by per the ad; walkable to Talpiot Market and the Massada café strip
  • Reads like a private landlord — likely no broker fee and more flexible on a short stay
  • ₪2,500 rent is cheap for 69sqm + yard
–
  • NO photos at all — light, condition, cleanliness and above all the kitchen are completely unknown
  • The ad lists only 'ארונות מטבח' (kitchen cabinets) — no cooker, no oven, no fridge mentioned, which is a bad sign for the #1 criterion
  • Furniture list is bare: two wardrobes and two beds, no sofa, no table, no desk
  • Shower stall in the toilet room ('בחדר השירותים יש מקלחון') — suggests a cramped combined bathroom
  • Mid-Hadar can be scruffy; the exact street conditions on Yosef are unverified
?
  • Request photos — especially the kitchen, the two rooms and the yard
  • Ask explicitly: is there an oven, a cooktop, a fridge? None are mentioned
  • Ask what else is furnished beyond two beds and two wardrobes
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — short_term unknown
  • Ask what electricity/water run to (arnona stated at ₪300/mo)
52
Taanach 34, Neve Yosef — ₪2,100 all-in basement 2-room, has a desk and a real bed, but no cooktop and it's floor minus-1 MAYBE
Haifa · נווה יוסף · ₪2,100/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪2,100/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
A cheap, tidy, genuinely lived-in-looking 2-room at ₪2,100/mo stated to include arnona, water and electricity. Unusually for this price band it comes with an actual made bed, a sofa, a coffee table and a desk with a chair by a window with greenery. The kitchen, though, is a compact white run with a microwave, fridge, sink and washing machine and no cooktop or oven, and the flat is on floor minus-1 — a basement, which caps the light.
+
  • ₪2,100/mo stated all-in (arnona, water, electricity) — the cheapest all-in in the cluster; ~₪2,400/mo left over for a car and co-working
  • A desk and chair are already set up by a window, plus a sofa and coffee table — the furnishing is real, not staged filler
  • Proper double bed with bedding, ceiling fan, AC
  • Close to public transport, supermarkets, parks per the ad; no broker fee
  • There's a long dark counter surface visible at the edge of one photo — possibly more prep space than first appears
–
  • Kitchen has NO cooktop and NO oven — microwave only, and the counter is short (~1m) and shared with the sink
  • Floor minus-1 (basement) — light will be limited, which matters a lot for two people indoors all day in a hot September
  • Neve Yosef is a modest eastern-slope neighbourhood; nothing walkable and pleasant nearby
  • 2 rooms, small; the desk is the only work spot — the second person has nowhere
  • Agency listing (ספיר פרימיום) with template hard-sell copy
?
  • Ask whether a cooktop and oven can be added — the price leaves plenty of room to buy both
  • Ask how much daylight actually reaches a minus-1 flat, and visit in the afternoon
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — short_term unknown
  • Get the all-in claim in writing
  • Note: 0fa5e9f9ed598849 is the same flat re-listed — don't double-count it
52
Nof HaGalil, Yizrael 11 — spacious with an open view and a long kitchen counter, but dated and the weakest location for family/transport MAYBE
Nof HaGalil · ספיר · ₪2,900/mo rent · 3 rooms · 76 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,550/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
car recommendedconfirm short-term
A spacious 3-room flat with an open view, currently lived in and shown as such: a long galley kitchen with a full run of dark granite counter and plenty of cabinets, a dining room with a black table for four, and a bright living room with a big sofa. It is clean and homely but distinctly dated. Two structural problems: the ad offers only partial furniture ('ריהוט חלקי', and it can be rented without it at all), and Nof HaGalil is the weakest town here — no train, the longest run to Hazorea, and at ₪2900 it is priced like the good central-Afula units.
+
  • Long galley kitchen with a genuinely generous run of granite counter and lots of storage — real prep space
  • 3 rooms, described as very spacious with an open view ('פתוח לנוף, דירה מאוד מרווחת')
  • Lived-in and homely: dining table for four, large sofa, AC, bright rooms
  • Nof HaGalil is a calm town — the quiet criterion is probably satisfied
–
  • 'ריהוט חלקי' — only partial furniture, and the ad offers it unfurnished; the couple own no furniture at all
  • No oven or hob clearly visible in the kitchen photo — the appliance situation is unverified
  • Nof HaGalil is the worst-placed town in this cluster: no train station at all (bus 833 to TLV, 2h+), and ~30–35 min to Kibbutz Hazorea
  • ₪2900 + est. ₪650 ≈ ₪3550 all-in — same money as the far better central-Afula units
  • Dated fittings and bathroom; only 4 photos
  • 'שתי קומות לרדת' — two floors down; check daylight and access
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability (short_term unknown)
  • Establish exactly what 'ריהוט חלקי' includes — is there a bed? a sofa? a table? — this likely decides it
  • Confirm there is an oven and/or hob and photograph the kitchen appliances
  • Ask what 'two floors down' means for light and for the view claim
  • Get arnona + bills figures for Nof HaGalil
52
Tiberias Shikun Bet garden flat: superb 6-burner range and 100 m² fully furnished — but ₪4,000 rent puts all-in ~₪4,650, over the cap SKIP
Tiberias · ₪4,000/mo rent · 4 rooms · 120 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,650/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad states no arnona and no bills figures — ₪650 fallback plus est. arnona puts all-in ~₪4,650, over cap. · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A 100 m² 4-room ground-floor garden flat, renovated and fully furnished, with a shaded stone terrace. The kitchen is excellent: a long counter run with a sink, a large freestanding 6-burner gas range with a full oven, and a big American fridge. The living space is large and bright with a big sectional, a dining table and AC everywhere. But at ₪4,000 rent with neither arnona nor bills stated, the realistic all-in is ~₪4,650 — over the hard ₪4,500 cap — and Tiberias in September is the worst climate of the four towns for people working indoors all day.
+
  • Excellent kitchen: long counter run plus a large 6-burner gas range with a full oven and a big fridge
  • 100 m², 4 rooms (3 bedrooms + salon) — a real closable office; fully furnished 'קומפלט'
  • Ground floor with ~20 m² garden and a shaded stone terrace; renovated; AC in every space
  • Bright, clean, coherently furnished — one of the more inviting interiors here
  • Direct bus to Tel Aviv (~2h10); Hazorea ~1h drive
–
  • All-in ≈₪4,650 (₪4,000 + est. arnona ~₪400 + est. bills) — over the hard ₪4,500 cap, and nothing is stated to argue otherwise
  • Tiberias in September: lake-basin heat and humidity, routinely mid-to-high 30s — AC will run constantly on a 100 m² ground floor
  • Even if it squeaked under the cap, ₪4,000 leaves nothing to fund the car that a no-train town needs
  • Neither arnona nor bills are stated anywhere in the ad
  • Likely duplicate of 33a4a0ab77d190ab ('דירה · שטרית · שיכון ב · טבריה', flagged 0m away, similar price) — same street, same neighbourhood, same agency; unconfirmable because that listing has no photos
  • Agency listing (S.M. Nadlan) with heavy boilerplate
?
  • Resolve the duplicate against 33a4a0ab77d190ab (same street, 0m, similar price) — request photos on that listing to compare
  • If the rent negotiates to ~₪3,400 for a prepaid 2-month let, the all-in clears the cap and this becomes a maybe on kitchen strength
  • Ask for the arnona figure and summer electricity before assuming anything
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
  • Weigh the September heat honestly — this is a ground-floor flat with a garden they'd be too hot to use
50
Cookable galley kitchen, no oven; cramped studio for two SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,319/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,969/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,319 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
Kitchen is genuinely cookable: a real continuous countertop with a portable 2-burner gas stovetop and a full top-freezer fridge/freezer — only an oven is missing (negotiable, they can add a countertop oven, though it would eat into the modest counter). Everything else is a budget compromise: a small single-room studio with just one narrow wall table and a single chair, sparse furniture (bare mattress), and a lively, gritty Hadar location steps from Talpiot Market — excellent for fresh vegan produce but not the quiet they want. For two people both working remotely from home all day, the single tiny work surface and cramped room are the real weakness here, more than the kitchen.
+
  • Real cookable kitchen: continuous counter + portable 2-burner gas stovetop + full fridge/freezer; only the oven is missing (and that's negotiable for them)
  • Steps from Talpiot Market (Shuk Talpiot) - fresh produce ideal for vegan home cooking, plus supermarkets and cafes walkable
  • Under budget at 4,339/mo
  • Fully available across the window (61/61 days, 1-night min) - easy to book a ~2-month stay
  • Renovated, clean bathroom; in-unit washing machine; A/C; courtyard-facing side offers some noise buffer
  • Central Haifa with frequent Metronit/buses; reasonable base for reaching Kibbutz Hazorea
–
  • No oven - they'd need to buy a countertop one, which would consume scarce counter space
  • Only a small portable 2-burner cooktop and a single-run, modest counter - tight for a couple cooking most of their meals
  • Tiny single-room studio: cramped for two people who are both home working all day
  • Workspace inadequate for two: one narrow wall table and a single chair; they bring only one desk and there's no separate spot
  • Sparse, basic furnishing (bare mattress, dated budget feel)
  • Hadar by the shuk is lively and gritty, not quiet - a mismatch for their stated quiet preference
  • No one-seat ride to Tel Aviv; must get downhill to a train/bus station first
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,989/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Confirm the host will do a ~2-month Sept-Nov stay at the monthly rate - it's an Airbnb short-term listing; availability looks fully open but the long-stay price/terms are unconfirmed
  • Ask if a second work-friendly chair/surface can be added and get room dimensions - two remote workers need two viable spots
  • Verify real street-noise levels (courtyard vs street side) given the busy market area
  • Confirm gluten-free packaged grocery options nearby (fresh produce is covered by the shuk; GF specialty items less certain)
50
Sleek Haifa studio, great transit, but tiny kitchenette & no oven SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,137/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,787/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,137 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen is a small but real kitchenette: sink, wood cabinets, a 2-burner electric cooktop, microwave, kettle and fridge — but NO oven and only minimal prep counter, which the host frames as 'light meals' only. For a couple cooking most of their vegan/GF food that's workable-but-tight (stovetop yes, buy a countertop oven), not comfortable. It's also an open-plan studio, so two people on video calls share one room, and the effective price rises above budget once 18% VAT for Israelis is added (~₪4,914). Upside: beautifully renovated, quiet-insulated, superbly located Downtown for a car-free couple.
+
  • Beautifully renovated modern studio: AC, fast Wi-Fi, smart TV, elevator
  • Real (if small) kitchenette with 2-burner cooktop + full-size fridge — cooking possible
  • Excellent car-free transit: ~10-15 min walk to Haifa Center-HaShmona train + Metronit
  • Groceries close (Wadi Nisnas produce, downtown supermarkets), vegan-friendly German Colony
  • Base ₪4,164 under budget; short 2-month stays accommodated
–
  • No oven; minimal prep counter — host calls it a 'light meal' kitchenette
  • Open-plan studio: two remote workers, one room, no call privacy
  • Only a fold-down bar shelf + 2 stools for workspace — cramped for two
  • 18% VAT for Israelis pushes effective rent to ~₪4,914/mo — over budget
  • Downtown/port nightlife area — quiet is a mild risk
?
  • Confirm total price incl. 18% VAT before pursuing — base rate is misleading
  • Clarify whether Israeli-citizen renters are eligible (operator asks passport + tourist visa)
  • Confirm exact Sept-Nov window + monthly rate (61/61 days open, min 3 nights)
  • Ask about a second work surface given the open studio
  • Hazorea reachable by train+bus; Gedera is a long ~2h haul south

Cramped single-hob kitchenette; great market, lively area SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,434/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪5,084/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,434 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen — their #1 criterion — is a small, cramped kitchenette: a single portable electric hotplate, no oven, and little clear counter, though a full fridge/freezer plus a microwave sit on the adjacent enclosed balcony, and the balcony bistro table offers overflow prep space. It's a notch above a token kitchenette because cooking gear is provided, but it falls short of a comfortable cook's kitchen for two people cooking most of their meals. The big offsetting draw is location: it's right on Talpiot Market (fresh produce plus a vegan food store and bakery) — ideal for vegan+GF whole-food cooking — and it's under budget, furnished, and centrally walkable with no car needed. The catch is that the same market brings nightlife (night fair, bar, brewery), so their preference for QUIET is a real question.
+
  • Cooking gear already provided: full-size fridge/freezer + microwave (on the enclosed balcony) and a portable hotplate — nothing to buy
  • Right on Talpiot Market — fresh-produce market with a vegan food store and bakery; outstanding groceries at the doorstep for their vegan + gluten-free diet
  • Under budget at ~₪4,434/mo and fully furnished for a ~2-month stay bringing only a desk
  • Spacious, bright main room with AC; enclosed balcony with sliding glass doors gives a plausible semi-separate second workspace for two remote workers
  • Central, dense, walkable Hadar HaCarmel location — no car needed; Carmelit funicular + frequent buses reach downtown train (Haifa Center HaShmona) for Tel Aviv
–
  • Kitchen is the weak point and it's their #1 criterion: small cramped kitchenette, only a single portable hotplate, NO oven, and minimal clear counter for two people cooking most meals
  • Sits on top of Talpiot Market's nightlife (night fair, bar, brewery, food stalls) — lively and potentially noisy, at odds with their preference for QUIET
  • No dedicated second room; both work areas are within/adjacent to one main room
  • Older building; kitchen and fixtures are dated and utilitarian
  • Over budget: ≈₪5,084/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Short-term availability is listed (Airbnb, all 61 days open in the window) — but confirm the ~₪4,434 monthly rate holds for the full ~2-month stay and that specific Sept-Nov 2026 dates are open
  • Ask the host whether a second burner can be added and whether the enclosed-balcony table is usable as real prep/overflow surface
  • Check actual noise from the market/bar/brewery below, and whether the main room/balcony face the quieter interior courtyard (the balcony view suggests a back courtyard)
  • Confirm there is truly no oven; ask if a countertop oven may be added

  • private roof terrace + separate entrance — light, quiet, inviting
  • 66sqm — two work corners feasible
  • very low stated arnona (₪75) keeps all-in ≈ ₪4,375

–

  • rooftop heat in September

  • 4th floor, likely walk-up
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,725/mo all-in exceeds the ₪4,500 cap (auto-downgraded to skip)

  • verify kitchen (no photos reviewed)

~₪3,700/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,800 + arnona ₪250 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
no photos
Renovated-looking 3.5-room (60sqm, floor 3) in Givat HaRakafot, Kiryat Ata at ₪2,800 + stated ₪250 arnona → all-in ≈ ₪3,400. The advertised 'stunning kitchen' is real cabinetry: a big bordeaux kitchen with generous counters and storage, fridge and washing machine — but no cooktop and no oven are visible anywhere, and the bedroom photo shows a bare room with only a built-in wardrobe (no bed), with just a recliner and coffee table elsewhere. Cleaner and brighter than its Palmach-street neighbors, but two make-or-break unknowns (cooking appliances, actual furniture) keep it at a cautious maybe.
+
  • Best kitchen WORKSPACE in the Kiryat Ata trio: long counters, huge storage, fridge + washer
  • Renovated, clean, bright finish; 3.5 rooms = easy two-workspace layout if furnished
  • Quiet location (stated) in residential Givat HaRakafot, near the neighborhood's shops/Metronit
  • All-in ≈ ₪3,400 — comfortable headroom; immediate availability; no agent fee
–
  • No stovetop AND no oven visible — worst-case it's a beautiful kitchen you can't cook in without buying both
  • Photos show no bed and almost no furniture despite the 'furnished' flag
  • ₪500/mo pricier than the equally-located Palmach 10 / HaMaccabim 10 options
  • No train in Kiryat Ata — TLV ~1h45m-2h
?
  • Confirm landlord agrees to ~2-month Sept-Nov stay (short_term unknown)
  • Ask directly: is there a gas hob/cooktop and an oven? (nothing visible in photos)
  • What furniture is actually included — is there a bed, sofa, dining table?
  • If unfurnished in practice, drop it — 2-month stays can't absorb furnishing

Shmuel HaNatziv, Netanya: ₪3,900 genuinely all-inclusive with elevator — but a single bed and a token counter MAYBE
Netanya · צפון מערב מרכז העיר · ₪3,900/mo rent · 2 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪3,900/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
A 2-room flat in central Netanya where ₪3,900 covers electricity, water, arnona and vaad — total cost certainty and no bill surprises, with an elevator (rare in this belt). But the photos undercut it: the kitchen is a short galley whose counter is almost entirely consumed by the sink, with a microwave and at best a small domino hob and no oven; the bedroom holds a single bed; and the living room is a sofa and a coffee table in a bare white box. At ₪3,900 all-in there's only ~₪600 of headroom for a place that offers little.
+
  • ₪3,900 all-inclusive stated explicitly (electricity, water, arnona, vaad) — complete monthly cost certainty, under the cap
  • Elevator in the building — genuinely uncommon in central Netanya at this price
  • 2 rooms: a separate bedroom that closes
  • Fully furnished with appliances; renovated bathroom with a modern glass shower
  • Central location on Shmuel HaNatziv (מרכז העיר צפון), walkable to the centre and Herzl buses
  • Immediate entry
–
  • Kitchen counter is tiny — the sink eats nearly all of it; no oven visible and at best a small domino hob. This fails the #1 criterion on the photos
  • Bedroom shows a SINGLE bed — no double for a couple
  • Living room is sparse and bare-walled; not an inviting place to spend all day
  • ₪3,900 all-in leaves only ~₪600/mo of headroom — expensive for what's shown, when ₪3,000-and-under options here have better kitchens
  • Only 4 photos, none showing the flat as a whole
  • Netanya train station ~2.5–3 km east, bus-only
  • Agency listing (Refresh) — likely brokerage fee
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
  • Ask whether the bed can be swapped for a double and whether there is any hob/oven at all
  • If pursued, push back on price given the kitchen
50
HaHashmal 12, Neve Paz — ₪2,600 all-in, bright and homey with treetop views, but a token kitchenette and bay air pollution MAYBE
Haifa · נווה פז · ₪2,600/mo rent · 2 rooms · 35 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪2,600/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
A bright, cheerful, currently-occupied 2-room at ₪2,600 stated to include all bills. The photos show it lived in and homey — big windows with treetop views, plants, sofa, round coffee table, a double bed and a wardrobe — noticeably more inviting than most of this price band. The kitchen is the weak point: a small white kitchenette with a toaster oven perched on the fridge, a tiny counter and the washing machine, and it sits on HaHashmal in Neve Paz, the street known for Haifa Bay industrial air pollution.
+
  • Bright and genuinely homey — big windows, treetop views, warm feel; a decent place to be
  • ₪2,600/mo stated all-in (all bills) — ~₪1,900/mo of budget left over
  • Furnished completely ('מרוהטת קומפלט'): double bed, wardrobe, sofa, table, TV, fridge, washing machine, AC
  • Fully renovated, clean modern finishes
–
  • Kitchen is a token kitchenette — tiny counter, no proper cooktop, toaster oven on top of the fridge; fails the #1 criterion
  • HaHashmal St / Neve Paz is specifically known for Haifa Bay industrial air pollution — bad for people at home all day with windows open
  • Neve Paz is a 1970s rehabilitation-project neighbourhood, steep slopes and many stairs, little to walk to
  • No desk; only a small coffee table for two laptops
  • Currently occupied — the photos are of someone else's things; unclear what stays
  • Listed by a generic investment agency
?
  • Check live air-quality readings for Neve Paz / Haifa Bay
  • Ask whether a proper cooktop and counter space can be added
  • Ask what furniture stays once the current tenant leaves
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — short_term unknown
  • Get the all-in claim in writing
50
Karmiel Ramat Rabin: large, bright, well-furnished studio at ₪2,800 truly all-in — but a kitchenette with no hob or oven MAYBE
Karmiel · ₪2,800/mo rent · 1 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪2,800/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪2,800 · Ad: 'כולל ארנונה, מים, אינטרנט ו- yes' — arnona, water, internet and TV included in ₪2,800. Electricity not mentioned.
car recommendedborder areaconfirm short-term
A big, well-lit basement-level studio on Netiv HaSayarim, fully accessible, at ₪2,800 including arnona, water, internet and TV. It's well furnished (double bed, sofas, dining table, TV, fridge) and the space is genuinely large and cheerful, though the LED-lit aquarium bar is a matter of taste. The kitchen is the problem: a modest cabinet run with a sink, microwave and upper units — no hob and no oven anywhere in the photos, and the ad's own furniture list confirms it ('מזגן, מקרר, ספה, מיטה זוגית, ארון, פינת אוכל, טלויזיה, דוד שמש' — no cooking appliance at all). It's one open room, so there's no separating a work spot from the bed.
+
  • ₪2,800 genuinely all-in — arnona, water, internet and TV included; only electricity may be extra
  • Large, bright, cheerful open space; well and completely furnished incl. a double bed
  • Ramat Rabin is a quiet, well-regarded Karmiel neighbourhood; full accessibility, immediate entry
  • Karmiel train to TLV (~2h); Hazorea ~50 min drive
  • Money left over (~₪1,700/mo) to fund a car and a co-working desk
–
  • No hob and no oven visible, and none in the ad's furniture list — cooking would need both a portable hob AND an oven bought in
  • Counter space is modest — a single short run beside the fridge; not enough for two people cooking vegan+GF from scratch daily
  • One open room: bed, sofa, dining table and both work spots all share one space — no closable second workspace
  • Reads as a lower/garden level; daylight is unclear (heavy artificial and LED lighting in every shot)
  • Agency listing (RE/MAX) with heavy boilerplate
  • Aquarium bar + LED strips is a strong decor commitment
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
  • Ask directly: is there any hob/oven, and is there room on the counter to add a portable induction + countertop oven?
  • Ask how much daylight the unit gets — the photos are all artificially lit
  • Confirm electricity is also included (the ad lists arnona/water/internet/yes but not חשמל)
  • Check possible duplicate candidates around the same area — none had photos to compare
50
Kfar Vradim: quiet, green 3.5-room garden unit at ₪3,700 incl. arnona+water — but only single/kids' beds and a car is mandatory MAYBE
Kfar Vradim · שאר העיר · ₪3,700/mo rent · 3.5 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,350/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪3,700 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'מחיר כולל ארנונה ומים' — arnona and water included in ₪3,700. Electricity not mentioned.
car recommendedborder areaconfirm short-term
A spacious 3.5-room unit on Shnir in Kfar Vradim, opening onto a green patio, at ₪3,700 including arnona and water. Multiple rooms mean a real closable workspace, and there's already a work table by the kitchen window. The kitchen is adequate rather than good: a wood galley with a small hob and a large countertop oven, with a modest counter run. The furnishing is the catch — the ad lists 'a room with 2 children's beds' and 'a room with one bed', and the photos confirm singles and slipcovered day-beds. There is no double bed for a couple who own no furniture.
+
  • 3.5 rooms — the most room-separation in the cluster; a genuine closable second workspace, plus a work table already by the window
  • ₪3,700 includes arnona and water; all-in ≈₪3,950 — under cap
  • Kfar Vradim is a green, affluent, extremely quiet mountain village — the calmest option here and pleasant to be in
  • Has a hob and a (countertop) oven; patio and greenery right outside
  • Sits just south of the 33.0N exclusion strip (32.994N) — in scope
–
  • No double bed — only singles and children's beds per the ad and photos; they own no bed
  • ₪3,700 is the second-priciest here and leaves little to fund the car that Kfar Vradim absolutely requires (no train, minimal buses)
  • Tel Aviv is ~3h each way by public transport; even Karmiel is a ~25–30 min drive
  • Kitchen counter is modest and the oven is a countertop unit, not built-in; galley layout, one work surface
  • Dated fittings (terrazzo floors, radiators, slipcovered day-beds) — clean but not inviting
  • Groceries are a small local centre; no shuk, limited vegan/GF range without driving to Ma'alot or Karmiel
  • Closer to the Lebanon border than anything else here — worth an honest look at the 2026 security picture
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
  • Ask whether the landlord will supply a double bed (or whether two singles can be pushed together) — this is close to a dealbreaker as listed
  • Verify the exact location: if the unit is further north/right on the border than Kfar Vradim proper, this becomes a skip
  • Ask the electricity estimate (radiators suggest an all-electric unit)
  • Confirm AC coverage — one 2.5hp unit is listed; check the bedrooms

Afula, Wolfson — ₪2500 all-in, furnished and bright, but the kitchen is a token kitchenette with almost no counter MAYBE
Afula · ₪2,000/mo rent · 2 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪2,500/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,000 + arnona ₪200 + utilities ₪300 (stated in ad) · Ad itemises: '2000 שכירות + 150 חשמל + 100 מים + 200 ארנונה + 50 ועד = 2500 ש"ח כולל חשבונות'. bills_stated_month 300 = electricity 150 + water 100 + vaad 50.
confirm short-term
A cheerful, well-renovated 2-room unit that is properly furnished — grey sofa and armchair, nesting coffee tables, double bed, wardrobe, fridge, microwave — with the all-in cost itemised at ₪2500 (₪2000 + ₪150 electricity + ₪100 water + ₪200 arnona + ₪50 vaad). It is clean, bright and would be pleasant to sit in. But the kitchen is the problem and it is the #1 criterion: a single short black run where the sink and the microwave consume nearly the whole worktop, no oven, no hob visible, leaving perhaps 40 cm of free surface. For a couple cooking vegan and gluten-free twice a day, that is a fail unless a bigger surface exists off-camera.
+
  • ₪2500 fully itemised all-in (ad: '2000 שכירות+ 150 חשמל+ 100 מים+ 200 ארנונה+ 50 ועד = 2500 כולל חשבונות') — ~₪2000/mo under cap
  • Genuinely furnished: sofa, armchair, coffee tables, double bed + mattress, wardrobe, fridge, washer, microwave, 2 ACs
  • Bright, freshly renovated, clean; nice light in the living room
  • Wolfson is a residential south-Afula street — quieter than the centre
  • Afula: 825 direct to TLV ~1h45, ~20–25 min to Hazorea
–
  • KITCHEN FAILS THE TOP CRITERION: one short run, sink + microwave eat the counter, ~40 cm of usable prep space, no oven, no hob visible
  • Ad lists 'כיריים' but nothing resembling a hob appears in the photos
  • Only 2 rooms and a small footprint — one work spot; the sofa/coffee-table living room won't host two desks
  • Only 4 photos, no dining table anywhere
  • The washing machine is squeezed into the bathroom under the boiler
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability (short_term unknown)
  • PHOTOGRAPH THE FULL KITCHEN and measure the free counter — if there is genuinely no more surface than the photo shows, skip regardless of price
  • Ask where the 'כיריים' is and whether there's an oven
  • Ask whether a dining/work table can be added — where would two people work?
  • Same agency also lists a 3-room unit on Yoseftal (f925417e67465c35) — compare

no photos
50sqm 2.5-room unit of a cottage at Yokheved Bat Miriam 34, Tel Baruch North — a calm, green residential neighborhood in north TLV. ₪4,000 including arnona and water (electricity on top → ≈₪4,200-4,300 all-in), furnished, immediate entry, aimed at 'student or couple', includes a mamad. Only 2 photos exist and both show dated 80s-style furniture; the ad itself says 'מטבחון' (kitchenette) and there is no kitchen photo — so the #1 criterion is an open risk. Space-wise 2.5 rooms works well for two workspaces.
+
  • ₪4,000 including arnona + water → ≈₪4,200-4,300 all-in, under cap
  • 2.5 rooms / 50sqm — room for two separate work spots + their own desk
  • Very quiet residential street; private-house neighborhood
  • Mamad (safe room) in the unit; washing machine, fridge, AC, vacuum/iron included
  • 'Suits a couple', immediate entry — landlord already expects flexible tenants
–
  • Kitchen described only as 'מטבחון' and never photographed — high risk it fails their cooking bar (oven unlikely)
  • Visible furniture is dated (grandma-style cabinets, old beds) — livable but not the bright/inviting brief
  • Ground floor of a cottage — light and privacy depend on the garden setup, unverified
  • Bus-dependent: no train/light-rail near (Bnei Brak rail ~2.3km); buses via Ramat HaHayal/Atidim
  • Only 2 photos — bathroom, living space largely unseen
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,650/mo all-in exceeds the ₪4,500 cap (auto-downgraded to skip)
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short_term 'likely', not stated)
  • Ask for kitchen photos/video first: oven? cooktop? counter length? fridge size? — decisive
  • Bed setup: photos show two narrow beds — is there a proper double?
  • Electricity billing arrangement (separate meter for the unit or flat fee?)
  • Internet included/available?
48

HaHagana, Petah Tikva — bright renovated studio, ₪3,500 truly all-in, but tiny with an apparent single bed MAYBE
Petah Tikva · לב המושבה / מרכז העיר · ₪3,500/mo rent · 1 rooms · 20 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪3,500/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-termvery small
A clean, bright, renovated studio in Lev HaMoshava where the ad explicitly states the price includes water, electricity AND arnona — a genuine ₪3,500 all-in with ₪1,000/mo of headroom. Zoomed in, the kitchen is better than it first looks: a ~2m counter with a 2-burner hob to the right of the sink, a countertop oven and microwave stacked on the left, wall cabinets, and a dining table that can double as prep overflow. The problems are size and the bed: the mattress sits in a narrow alcove between a wardrobe and the wall and looks single-width.
+
  • ₪3,500 ALL-IN, stated in the ad ('המחיר כולל את כל החשבונות (מים, חשמל וארנונה)') — ₪1,000/mo under the cap, the best price certainty in the batch
  • Bright, white, genuinely renovated and inviting; 'walk in with suitcases' furnished
  • Kitchen is workable-ish: 2-burner hob + countertop oven + microwave + a real (if short) counter run, washing machine, wall storage
  • Dining table + chairs = one proper work surface, with room for their own desk
  • Lev HaMoshava is PT city centre: the shuk, supermarkets and Super-Pharm are all walkable, and the Red Line light rail terminus plus the central bus station are close — direct light rail to Tel Aviv centre
  • AC, modern shower room
–
  • Very small single-room studio — two people inside it all day through a hot Sept/Oct is the main risk
  • The bed appears to be a single/narrow mattress wedged into an alcove — a real problem for a couple
  • Prep space is tight: the counter is shared between hob, sink and the oven/microwave stack; maybe 40–50cm genuinely free
  • Only one workable spot; no second room and no separation for two on calls
  • HaHagana sits near the central bus station — worth checking street noise
  • Broker listing (Real Unique)
?
  • Confirm the bed size — if it's a single, this is dead; if a double can be swapped in, it rises
  • Ask for the sqm and a wide shot of the whole room
  • Confirm the hob works and how much free counter there really is
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (short_term unknown)
  • Ask which floor and how noisy HaHagana is at street level
  • Ask whether the broker fee applies
48
Inviting Raanana villa basement unit — the nicest interior here, but all-in ~₪4,570 breaks the cap SKIP
Raanana · קרית שרת · ₪4,150/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,800/mo all-inincl. arnona · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,150 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'המחיר כולל ארנונה', 'חשמל על פי מונה', 'מים 140 ש"ח לחודשיים' (₪70/mo). Electricity amount not stated.
confirm short-term
Interior-wise this is the most appealing unit in the cluster: warm wood-look floors, a real living room with a dining table, sofa and TV, an existing desk-and-monitor work setup, a separate bedroom with a proper double bed, and a long grey kitchen counter run. It's a basement unit of a new villa in Kiryat Sharet, quiet northern Raanana near Raanana Park, with a private entrance and plentiful parking. Price kills it: ₪4,150 includes arnona, but electricity is by meter on top plus ₪70/mo water — a September–October with heavy AC realistically lands around ₪4,500–4,600 all-in, over the hard cap. No hob or oven is visible either.
+
  • By far the most inviting interior in this cluster — warm floors, a real furnished living room, sofa, dining table, TV and an existing desk with monitor
  • Separate bedroom with a proper double bed and wardrobe — a genuinely closable second space for two workers
  • Long kitchen counter run (~2.5m) with generous prep space
  • ₪4,150 includes arnona; water stated at only ₪140 per two months (₪70/mo)
  • Private entrance, lots of privacy, plentiful parking, quiet residential Kiryat Sharet near Raanana Park
  • Washing machine inside the unit; renovated bathroom and toilet
–
  • All-in breaks the ₪4,500 cap: ₪4,150 + ~₪70 water + unmetered electricity (~₪350 in an AC-heavy Sept–Oct) ≈ ₪4,570. NOTE: the overage rests on the ELECTRICITY ESTIMATE — the ad states neither a figure nor an average, so a low-usage month could land just inside
  • No stovetop and no oven visible anywhere in the kitchen photos, despite the counter length
  • Basement unit with an English courtyard — windows are small and light is borrowed; Sept–Oct heat in a low unit is a comfort question
  • Ad is explicit that it's for long-term ('לתווך ארוך') — a 2-month let contradicts the listing
  • Ad opens with 'לא מתאים לעובדים זרים, ולא לאנשים מבוגרים' — a discriminatory screen that says something about the landlord
  • Raanana has no coastal-line station: Raanana West is on the Herzliya–Kfar Saba branch, a few km away — Tel Aviv is a bus or a connection, not a direct train
?
  • Only worth reopening if the landlord states a real electricity average that brings all-in under ₪4,500, or negotiates rent down
  • If reopened: confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability and ask about a hob/oven
48
Fully-furnished HaTikva 2-room, 7-12 min walk to HaHagana train, arnona+water included — but zero photos MAYBE
Tel Aviv · התקוה, בית יעקב, נווה צה"ל · ₪3,600/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,250/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪3,600 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'המחיר כולל ארנונה ומים' — arnona and water included. Electricity/gas not mentioned.
confirm short-term
no photos
A 2-room on David Tidhar in HaTikva, advertised as fully furnished ('מרוהטת קומפלט'), bright, immediate entry, ₪3,600 with arnona and water included — which would put all-in comfortably under the cap with real money left over. On paper it's the best-value transport proposition in the batch: David Tidhar sits 7-12 min walk from HaHagana railway station depending on house number. The blocker is that the listing has NO photos at all, so the #1 criterion — a real cookable kitchen — is completely unverified, as is 'fully furnished' and 'bright'.
+
  • Arnona and water included in the ₪3,600 — all-in likely ~₪4,000-4,250, leaving ₪250-500/mo of headroom
  • Excellent rail access: ~7-12 min walk to HaHagana train station (direct to HaShalom/Savidor, and a straight run south to Gedera-side connections)
  • Advertised as fully furnished with immediate entry — matches a couple who own no furniture
  • Shuk HaTikva is walkable: cheap, excellent fresh produce, which suits vegan cooking well
  • Ad explicitly claims the flat is bright ('דירה מוארת')
  • Long-established local agency (est. 1945) specialising in exactly this neighbourhood — likely to know the stock
–
  • ZERO photos — the kitchen, the furniture, the light and the condition are all pure unknowns. Every positive here is the agent's word, and in this batch agent copy has repeatedly contradicted the photos
  • HaTikva is a rough-edged neighbourhood; street-level quiet and feel vary sharply block to block and can't be judged without the address/photos
  • Electricity and gas not addressed by the ad
  • David Tidhar runs near the Ayalon — sections of it are exposed to motorway noise
  • Agency listing — broker fee likely
?
  • REQUEST PHOTOS before anything else — specifically the kitchen (counter, oven, hob), and confirm what 'מרוהטת קומפלט' actually includes (bed? desk? table?)
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (Yad2 regular rental, short_term unknown)
  • Get the house number — the walk to HaHagana station and the Ayalon noise exposure both swing on it
  • Ask for the electricity/gas monthly figure and whether the broker fee applies to a short let
  • AC not mentioned — confirm it exists (near-universal, but verify)
48
Over cap at ~₪4,750 all-in — spacious Nahariya grandma-flat with a huge vintage kitchen SKIP
Nahariya · עיר, מרכז העיר · ₪3,700/mo rent · 3.5 rooms · 85 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,750/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,700 + arnona ₪400 + est. utilities ₪650
border areaconfirm short-term
An 85sqm 3.5-room on Herzl in central Nahariya, in a building with an elevator, storage, shelter and private parking. The kitchen is enormous — a ~4m run of retro cream/avocado cabinets with a marble-look counter and an eat-in table, which is a lot of prep space — and there is a laundry porch with washer and dryer. But everything is deeply 1970s: floral sofas, china cabinets, an old bedroom set, a tired bathroom with a worn door. It is also over budget.
+
  • Enormous kitchen counter run plus an eat-in table — the most prep space in the cluster
  • Spacious 85sqm / 3.5 rooms with an elevator, storage, shelter and private parking
  • Central Nahariya: Gaaton boulevard, shops and services within walking distance
  • Washer and dryer on a laundry porch; Nahariya station has direct TLV trains (~1h44, every 30 min)
–
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,750 (₪3,700 + ₪400 arnona + ₪650 est. bills) — over the ₪4,500 hard cap, so it is a skip
  • Very dated: 1970s kitchen, floral sofas, china cabinets, tired bathroom with a worn door
  • Only 'partially furnished' per the ad despite the full-looking photos
  • Nahariya is ~9km from the Lebanese border — at the edge of the siren zone the search is meant to avoid
  • TLV is ~1h44 by train, the longest of the viable towns here
  • Ad says entry from 1 June — the listing may be stale
?
  • The overage rests partly on the ₪650 estimate: rent + stated arnona alone is ₪4,100, so if real bills come in under ~₪400/mo it would fit — worth a call only if nothing better survives
  • Confirm Nahariya's border-proximity status against the exclusion rules before pursuing any Nahariya listing
  • Confirm what 'partially furnished' actually leaves in place
48
₪1,900 furnished 3-room a block from the Kiryat Yam beach — dated and worn throughout MAYBE
Kiryat Yam · ₪1,900/mo rent · 3 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪2,550/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪1,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
First-line-to-the-sea 3-room on the 3rd floor at Yoseftal 34, furnished exactly as photographed: an old cream bedroom set with a double bed, a floral sofa and dark wall unit, a narrow galley kitchen with old cabinets and a moderate counter, and a tired bathroom with an old tub and a very dated separate toilet. Everything works and it is cheap, but nothing has been touched in decades. Keep it as a fallback rather than a target.
+
  • ₪1,900 for a furnished 3-room — all-in ≈ ₪2,550, enormous budget headroom
  • Actually furnished (double bed, wardrobe, sofa, wall unit, fridge, washer)
  • First line to the sea in Kiryat Yam — beach within a short walk
  • 3 rooms means a closable work room is possible
  • Galley kitchen has a real counter run along the wall
–
  • Dated and worn everywhere: 1970s bathroom, old toilet, tired galley kitchen, heavy old furniture
  • No hob or oven visible in the kitchen photos
  • Not a place you would enjoy being in all day — the opposite of bright and inviting
  • No train in Kiryat Yam; Metronit + train for Tel Aviv trips
  • Agent listing ('what you see in the photos') with no arnona or bills stated
?
  • Confirm ~2-month availability Sept-Nov 2026 (short-term not stated)
  • Confirm the kitchen has a working hob/oven
  • Ask for the arnona figure
  • Ask how far the actual beach walk is and whether the flat gets sea light
48
Nof HaGalil, Rimon — ₪1900 for a fully furnished 3-room, but tired throughout and the worst-placed town here MAYBE
Nof HaGalil · צפונית / רסקו · ₪1,900/mo rent · 3 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪2,550/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪1,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
car recommendedconfirm short-term
Very cheap and fully furnished ('מרוהטת קומפלט'): 3 rooms near the municipality for ₪1900, which at roughly ₪2550 all-in leaves nearly ₪2000/mo spare. The kitchen is real — white units, a freestanding stove with oven, fridge — though the counter is short. Everything else is tired: heavy brown 1970s display cabinets, a dark leather armchair, terrazzo floors, a grim service balcony and a bathroom with a shower curtain, mop and buckets. The price is a genuine advantage, but 'a nice place to BE' is a stated top priority and this is the opposite, in the town with no train and the longest drive to Hazorea.
+
  • ₪1900 rent — cheapest 3-room in the cluster; ~₪2550 all-in with the fallback estimate, ~₪1950/mo under cap
  • Fully furnished ('מרוהטת קומפלט') — nothing to buy
  • Real kitchen appliances: freestanding stove with oven, fridge, sink
  • 3 rooms — space for a closable second workspace
  • Near the municipality, so central for shops and services; immediate entry
–
  • Tired and uninviting throughout: dated brown furniture, terrazzo floors, shabby service balcony, dated bathroom with a shower curtain — fails the 'bright, clean, inviting' priority
  • Kitchen counter is short — real appliances but limited prep space
  • Nof HaGalil: no train station (bus 833 to TLV, 2h+), ~30–35 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea — worst on both transport and family
  • 'חובה שקים' (cheques required) — awkward for a 2-month let
  • No stated utilities; only 4 photos
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability (short_term unknown)
  • Worth a viewing ONLY if the cheaper Afula options fall through — the money saved is real but the living quality is poor
  • Ask for photos of the third room and the bedrooms (none shown)
  • Measure the kitchen counter and confirm the oven works
  • Get arnona + bills figures
47
Rehovot Marmorek — all-inclusive ground-floor flat with a work desk, but a weak galley kitchen MAYBE
Rehovot · מרמורק · ₪4,100/mo rent · 2.0 rooms · 40.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,750/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,100 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
KITCHEN is a narrow galley: white cabinets, a white counter, a sink and a fridge, but no oven or stovetop visible (at most a possible portable single hob) and only modest prep space — marginal for a couple who cooks most meals. The unit is lived-in and somewhat worn/cluttered, but it does offer a real desk with a monitor and chair (a proper workspace), a renovated bathroom with a rain shower, and everything bundled into the rent (electricity, water, taxes, cable, internet). Rehovot's location near Gedera is the main draw.
+
  • All-inclusive ₪4,100 (electricity, water, taxes, cable and internet) — predictable cost
  • Real work setup present (desk + chair), plus a renovated rain-shower bathroom
  • Rehovot: train to Tel Aviv ~30 min, near Weizmann, ~15 min to the Gedera family
  • Ground floor — no stairs
–
  • Galley kitchen with no visible oven/stovetop and limited counter — poor fit for the couple's top priority
  • Lived-in, worn and cluttered condition; open clothing rack rather than a proper wardrobe
  • Ground floor can mean less quiet/privacy
  • ₪4,100 is mid-range for what is a small, dated-feeling 40m² unit
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month (Sept–Nov 2026) short-term let is acceptable — listing is annual-oriented
  • Ask specifically about cooking appliances: is there a stovetop and/or oven, or only a sink + fridge?
  • Clarify whether the unit will be cleared/cleaned and what furniture stays for a short-term tenant
  • Ask about noise and privacy on the ground floor

46
Clean studio, but 1-burner kitchenette, no oven, small counter SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,447/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪5,097/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,447 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen is clean but modest: a real gray counter with base cabinets/drawers, a full-size fridge/freezer and a proper sink, but NO oven and only a single portable electric hotplate (one burner) plus a microwave — thin for a vegan+GF couple who cook most meals, and prep space shrinks once the sink is accounted for. It is a freshly renovated, furnished studio (bed, sofa-chair, wheeled table, wardrobe, folding dining table) with a standout private wooden terrace overlooking Haifa bay, priced just under budget at 4,447. The appliance gaps are 'negotiable' by the couple's own rule — they can add a countertop oven / 2-burner hob — but the small counter would get crowded, and the single room gives no acoustic separation for two simultaneous video calls. It sits up the Carmel slope on Wingate Blvd: a mini-market is next door, but reaching Tel Aviv means a local bus/Metronit down to the train (~2h), a real friction with no car, plus a long outdoor staircase to the unit.
+
  • Freshly renovated, clean and bright; new tiling and fixtures throughout
  • Standout private wooden terrace with panoramic Haifa bay/port view (rare plus)
  • A genuine if small kitchen — full-size fridge/freezer, real inset sink, base cabinets + a bank of drawers (not a hotplate-on-minifridge)
  • Furnished (bed, blue sofa-chair, wheeled table, wardrobe, folding dining table + 2 chairs) — livable for 2 months bringing only a desk
  • Price 4,447 just under the 4,500 budget
  • Mini-market next door at Wingate 6; fuller supermarkets ~0.7 km in Hadar
  • Full window available (61/61 days) and monthly stays allowed
  • Mild Sept-Nov Haifa weather makes the terrace usable as an outdoor work/call spot
–
  • No oven and only a single portable electric hotplate (one burner) — weak for a couple cooking most meals vegan + gluten-free (lots of from-scratch cooking, grains, legumes, roasting)
  • Counter/prep space is only modest and the sink eats into it; adding a countertop oven plus a bigger hob would crowd the limited surface
  • Studio layout: one room for two simultaneous remote video calls, no second workspace or acoustic separation
  • Up on the Carmel slope — intercity buses and trains to Tel Aviv are down at sea level (~2.5 km); TLV trip is ~2h via a local-bus/Metronit + train transfer, a real friction with no car
  • Access is via a long outdoor wooden staircase — awkward hauling groceries without a car
  • Wingate is a through-boulevard with a local reputation for traffic noise/honking (this set-back unit may be insulated, but worth verifying)
  • Vegan/GF specialty items likely need a trip to a larger store in Hadar or Carmel Center
  • Over budget: ≈₪5,097/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Confirm exact Sept-Nov dates and that the host accepts a ~2-month monthly booking at this rate (short-term availability listed as open but unconfirmed for the specific stay)
  • Verify the specific unit's quietness given Wingate's traffic-noise reputation — check it is set back from the road
  • Ask whether the host will add/allow a countertop oven and a 2-burner hob, and whether counter space fits both
  • Check how many steps the outdoor staircase has — relevant for a 2-month stay with no car and grocery runs
  • Confirm frequency of buses 76/132/133 and Metronit 5 down to the train for Tel Aviv, and connections to family in Kibbutz Hazorea and Gedera

Bat Yam · לב העיר · ₪3,299/mo rent · 1 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16

landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
A 1-room studio on Yerushalayim 74 in Lev Ha'ir, 2nd floor with a lift, described as rear-facing and quiet, with the price including everything except electricity — an all-in of roughly 3,600, the second cheapest here. The kitchen is a genuine ~1.5m stone countertop with cabinets and a sink (washing machine underneath), which is real prep space, but there is no stovetop, no oven and no fridge in frame. The flat is otherwise bare — a mattress on a frame and a wall TV — and, as with the other listing from this agent, the photo set mixes units: one bathroom is a clean new one, another is grubby with a yellow curtain.
+
  • All-in ~3,600 (arnona and vaad included, electricity extra) — leaves ~900/mo spare
  • A real ~1.5m countertop with cabinets — the prep space a portable induction hob + countertop oven would need
  • Ad says rear-facing and quiet — away from the street noise
  • 2nd floor with a lift; central Lev Ha'ir, walk to the beach, trains and the mall
  • Renovated, clean and bright in the newer photos
–
  • No stovetop and no oven shown — cooking gear would have to be bought outright, and there's no fridge in any photo either
  • Very bare: a bed and a TV, no table, no sofa, no work surface — thin for a furnished 2-month stay
  • Photo set mixes at least two apartments (one clean new bathroom, one grubby old one), so condition is unverified
  • Single room — no second workspace or closable door
  • Broker listing (שיא מרכז) — fee likely
?
  • Ask which photos are actually this unit, and request fresh ones
  • Confirm whether a hob/oven and fridge are supplied, or negotiate them in
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (short_term unknown)
  • Ask what electricity typically runs per month

Fichman, Holon — ₪3,600 with arnona/water/vaad included, but a hotplate-and-1m-counter kitchenette MAYBE
Holon · מרכז, קרית עבודה · ₪3,600/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,250/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪3,600 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'דמי השכירות כוללים ארנונה מים וועד' — arnona, water and vaad included; electricity NOT stated as included, so the estimate still applies to it.
confirm short-term
A small, tidy 2-room in central Holon (Kiryat Avoda) where the rent explicitly includes arnona, water and vaad — so the real all-in is close to ₪3,600 + electricity, leaving real budget headroom. Furnished with a sofa, coffee table, a large wooden sideboard, a double bed and a closet nook. The catch is the kitchen: a short wood kitchenette wall in the living room with an electric hotplate, a microwave and roughly a metre of counter that the sink eats into. No oven, no dining table.
+
  • Rent stated to include arnona, water and building vaad — all-in lands ~₪4,250 even with the estimate, and realistically ~₪3,900 once only electricity is added. Around ₪600–900/mo of leftover budget
  • Furnished and lived-in-ready: sofa, coffee table, sideboard, double bed, closet, AC, renovated bathroom with a glass shower
  • Central Holon (Kiryat Avoda/Merkaz), near Sokolov — walkable shops and supermarkets, dense bus service to Tel Aviv
  • Cheapest genuinely liveable unit here
–
  • Kitchen is the weak point and it's the #1 criterion: electric hotplate only (no oven), ~1m of counter with the sink in the middle, no real second work surface
  • No dining table and no desk — the only work surface is a low coffee table; workspace for two is poor
  • Small 2-room; the 'second room' is a bedroom, not a closable office
  • Photos date from Feb 2024 — the ad has been recycled; the unit may not look like this now
  • Broker ('סוכנות תיווך ישיר') — expect a fee unless it's a landlord-paid listing
?
  • Request current photos of the kitchen wall and confirm the actual counter length — this is the make-or-break
  • Ask whether a countertop oven and a small table can be added; with the price headroom they could buy both
  • Confirm electricity is the only extra bill, and get the average monthly figure
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (short_term unknown)
  • Ask whether the broker fee applies
46
Bright white Givatayim duplex with a loft and great bones — undermined by a token kitchenette, no bed, and 17-month-old photos MAYBE
Givatayim · פועלי הרכבת · ₪3,500/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A 2-room duplex on Sirkin in Givatayim's Po'alei HaRakevet, ₪3,500 (~₪4,150 all-in, comfortably in cap). It is the most appealing SPACE in the batch — bright white walls, pale wood floors, an open lower level and a mezzanine reached by a staircase, which could plausibly give two separated work zones. But the kitchen is a token kitchenette: roughly a metre of counter with two small cabinets and a small hob, no oven, essentially no prep space. Furnishing is thin (a sofa, a coffee table, one chair — no bed, no table, no desk), and the photos date from February 2025, so the listing is likely stale.
+
  • Genuinely bright and inviting — white walls, pale wood floors, good light; the nicest space to spend a hot Sept-Oct in, of everything here
  • Duplex with a mezzanine level: rare vertical separation that could give the two of them distinct work zones without a second closable room
  • All-in ~₪4,150 leaves ~₪350/mo of headroom
  • Po'alei HaRakevet is a quiet, well-off pocket (socioeconomic 8/10) of ~3,000 residents — calm street, not a through-route, and the ad confirms the unit is rear-facing
  • Walking distance to the Katsnelson strip (bars, restaurants, Oved's sabich per the ad) and to shops; frequent buses to Tel Aviv along Katsnelson/Ben Gurion
  • Givatayim is well placed for both Gedera (via Tel Aviv) and Kibbutz Hazorea (via Savidor) trips
  • Closets and AC confirmed in the ad
–
  • Kitchen is close to a FAIL: ~1m of counter with a sink in it, two small cabinets, a small hob, no oven (photos 00, 01). There is nowhere to prep — for two people cooking most meals vegan and gluten-free, this is the wrong kitchen
  • NO BED shown or mentioned. The ad lists only closets and AC; the visible furniture is a sofa, a coffee table and one chair. For a couple who own no furniture, this may be effectively unfurnished
  • Photos are dated February 2025 — ~17 months old. The listing is very likely stale, and the photos may not reflect current condition or contents
  • No desk or dining table anywhere in the photos
  • The mezzanine is reached by a steep open staircase and the upper space is empty and low — its usefulness as a work zone is unverified
  • The planned Katsnelson-Ben Gurion metro station is years away — it is not a current transport benefit, and there is no light rail or train within walking distance today
  • Agency listing (תיווך ירושלמי) — broker fee likely; the ad text is two lines and tells us almost nothing
  • Only 4 photos, and none of the mezzanine level
?
  • CONFIRM IT STILL EXISTS — Feb-2025 photos on a July-2026 board strongly suggest a stale or recycled listing. Check liveness before spending effort
  • Request current photos, specifically the kitchen, the mezzanine, and whatever furniture is actually in place now
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (Yad2 regular rental, short_term unknown)
  • Ask directly: is there a bed? A table? Is the hob the only cooking appliance, and could a countertop oven be added?
  • Get the house number on Sirkin — the Katsnelson end is livelier/noisier than the Po'alei HaRakevet end
  • If the kitchen could be improved, the space and location would make this a strong contender — it's the bones that are good here
46
Arlozorov 108, Upper Hadar — ₪1,500 (!), step-free access and a butcher-block prep island, but no bed and no cooktop in the photos MAYBE
Haifa · הדר עליון · ₪1,500/mo rent · 1.5 rooms · 25 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪2,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪1,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
By far the cheapest listing in the cluster at ₪1,500/mo for 1.5 rooms — roughly ₪2,150 all-in, which would leave over ₪2,300/mo of budget. Genuinely interesting features: completely step-free access from the street, an enclosed balcony counted as half a room, a wooden butcher-block island giving extra prep space next to the kitchen, and a dining table with chairs. But the photos show no bed anywhere, no cooktop, and the flat is dated and dim with a bare bulb and old cream cabinets.
+
  • ₪1,500/mo — extraordinarily cheap; ~₪2,150 all-in leaves >₪2,300/mo for a car, co-working and everything else
  • A wooden butcher-block island plus the counter run and a dining table with chairs — more prep and work surface than most of the ₪2,500 units
  • Completely step-free access from the street ('הגישה לדירה ללא מדרגות בכלל!!!') — no Hadar stair-climbing
  • Enclosed balcony as a half-room, olive-tree greenery outside the window
  • Fridge, washing machine, AC, window screens included; no broker fee
  • Established local broker (Deutsch, operating since 1963) — likely a straight dealer
  • Upper Hadar: walkable to the Massada café strip and Talpiot Market, dense buses
–
  • NO bed anywhere in the photos and none mentioned in the ad — the couple owns no bed, so this may be effectively unfurnished
  • No cooktop or oven visible — only a toaster oven on the island
  • Dated and dim: bare bulb, old cream cabinets, tired tiling; not an inviting place to spend a hot September indoors
  • 1.5 rooms is very small for two people working from home all day
  • Arlozorov is a long busy Hadar artery — noise risk at number 108
  • 'מחיר מציאה' (bargain price) at ₪1,500 in Hadar is unusual — find out why
?
  • Ask what furniture is included — is there a bed at all?
  • Ask whether there's a cooktop, or whether one can be installed
  • Ask why it's this cheap — damp, basement-level light, or a problem with the building?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — short_term unknown
  • Ask about street noise at Arlozorov 108 and which side it faces

Nof HaGalil, HaShoshanim 21 — fully furnished 3-room, but a cramped galley kitchen and the priciest thing in the weakest town MAYBE
Nof HaGalil · בן גוריון · ₪3,000/mo rent · 3 rooms · 76 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,650/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
car recommendedconfirm short-term
A fully furnished 75 sqm 3-room flat on floor 2 with plenty of parking, close to transport and shops, in a quiet area. Eight photos show it honestly: three furnished bedrooms (one with a desk and TV), a separate WC, a decent renovated shower room and a beige living room with recliner seating. The kitchen is the weak point and it is the point that matters most — a narrow galley with a short counter and a tiny two-seat table wedged at the end. At ₪3000, plus a rough peeling service balcony and Nof HaGalil's poor transport and 30+ minutes to Hazorea, the value is not there.
+
  • Fully furnished ('מרוהט מלא') — three bedrooms, sofas, a desk with a chair in one room
  • 3 rooms / 75 sqm on floor 2, plenty of parking, close to transport and shops
  • Ad describes a good, quiet area ('אזור טוב ושקט')
  • Renovated shower room; ACs visible in bedrooms
  • Eight honest photos — you can see what you'd get
–
  • Cramped narrow galley kitchen with a short counter — the single most important criterion, and it's poor
  • ₪3000 + est. ₪650 ≈ ₪3650 all-in — the most expensive option in Nof HaGalil for the least kitchen
  • Nof HaGalil: no train (bus 833 to TLV, 2h+), ~30–35 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea
  • Dated throughout: terrazzo floors, old wooden bedsteads, beige recliner suite
  • Service balcony is rough — peeling paint, exposed surfaces
  • 'חובה שקים' (cheques required)
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability (short_term unknown)
  • Measure the kitchen counter and check for an oven — if there's no realistic prep space, skip
  • Only pursue if the Afula and Migdal HaEmek options all fail
  • Get arnona + bills figures for a 75 sqm flat
  • NOTE: possible-dup candidate 2a564a0ae457e878 (Yizrael 11) is a DIFFERENT unit — that one has a dark granite galley, this one a white galley

Nice studio, great transit — but no stovetop AND no oven MAYBE
Haifa · ₪3,745/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,395/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,745 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen, their #1 criterion, is the weak point: a real single-run granite counter with a full-size fridge/freezer, microwave, kettle and Nespresso — but NO stovetop and NO oven anywhere, only a microwave. The couple said missing ONE appliance is negotiable, but here BOTH are absent, so they'd have to buy a portable hob AND a countertop oven, and prep space is only modest single-run counter (no dedicated second surface). It's clean, fully furnished, well under budget, and the Lower City location is superb for a car-free couple (walk to HaShmona train + Metronit BRT). Downsides: it's essentially a studio (tight for two on calls) on a lively, not-quiet downtown street.
+
  • Full-size fridge/freezer (top-freezer) plus a real granite counter run — genuine prep surface, not a mini-fridge kitchenette
  • Excellent car-free transport: walkable to Haifa Center–HaShmona train (Tel Aviv ~1hr, 4 trains/hr peak) and Metronit BRT lines 1 & 2
  • Well under budget at ₪3,745/mo (cap ₪4,500)
  • Clean, bright, freshly renovated and fully furnished — livable for 2 months bringing only a desk
  • Downtown Lower City has walkable groceries plus vegan spots and GF bakeries
–
  • No stovetop AND no oven — both primary cooking instruments missing; only a microwave. Would need to buy a portable hob and a countertop oven
  • Counter is a single modest run largely taken up by microwave + appliances + sink; no dedicated second prep surface
  • Effectively a studio: bed, dining and kitchen in one room — hard for two remote workers to take calls simultaneously
  • Lower City downtown is lively with bars/nightlife — a real concern for a couple that values quiet
  • Only one small dining table as a work surface beyond the desk they bring
?
  • Confirm ~2-month availability within Sept–Nov 2026 (availability_window not listed; it's an Airbnb monthly stay)
  • Ask the host whether a stovetop/hob and/or a small oven can be provided — that would flip the kitchen verdict
  • Verify evening/weekend street noise on Sderot HaMaginim before committing (quiet is a stated priority)
  • Clarify whether it is truly a single studio room or has any partition for a second workspace
45

rent ₪4,500 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term

3-room yechidat diur (floor 0) in Rishon's prestigious, quiet Nobel-laureates quarter. Photos show near-new finishes, a bedroom with a real desk + office chair, and two modern bathrooms — but small, high windows throughout give a dim semi-basement feel, and the 'kitchen' is a single ~2m counter with sink and fridge: no visible cooktop, no oven, no upper cabinets. ₪4,500 includes arnona + cables, so electricity/water push all-in to ~₪4,800+, over the cap.   • Real workspace: desk + office chair already in place; 2 bathrooms

  • All-in ≈ ₪4,800+ once electricity/water added — over the ₪4,500 cap
  • Standard rental, short-term not stated — needs landlord agreement
  • Train not walkable (~2.6km to Moshe Dayan); buses good (College of Management corridor) but car-lite living is bus-dependent

Ad honestly says 'partial furniture' and 'not including bills' (~₪3,250–3,400 est. all-in with arnona ~₪300 est. + utilities). Clean, plain 30sqm studio in Kiryat Haim East (Moshe Haim Shapira St): double bed + 6-seat dining table in one room, renovated bathroom, and a kitchenette corner of ~1m counter with sink, washer and black fridge — no cooktop, no oven, minimal prep. All bills included at ₪1,800 ('כולל כל החשבונות') = the cheapest all-in of the cluster with ~₪2.7k headroom. Transit is a real strength: Kiryat Motzkin station (inter-city coastal line, ~36 direct southbound trains/day) is ~700m ≈ 10 min walk. But for a couple cooking most vegan+GF meals, this kitchen most likely fails on viewing; the big table would have to double as prep space. Photos are NOT the same unit as HaShomer 30 (different rooms/kitchens) — the dup flag is same-template noise.
+
  • Cheapest all-in of the cluster: ₪1,800 incl. bills → ~₪2.7k headroom (funds co-working, appliances, car)

  • Agency-managed, checks required; short-term unknown

landlord states arnona + utilities included

35sqm 2-rooms at Ta'anach 36 (Neve Yosef), floor minus-3 on the hillside, managed by an agency and priced at ₪2,000 including arnona, water AND electricity — the lowest all-in cost in the Haifa cluster. Photos show a fresh white renovation: black sofa and office chair mid-staging, double mattress on a platform, wet-room shower, and a neat kitchen counter with sink and washing machine — but no fridge, hob or oven visible anywhere. Same building (and same renovation spec) as listing 510ad6da943402b8; layouts and bathrooms differ, so they read as two subdivided units rather than one duplicate. Neve Yosef is a weak, bus-dependent neighborhood; for two indoor-working cooks the basement-level light and unverified kitchen are the deal risks.
+
  • ₪2,000 with EVERYTHING included (arnona+water+electricity stated) — ₪2,500/mo headroom
  • Freshly renovated, clean finish; AC; washing machine
  • Property-management agency — flexible terms plausibly negotiable
  • Buses nearby toward University/Technion and city center (Arad/Ta'anach stops)
–
  • Kitchen shows NO fridge, hob or oven in photos — unverified for a couple whose #1 need is cooking
  • Floor minus-3 (hillside) — daylight likely limited; windows barred
  • 35sqm two small rooms — cramped for two simultaneous remote workers
  • Neve Yosef: socio-economically weak area, bus-dependent, no walkable shuk/train
  • Short-term unknown (agency ad)
?

  • Verify fridge + cooking appliances are provided ('full furniture' claimed but not photographed)
  • Ask about actual daylight at floor -3 and which direction windows face
  • Same building as the private ad at Ta'anach 36 (510ad6da943402b8) — check whether it's the same landlord/unit before viewing both

~₪3,185/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪3,000 + arnona ₪185
confirm short-term
A 2-room semi-basement unit in Maas, a small moshav near Petah Tikva. The kitchen is a modest white run with a sink, microwave and (per the ad) an electric hob — real but small counter space, no oven. The interior is plain and dim: dark leather sofas, a small glass table, bare walls, tiled throughout. It is cheap enough to leave real headroom, but nothing about it is inviting and the location has no transit.
+
  • All-in ~₪3,835 with the fallback estimate — around ₪650/mo of headroom
  • Two rooms: a separate bedroom, so the living room could be a work space
  • Kitchen has a counter, cabinets, a full-size fridge and an electric hob per the ad
  • Separate entrance, two exposures, no parking problem on the street
  • Furnished: sofas, a small table, bed, wardrobe
–
  • Dim and plain — dark leather furniture, bare white walls, no styling. Uninviting for two people indoors all day
  • Entrance is below street level; the photos read semi-basement despite the ad's claim of windows and two exposures
  • No oven visible or mentioned; counter space is modest
  • Maas is a small moshav with no train station and thin bus service — a car is effectively mandatory, and the TLV run would be slow
  • No yard use ('אין שימוש בחצר')
  • No workspace furniture at all — only a small glass dining table
  • Far from family in Gedera relative to the rest of this cluster
?

  • Ask whether an oven can be added
