120sqm 4.5-room near Afeka College with a genuine full kitchen (oven + hob) and short-term explicitly offered — but the price is undisclosed and almost certainly double the budget MAYBE
Tel Aviv · ₪1/mo rent · 4.5 rooms · 120 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪651/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪1 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
The only listing in the batch with a real, proper kitchen: the photos show a full run of cabinets with a built-in oven and hob, a long counter, and a round dining table for six, plus a large living room with a corner sofa. It's a 120sqm 4.5-room flat near Afeka College in north-east Tel Aviv (Bnei Efraim), and the ad explicitly says it can be let long OR short term and furnished-partially or empty. The blocker is that the listed price is a placeholder (1), meaning undisclosed — and a 120sqm 4.5-room Tel Aviv flat realistically prices at roughly double the 4,500 all-in cap. It's marketed to flatmates (לשותפים), which may mean per-room pricing.
+
  • A genuinely cookable kitchen — built-in oven AND hob AND a long counter run. This is the only listing in the batch that clears the #1 non-negotiable outright
  • Round dining table seating six plus a large corner sofa: a real eating surface and an obvious second work spot
  • 4.5 rooms / ~120sqm — the '3-room flats shine' case, taken further: each of them could have a closable room to work in
  • Short-term explicitly offered in the ad ('אפשרי להשכרה לתקופה ארוכה או קצרה') — rare, and it removes the usual gate
  • Flexible furnishing: can be taken partially furnished or empty
  • Bright living room with large curtained windows; classic terrazzo floors, clean and well kept
  • North-east Tel Aviv (Bnei Efraim, near Afeka College): quiet residential streets, and good road access north to Kibbutz Hazorea
  • Bus stop 'Afeka College / Bnei Efraim' ~3 min walk; Tel Aviv University rail station is the nearest train
–
  • PRICE UNDISCLOSED — the listing carries a placeholder of 1. A 120sqm 4.5-room Tel Aviv flat is realistically 8,000-11,000/mo, roughly double the all-in cap. This is very likely a skip once the number arrives
  • Marketed 'to flatmates' (להשכרה לשותפים), which suggests per-room letting or sharing with strangers — not a couple taking the whole flat
  • Only 2 photos, and neither shows a bedroom, a bathroom or the state of the rest of the flat
  • The furniture shown is dated (heavy wood coffee table, terrazzo, old cabinetry), and 'partially furnished or empty' means a bed is not guaranteed
  • North-east TLV is the far side of the city from Roy's usual Tel Aviv centre — no light rail walkable; buses or the TAU train, and Afeka is ~28 min walk from the nearest rail station
  • Listed by a commercial/industrial brokerage (ע.ש.ר), whose stated speciality is warehouses and offices — not a residential specialist
?
  • ASK THE PRICE FIRST — this is the single question that decides it. One WhatsApp; if it's over ~3,850 rent it's dead
  • If the price is per-room for flatmates, ask what the whole flat costs for 2 months
  • Confirm the exact Sept-Nov window and what furniture (especially a double bed) would be included
  • Ask for photos of the bedrooms and bathroom
  • Ask what arnona and bills run on a 120sqm flat — on this size they will be heavy (arnona alone could be 700+/mo), which likely blows the cap even if rent were miraculously low
  • AC: a split unit is visible in the living room; confirm the bedrooms have them
45
3,000 garden flat on Balfour with a yard, bills included — but zero photos MAYBE
Bat Yam · הגבול רוטשילד · ₪3,000/mo rent · 1 rooms · 34 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,650/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪3,000 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: "כולל ההוצאות ... בלי חשמל" — expenses included, electricity excluded
confirm short-term
no photos
The cheapest listing in the batch: a 1-room garden apartment with a small yard on Balfour, near the beach and the train, with the price explicitly including expenses except electricity — an all-in of roughly 3,300, leaving ~1,200/mo of budget spare. The problem is total opacity: there are no photos at all, so the kitchen, furnishing level, condition and light are all unknown. Balfour is a genuinely good address for this couple — a Red Line stop on the street, Shufersal Deal at Balfour 25 and Balfour Market at 97 — which makes it worth one message asking for photos rather than a silent skip.
+
  • Lowest price in the batch: ~3,000 + electricity ≈ 3,300 all-in, ~1,200/mo left for a car or co-working
  • Private yard — rare at this budget and valuable for a hot Sept–Oct
  • Balfour has its own Red Line light-rail stop; direct ride to Tel Aviv
  • Walkable groceries: Shufersal Deal at Balfour 25, Balfour Market at Balfour 97
  • Ad states the price includes the bills except electricity
–
  • ZERO photos — kitchen, furniture, condition, light and size are all completely unknown; photo absence at this price is a real risk signal
  • 1 room only — no separate closable workspace for a second person
  • Ground-floor garden flats in Bat Yam are often dark and damp; nothing here rules that out
  • Broker listing (י.ג קפיטל) — fee likely
?
  • Request photos — especially of the kitchen (oven? stovetop? counter space?) and the main room
  • Confirm the furnishing level; the ad never says furnished
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (short_term unknown)
  • Ask what electricity typically runs per month
45
Best kitchen in the batch (real range + oven, granite counter run) — but priced at the cap on a hostile traffic artery SKIP
Tel Aviv · ₪4,200/mo rent · 2 rooms · 55 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,850/mo all-inincl. arnona · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,200 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'המחיר כולל תשלום ארנונה, מים וועד בית. לא כולל חשמל וגז.' — arnona, water and vaad included; electricity and gas excluded and not quantified.
confirm short-term
Ground-floor 40sqm 2-room off Derech Bar Lev / Lehi in the Kfar Shalem–Yad Eliyahu belt, with a 15sqm yard. The photos show the only genuinely cookable kitchen in this batch: a long granite counter run, a full freestanding gas range WITH an oven, and a big fridge, opening onto a large, bright, properly furnished living space (sofa, TV unit, dining table, double bed, wardrobe). The catch is money and street: 4200 includes arnona/water/vaad but explicitly excludes electricity and gas, so realistic all-in lands ~4550-4800 — at or over the hard cap — and the ad's claim of 'very quiet' contradicts what Bar Lev/Lehi actually is.
+
  • Only listing here with a real oven + full gas range plus a long granite counter — the #1 criterion is genuinely met
  • Large, bright, open living/kitchen space; feels roomy rather than cramped, unusual at this price in TLV
  • Properly furnished: sofa, dining table, double bed, wardrobe, TV unit, AC — they'd need to bring almost nothing
  • Private 15sqm yard and a covered outdoor sitting area
  • Rent explicitly includes arnona, water and vaad bayit — fewer bill surprises
  • Ample free street parking per the ad, if they end up funding a car
–
  • All-in over the ₪4,500 cap: ₪4,200 + electricity + gas (both excluded). Even a modest ₪350-450/mo for a couple running AC through Sept-Oct puts it at ₪4,550-4,650; the ₪650 fallback estimate would make it ₪4,850
  • The 'האזור שקט מאוד' claim is doubtful — Tel Aviv's own Lehi/Bar Lev policy document describes the axis as heavy commuter traffic with discontinuous sidewalks and streets that are hard to cross on foot
  • Poor rail access for this cluster: ~25 min walk to HaHagana train station, ~14 min to the nearest Red Line stop
  • Bathroom is dated and tired (photo 06/07) — plywood-clad, old fittings
  • The yard is scruffy and neglected rather than an amenity (photo 08/09) — bare dirt, junk, patchy artificial turf
  • Ground floor on a busy-road neighborhood — security/noise question worth asking
  • Agency listing (נדל"ן השדרה) — expect a broker fee on a 2-month deal
  • No obvious second workspace; one dining table for two laptop workers
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (Yad2 regular rental, short_term unknown)
  • Get the actual monthly electricity + gas figure — this is the whole ballgame. Under ~₪300/mo it is borderline in-cap; anything more and it's out
  • Ask whether the broker fee applies to a short let, and whether the ad's 'quiet' refers to a set-back side street rather than Bar Lev itself
  • Ask for the exact address/pin — 'near Bar Lev and Lehi' is vague and the noise verdict depends entirely on it
45

Sounds promising and prices well (all-in ~₪3,650, well under cap): a renovated, well-planned 2-room unit with a large yard in Ir Ganim, southwest Jerusalem, and the ad explicitly says the price is flexible. But there are no photos at all, so the kitchen — the non-negotiable — is completely unverified, and 'ריהוט חלקי' (partial furniture) does not guarantee a bed. In scope: Ir Ganim is well west of the Green Line.
+
  • All-in ~₪3,650 with the fallback estimate — around ₪850/mo of headroom
  • Ad says 'גמיש במחיר' (flexible on price) — real negotiating room
  • Described as renovated, attractive and well-planned, with a large yard
  • Two rooms → a possible separate work space
  • In scope (west Jerusalem, well inside the Green Line)
  • Jerusalem Yitzhak Navon offers a ~30 min fast train to Tel Aviv
–
  • NO PHOTOS — the kitchen, the light, the condition and the furniture are all unverifiable. Everything above is the agent's own copy
  • 'ריהוט חלקי' (partial furniture) — with no bed guaranteed, and they own no furniture
  • Ir Ganim/Kiryat Menachem is ~2–3 km from the Mt Herzl light rail terminus — buses only from the door, then light rail, then the train for a TLV trip
  • Furthest from family in Gedera along with Mevaseret
  • Minimal ad detail overall — a bare-bones broker listing
?
  • REQUEST PHOTOS before anything else — especially the kitchen (oven? hob? counter?) and the natural light
  • Ask exactly what 'partial furniture' includes — is there a bed, a sofa, a table?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability, and test how flexible the price really is
  • Check the bus route from HaNurit to Mt Herzl light rail

Migdal HaEmek, Derech Hativat Golani — beautifully renovated with a brand-new oven and hob, but completely empty and on a main road MAYBE
Migdal HaEmek · רמת בלפור · ₪2,700/mo rent · 3 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,350/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,700 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
car recommendedconfirm short-term
The freshest renovation in the cluster: 62 sqm, 3 rooms, new light-oak laminate throughout, a new white kitchen with a brand-new gas hob and built-in oven (the Electra boxes are still standing next to them), a new bathroom and big bright windows with a view. If it came furnished it would be near the top of this list. It doesn't — every room is bare, and this couple own nothing but a desk. It also sits on Derech Hativat Golani, a main road through the city, which cuts against the quiet criterion.
+
  • Beautiful, genuinely fresh renovation: new laminate floors, new kitchen, new bathroom, bright and airy
  • Brand-new gas hob + built-in oven, with a clean single-run counter of decent length
  • Big windows with a view; AC fitted in the living room
  • 3 rooms / 62 sqm on floor 2 — a closable second workspace is possible
  • ₪2700 + est. ₪650 ≈ ₪3350 all-in, well under cap
  • ~15–20 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea
–
  • COMPLETELY UNFURNISHED — no bed, no sofa, no table; the ad mentions no furniture at all, and they own none. For a 2-month stay this is close to disqualifying
  • 'דרך חטיבת גולני' is a main road through Migdal HaEmek — the quiet criterion is at risk; verify which side the windows face
  • 62 sqm is compact for 3 rooms
  • No stated utilities
  • Migdal HaEmek has no walkable train station; TLV ~1h45 by bus
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability (short_term unknown)
  • ASK WHETHER THE LANDLORD WOULD FURNISH IT — if he'd put in a bed, sofa and table this jumps to a pursue; if not, skip
  • Check whether the windows face the Golani road and how loud it is at the flat
  • Confirm the boxed appliances (hob/oven) are actually installed and included
  • Get arnona + bills figures
45
Karmiel Yehiam: cheapest 2-room at ₪2,400 + ₪275 arnona — but zero photos, so the kitchen is unknowable MAYBE
Karmiel · ארבל - יחיעם · ₪2,400/mo rent · 2 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,325/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,400 + arnona ₪275 + est. utilities ₪650 · Yad2 field: 'ארנונה לחודשיים: 550' = ₪275/mo. No electricity/water figures given.
car recommendedborder areaconfirm short-term
no photos
A 2-room flat on Yehiam near the mall, ₪2,400 with a stated ₪275/mo arnona, described as having 'most of the furniture'. It cannot be judged: the listing has no photos at all, and the kitchen — the couple's #1 non-negotiable — is unverifiable. The nearby listing 55c754ab3f031246 (same street, flagged 0m away) does have photos showing an occupied 2-room with a long white counter, countertop oven and a two-desk study room, but there is no photographic overlap to confirm they are the same unit, so both are being kept.
+
  • Cheapest rent in Karmiel at ₪2,400; all-in ≈₪3,325 with the stated arnona — leaves ~₪1,200/mo for a car
  • Close to the mall, public transport and health clinics; ample parking per the ad
  • 'יש את רב הריהוט בדירה' — most furniture included
  • Karmiel train to TLV (~2h); Hazorea ~50 min drive
  • If it is the same unit as 55c754ab, that one's photos show a long counter and a two-desk study room — a promising profile
–
  • NO PHOTOS — the kitchen, condition, light and furnishing are all unknown; this is the single biggest gap in the cluster
  • 'Most of the furniture' explicitly means something is missing — likely the bed or sofa
  • Agency listing (Levin Nadlan) with pure boilerplate; nothing concrete about the flat itself
  • Bills not stated beyond arnona
  • Possible duplicate of 55c754ab3f031246 on the same street — unresolvable without photos
?
  • Request photos or a video walkthrough before anything else — specifically the kitchen counter, hob and oven
  • Ask explicitly which furniture is missing ('רב הריהוט')
  • Ask the agent whether this is the same unit as the other Yehiam listing (55c754ab3f031246) or a different flat in the same building
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
  • Ask for the electricity/water estimate; arnona is stated at ₪550/2 months

Netanya (Amat Poleg) semi-basement unit — weak kitchenette, top of budget SKIP
Or Akiva · אמת פולג · ₪4,500/mo rent · 2.0 rooms · 50.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪5,150/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
car recommendedconfirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is a compact kitchenette that undercuts this couple's #1 priority: a short black counter mostly taken up by the sink, a small countertop toaster-oven, white cabinets and a washing machine under-counter — but no visible stovetop/hob and no built-in oven, and little real prep surface. It presents as a renovated semi-basement/garden-level dwelling unit (drop ceiling, fluorescent tube, small window wells) so natural light is limited, though there is a small outdoor patio. It is otherwise tidy and fully furnished with a real bedroom and a long wooden bar that would work well for two laptops, but at 4,500 it is the top of budget for a modest kitchen.
+
  • Fully furnished and move-in ready; renovated laminate floors, AC, private (uncovered) parking, immediate entry
  • Long wooden bar doubles as a good two-laptop workspace; real separate bedroom
  • Small private patio with greenery; washing machine and full-size fridge included
  • Netanya, coastal; train to Tel Aviv (~30 min)
–
  • Kitchenette fails the core need: sink + countertop toaster-oven only, no visible stovetop or oven, minimal prep counter — poor for cooking most meals
  • Reads as a semi-basement/ground dwelling unit — drop ceiling, fluorescent lighting, small window wells = limited daylight
  • 4,500/mo — top of budget for a small (50 m²) modest unit
  • Amat Poleg is a car-oriented residential pocket in south Netanya — grocery/errands likely need a bus or a walk; far from family in Gedera and Hazorea
?
  • CONFIRM ~2-month short-term availability — listed as annual (12 payments), screened as annual-only
  • If cooking matters, ask whether a cooktop/hob (even a portable induction) and oven can be added — as shown it is a kitchenette, not a cook's kitchen
  • Verify how much is below grade and how much natural light the unit actually gets; check bathroom (some mold/wear visible)
  • Photos 09–11 (grey-tile kitchens/room) are the 'similar ads' carousel, not this unit — real unit is photos 00–08
44

That said, it is a genuinely modern, freshly renovated serviced unit with flexible short-term terms (all-inclusive: utilities, internet, Netflix) and a great Givatayim location minutes from Tel Aviv. A 30 m² 1.5-room with a comfortable bed and a clean modern bathroom; at 4,300 all-in it is pricey given the cooking limitation.
+
  • Genuine flexible short-term / serviced unit (all-inclusive incl. internet)
  • Modern, freshly renovated, clean; comfortable furnishing
  • Excellent Givatayim location bordering Tel Aviv
  • Full-size fridge and microwave present
–
  • Token kitchenette: no stovetop, no oven — fails the #1 cooking priority
  • 4,300 all-in is expensive for 30 m²
  • Small 1.5-room; no dedicated desk pictured for two laptops
?

The genuinely-this-unit photos show only empty, clean but uninformative rooms (a hallway and a room with an AC), while several other photos are screenshots lifted from unrelated ads.   • Top (4th) floor — elevator status unclear; only 40m²

Quiet Tzahala 2-rm sublet with real oven+stove and a work nook — but pre-TAMA with only 30 days' eviction notice, and no bed SKIP
Tel Aviv · גני צהלה, רמות צהלה · ₪3,900/mo rent · 2 rooms · 48 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪4,550/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
no photos
48sqm 2-room + hall ('הול לאזור עבודה אישי') at Ir Shemesh 43, Ramot Tzahala/Neve Sharett — leafy, genuinely quiet garden surroundings, masses of parking, 'very low arnona'. Kitchen is old but real: freestanding oven+4-burner stove, fridge, microwave — small counter though. The dealbreaker risk is explicit in the ad: the building goes to TAMA 'very soon' and the tenant gets ONE MONTH'S notice before eviction — a mid-stay forced move during a planned Sept-Nov stay is a live possibility. Bedroom has a closet but NO bed, and the couple owns no bed. All-in ≈ ₪4,400-4,550 (3,900 + low arnona + ~₪450 bills) — at the cap.
+
  • Real oven + stovetop (photo-verified) — cookable, if tight on counter
  • Dedicated hall/work-nook advertised for a personal work area + separate living room = two workspaces feasible
  • Very quiet green pocket; garden view; abundant parking (+ lot 50m away)
  • 'Very low arnona' per ad; pets allowed; balcony
  • Owner explicitly renting short/flexibly because of the TAMA timeline — no landlord-agreement friction
–
  • Eviction with 30 days' notice once TAMA starts ('בקרוב מאוד') — real risk of a forced move mid-stay; planning poison for a fixed Sept-Nov window
  • No bed in the bedroom — they'd have to buy bed + mattress for 2 months
  • Dated and tired: worn sofa, old bathroom, small kitchen counter; not the bright-inviting brief
  • Weak transit corner of TLV: no train/light-rail near, bus-dependent (~30+ min to center)
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,400-4,550 — little to no headroom under cap once furniture purchase is added
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,550/mo all-in exceeds the ₪4,500 cap (auto-downgraded to skip)
?
  • The decisive question: what is the actual TAMA permit/start status? Can the owner commit in writing that Sept-Nov won't be interrupted (or offer a discount/exit clause)?
  • Confirm exact all-in: arnona amount, va'ad, typical electricity
  • Will the owner provide a bed/mattress for the stay?
  • Check bus lines from Devora HaNevia/Ramat HaHayal to central TLV for Roy's weekday pattern
  • Washing machine present? (not listed)

  • No hob, no oven and no fridge visible in the kitchen
  • Bare white boxes — uninviting for two people spending their days inside

Karmiel HaRimon: cheapest in the cluster at ₪2,200 for 3 central rooms — but only partial furniture, no double bed, and a tired kitchen MAYBE
Karmiel · טנה · ₪2,200/mo rent · 3 rooms · 80 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,170/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,200 + arnona ₪320 + est. utilities ₪650 · Yad2 field: 'ארנונה לחודשיים: 640' = ₪320/mo. No electricity/water figures given.
car recommendedborder areaconfirm short-term
The cheapest listing here: 3 rooms in central Karmiel at ₪2,200 + a stated ₪320/mo arnona, in a corner building with open views from the bedrooms, near parks, shops and the culture hall. The photos show a largely empty flat. The kitchen has an old freestanding gas range with an oven, but it's grimy and the counter run beside the sink is short and dated. The ad is honest that furnishing is partial: 'מטבח עם תנור וארון מזווה, סלון עם שידה ושולחן, חדר שינה גדול עם ארון, חדר נוסף עם ארון ומיטה נפתחת' — a cupboard, a chest, a table and a fold-out bed. For a couple who own only a desk, that means no bed and no sofa.
+
  • ₪2,200 is the cheapest rent of the 16; all-in ≈₪3,170 with stated arnona — the biggest leftover budget for a car
  • 3 rooms and central: parks, shopping, culture hall and buses on the doorstep; open views from the bedrooms
  • Has a gas range with an oven, so cooking is at least possible
  • Building has a shelter; immediate entry; Karmiel train to TLV (~2h); Hazorea ~50 min
  • Not a duplicate of f01cc7a11b50e351 — that flat's photos (brown leather sofa, yellow walls, orange wardrobes) are clearly a different unit
–
  • Only partially furnished, and the missing pieces are the expensive ones: no double bed (a fold-out only), no sofa
  • Kitchen is tired and grubby, with a short dated counter — no real prep space for cooking every meal
  • The flat reads bare and institutional in the photos; terrazzo floors, empty rooms — not an inviting place to spend the day
  • Central location means street noise is a live risk; unverified
  • Bills not stated beyond arnona (₪640/2 months, the highest stated arnona in the cluster)
  • Agency listing (SK Nadlan)
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
  • Ask whether the landlord will add a double bed and a sofa for a short let — if yes, the price makes this interesting; if no, it fails on 'furnished'
  • Ask whether the kitchen range stays and whether the counter can be extended (a portable prep table would help)
  • Ask about AC — the photos show none in several rooms; unlisted ≠ absent, but confirm
  • Check street noise on HaRimon given the central/corner position

Dirt-cheap Hadar studio — has a portable cooktop + toaster oven but shabby, dark and tiny SKIP
Haifa · הדר · ₪1,500/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 25.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪2,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪1,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is a small stone counter with base cabinets, a portable 2-burner gas stove and a little toaster oven plus fridge (photos 00, 03) — so more cooking capacity than a token kitchenette, but the prep space is very limited and the whole kitchen looks tired and dim. Condition overall is shabby (thin mattress on a metal frame, grimy window frame, cramped shower). It's 25 m², one room, at just ₪1,500. The listing's photo montage mixes in much nicer apartments that are not this unit — the real studio is the modest one.
+
  • Extremely cheap at ₪1,500 (12 payments, no listed arnona)
  • Has a portable 2-burner cooktop plus a small toaster oven — basic cooking is possible
  • Central Hadar, Metronit-served, immediate entry
–
  • Shabby and dark, with minimal counter/prep space
  • 25 m² studio — cramped for two people living, cooking and working
  • Furniture looks basic/worn (thin mattress on a frame); small dim shower
  • Heavily mixed photo montage makes the real unit hard to pin down
?
  • Confirm the landlord will do a ~2-month short-term let (listing is annual, immediate entry)
  • Insist on photos/video of THIS unit only, given the misleading montage
  • Check quiet, light and cleanliness in person; ask what furniture actually stays
  • Verify the cooktop/toaster-oven are included

Cheap but weakest kitchen — no oven, only partial furniture; skip for a cooking couple SKIP
Nahariya · ₪2,500/mo rent · 3.0 rooms · 70.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪3,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
border areaconfirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is the weak point, and it is this couple's #1 priority: a small galley with white/wood cabinets, a black stone counter, sink and fridge, but no oven (gas cooktop only) and little prep space — thin for a couple who cooks most meals and bakes gluten-free. The unit is also only PARTIALLY furnished (AC, fridge, cooktop, closets — no sofa/bed/table shown), so it is not turn-key for a 2-month furnished stay. It is a cheap (2,500) older 3-room / 70 m2 on the top floor with terrazzo floors and only public parking, in the same Nahariya border-risk area.
+
  • Cheap at 2,500/mo, under budget
  • Bright rooms with greenery, 3-room / 70 m2
  • Elevator in the building; central, walkable town
–
  • No oven and a small galley kitchen with little counter — fails the couple's top priority of a good kitchen
  • Only partial furniture (no sofa/bed/table listed) — not turn-key for a short furnished stay
  • Top floor (4 of 4), older terrazzo finish
  • Only public parking (shared lot), no private spot
  • Ad padded with the same shared 'promo gallery' photos as the other two Nahariya ads, so several images are not this unit
?
  • Confirm whether a ~2-month SHORT-TERM let is possible (annual listing, 12 payments/yr) AND exactly what furniture/appliances — especially an oven — would be provided
  • Border/siren caveat: Nahariya is near the Lebanon border with rocket sirens and impacts in 2026 — verify current security for Sept-Nov and comfort with siren risk before pursuing
42
Immaculate 16m2 center studio, but only a minimal kitchenette and likely annual lease MAYBE
Jerusalem · מרכז העיר · ₪3,750/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 16.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,400/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,750 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-termvery small
no photos
Cooking most meals for two here would be a real stretch: the kitchen is a single short run of counter with a sink, a mini-fridge tucked underneath, an electric cooktop and one small stretch of prep space - no oven and no second surface. It's a clean, functional kitchenette rather than a cook's kitchen, and for a vegan/GF couple doing most meals it would feel cramped fast. Everything else is genuinely lovely: fully renovated, bright and spotless, with a brand-new bathroom and AC, and it sits as a quiet rear ground-floor unit bordering Independence Park just off King George in the city center - central yet reportedly quiet. But at 16m2 it's the smallest option, furnishing is only partial, there's no real desk for two laptops, and the terms point to a 12-month lease rather than a 2-month stay.
+
  • Fully renovated, bright, spotless; new bathroom and new AC
  • Quiet rear/garden position bordering Independence Park despite central location
  • Very central - walkable to shuk, light rail and Navon train station (~35 min to Tel Aviv)
  • Cheapest option at ₪3,750
–
  • Minimal kitchenette: short counter, cooktop + mini-fridge, no oven, little prep space - poor for a couple who cook most meals
  • Smallest unit at 16m2 - very tight for two who cook and both work from home
  • Screening flags annual-only; description shows 12 payments/year (long-lease signal)
  • Only partially furnished; no proper desk/workspace for two laptops
?
  • Confirm ~2-month short-term availability - this looks like a 12-month annual lease, which likely disqualifies it
  • Confirm exactly what furniture is included (listed as partial)
  • Ask about real usable counter space and whether any oven can be added
  • Verify the 'quiet rear' claim against King George street noise
42

  • Gas cooktop only — oven not mentioned (matters for GF baking?)

  • Quiet + bright + attached garden explicitly described; rear-facing
  • Strong kitchen signal: oven with gas hob, hood, dishwasher, fridge
  • No broker fee; Aug 1 availability aligns with a Sept start
  • Carmel Center is transit-rich; Hazorea ~35 min

  • TA is ~1h15 door-to-door

Beautiful Hadar Elyon vacation flat with sea view — but rent alone eats the whole all-in budget and the kitchen may be SHARED SKIP
Haifa · הדר עליון · ₪4,400/mo rent · 2 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪5,050/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,400 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
Styled, genuinely inviting 45sqm vacation apartment on Golomb Blvd (Hadar Elyon): bright living room with bay view, separate bedroom, designer bathroom, smart TV, linen/cleaning service, rentable by day/week/month — short-term native, Carmelit Golomb station ~2 min away. Two killers: (1) none of the 7 photos shows the kitchen, and the ad text 'מטבח משוטף בין עוד יחידה פשוטה יותר' reads as a kitchen shared with another unit, equipped only with coffee machine/kettle/hob/microwave — no oven; (2) ₪4,400/mo is the entire ₪4,500 all-in ceiling before bills (if bills aren't included it busts the budget outright).
+
  • Short-term native (day/week/month) — zero availability risk
  • Bright, designed, sea/bay view — the best 'nice place to BE' interior in the cluster
  • Separate bedroom → one closable workspace possible
  • Carmelit Golomb at the door → 3 stops to downtown/train; Talpiot market ~10 min walk
  • Cleaning + linen service included
–
  • ₪4,400 rent ≈ the whole all-in budget; bills inclusion not stated — likely over ₪4.5k total
  • Kitchen not photographed and wording suggests it is SHARED with a second unit — likely fails the #1 non-negotiable
  • No oven listed (only hob/microwave/coffee gear)
  • 45sqm; vacation-flat pricing rarely negotiates down to their comfort zone
  • Ad text is garbled agency boilerplate — expect upsell friction
?
  • Only if smitten: is the kitchen PRIVATE to this unit? Demand kitchen photos (counters, hob, oven)
  • Exact monthly all-in price for a 2-month Sept-Nov stay incl. electricity/water/arnona — get it in writing
  • If they offer the 'simpler unit' instead — reassess from scratch
42

Big Katzrin house sublet with a superb cook's kitchen — but ₪4,820 before utilities blows the budget, and it's the Golan SKIP
קצרין · גמלא · ₪4,000/mo rent · 4 rooms · 232 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪5,470/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona ₪820 + est. utilities ₪650
no photos
A genuine sublet (short-term explicitly fine) of a large, well-kept private house 2 min walk from Katzrin's commercial center: the kitchen is the best in the cluster — gas cooktop, hood, built-in wall oven, big fridge, long L-counters — plus renovated per ad, furnished rooms, yard, and an in-house shelter. But the math kills it for this couple: ₪4,000 rent + ₪820 stated arnona = ₪4,820 before electricity/water for a 232 sqm house — realistically ₪5,500+, ~₪1,000+ over their all-in ceiling, for space they don't need. Add Katzrin's near-once-daily 3h15m bus to Tel Aviv and the Golan security picture (~18-19 km from the ceasefire line; calm mid-2026 but unresolved, and Katzrin took direct rocket hits in 2024), and there's no advantage left that price was supposed to provide.
+
  • Sublet — short-term is explicitly the offer; available from Jul 22
  • Outstanding real kitchen: gas burners + hood + built-in oven + large fridge + long counters — exactly what they cook-everything couple wants
  • Whole well-kept furnished house with big yard, front porch, two bathrooms, washing machine, in-house shelter (miklat)
  • 2 min walk to Katzrin commercial center (supermarket) and country club — best grocery walkability in the cluster
  • Quiet residential street in a small town; Golan elevation cooler than Tiberias in Sept
–
  • Over budget: ₪4,000 + ₪820 stated arnona = ₪4,820/mo before electricity/water/internet on a 232 sqm house — plausibly ₪5,500+, well past the ₪4,500 all-in ceiling
  • Golan Heights, ~18-19 km from the Israel-Syria ceasefire line: mid-2026 calm with security talks ongoing but unresolved (IDF in buffer zone); Katzrin homes took direct rocket hits in 2024 — clashes with the 'not near borders' preference (capped at maybe at best)
  • Tel Aviv transport is dire: direct 843 bus runs about once a day (~04:50, ~3h15m); otherwise bus to Tiberias then 836 — 4h door-to-door; car effectively required
  • Far more house than a couple needs — they'd be paying the over-budget premium for unused rooms
  • Dated 90s interior (tidy but not the bright/inviting brief); no living-room or yard photos shown despite ad claims
?
  • Only if they fall in love with Katzrin: ask whether utilities can be capped/included and get real winter-free Sept-Nov electricity numbers
  • Confirm exact available window covers Sept-Nov (listed from Jul 22)
  • Ask to see living room and yard photos (not in the ad)
42

Shimshit garden unit — lovely and quiet but over budget all-in, car required, modest kitchen SKIP
Shimshit · ₪4,000/mo rent · 2 rooms · 165 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪4,983/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona ₪333 + est. utilities ₪650 · ארנונה לחודש - 333 ש"ח. צריכת חשמל ומים בנפרד (electricity and water paid separately)
car recommendedconfirm short-term
Furnished 2-room garden unit in the upscale community yishuv Shimshit, with a genuinely beautiful private ~100sqm garden (fruit trees, patio) and total quiet. But the kitchen is a single modest run (no oven visible, hob unconfirmed), and the math breaks the budget: 4,000 rent + 333 arnona + separate electricity/water lands around 4,650-4,800/mo. No real bus service — a car is effectively mandatory. ~22-25 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea.
+
  • Beautiful private garden with fruit trees and patio — rare quality-of-life asset for two people home all day
  • Very quiet rural-suburban setting; pets allowed; parking included
  • Furnished (sofa, TV, double bed, dining table) and AC present
  • Agency states short-term 'likely'; listed since 2025 so landlord may be flexible
  • ~20-25 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea (family)
–
  • All-in ~4,650-4,800/mo — exceeds the 4,500 cap
  • Kitchen is a modest single counter run: no oven visible, stovetop unconfirmed — weak for a cook-most-meals couple
  • No practical public transport (sparse regional buses 310/342 at yishuv entrance); car needed, adding cost on top of an already over-budget rent
  • No second room for a closed office; workspace = dining table only
  • Agency ad — possible brokerage fee on top
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability explicitly
  • Ask whether rent is negotiable down (long-listed unit) — only way it fits budget
  • Verify stovetop/oven existence and ask for current kitchen photos
  • Ask about brokerage fee for a 2-month stay
  • Confirm what electricity/water actually run in summer (garden irrigation? AC)

'Here We Art', Hadar Airbnb — beautiful vintage charm, private garden and rooftop, but a genuine token kitchenette at ₪4,350 SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,350/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,350/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪4,350 + arnona n/a · Airbnb monthly booking — utilities included in the rate by platform convention · missing arnona not stated
The most charming interior in the batch: a vintage Hadar flat with original Templer-era patterned floor tiles, a Buddha canvas, a private planted garden with a table, and access to a big shared rooftop with lawn, firepit and panoramic views. Steps from supermarkets, cafés, the Metronit and the Carmelit. But the kitchen is exactly what the brief calls a FAIL — a countertop oven perched on a chest of drawers, a sink unit with maybe 50cm of usable counter, no cooktop — and at ₪4,350/mo it's the most expensive thing here.
+
  • Beautiful and full of character — original patterned floor tiles, thoughtful vintage styling; genuinely a nice place to be
  • Private planted garden with a table, plus a large shared rooftop with lawn, firepit and panoramic city views
  • Prime Hadar location: steps from supermarkets, cafés and cultural venues; easy Metronit and Carmelit access
  • Airbnb monthly booking — utilities included, no lease, no guarantor, 2-month stays are the native use case
  • Real double bed with proper linen, AC, TV, wifi
–
  • The kitchen is a token kitchenette: ~50cm of counter, a countertop oven on a chest of drawers, no visible cooktop — this is the exact FAIL case in the brief, and the kitchen is the #1 non-negotiable
  • ₪4,350/mo — the most expensive listing in the cluster and right at the ₪4,500 cap, with no budget left over for a car or co-working
  • No indoor table or desk — only a small sofabed and a coffee table; the garden table is the only work surface, and that's outdoors
  • Small: 2 rooms, and the sofa is a compact sofabed
  • Bathroom is a narrow shower-curtain cubicle
?
  • Ask the host whether a proper cooktop and additional counter/table can be added — the rest of this flat is excellent
  • Ask for the actual Sept–Nov 2026 monthly quote including fees, and whether a long-stay discount applies
  • Ask whether an indoor work table for two can be provided
42
3 rooms at 3,500 with a genuine oven+hob kitchen — but shabby, cluttered and the photos are from 2023 MAYBE
Bat Yam · לב העיר · ₪3,500/mo rent · 3 rooms · 77 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
The only listing here with an unambiguously real kitchen: a freestanding oven with a gas hob, a proper counter run and a full-size fridge. It is also 3 rooms for 3,500, which on paper is the space this couple wants. Everything else is against it — the photos (uploaded April 2023, so three years stale) show a tired, cluttered, occupied flat with dark old furniture, worn terrazzo, sagging slipcovered sofas and dim light, and the ad says only 'partially furnished'. All-in ~4,150.
+
  • Real cookable kitchen: freestanding oven + hob, counter space, full fridge — the only certain one in this batch
  • 3 rooms for 3,500 — room for two separate workspaces
  • All-in ~4,150 leaves some headroom
  • Lev Ha'ir location, central and walkable
–
  • Shabby and uninviting — dated brown kitchen, cluttered rooms, dim light, worn floors. Fails the 'nice place to be' test
  • Only partially furnished ("רהוט חלקי") — they own no bed or furniture, so gaps are a real problem
  • Photos are from April 2023; the listing may be stale or the flat may have changed hands
  • Broker listing (י.ג קפיטל) — fee likely
?
  • Ask for 2026 photos and exactly what furniture stays — the whole case rests on that
  • Confirm the listing is still live at this price
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (short_term unknown)
42
Barak 2, Tel Hanan, Nesher — long counter with a 4-burner gas cooktop, but the flat is empty: no bed, no sofa SKIP
Nesher · תל חנן · ₪3,000/mo rent · 3 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,650/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A 3-room in Tel Hanan, Nesher with one real asset: a long (3m+) kitchen counter with a 4-burner gas cooktop, sink, fridge and washing machine — generous prep space, exactly the 'dream' geometry, though the gap under the counter shows there's no oven. The problem is everything else: the ad says only 'partially furnished — fridge, table, chairs', and the photos confirm it. Every room is bare, lit by a fluorescent strip, with no bed and no sofa.
+
  • Long 3m+ kitchen counter with a 4-burner gas cooktop — real prep space, the best counter geometry in the cluster
  • 3 rooms, ₪3,000 + est. ₪650 ≈ ₪3,650 all-in — decent headroom
  • Ad claims bright, renovated and well kept; AC installed
  • Nesher is quiet and convenient for Kibbutz Hazorea; supermarket, restaurants and services close per the ad
–
  • Only PARTIALLY furnished ('רהוט חלקי, מקרר, שולחן כסאות') — no bed, no sofa, no wardrobe; the couple owns no furniture, so this is a non-starter for a 2-month stay
  • Photos show every room completely empty — bare walls, bare floors
  • No oven (the gap under the counter is empty)
  • Institutional feel: fluorescent strip lighting, no warmth at all
  • Nesher means a bus ride to everything in Haifa, and to Hof HaCarmel/Lev HaMifratz for the Tel Aviv train
?
  • Ask whether the landlord would furnish it (bed + sofa at minimum) for a 2-month let — if yes, the kitchen alone would make this worth revisiting
  • Ask whether an oven can be fitted in the counter gap
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — short_term unknown
  • Ask for arnona + bills figures
42
David Elazar 10, Hof HaCarmel — walkable to the Tel Aviv train, but the only 'photo' is a stock aerial shot of the beach MAYBE
Haifa · חוף הכרמל · ₪3,400/mo rent · 1 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,050/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,400 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A studio near Hof HaCarmel described as fully furnished and move-in-ready ('להגיע עם מזוודות ולגור'). Location is the one real asset: David Elazar 10 is within walking distance of Hof HaCarmel train station and mall — the best Tel Aviv connection of anything in this cluster — and near the sea. But the single 'photo' attached is a generic aerial stock image of the Bat Galim/Dan Panorama seafront, not the apartment. There is nothing to judge.
+
  • Hof HaCarmel train station and mall within walking distance — the strongest Tel Aviv connection in the cluster, ideal for Roy's weekly trips
  • Near the sea and the beach promenade
  • Advertised as completely furnished and ready to move into with just suitcases ('מגיע עם ריהוט קומפלט')
  • ₪3,400 + est. ₪650 ≈ ₪4,050 all-in — under the cap
–
  • Zero real photos — the only image is a stock aerial of the seafront; the kitchen, condition and light are entirely unknown
  • ₪3,400 for a 1-room studio is expensive — nearly double the ₪1,900-2,300 studios elsewhere in this batch, with nothing shown to justify it
  • 1 room for two people working all day, with no separation
  • Two-line ad, duplicated text, no detail on furniture, bills or arnona — a low-effort listing
  • Utilities not stated; the all-in figure rests entirely on the ₪650 estimate, leaving only ₪450 of headroom
?
  • Request real interior photos — nothing else can be judged until then
  • Ask specifically about the kitchen: counter length, cooktop, oven
  • Ask why ₪3,400 for a studio, and whether it's negotiable
  • Confirm the exact walking time to Hof HaCarmel train station
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability and ask for arnona + bills figures
42
₪1,599 including arnona and water — cheapest here, but a bare small unit with a stub of a counter SKIP
Kiryat Yam · קרית ים ב' · ₪1,599/mo rent · 1.5 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪2,249/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪1,599 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A renovated 1.5-room unit at Ben Zvi 29 in Kiryat Yam ב' at ₪1,599 including arnona and water, near the Nitzan centre and public transport. The kitchen is a short gray-wood run with a sink, a ~1.2m counter, a washing machine and a fridge — no hob and no oven visible, though the ad claims a cooktop. The rooms photograph as bare white boxes with a lone chest of drawers and a mattress edge. Cheap, clean, and not somewhere to spend two months working.
+
  • ₪1,599 including arnona and water — the lowest headline cost in the cluster
  • Renovated and clean, with new-ish bathroom and fittings
  • Ad lists fridge, washing machine, cooktop, bed and wardrobe
  • Close to the Nitzan centre and public transport
–
  • Counter is a stub (~1.2m) with no hob or oven visible — not a kitchen for two people cooking daily
  • 1.5 rooms and bare white rooms — nowhere to work, nowhere pleasant to be
  • No train in Kiryat Yam
  • Agency listing with minimal detail; furnishing looks thinner than claimed
?
  • Confirm ~2-month availability Sept-Nov 2026
  • If the claimed cooktop and bed are real and the price holds, it could serve as an emergency fallback only
42
Brand-new, never-lived-in Kiryat Ata unit at ₪3,000 incl. arnona and water — but the kitchen is a token kitchenette SKIP
Kiryat Ata · ₪3,000/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,650/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪3,000 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A 40sqm 2-room unit on Katznelson, brand new and still in its wrappers: new bed, sofa, TV, marble-look floors, a small balcony with a green view. Priced at ₪3,000 including water and arnona with electricity by meter. The dealbreaker is the kitchen: a single short dark run with a sink tucked into the entry hallway, no hob, no oven and barely any counter. For a couple cooking most meals from scratch, that fails the #1 test.
+
  • Brand new and never lived in — everything clean and fresh
  • ₪3,000 includes water and arnona; electricity by meter
  • New furniture in place: double bed, sofa, TV
  • Balcony with a green outlook; central Kiryat Ata location
  • Immediate entry, agency claims no fee
–
  • Token kitchenette: one short run with a sink in the hallway, no hob, no oven, almost no counter — fails the kitchen requirement
  • Only 40sqm — small for two people working from home
  • The second 'room' is a small living space, not a closable office
  • Kiryat Ata has no train station
  • Real all-in is ₪3,000 + electricity, so the ₪650 fallback estimate overstates it — but the kitchen decides this one regardless
?
  • Only worth revisiting if the landlord would install a hob and a work surface
  • Confirm ~2-month availability Sept-Nov 2026
42
Talpiot loft studio, ₪3,100 + ₪400 stated bills — cheap and central, but a bleak room with a sink-only kitchen MAYBE
Jerusalem · תלפיות · ₪3,100/mo rent · 1.5 rooms · 25 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,650/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,100 + arnona ₪150 + utilities ₪400 (stated in ad) · Ad: 'למחיר יש להוסיף 400 ש"ח עבור חשבונות: מים, חשמל, ארנונה, ועד בית, אינטרנט מהיר, טלויזיה בכבלים' — a single stated ₪400/mo covering ALL utilities including arnona, vaad and internet. All-in = ₪3,500.
confirm short-term
The cost picture is excellent and unusually transparent: ₪3,100 plus a stated ₪400/mo covering water, electricity, arnona, vaad, fast internet and cable — an all-in of ₪3,500 in central Jerusalem. But the photos are grim: a bare room with a ladder-stair loft used as the bedroom, a mini-fridge, tired lighting, and a kitchen that is a counter with a sink and cabinets and nothing else — no hob, no oven. Central Talpiot means lots of transit, but this is not a nice place to spend two months.
+
  • All-in ₪3,500, with bills stated precisely in the ad and even including fast internet and cable — ~₪1,000/mo of headroom
  • Central Talpiot opposite Hadar Mall: 'שפע של תחבורה ציבורית', walkable shops and groceries
  • The kitchen counter run is genuinely ~2m — the prep space exists even though the appliances do not
  • Bed, wardrobe and fridge included; balcony and a washing machine
  • Renovated per the ad, and described as quiet
  • Jerusalem Navon gives a fast (~30 min) train to Tel Aviv
–
  • Kitchen has NO hob and NO oven — only a sink and cabinets. Both would have to be bought
  • Bleak and dim: bare walls, a mini-fridge, dated fittings, no sofa and no dining table in any photo
  • The bedroom is a low-ceilinged gallery reached by an open wooden ladder-stair — awkward and not restful for a two-month stay
  • Only a chest of drawers to work at — no desk, no table, and the loft can't host one
  • Talpiot is a busy commercial district; Hadar Mall opposite means traffic, not quiet
  • Talpiot → Navon station still needs light rail or a bus for the TLV run
?
  • Only worth a look if they will accept a bare, functional box for two months in exchange for ₪1,000/mo of savings
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability
  • Ask whether a hob, oven and a table/sofa can be added
  • The stated ₪400 bundle is a rare, verifiable number — worth confirming it is genuinely capped
41

Nesher (Keren Hayesod) 4.5-room 100 m², ₪4,200 — large new kitchen but delivered empty, appliances not installed SKIP
Nesher · ₪4,200/mo rent · 4.5 rooms · 100.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,850/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,200 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is large and freshly renovated — an L-shaped run of light-wood cabinets with a long dark-stone counter and sink, plus central gas and a dishwasher per the ad — so prep space is excellent, BUT the photos show it with no appliances installed (a loose gas cooktop sits on the living-room floor; no oven or fridge fitted) and the whole flat is empty and unfurnished. For a 2-month furnished, move-in-ready stay where cooking is the priority, that is a real problem: you would arrive to a shell with an uninstalled kitchen and no bed or sofa. It is also the most expensive, far larger (4.5 rooms) and higher-fee (arnona ₪600/2mo) than a couple needs. Floor 1, facing a park; several photos (furnished rooms, high-rise views, a cat) are contamination from other ads.
+
  • Very large, freshly renovated kitchen: lots of cabinets + long stone counter + sink; gas + dishwasher per the ad
  • Spacious 100 m², floor 1 facing a park, two bathrooms
  • Pet-friendly with plenty of storage
–
  • Appears unfurnished and post-renovation — appliances (cooktop/oven/fridge) not installed in the photos
  • Fails the 'furnished, move-in-ready' need for a 2-month stay
  • Most expensive (₪4,200) and far bigger than needed; arnona ₪600/2mo
  • Photo set contaminated with other apartments
  • Framed as long-term for 'serious' tenants
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month short-term is even on the table (ad wants long-term)
  • Critically: confirm it comes FULLY furnished with an installed, working oven/cooktop/fridge — otherwise not viable
  • If pursued, negotiate the price down given the size is surplus for two

Same building and same white-renovation spec as agency listing 58b9e788e401b032; layouts and bathrooms differ, so these look like two subdivided units, not one duplicate — worth asking, since the other is ₪2,000 with ALL bills included and 10sqm larger on paper. Photos show a tidy unit: kitchenette counter with washing machine, full-size fridge, dark sofa, bedroom with wardrobe, greenery outside the window. No hob or oven visible. For two home-working cooks, 25sqm at basement level in a weak bus-dependent neighborhood is too small a life for two months.

rent ₪2,400 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
Agency listing with no address (coords put it south of the center near the hotel area): a lived-in family flat with laundry-draped balcony over a parking lot/street by a hotel. The kitchen does have a real freestanding gas cooker (stove + oven), granite counters and a microwave, but it's cramped enough that the fridge sits in the living room, and every photo shows the current tenants' heavy clutter — impossible to know what stays or how it presents empty. At ~₪3,200 all-in it's the same money as the freshly renovated Derech HaGvura flat and ₪350/mo more than Jabotinsky 2, while being the most tired of the three. Elevator and parking are the bright spots. Nothing here wins on price, quiet or kitchen versus its in-town rivals.

  • ~₪3,215/mo all-in (₪2,400 + ~₪215 arnona + bills) — under budget

–
  • Tired, cluttered, occupied flat — dated furniture, kids-room decor, laundry balcony; fails the 'bright, inviting place to BE' bar
  • Kitchen is cramped — the fridge is in the living room; prep is a single short run
  • No address in the ad — street noise unjudgeable (balcony photo suggests hotel/parking frontage; possible evening noise)
  • Same all-in cost as the renovated d20ab29a flat and more than Jabotinsky 2, with worse condition than both
  • Short-term unknown; agency ad; unclear what furniture stays
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (not stated)
  • Get the exact address to judge the street (hotel-strip noise vs quiet side street)
  • Ask what furniture stays after the current family leaves and how the flat presents empty

Rehovot Y.L. Peretz roof unit with a 150 sqm private terrace — lovely, but all-in lands ~₪4,550, over the cap SKIP
Rehovot · ג' / מרכז מזרח העיר · ₪4,200/mo rent · 1.5 rooms · 35 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,850/mo all-inincl. arnona · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,200 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'המחיר כולל ארנונה, מים וועד בית בכפוף לצריכת מים סבירה, חשמל בנפרד' — arnona, water and vaad included; electricity extra, amount not stated.
confirm short-term
The most tempting near-miss in the cluster. A 35 sqm renovated unit with a 150 sqm private paved roof: stone seating, a pergola, plants, a city view and a second outdoor kitchen. Inside there is a warm bedroom with wooden shutters and a wood floor, a long black kitchen counter with a hob, and a wardrobe with a proper desk and office chair. Central Rehovot, lift, parking, separate entrance. It fails only on arithmetic: ₪4,200 includes arnona, water and vaad, but electricity on top realistically lands the all-in around ₪4,550.
+
  • 150 sqm private roof terrace with a pergola, stone seating, plants, a view and an outdoor kitchen — extraordinary outdoor space
  • Long black counter with a hob and real prep space; a second kitchen outside
  • A desk and office chair already in place
  • Warm, characterful interior — wooden shutters, wood floor, wood ceiling
  • Central Rehovot with a lift, parking and a separate entrance
  • Price includes arnona, water and vaad — unusually transparent
  • Rehovot station gives a direct TLV line; ~15 min to Gedera
–
  • OVER THE HARD CAP: ₪4,200 + electricity. With the ₪650 fallback estimate that is ₪4,850; even on a realistic ₪350 summer electricity bill it is ~₪4,550 — still over ₪4,500. This is not a marginal estimate artifact, it genuinely exceeds the budget
  • Only 35 sqm built — one room serving as living room and bedroom
  • No oven listed (hob only)
  • Only one desk, and 1.5 rooms means no separate closable work room
  • A top-floor roof unit will be hot in September, and the roof itself is unusable in midday heat
?
  • The only route in is a price cut: ask whether ₪4,200 is negotiable to ~₪4,050–4,100, which would bring the all-in inside the cap. Given the quality, this is worth one phone call
  • Ask the average electricity bill — if it is genuinely low (~₪250) the sums get closer
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability
  • Ask about heat/insulation on the top floor
40
Clean little Florentin 1.5-room with mamad and a real (if small) galley kitchen — but ₪4,000 pushes all-in over cap SKIP
Tel Aviv · פלורנטין · ₪4,000/mo rent · 1.5 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,650/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A tidy 1.5-room plus mamad on HaHalutzim in Florentin, in a managed building with keypad entry and CCTV. The kitchen is modest but real — a proper galley run with upper and lower cabinets, a genuine stretch of counter, sink and a gas hob (no oven) — which is a meaningful step above the hotplate-on-a-table units elsewhere in this batch. Condition is clean and neutral, with a bedroom that fits a bed and a full wardrobe. The problem is arithmetic: ₪4,000 rent with nothing stated about bills means all-in ~₪4,650, over the hard cap, for 1.5 rooms.
+
  • Kitchen is a real galley: continuous counter, upper + lower cabinets, sink, gas hob — workable prep space, unlike most of this batch
  • Clean, neutral, well-maintained condition throughout — not shabby
  • A mamad is a genuine plus for a Sept-Nov 2026 stay in Tel Aviv, and rare at this size
  • Managed building with keypad and CCTV — sensible for short-term tenants
  • Florentin is prime for this couple's needs: walkable to Levinski market (outstanding for vegan/GF shopping), dense cafés, and the Allenby Red Line stop for Roy's weekly Tel Aviv commitments
  • Ad claims the best and quietest position within the neighbourhood
  • Fitted wardrobe and bed already in place
–
  • All-in over cap: ₪4,000 + ~₪650 est. bills ≈ ₪4,650. NOTE: this overage rests entirely on the ₪650 fallback estimate — the ad states nothing about arnona or bills
  • No oven — the ad lists only fridge, AC and a gas hob. Negotiable in principle given the counter, but it stacks on top of the price problem
  • 1.5 rooms is tight for two people working laptops all day; no second closable room (the mamad is small) and no desk or dining table shown
  • Counter run is short — fine for one cook, cramped for two people cooking most meals
  • Florentin street noise is real; 'quietest in the neighbourhood' is the agent's claim, unverified
  • Agency listing (חי נכסים) — broker fee likely
  • Only 4 photos, none showing the living area as a whole
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (Yad2 regular rental, short_term unknown)
  • Worth re-opening ONLY if the landlord confirms arnona+bills ≤ ₪500/mo, which would bring all-in to ~₪4,500 — ask for the actual figures rather than accepting the estimate
  • Ask for the exact building/house number on HaHalutzim to judge the street
  • Ask whether a countertop oven could be added
40
The best kitchen in the batch — 2.5 rooms on Pinkas 24 — but all-in ~4,650 breaks the cap on the bills estimate SKIP
Bat Yam · לב העיר · ₪4,000/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,650/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A 2.5-room flat in Lev Ha'ir with the strongest kitchen here: a long galley with a full run of counter on both sides, a real hob, upper shelving and a large fridge — the generous prep space this couple actually wants. It also has a decent living room with two sofas and a balcony, a bedroom, and a small third room that could be a closable office. The photos are of an occupied, cluttered flat, so condition is hard to read, and the bathroom is old. It fails on price: 4,000 + estimated 650 bills = ~4,650, over the 4,500 cap — but note that overage rests entirely on the estimate, since no arnona or bills figure is stated.
+
  • By far the best kitchen: long two-sided counter run, real hob, big fridge, plenty of storage
  • 2.5 rooms — living room, bedroom, plus a small third room that could close off as a workspace
  • Living room is a decent size with a balcony and curtained window wall
  • Fully furnished per the ad; Pinkas is a quiet one-way residential street in central Bat Yam, walk to everything
–
  • All-in ≈ 4,650 (4,000 + est. 650) — over the hard 4,500 cap. This overage rests ONLY on the 650 estimate; no arnona or bills figure is stated in the ad
  • Even at the cap there is zero budget headroom for a car or co-working
  • Photos show a cluttered, occupied flat — real condition, light and cleanliness unverifiable
  • Bathroom and toilet are old and tired
  • Not on the light-rail street — a walk to the nearest Red Line stop
  • Broker listing (מרכז הנדלן) — fee likely
?
  • Ask for the actual arnona and monthly bills — if all-in comes in at or under 4,500 this jumps back to a pursue on the strength of the kitchen
  • Request photos of the empty flat
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (short_term unknown)
40
Over cap at ~₪4,850 all-in, and furniture is only an 'option' SKIP
Akko · מרכז העיר · ₪4,200/mo rent · 4.5 rooms · 120 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16

rent ₪4,200 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated

A large 4.5-room on the 5th floor at Moshe Tzuri 19 in central Akko, currently lived in and quite glossy: black marble floors, big gray sectionals, feature walls, an open white-and-black kitchen and a sea view from the balcony. But at ₪4,200 rent before any bills it breaks the ₪4,500 all-in cap on its own, and the ad says furniture and appliances are merely 'an option' — the sofas in the photos are the current tenants'.
+
  • Large 4.5 rooms with a genuine sea view from the 5th floor
  • Glossy, modern finish with an open kitchen and black counter
  • Central Akko; Akko station has direct TLV trains (~1h35, hourly)
–
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,850 (₪4,200 + ₪650 est. bills) — over the ₪4,500 hard cap even before arnona, which is not stated
  • Furniture and appliances are explicitly only 'an option' — the flat may arrive empty
  • Rent alone (₪4,200) leaves no room for arnona under the cap; this cannot work at this price
  • Far more space than two people need, with the bills to match
  • Central Akko 5th floor on a through-street — noise unknown
?
  • Only reconsider if the rent drops below ~₪3,500 AND furniture is confirmed included
  • Arnona is not stated and would push it further over

One photo only, and it shows a cluttered, dated Nahariya kitchen SKIP

A ₪2,600 3-room in Nahariya with a stated arnona of ₪400/mo, marketed by a local agency. The single available photo shows a small, cluttered, currently-occupied kitchen — wood cabinets, a gas hob, a microwave, lace curtains, bric-a-brac everywhere. There is a real hob and some counter, but with one photo and no view of the living space or bedrooms there is nothing to judge, and what is visible is not appealing.
+
  • Cheap for a furnished 3-room: all-in ≈ ₪3,650 (₪2,600 + ₪400 arnona + ₪650 est.)
  • Kitchen does have a gas hob and some counter
  • Ad claims furnished with a large balcony
  • Nahariya station has direct TLV trains (~1h44, every 30 min)
–
  • Only one photo exists — the flat is unassessable and the one view is cluttered and dated
  • What is visible looks like an occupied, tired, old-fashioned home
  • Nahariya is ~9km from the Lebanese border, at the edge of the siren zone the search avoids
  • TLV is ~1h44 by train — the longest of the viable towns
  • Listing photo dates to Nov 2025 — likely stale
?
  • Request a full photo set before spending any more time on this one
  • Confirm the listing is still live (photo is from November 2025)
  • Confirm Nahariya's border-proximity status against the exclusion rules

Hativat Carmeli 24, Neve Paz — ₪2,500 all-in but a 1m kitchenette, bare mattress and grey boxy rooms SKIP
Haifa · נווה פז · ₪2,500/mo rent · 2 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪2,500/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
One of a series of near-identical micro-units marketed by the same agency across eastern Haifa. ₪2,500 stated all-in for a small 2-room: a compact living room with a sofa, a bedroom with a bare mattress on a pallet, and a kitchenette of about 1m with a sink, a 2-plate hotplate, the washing machine and a fridge. Clean and renovated, but grey, boxy and characterless, in a steep rehabilitation-project neighbourhood facing the polluted Haifa Bay.
+
  • ₪2,500/mo stated all-in ('המחיר כולל הכל') — cheap, ~₪2,000/mo of budget left over
  • Clean recent renovation, AC, immediate entry
  • Has a 2-plate hotplate — at least some cooking capability
  • Close to public transport per the ad; ad claims a quiet area
–
  • Kitchenette is ~1m with the sink, hotplate and washing machine all on it — essentially no prep space; no oven. Fails the #1 criterion
  • Bare mattress on a pallet, no bedding, no bed frame — minimal furnishing despite the 'ריהוט מלא' claim
  • Grey, boxy, characterless rooms with small windows — not an inviting place to spend a hot September indoors
  • Neve Paz: 1970s rehabilitation-project neighbourhood, steep slopes and stairs, and HaHashmal-area industrial air pollution nearby
  • No desk or table anywhere; 2 small rooms for two people working all day
  • Template agency copy ('סופר מרווחת') that the photos contradict
?
  • Only worth a call if the top options fall through — ask for a real bed and a cooktop/counter upgrade
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability — short_term unknown
40
Golomb 15 studio — ₪2,000 all-in, bright with a green view, but the 'kitchen' is a sink and a washing machine SKIP
Haifa · ₪2,000/mo rent · 1 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪2,000/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
A small, freshly renovated studio at ₪2,000 stated to include everything. It is genuinely bright, with a big window onto a green hillside and marble-look finishes throughout. But there is no cooking capability at all: the kitchen run is a sink, a short counter and a washing machine — no cooktop, no oven — and the bed is a bare mattress on a frame with no linen. A student unit, not a home for two people who cook every meal.
+
  • ₪2,000/mo stated all-in ('שכ"ד 2000 כולל הכל') — very cheap, ~₪2,500/mo of budget left over
  • Genuinely bright with a green hillside view from the main window
  • Freshly renovated, clean marble-look finishes; floor 3
  • AC, fridge, wardrobe, washing machine; no broker fee ('ללא תיווך')
  • Next to Bnei Zion hospital in upper Hadar — dense bus network
–
  • NO cooktop and NO oven — the kitchen is a sink plus a short counter half-occupied by the washing machine. Both cooking elements missing plus minimal prep space = fails the #1 criterion outright
  • Bare mattress, no linen; minimal furniture, no sofa, no table, no desk
  • One small studio room for two people working all day
  • Golomb is a main arterial road by the hospital — traffic and ambulance noise
  • Marketed as an agency listing despite the 'no broker' claim ('סוכנות אמפייר סטייט')
?
  • Not worth pursuing unless everything else falls through; if so, ask whether a cooktop and a table can be added
40
Yosef St, Hadar — superb full kitchen (oven + range + L-counter) and high ceilings, but completely UNFURNISHED SKIP
Haifa · ₪3,000/mo rent · 3 rooms · 65 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪3,000/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
Painful near-miss. This 65sqm 3-room on the good side of Yosef has arguably the best kitchen in the cluster: a freestanding oven-and-stove range plus a large L-shaped counter with generous prep space and a plate rack — exactly the 'big countertop / two work surfaces' dream. High ceilings, big glazed balcony with green views, brand-new AC in every room, all freshly renovated. But every single room is completely empty, and the couple own no bed and no furniture.
+
  • Outstanding kitchen: freestanding oven + stovetop range, large L-shaped counter, real prep space, plate rack
  • 3 rooms in 65sqm with high ceilings and large volumes — plenty of space for two to work apart
  • Bright, freshly renovated, brand-new AC in every room
  • Beautiful full-height glazed balcony with green treetop views over the street
  • Ad says the good, quiet side of Yosef, close to everything in the neighbourhood; landlords described as attentive and pleasant
  • ₪3,000 + est. ₪650 ≈ ₪3,650 all-in — good headroom
–
  • COMPLETELY UNFURNISHED — every room is bare. The couple own only a desk and small items; furnishing a flat for a 2-month stay is not viable
  • Landlord wants 'organised tenants with cheques and a guarantor' ('רק לשוכרים מסודרים עם צ'קים וערב') — a long-term-lease posture, hostile to a 2-month stay
  • Agency listing (Enriq Group) — likely a broker fee
  • Mid-Hadar street conditions unverified
?
  • If the landlord would furnish it (bed, sofa, table) this jumps straight to pursue — the kitchen and the space are genuinely excellent; worth one phone call to ask
  • Ask whether they'd waive the guarantor/cheques for a 2-month furnished let
38

landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
Renovated hotel-like units (ad covers several) on Shoshanat HaCarmel, Carmel Ma'aravi, ₪2,900 'price includes everything', explicitly short-or-long term, guard in building. Photos show a clean modern studio (bed, TV, small dining table) and a narrow galley kitchenette: sink + ~1.5m counter + cabinets, with no cooktop, no oven and no fridge visible — it's also marketed as clinic/office space. For a couple cooking most meals vegan+GF this is a token kitchenette. Quiet, green area near Horev, but bus-only commute (no train nearby) is the cluster's weakest for Roy's Tel Aviv weeks.
+
  • All-in ₪2,900 stated ('מחיר כולל הכל') — clean budget picture
  • Short-term explicitly welcomed ('שכירות ארוכה וקצרה')
  • Quiet, pleasant Carmel Ma'aravi; Horev-area shops walkable
  • Modern, clean, hotel-grade furnishing; guard in building; immediate entry
–
  • Kitchenette fails the #1 need: no hob, no oven, no fridge visible, narrow counter
  • Marketed as clinic/office too — residential kit likely thin
  • Workspace = one small dining table in a 1.5-room studio
  • No train access — bus to Hof HaCarmel/downtown first, longest TLV commute in cluster
?
  • Only if reconsidered: which exact unit is offered and what cooking equipment exists — ask for a full kitchen photo
  • Would the operator allow adding a portable induction hob + mini oven, and is there counter/outlet capacity?
  • Confirm exact Sept-Nov 2026 availability and the all-inclusive terms in writing

≥₪2,650/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-termvery small
A ₪2,000 studio marketed at Technion/University students. It has a decent-length kitchen run (~2m) with a sink and a small 2-burner cooktop and a washing machine, and it's reasonably light with a balcony. But it is spartan to the point of bleak: a mattress on a wooden pallet, one chair, a folding table, bare white walls with nothing on them. Nothing about it is a nice place to spend two months indoors.
+
  • Has a 2-burner cooktop and roughly 2m of counter run — more cooking capability than most studios at this price
  • ₪2,000/mo — very cheap, would leave ~₪1,850/mo of budget even with the estimate
  • Renovated, reasonably bright, with a balcony a few steps from the flat
  • Convenient for the Technion and University of Haifa per the ad
–
  • Bare and bleak: mattress on a pallet, one wooden chair, a folding table, nothing on the walls — the opposite of 'bright, clean, inviting'
  • No oven
  • One studio room for two people working from home; a single chair and a tiny table
  • Utilities NOT stated (unlike the other ₪2,000 units here) — ~₪2,650 all-in on the estimate
  • The photo set looks like it mixes two different units (photo 4 shows a different kitchen and layout) — the listing may not be what's shown
  • Agency listing (LaByte Estates) with a vague street address ('השומר', no number)
?
  • Not worth pursuing — no advantage beyond price, and cheaper all-in options in this batch are better furnished

car recommendedborder areaconfirm short-term
A 2-room unit on HaPrachim, originally half of a split apartment, on floor 2/3 at ₪2,500 including arnona and water for the first year. It is clean, white and freshly done, with its own electricity meter. But the kitchen fails the couple's first test: a single short cabinet strip where the washing machine occupies the base run, a small two-burner hob, no oven, and barely any counter beyond the sink — with a fold-down wall shelf standing in for a table. Furnishing is sparse (blue sofa, glass table, bare mattress on a frame, TV) and the rooms are small and windowless-feeling.
+
  • ₪2,500 including arnona and water (first year); own electricity meter, so no shared-bill surprises
  • Clean, white, freshly renovated; shelter in the building
  • Good Karmiel location — near the new mall, shops and transport
  • Cheap enough to fund a car; Karmiel train to TLV (~2h); Hazorea ~50 min
–
  • Kitchen fails: a single short strip shared with the washing machine, two-burner hob, NO oven, essentially no prep counter — a token kitchenette
  • Sparse furnishing: bare mattress on a frame, one small sofa, no proper table (a fold-down wall shelf)
  • Originally a split apartment ('במקור דירה חולקת') — thin walls and awkward proportions; small boxy rooms with little daylight
  • No workspace beyond the sofa; nowhere to put a desk comfortably
  • 'כולל ארנונה ומים (לשנה הראשונה)' is framed around an annual lease
  • Agency listing (Tzameret Nechasim)
?

  • If pursued despite the kitchen, confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability and whether the arnona+water inclusion applies to a short let

Token kitchenette: no oven, plank counter, almost no prep space SKIP
Haifa · ₪3,902/mo rent · 3 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,552/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,902 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
Kitchen is a token kitchenette and the couple's stated dealbreaker: a portable two-burner hotplate on a narrow wooden plank that's shared with the sink, with no oven anywhere and essentially no fixed prep surface — the dining table is the only real work area. It does have a full-size two-door fridge/freezer, and the hotplate is exactly the "portable stovetop" they said they'd otherwise buy, but there's nowhere near the counter space a couple cooking most of their vegan+GF food wants. The rest is decent value: ₪3,902/mo (under budget), furnished for two months with multiple beds/desks and AC/WiFi so both can work in separate rooms, private entrance and courtyard. But the unit reads rough and semi-finished (raw brick, bare concrete), the toilet opens straight onto the kitchen prep area, and it sits on busy HaGiborim St with no walkable train.
+
  • Full-size two-door fridge/freezer (real cold storage, not a mini-fridge)
  • Portable two-burner hotplate already provided — the 'portable stovetop' they said they'd otherwise have to buy
  • ₪3,902/mo, comfortably under the ₪4,500 budget
  • Well furnished for a 2-month stay: multiple beds, tables/desks, chairs, AC and WiFi — two people can work in separate rooms
  • Private separate entrance and inner courtyard/garden; fully available (61/61 days), long stays accepted
–
  • No oven anywhere — they'd have to add a countertop oven
  • Almost no fixed prep counter: just a narrow wooden plank shared with the sink. Only real work surface is the dining table. This is the couple's explicit dealbreaker (lack of cookable SPACE)
  • Toilet/bathroom opens directly onto the kitchen prep area — unappealing for people who cook most of their own food
  • Rough, semi-finished feel: exposed raw brick, bare concrete ceilings/floors, basic wet-room bathroom; some rooms dim (small frosted window)
  • On HaGiborim St, a major arterial to the Carmel Tunnels — likely traffic noise despite the 'quiet building' claim; no walkable train station
?
  • Confirm a full ~2-month booking within Sept-Nov and the effective monthly price (₪3,902 shown; availability looks open but unconfirmed for their exact dates)
  • Verify real quiet — HaGiborim is a busy road; ask whether the courtyard unit is insulated from traffic noise
  • Check bus frequency to central Haifa and connections to Tel Aviv from Halisa/Tel Amal; no walkable train (stations are down by the bay, ~2-3 km)
  • Ask the host whether an oven can be added, and whether prepping on the dining table is acceptable to them
  • Locate nearest supermarket and any vegan/GF specialty shopping — likely a trip into central Haifa
36
Cookable kitchenette but minimal prep space; cramped for two SKIP
Haifa · ₪3,648/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,298/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,648 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen is the deciding weakness: a short granite counter with a sink, a small "Gold Line" countertop mini-oven, a likely portable hotplate resting on it, and a full-size fridge/freezer with upper/lower cabinets. So basic cooking is possible, but usable prep space is minimal — the sink and mini-oven eat most of the counter, there's no second work surface, no built-in oven or hob. For a couple who cook most of their (vegan + GF) meals and specifically need cooking SPACE, that is the stated dealbreaker. It's within budget and an Airbnb with full short-term availability, and Talpiot Market plus supermarkets in Hadar make vegan shopping easy — but the compact unit (one main room + small bedroom), shabby mid-setup condition, and no room for two remote workers to set up push this toward skip.
+
  • Full-size fridge/freezer, granite counter with sink, a countertop mini-oven and a likely portable hotplate — basic cooking is possible without buying much
  • Within budget: 3,648/mo vs 4,500 cap
  • Airbnb with 61/61-day availability and 1-night minimum — a 2-month stay is straightforward
  • Excellent fresh-produce shopping for vegan cooking: Talpiot Market (Sirkin St) and supermarkets walkable in Hadar HaCarmel
  • Low floor / minimal stairs; AC in both living room and bedroom; furnished with double bed, sofa and TV
  • Near Carmelit (Massada station) and Hadar bus network — manageable for daily errands without a car; Haifa is well placed for family in Kibbutz Hazorea
–
  • Kitchen prep space is minimal — short counter largely taken by the sink and the mini-oven, and there is no second work surface. This is exactly the couple's stated dealbreaker (lack of cookable space)
  • Only a small countertop oven and, at best, a portable hotplate — no built-in oven and no proper stovetop
  • Compact unit (one main room + a small bedroom) with no dedicated desk/workspace; very tight for TWO simultaneous remote workers when they bring only one desk
  • Condition looks shabby / mid-setup: cracked paint above the cabinets, dusty floor with debris, ladder and broom in shots; dark, worn bathroom
  • Hadar HaCarmel is a dense, busy neighborhood — quiet not guaranteed; the ground-level/low window raises light, damp and street-noise questions
  • Train station (Haifa Center) is ~1.5 km downhill, not comfortably walkable on the Carmel slope; buses/Carmelit needed for Tel Aviv trips
?
  • Confirm the black item on top of the mini-oven is a working portable hotplate/stovetop, and whether the host permits adding a portable two-burner
  • Verify the exact floor/street — whether it is semi-basement (light, damp, street noise) given the near-ground-level window
  • Two remote workers need two work spots: check whether the small rooms can fit a second work surface beyond the one desk they bring
  • Reconfirm the specific 2-month block within Sept-Nov 2026 and the monthly rate (Airbnb shows availability but confirm directly)
  • Verify quietness of the specific street in Hadar
36
