Cluttered, worn Kiryat Ata flat set up for three tenants — cheap but genuinely shabby SKIP
Kiryat Ata · גבעת הרקפות, גבעת תל חי · ₪2,300/mo rent · 3 rooms · 63 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,200/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,300 + arnona ₪250 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A ₪2,300 3-room at Hashiv'a 44 in Givat Rakafot with a stated arnona of ₪250/mo. The ad promises 'good energies', three beds, a fridge, oven, gas, microwave, washing machine and TV — and the photos show exactly that: an occupied, cluttered flat with scuffed walls, a narrow galley kitchen crowded with stuff, mismatched old furniture, a bare mattress on a single bed, and a dated bathroom. The kitchen does have a gas hob and cabinets, but the counter is short and buried.
+
  • Cheap: all-in ≈ ₪3,200 (₪2,300 + ₪250 arnona + ₪650 est.)
  • Has the appliances that matter per the ad: fridge, oven, gas hob, microwave, washing machine
  • 3 rooms; shelter behind the building
  • Furnished, immediate
–
  • Genuinely shabby: scuffed and damaged walls, clutter everywhere, worn dated bathroom
  • Narrow galley kitchen with a short, cluttered counter — little usable prep space
  • Set up as a 3-bed rental (likely for workers/roommates) rather than a home for a couple
  • Dim and uninviting in every photo — the opposite of a nice place to spend a day
  • Givat Rakafot, Kiryat Ata: bus-dependent, no train
?
  • Only revisit if nothing else survives and the landlord would clear and clean it
  • Confirm ~2-month availability Sept-Nov 2026

HaGiborim 24, Halisa — ₪2,200 all-in micro-unit, no cooktop, tiny dark rooms SKIP
Haifa · חליסה · ₪2,200/mo rent · 2 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪2,200/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪2,200 · Yad2 ad: 'המחיר כולל מים חשמל וארנונה'
confirm short-term
One of the same agency's series of tiny renovated units in Halisa. ₪2,200 stated all-in. The living room is small but has a sofa, TV and rug; the bedroom is a narrow box with a bare mattress. The kitchen is a ~1.2m run of sink plus washing machine with a toaster oven balanced on the wall cabinets and no cooktop at all. Halisa is one of Haifa's poorest, roughest neighbourhoods, on the eastern slope facing the industrial bay.
+
  • ₪2,200/mo stated all-in (water, electricity, arnona) — cheap, ~₪2,300/mo of budget left over
  • Renovated, clean; has a sofa, TV, rug, AC
  • Central and close to public transport and shops per the ad; no broker fee
–
  • No cooktop at all — sink, washing machine and a toaster oven on top of the cabinets, on ~1.2m of counter. Fails the #1 criterion
  • Rooms are tiny and dark; the bedroom is a narrow box with a bare mattress
  • Halisa is a poor, rough neighbourhood on the eastern slope facing the industrial bay — not a pleasant place to walk, and air quality is a concern
  • No desk, no table; nowhere for two people to work
  • Template agency copy ('דירת 2 חדרים מרווחת ומשופצת') that the photos contradict
?
  • Not worth pursuing

  • Workspace poor: no obvious table/desk arrangement in photos
?
  • Only if pursued: demand CURRENT photos (2026) of kitchen and appliances
  • Clarify real rent (2,000 or 2,300?) and confirm the 2,500 all-in figure
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability
  • Ask brokerage fee
35
Herzliya rooftop unit with an 80 sqm terrace — but 28 sqm inside, a token kitchen, and ~₪4,650 all-in SKIP
Herzliya · מרכז העיר · ₪4,000/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 28.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,650/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A 28 sqm rooftop unit in central Herzliya whose selling point is the 80 sqm terrace: a big tiled roof with a pergola, seating, a hanging chair and a west breeze — a real asset for a couple who are home all day. Inside is the problem. 28 sqm holds a double wooden bed, a wardrobe and a sofa, under a dark wood-clad ceiling, and the kitchen is a short counter with a sink and a microwave — no oven, no stovetop, almost no prep space. At ₪4,000 plus an estimated ₪650 of bills it also breaks the cap.
+
  • 80 sqm private roof terrace with a pergola, shaded seating and a genuine west breeze — an exceptional outdoor living space
  • Fully furnished to an unusual degree (ad lists an espresso machine and a Tami4), double wooden bed and a large sofa
  • Central Herzliya (העצמאות, מרכז העיר) — walkable shops and cafés, buses to Tel Aviv, and the Herzliya station is a short bus ride
  • Flexible entry date per the listing
  • Renovated per the listing, with AC in the living area and a mamad
–
  • All-in ~₪4,650 (₪4,000 + ₪650 estimated bills) breaks the ₪4,500 cap. NOTE: the overage rests on the ESTIMATE — the homeless listing shows 'ארנונה לחודשיים: 0', which is an unfilled field rather than a statement that arnona is included
  • Kitchen is a token nook: short counter mostly taken by the sink, a microwave, no oven and no stovetop — fails the #1 non-negotiable outright
  • 28 sqm interior for two people working from home; the terrace doesn't help on a hot September afternoon or during a work call
  • Dark wood-clad ceiling makes the interior feel low and closed-in despite the top floor
  • Floor 4 of 4 with an elevator listed, but a top-floor rooftop unit in September will bake
  • Photos 08 and 09 are clearly the homeless 'similar ads' carousel padding, not this unit — real evidence is only ~7 images
?
  • Only worth reopening if the landlord confirms arnona is genuinely included and gives a bills figure bringing all-in under ₪4,500 — and even then the kitchen is the blocker
35
Herzliya 8 studio — ₪2,000 all-in, but no cooktop, no oven, and a fridge parked in the middle of the room SKIP
Haifa · הרצליה · ₪2,000/mo rent · 1 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪2,000/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
A ₪2,000 studio stated to include everything. The kitchen run is a dark counter of maybe 1.5m holding the sink and the washing machine, with no cooktop and no oven; a full-height fridge stands awkwardly in the living space. Furniture is a bare mattress on a low frame and a grey sofa with a coffee table. Renovated and clean, but cramped, charmless and — despite the 'spacious and very bright' claim — visibly dim.
+
  • ₪2,000/mo stated all-in (arnona, water, electricity) — very cheap, ~₪2,500/mo of budget left over
  • Renovated with clean finishes; marble-look bathroom with a glass shower enclosure
  • Has a sofa, coffee table, fridge, washing machine, wardrobe, AC
  • Central location; no broker fee
–
  • No cooktop and no oven — sink and washing machine on ~1.5m of counter. Fails the #1 criterion
  • Fridge parked in the middle of the living space — the layout is awkward and cramped
  • Bare mattress, no linen; minimal furniture
  • Reads dim and charmless despite the 'משופצת ומאורת מאוד' claim
  • One studio room for two people working all day
  • Template agency copy from the same operator as the Halisa units
?
  • Not worth pursuing
35
HaGiborim 28, Halisa — ₪2,000 all-in, no cooktop, cramped, in Haifa's roughest neighbourhood SKIP
Haifa · חליסה · ₪2,000/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪2,000/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
Sister unit to HaGiborim 24 from the same agency, on the same Halisa street. ₪2,000 stated all-in. It has a sofa, AC, a small window and a patterned-tile bathroom, but the kitchen is a ~1.2m sink-and-washing-machine run with a toaster oven perched on the fridge and no cooktop, and the bedroom is a windowless-feeling narrow box with a single bare mattress. Halisa is poor, rough and on the polluted eastern bay slope.
+
  • ₪2,000/mo stated all-in (arnona, water, electricity) — the joint cheapest all-in here, ~₪2,500/mo left over
  • Renovated with clean finishes; sofa, fridge, AC included
  • Central and no broker fee ('ללא עמלת תיווך')
–
  • No cooktop — ~1.2m of counter shared with the sink and washing machine, toaster oven on the fridge. Fails the #1 criterion
  • Bedroom is a narrow box with a single bare mattress; cramped throughout
  • Halisa: one of Haifa's poorest and roughest neighbourhoods, on the industrial bay slope — poor air quality and not a place to walk around
  • No desk or table for two people working from home
  • Template agency copy ('משופצת יפיפייה', 'מומלץ ביותר') that the photos don't support
?
  • Not worth pursuing

Warburg 11, Kiryat Haim West — cheap 3-room near the train, but shabby, near-empty and no kitchen photo at all SKIP
Haifa · קרית חיים מערבית · ₪2,200/mo rent · 3 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,050/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,200 + arnona ₪200 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
₪2,200 for a 3-room in Kiryat Haim West with arnona stated at ₪200/mo — cheap, and Kiryat Haim has its own railway station and the beach. But the ad's claim that it's furnished 'as you can see in the photos' is contradicted by the photos: fluorescent strip lights, old terrazzo and worn tiling, two bare mattresses, a console table, an empty brick-walled veranda, and not a single shot of the kitchen.
+
  • ₪2,200 + ₪200 stated arnona + est. bills ≈ ₪2,850 all-in — cheap, ~₪1,650/mo of budget left over
  • 3 rooms; Kiryat Haim railway station and the beach are in the neighbourhood, giving a direct Tel Aviv line
  • Arnona figure is stated, so part of the cost picture is grounded
  • Ad claims walking distance to public transport, supermarkets and shops, and a quiet street; no broker fee
–
  • NO kitchen photo whatsoever — the #1 criterion is entirely unverified in a listing that shows six other rooms
  • Shabby and dim: fluorescent strip lighting, worn terrazzo, tired paintwork — the opposite of bright and inviting
  • 'מרוהטת ניתן לראות בתמונות' but the photos show only two bare mattresses and a console table — effectively unfurnished
  • No sofa, no table, no desk anywhere
  • Kiryat Haim is far from Haifa's café/culture life and sits in the Haifa Bay air-pollution belt
  • Managed by a national property-management agency with boilerplate copy
?
  • Not worth pursuing unless everything else falls through — if so, demand kitchen photos and a furniture list first
35
₪2,500 all-inclusive next to the Motzkin train — but it is a converted office box with a sink for a kitchen SKIP
Kiryat Motzkin · ₪2,500/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪2,500/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
Marketed as a 2-room on Weizmann 10 in Motzkin Vatika at ₪2,500 'including everything', a walk from the train. The photos tell a different story: suspended office ceilings with fluorescent panels, bare white rooms, a mattress-bench and a small desk for furniture, and a 'kitchen' consisting of one 1.2m cabinet with a sink and a microwave perched on the fridge. The large pergola sun-balcony is the one genuinely nice feature. It reads as a converted commercial space, not a home.
+
  • ₪2,500 stated as including everything — no bill risk
  • Walking distance to Kiryat Motzkin train station per the ad (direct TLV ~1h25, every 30 min)
  • Large sun balcony with a pergola and an open view
  • Central Motzkin Vatika location, immediate entry, no agency fee claimed
–
  • Kitchen is a single sink cabinet — no hob, no oven, almost no counter: a hard fail
  • Suspended office ceilings and fluorescent panels throughout — institutional, not a home
  • Barely furnished: a mattress-bench and a small desk
  • Feels like a converted office/basement unit; uninviting to spend two months in
  • Toilet and washing machine share a bare utility room
?
  • Skip unless the agency has other, genuinely residential stock near the Motzkin station — that location is worth asking about
  • If revisited, confirm what 'כולל הכל' covers
35
Migdal HaEmek, Tashi"g 12 — great view and a big L-kitchen, but essentially unfurnished, very tired, and let long-term SKIP
Migdal HaEmek · ₪2,600/mo rent · 4 rooms · 90 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,250/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,600 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
car recommendedconfirm short-term
A 4-room flat in a quiet boutique building with only two flats per floor and a genuinely breathtaking view over the valley from big windows. The kitchen is L-shaped with two work surfaces and decent counter length — but it is dated brown-tiled 1970s, and where the cooker should be there is an empty gap and an open shelf. More decisive: the ad lists exactly one item of furniture, 'ספה בסלון' — a sofa in the living room. Everything else in the photos belongs to the current tenant. The bathroom is original and worn, and the ad states it's rented long-term.
+
  • L-shaped kitchen with two work surfaces — the counter length itself is good
  • Genuinely lovely view over the valley; big windows, bright and airy living room
  • 4 rooms, quiet boutique building with only 2 flats per floor and plenty of parking
  • ₪2600 + est. ₪650 ≈ ₪3250 all-in — cheap for 4 rooms
  • ~15–20 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea
–
  • Essentially UNFURNISHED — the ad lists only 'ספה בסלון'; the furniture in the photos is the current tenant's and leaves with them
  • No cooker or oven — there's an empty gap in the kitchen run where one should be
  • Very tired: 1970s brown-tiled kitchen, original worn bathroom and separate WC, old terrazzo floors — fails the 'nice place to BE' bar badly
  • Ad states 'מושכרת לטווח ארוך' — explicitly long-term
  • 'צקים חובה' (cheques required)
?
  • Only revisit if the landlord would both furnish it AND install a cooker — otherwise there is nothing here for a 2-month stay
  • If pursued at all: confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
35
Afula, Etrog 1 — cheap and includes water + arnona, but a shabby bathroom, no cooker, and barely furnished SKIP
Afula · מרכז העיר · ₪2,400/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,050/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪2,400 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'תשלום כולל חשבונות מים וארנונה!' — water and arnona stated included; electricity not mentioned.
confirm short-term
A 2-room second-floor unit with an internal balcony at ₪2400 including water and arnona, and a genuinely lovely green outlook over a garden. That is where the good news ends. The photos show it mid-occupancy and chaotic — clothes and boxes across every room — but the fixed problems are clear enough: the bathroom is bare and shabby with exposed pipework and a shower curtain over a rough floor, the kitchen is a single wood-front run with no hob and no oven, and the ad's entire furniture list is 'ארונות ומקרר' — cupboards and a fridge.
+
  • ₪2400 including water and arnona (ad: 'תשלום כולל חשבונות מים וארנונה') — cheap all-in
  • Lovely green view over a garden and mature trees
  • Internal balcony; immediate entry
  • Central Afula — walkable, 825 to TLV ~1h45, ~20–25 min to Hazorea
–
  • Shabby bathroom: bare walls, exposed pipes, shower curtain, rough chipped floor — genuinely poor
  • No hob and no oven visible; the kitchen counter is half-taken by appliances
  • Furniture list is just 'ארונות ומקרר' — cupboards and a fridge; no sofa, no table, no bed promised
  • Photos show a heavily cluttered, mid-move flat — impossible to assess the space, and a bad sign about handover condition
  • One of the 7 photos is just the agency logo
?
  • Skip unless the landlord commits in writing to renovating the bathroom and furnishing it — neither is likely
  • If revisited: confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability and demand photos of the flat empty and clean

Lod's rail hub is superb (TA ~15-20 min by train; Gedera ~25 min by car), but ₪4,200 rent puts all-in around ₪4,8-4,9k — well over. Note: the same flat is also listed via an agency (63904feae058b524), and has apparently been sitting since spring — negotiable, perhaps.
+
  • 'מטבח גדול' stated; renovated plumbing/bathrooms, fresh paint
  • Lod rail: TA ~15-20 min; Gedera ~25 min
  • Quiet side street; private landlord, no fee
  • Two WCs; ACs in bedrooms and living room
–
  • All-in ~₪300-400 over cap
  • Sharet is one of Lod's weaker areas
  • Hob but no oven listed; furniture 'basic'
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability with landlord
  • Verify kitchen (no photos reviewed)
  • If pursued, negotiate hard — the duplicate agency listing suggests it isn't moving

33
Rehovot Max Nordau 2-room ground floor, ₪3,800 incl. arnona — no photos, and an explicitly haredi-only building MAYBE
Rehovot · ג' / מרכז מזרח העיר · ₪3,800/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,800/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪3,800 · Ad: 'המחיר כולל ארנונה פרט לחשמל ומים' — arnona included; electricity and water extra, amounts not stated.
confirm short-term
no photos
A bare broker listing with no photos whatsoever. What detail exists is discouraging for this couple: the ad describes it as a religious/haredi area, states that everyone in the building is haredi, and sells proximity to the PA"I synagogue — the landlord is clearly filtering for a haredi tenant, which makes a secular vegan couple a poor and probably unwelcome fit. With no photos the kitchen is unverifiable.
+
  • Price includes arnona (electricity and water excluded) → all-in ~₪4,450, just inside the cap
  • Ground floor, 2 rooms
  • East-central Rehovot: close to Gedera (~15 min) and Rehovot has a real train station to TLV
–
  • NO PHOTOS — kitchen, condition, light and furniture are all completely unverifiable
  • The ad explicitly frames it as an 'אזור דתי/חרדי' with 'בבניין כולם דיירים חרדיים' — the landlord is selecting for a haredi tenant and this couple would be a poor social fit
  • Nothing in the ad says it is furnished at all — most of the text is the agency's own boilerplate
  • All-in ~₪4,450 leaves almost no headroom for a listing with zero verified advantages
  • Agency listing → likely broker fee
?
  • Request photos, and ask directly whether the landlord will rent to a secular couple for ~2 months
  • Ask whether it is furnished at all, and what the kitchen has
  • Realistically this is a low-priority call — no photos, no furniture confirmation, a filtered building and no headroom
33
Cheapest here (₪2,800) and 7 min to the Red Line — but the 'kitchen' is a portable burner on a side table and it's near-empty SKIP
Tel Aviv · יפו ד', גבעת התמרים · ₪2,800/mo rent · 2 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,450/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,800 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A 45sqm 2-room on the 4th floor of Kehilat Kanada in Jaffa D, no broker, ₪2,800 — nearly ₪1,000/mo under cap. The window views are genuinely lovely (green treetops out to the skyline) and the Machrozet Red Line stop is ~7 min walk. But the photos kill it on both non-negotiables: the kitchen is a freestanding metal sink stand plus a single portable gas ring perched on a narrow side table — no counter, no cabinets, no oven — and the flat is essentially empty (bare rooms, wires on the floor), with 'ריהוט חלקי' amounting to a bed, a fridge and a washing machine.
+
  • By far the cheapest in the batch at ₪2,800 → all-in ~₪3,450, leaving ~₪1,000/mo for a car or co-working
  • No broker fee ('ללא תיווך!') — a real saving on a 2-month deal
  • ~7 min walk to the Machrozet Red Line station on Jerusalem Blvd, plus buses — strong for Roy's weekly Tel Aviv commitments
  • Genuinely nice green outlook and good light from the balcony side (photos 01, 07)
  • 45sqm with an internal balcony — not a cramped studio; 4th floor with no columns so it reads as 3rd
  • AC and solar water heater present; plentiful parking nearby
–
  • Kitchen is a hard FAIL on the #1 criterion: an IKEA-style metal sink frame and one portable gas ring on a side table (photo 02). No counter, no prep space, no oven, no cabinets. Nothing to fix here short of installing a kitchen
  • Effectively unfurnished — the rooms are bare in every photo. 'Partial furniture' = bed, fridge, washing machine. For a couple who own only a desk, this means buying out a flat for two months
  • Bathroom is very dated: old blue tile, wall-hung sink, shower curtain over a floor drain (photo 06)
  • Walls are scuffed and marked; the flat reads neglected rather than bright-and-inviting
  • Jaffa D / Givat HaTmarim is in the middle of large-scale pinui-binui renewal — construction noise and dust are a live risk for a Sept-Nov stay
  • No workspace of any kind and no dining table
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (Yad2 regular rental, short_term unknown)
  • Only revisit if the landlord would add a real kitchen (proper counter + hob + oven) and furnish it — at ₪2,800 there is budget headroom to negotiate that, but it's a big ask for a 2-month let
  • Check current pinui-binui construction activity on the block before considering
33
Tiberias garden flat: 140 m² and 5 rooms for ₪3,200, but shabby, dim, and furniture is only 'possible' SKIP
Tiberias · ₪3,200/mo rent · 5 rooms · 180 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,250/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,200 + arnona ₪400 + est. utilities ₪650 · Yad2 field: 'ארנונה לחודשיים: 800' = ₪400/mo. No electricity/water figures given.
confirm short-term
On paper the space is remarkable — 140 m², 5 rooms, a garden, ground floor, ₪3,200 + a stated ₪400/mo arnona. In the photos it falls apart: dim low-resolution shots of a tired interior, a weathered wooden deck, plastic chairs, a tablecloth thrown over a table, worn walls. The kitchen is a U-shape with a fair counter, sink, microwave and fridge — mediocre but not the worst here. The killer is that furnishing is conditional: 'יש אפשרות לריהוט' — furniture is merely possible, not included. Combined with Tiberias in September, this isn't a place to spend two months indoors.
+
  • Enormous for the money: 140 m², 5 rooms, ground floor with a garden and plentiful street parking at ₪3,200
  • All-in ≈₪4,250 with stated arnona — under cap
  • U-shaped kitchen does have a real counter run and a fridge
  • Ad claims a quiet, pleasant neighbourhood; AC in every space
  • Direct bus to Tel Aviv (~2h10); Hazorea ~1h drive
–
  • Shabby and dim throughout — worn deck, plastic chairs, tired walls; the opposite of 'bright, clean, inviting'
  • 'יש אפשרות לריהוט' — furniture is optional/negotiable, not included; the couple own nothing
  • Tiberias in September is extremely hot and humid — a poor bet for two people indoors all day
  • Photos are low-resolution and dark, which usually means the agent is hiding condition
  • 5 rooms is more space than they need at the cost of a much better small flat elsewhere
  • No train; bus or car only
  • Agency listing (S.M. Nadlan) with heavy boilerplate
?
  • Only worth revisiting if the agent supplies a fresh, well-lit photo set showing the flat furnished
  • If revisited, confirm exactly what furniture comes with it and at what price
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability
32
Western-Carmel 'hotel-style' studio — token kitchenette, fails the cooking priority SKIP
Haifa · כרמל מערבי · ₪2,900/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 25.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪3,550/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is a deal-breaker for this couple: the only real photo of it shows a token kitchenette — a sink, a mini bar-fridge and a sliver of counter, with NO cooktop and NO oven (photo 04). It's marketed as a '5-star hotel-level room, bring your clothes and move in,' i.e. serviced short-stay living, not a home for people who cook most meals. The attractive bedroom shots are explicitly labeled illustrations/renders. Upscale western-Carmel location, but the kitchen alone rules it out for cooking.
+
  • Upscale western Carmel (Derech HaYam) location
  • Furnished and marketed as immediate move-in
  • Cleaner/newer bathroom than the cheaper studios
–
  • Token kitchenette: sink + mini-fridge only, NO cooktop and NO oven — cannot cook most meals here
  • Key bedroom photos are renders ('image for illustration only'), so the real interior is unclear
  • Only 25 m² studio for two people living and working
  • ₪2,900 for what is effectively hotel-style living with no cooking
?
  • Confirm the landlord will do a ~2-month short-term let
  • Ask whether a real cooktop/oven and prep counter can be added — otherwise it's a pass
  • Request actual (non-render) photos of the specific room
  • Clarify what's really included vs. the illustrative images

Bare 1.5-room PT unit with no cooking appliances, no price, no short-term info — phone-only seller SKIP
Petah Tikva · קרית דוד אלעזר · ₪100/mo rent · 1.5 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪750/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪100 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
no photos
Renovated but nearly empty 45sqm 1.5-rooms on Gush Etzion St (Kiryat David Elazar, east Petah Tikva). Photos show a folded-up bed and a leaning mattress in otherwise bare rooms, and a kitchenette with an L-counter, hood and sink but NO cooktop or oven — the ad offers 'microwave possible', fridge, 2 ACs. Price is a ₪100 placeholder (unknown; arnona included per ad), short-term is unknown, and the seller answers calls only. Light rail is a 27-min walk (PT Central); it's a bus-dependent spot ~1h+ to TLV. Too many gates failed for a couple needing a real kitchen and two workspaces.
+
  • Renovated interiors (new bathrooms, tidy kitchenette cabinets); paved covered yard
  • Arnona included per the ad; quiet residential pocket, family atmosphere
  • Floor 1, immediate entry
–
  • Kitchen fails the gate: counter exists but zero cooking appliances (no stove, no oven; 'microwave possible')
  • Shown essentially unfurnished — folded bed and bare rooms; no sofa, table or desk in photos despite 'furnished' flag
  • 1.5 rooms / 45sqm — cramped for two people working remotely all day
  • Price unknown (placeholder ₪100) and short-term availability unknown
  • Light rail 27-min walk; TLV commute ~1h+ with a bus leg
  • Seller reachable by phone only — friction to even qualify the ad
?
  • REQUIRED (if called at all): confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability — completely unstated
  • Ask the actual rent and what bills are included beyond arnona
  • Is it furnished as advertised (bed assembled, table, wardrobe)? Photos say otherwise
  • Any stovetop/oven, or permission to add one?
  • Is this a rear yard unit of the family house? privacy and entrance

+
  • ₪2,100 all-in stated ('כולל כל החשבונות') — big theoretical headroom
  • Small work desk present; wardrobe and basic furniture in place

  • Condition uninviting: bare mattresses, patchy walls, mid-cleaning photos
  • 'חדש מקבלן' claim contradicted by the photos
  • Neve Paz/Halisa edge — weak area for two months of home-office evenings

₪1,500 'opportunity' in upper Tiberias — the single collage photo shows a tired, cluttered flat that fails the 'nice place to be' test SKIP
Tiberias · בן גוריון · ₪1,500/mo rent · 3 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪2,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪1,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
Cheapest listing in the cluster by far (₪1,500, ~₪2,350 all-in with arnona and bills) with a hilltop Kinneret view from the Ben Gurion neighborhood in upper Tiberias, near the Big center's supermarkets. But the agency posted one collage photo and it does the talking: dated pink-toned bedroom, a second room used as cluttered storage, worn 90s kitchen with a small table and old cabinets, and heavy red/white sofas — 'renovated' is not what the photos show. This couple will be indoors most of the day in end-of-summer heat; a shabby, uninviting interior is exactly what they said to skip, and the rock-bottom price is the only real advantage.
+
  • Extremely cheap: ~₪2,350/mo all-in (₪1,500 + ~₪250 arnona + bills) — massive headroom that could fund a car or co-working
  • Hilltop position with a genuine Kinneret/city view
  • Ben Gurion neighborhood is a short hop from the Big Tiberias center (large supermarkets for vegan/GF staples)
  • Furnished, AC + boiler, pets allowed, immediate entry
–
  • Photos show a tired, cluttered, dated apartment — fails their 'bright, clean, inviting place to BE' requirement outright
  • Kitchen looks old and small (table squeezed in, worn cabinets); no evidence of real prep space or a modern cooktop/oven
  • Only one collage photo from the agency — can't honestly judge kitchen, furniture, or workspace
  • Upper Tiberias is ~2 km uphill from the CBS/center — local bus or car needed before the already-long 836 to Tel Aviv (~2h10m+)
  • Short-term status unknown; standard rental via agency would need landlord agreement
  • Suspiciously cheap — likely reflects the condition
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short-term status unknown)
  • Request current, full photos — especially the kitchen (stove? oven? counters?) — before investing any more effort
  • Ask whether the clutter/storage is the outgoing tenant's and what the flat looks like empty

Herzliya single room near Reichman University — a student room with no kitchen shown and a single bed SKIP
Herzliya · הרצליה הירוקה, נווה עובד · ₪3,500/mo rent · 1 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A one-room unit in Herzliya HaYeruka / Neve Oved, marketed off its proximity to Reichman University, in a smart modern building. The single interior photo shows exactly what that means: a small room with a daybed, a dresser, a TV and a desk — a student's room, not a home for two. No kitchen photo exists at all, and the ad's own appliance list is 'microwave, electric hob and fridge' — no oven, no counter mentioned. At ₪3,500 + ~₪650 it's under the cap, but there's nothing here for a couple who cook every meal.
+
  • Attractive, well-kept modern building in a quiet green part of Herzliya
  • Reichman University is walking distance — its area offers library/study-space fallback for a second work spot
  • ₪3,500 + est. ₪650 = ~₪4,150 all-in, under the cap
  • Renovated bathroom, AC, immediate entry
–
  • No kitchen photo at all, and the ad lists only a microwave, an electric hob and a fridge — no oven and no evidence of any counter space. Fails the #1 non-negotiable
  • One small room with a single daybed — not a couple's home, and no second work spot
  • Cramped and uninviting for two people at home all day in September heat
  • ₪4,150 all-in for a student room when ₪3,000–3,200 flats with real kitchens exist in this same cluster
  • Herzliya railway station is not on the HaAtzmaut/centre doorstep — it's a couple of km east, a bus or a 25–30 min walk
  • Agency listing — likely brokerage fee
?
  • Not worth pursuing unless the agent produces kitchen photos showing real counter space and the room turns out to be far larger than it looks
32
3,050 studio near the light rail — spacious and cheap, but the kitchen is a 60cm corner with a hotplate SKIP
Bat Yam · ₪3,050/mo rent · 1 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,800/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,050 + arnona ₪100 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A large-ish, reasonably bright studio near a Red Line stop, 8 minutes' walk from the sea, with an owner who lives nearby and is a plumber/electrician — practical for a short stay. But the kitchen is a single corner cabinet run with a 2-burner hotplate dropped on a ~60cm wooden top, no oven, and essentially no prep space; and the furniture is a dated grab-bag (worn green velvet sofa, old wooden cabinet, low table) rather than an inviting set-up. All-in is only ~3,800 with stated arnona, which is the strongest thing about it.
+
  • Cheap: 3,050 + 100 stated arnona + est. bills ≈ 3,800 all-in, ~700/mo spare
  • Room is genuinely spacious for a studio with decent daylight
  • Close to a Red Line stop and 8 min walk to the beach; ad calls the area quiet
  • New AC; hands-on owner living nearby
  • Fully furnished with white goods per the ad
–
  • Kitchen fails the #1 requirement: a token corner kitchenette — 2-burner hotplate, no oven, almost no usable counter beside it. Two people cooking every meal would struggle
  • Furniture is old and mismatched; overall feel is dated rather than inviting
  • Single room — no second workspace, no closable door
  • Bathroom is old (curtain shower over a tiled floor)
  • Broker listing (ISRA-HOME) — fee likely
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (short_term unknown)
  • Only worth reopening if they'd accept minimal cooking in exchange for the price and the space
32
Pretty renovated Florentin gallery studio — but a 1m-high crawl-space bed, a tiny kitchen, and it busts the all-in cap SKIP
Tel Aviv · פלורנטין · ₪4,000/mo rent · 1 rooms · 22 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,800/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona ₪150 + est. utilities ₪650 · Yad2 structured field: ארנונה לחודשיים: 300 (=150/mo). Ad states no figure for electricity/water, so the fallback estimate applies.
confirm short-termvery small
A nicely renovated gallery (loft) studio on HaHalutzim St in Florentin, at 4,000/mo rent. The photos are the most attractive in the batch — wood floors, white metro tile, a mustard sofa, a good rain-shower bathroom, washing machine and a full-height fridge. But the kitchen is a small L of counter around a sink with a microwave and a two-ring electric hob squeezed on top of it, the only table is a low coffee table, and the bed sits on a gallery the ad itself describes as one metre of headroom — you crawl into bed for two months. At 4,000 + 150 stated arnona, electricity/water would have to come in under ~350/mo to stay inside the cap, which is not realistic for a couple running AC through September.
+
  • Genuinely attractive and fully renovated: wood flooring, metro-tile splashback, high ceiling, nice bathroom
  • Fully furnished and equipped per the ad — bed with mattress, wardrobe, sofa, coffee table, new Electra fridge, washing machine, electric hob
  • Florentin: ~500m / 6-8 min walk to the Red Line Elifelet station, and Shuk Levinsky (great for vegan/GF) is a short walk — excellent for Roy's weekly Tel Aviv interests
  • In-unit washing machine
  • Agent offers a video walkthrough, so it can be vetted remotely
–
  • OVER THE ALL-IN CAP: 4,000 rent + 150 stated arnona leaves under 350/mo for electricity+water — unrealistic for two people with AC in Sept-Oct, so all-in lands ~4,650-4,800 vs the 4,500 hard cap. (Note: the overage rests on the estimated bills figure, not a stated one.)
  • Kitchen is small — a short L of counter, most of it taken by the sink, microwave and a two-ring hob. Prep space for one person chopping, not for cooking most meals
  • No oven
  • The bed is on a gallery with ~1m of headroom (stated in the ad) — you cannot sit up in bed; punishing for a 2-month stay for a couple, and hot air collects up there
  • No dining/work table at all — only a low coffee table. Zero workspace for two, and no room to add a desk
  • Small footprint overall; the ground level is essentially kitchen + sofa + walkway
  • Florentin street noise: HaHalutzim is in the bar/nightlife belt
  • Agency listing (Shoresh Nadlan)
?
  • If the landlord would state bills as included at 4,000, this could come back inside the cap — worth one question before writing it off
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short_term unknown)
  • Ask for the video walkthrough to see the true gallery headroom and counter length
32
Wingate Ave, Ramat Hadar — quiet with a wadi view, but a shabby galley kitchenette and a very tired flat SKIP
Haifa · רמת הדר · ₪3,300/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,300 + arnona ₪200 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A 2.5-room on a quiet stretch of Wingate with a rear aspect onto a treed wadi and a bay view, walking distance to the Carmelit and Bnei Zion. Genuinely quiet. But the flat is very tired: a cramped galley kitchenette with mint-green cabinets, a small sink counter, an old washing machine and no visible cooktop or oven; a green 1970s bathtub; wicker chairs and a mattress-sofa; only partial furniture. At ₪3,300 it is badly priced for the condition.
+
  • Genuinely quiet: rear-facing onto a treed wadi with a bay view, on the quiet part of Wingate
  • Walking distance to the Carmelit and buses, and to Bnei Zion hospital
  • Balcony plus access to a small garden with steps down into greenery
  • Arnona stated at ₪200/mo, so part of the cost picture is grounded
  • 2.5 rooms, with the half-room as a gallery space
–
  • The kitchen is a cramped, shabby galley kitchenette — small sink counter, mint-green cabinets, no visible cooktop or oven, no prep space. Fails the #1 criterion outright
  • Very tired throughout: worn green bathtub, dated fittings, wicker chairs, a mattress on a low frame with a mosquito net — 'grandma's flat', not bright or inviting
  • Only PARTIAL furniture ('ריהוט חלקי')
  • ₪3,300 + ₪200 arnona + est. bills ≈ ₪3,950 all-in — expensive for this condition; other flats here at ₪2,500 are far better
  • No desk or dining table for two people working
?
  • Not worth pursuing
32
Karmiel HaCharuv 3: 'just bring your suitcases' — but the kitchen has no hob, no oven and no fridge, and half the photos are someone else's home SKIP
Karmiel · המיסדים · ₪2,799/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 75 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,674/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,799 + arnona ₪225 + est. utilities ₪650 · Yad2 field: 'ארנונה לחודשיים: 450' = ₪225/mo. No electricity/water figures given.
car recommendedborder areaconfirm short-term
Marketed as fully renovated and move-in ready at ₪2,799 with a stated ₪225/mo arnona. The photos contradict the copy. The kitchen is a narrow galley with a sink, a short counter and wall cabinets — no hob, no oven, no fridge, nothing. The rest of the set mixes bare empty rooms with rooms full of a current family's belongings (a baby crib, personal photos, cosmetics), so what's actually included is anyone's guess. For a couple whose first requirement is a cookable kitchen, this fails at the first hurdle.
+
  • ₪2,799 with a stated ₪225/mo arnona — all-in ≈₪3,675, under cap
  • 2.5 rooms, renovated finishes, clean bathrooms
  • Karmiel (HaMeyasdim/Founders area): train to TLV (~2h), in-town shops; Hazorea ~50 min
–
  • Kitchen has NO hob, NO oven and NO fridge in any photo — just a sink and a short counter; minimal prep space
  • Photos mix empty rooms with an occupied family flat (crib, personal effects) — the furnishing claim 'רק להביא את המזוודות' is not supported by anything shown
  • No furniture list at all in the ad, unlike every other agency listing here
  • Narrow galley kitchen with a cramped counter run even if appliances were added
  • Agency listing (Lev Karmiel) that is mostly self-promotion
  • Small rooms; light quality unclear
?
  • Only worth revisiting if the agent confirms the flat is genuinely furnished AND supplies photos of a kitchen with a hob, an oven and counter space
  • If revisited, confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability and get the electricity/water estimate

Kiryat Motzkin · מוצקין הותיקה · ₪2,200/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 30.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14

rent ₪2,200 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
This fails the couple's number-one need: there is no real kitchen. The listing lists only a large fridge, a microwave and a washing machine, with no cooktop and no oven, and no kitchen or prep surface appears in any genuine photo. It is a tidy, renovated 30 m2 studio with a grey-tiled main room, a sofa-bed, a folding table and a bookshelf, rear-facing and quiet near the Metronit and shops, and it is the cheapest at 2,200 - but for two people who cook most meals and both work on laptops, a sofa-bed studio with a microwave-only kitchenette is not workable for two months.
+
  • Cheapest: 2,200/mo
  • Clean, renovated and quiet (rear-facing)
  • Near the Metronit and shopping centres
  • Furnished (sofa-bed, folding table, shelving, large fridge, microwave, washer)
–
  • No cooktop and no oven - microwave-only 'kitchen', the dealbreaker for a cooking couple
  • No visible prep / counter space at all
  • Only 30 m2 and a sofa-bed - cramped and tiring for two over two months
  • Folding table seats one - poor for two laptops
  • Only photo 00 is this unit; the rest are 'similar listings' contamination
?
  • Confirm ~2-month short-term availability (listed as annual/12 payments)
  • Only worth it if they would accept adding a portable double hob - but there is still no counter to prep on
  • Ask to see actual kitchen photos to confirm how minimal it is

Agency-run suite in C-Tower (ex-Carmel apartment-hotel, Oved Ben Ami 2, seafront city center — the JSON coords are wrong). Photos show a hotel kitchenette (one short counter, 2-burner hob, under-counter mini-fridge, no oven), dated hotel furniture and a visibly stained mattress; the high-floor window faces east over a gas station and main road. ₪3,800 explicitly excludes arnona (₪300 stated), management fees, electricity, water and AC — realistic all-in is ₪4,800-6,000+, over budget. Short-term is easy here, but it fails the kitchen gate and the budget.
+
  • Genuinely short-term-friendly (holiday-apartment operator since 2006)
  • Beachfront/promenade location in central Netanya; supermarkets and city amenities walkable
  • Elevator, AC, immediate entry; hotel-grade bathroom
–
  • Kitchenette fails their #1 need: minimal counter, 2 burners, no oven, mini-fridge — not a daily-cooking kitchen for two
  • Price excludes arnona + management fees + electricity/water/AC — all-in very likely ₪4,800-6,000, over the ₪4,500 cap
  • Worn hotel furniture; stained-looking mattress; not an inviting long-stay home
  • Unit appears to face a gas station and main road (noise risk despite height)
  • Netanya main rail station ~2.5km — bus needed; TLV commute ~50-60 min door-to-door
  • Far from both Gedera and Hazorea family
?
  • Only if pursued anyway: get the exact management fee + typical utilities to compute true all-in
  • Confirm 2-month Sept-Nov price (hotel operators often quote seasonal/nightly rates)
  • 4 possible duplicate ads exist (no photos on disk to compare) — same operator lists many units; treat as agency stock

Location is transit-decent (HaHagana rail ~750m, shuk HaTikva ~400m for cheap produce) but the neighborhood is gritty and Purple Line civil works churn the area through ~Oct 2026. Cheap (~₪2,300–2,500 all-in with estimated Haifa arnona ~₪150 and bills), but price is its only advantage.

Ramla · נווה דוד · ₪4,000/mo rent · 3 rooms · 76 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,650/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
Spacious and properly equipped: 76 sqm, 3 rooms, fully furnished, on floor 1 of a 4-storey block on pillars. The kitchen is a real one — white cabinets, a granite counter, a freestanding oven with 4 burners, a big fridge and a dining table. The living room is large with sofas, a wall unit and a big window. Dated 1970s–80s finish throughout, and the price is the problem: ₪4,000 before arnona and bills puts the all-in near ₪4,900.
+
  • Real kitchen: freestanding oven + 4 burners + granite counter + a dining table in the kitchen
  • 76 sqm / 3 rooms — space for a separate closable work room
  • Fully furnished (sofas, wall unit, bed, wardrobes); service balcony with a washer
  • Central Ramla near Herzl St; Ramla station is central and runs to TLV
  • Very close to family in Gedera (~20 min)
–
  • OVER THE CAP: ₪4,000 + arnona (not stated, likely ~₪250–300) + bills ≈ ₪4,900 all-in
  • Dated throughout — 1970s–80s block, old terrazzo, tired bathroom, worn furniture
  • No workspace furniture at all — no desk in any photo
  • Neve David is a plain block estate; street quiet unverified
?
  • The 3.5-room Ramla listing (972682eed89c393e) is ₪500 cheaper with stated arnona, a desk and a 1.9.26 entry — strictly better
  • Ruled out on price unless it drops to ~₪3,500

₪2,500 all-in room in a divided Hod HaSharon flat — cheapest here, but bare, dated and on Highway 402 SKIP
Hod HaSharon · רמתיים · ₪2,500/mo rent · 1 rooms · 35 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
₪2,500/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
A room-unit inside a divided apartment on Derech Ramatayim, with private shower and toilet, at ₪2,500 covering all expenses — the cheapest certain cost in the cluster. The kitchen is actually more usable than most here: an old wooden run with a real counter, double sink, a two-burner portable hob and a full fridge. But the living space is bare — the photo shows a bed frame with slats and no mattress, a coffee table, and nothing else — the finish is dated throughout, and Derech Ramatayim is Highway 402, the main traffic spine of Hod HaSharon.
+
  • ₪2,500 with all expenses included per the ad — the lowest certain cost here, ~₪2,000/mo of leftover budget
  • Kitchen has genuine counter space, a double sink, a two-burner hob and a full-size fridge; washing machine present
  • Private shower and toilet rather than shared
  • Central Hod HaSharon with good bus access; Hod HaSharon–Sokolov station is on the Herzliya–Kfar Saba line nearby
  • AC, fridge and washing machine confirmed in the ad
–
  • It's a unit inside a divided apartment ('דירה מחולקת') — shared building fabric and probable neighbours through the wall
  • Effectively unfurnished: the photo shows a bed frame with bare slats and no mattress, and no sofa, table or work surface
  • Dated and uninviting throughout — tired bathroom, worn kitchen, nothing bright or pleasant about it
  • Derech Ramatayim IS Highway 402, the main Petah Tikva–Raanana road through Hod HaSharon — traffic noise on a main artery
  • No oven; hob is a portable two-burner
  • One room, no second work spot, and Hod HaSharon is well off the route to either Gedera or Hazorea
?
  • Only reconsider if it turns out to be properly furnished, set back from the 402, and notably larger than the photos suggest

rent ₪4,200 + arnona ₪200 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A recently renovated Lev Ha'ir flat styled like a dark boutique hotel room: black marble bathroom, black cabinetry, track lighting, and a nice touch — a white bar counter at the window with three stools, which is a genuine light-filled work spot. The kitchen is a narrow galley with sink, washer, fridge and what appears to be a built-in oven, but counter space is minimal and the whole flat is small and dim. It is priced out regardless: 4,200 + 200 stated arnona + estimated bills ≈ 5,050, well past the 4,500 hard cap, and that overage does not rest on the estimate alone.
+
  • Renovated and well equipped; internet included per the ad
  • A bar counter at a window with stools — a real, bright second work spot
  • Building was reinforced/renovated; 8 min walk to the sea; near a Red Line stop and bus routes
  • Appears to have a built-in oven — rare in this batch
–
  • All-in ≈ 5,050 (4,200 + 200 arnona + est. 650) — over the 4,500 cap by a clear margin, not an estimate artefact
  • Dark: black marble bathroom, black cabinetry, heavy curtains — a poor 'place to be' for people inside all day
  • Small: cramped bedroom, tight galley kitchen with little counter
  • Kitchen counter space is thin for two people cooking every meal
  • Broker listing (ISRA-HOME) — fee likely
?
  • Checked against homeless listing 1bc2f2d56934f4b6 (Amidar garden flat, 4,300): different unit — that one has a cream shaker kitchen, a blue sofa and a ground-floor garden layout. Not a duplicate.
  • Only reopen if the rent drops materially or bills turn out to be tiny

A 2-room, ~50 m² unit on Birnit in a genuinely pleasant, well-kept neighbourhood — landscaped stone paths, a paved garden, near the mall and buses — at ₪2,900 including arnona, water and internet. Inside it's close to empty: a bed frame with a bare mattress, a nightstand and a dark-wood coffee table are all that's there, matching the ad's own furniture list ('מיטה, שולחן, מקרר, מזנון'). The kitchen is a short run of dark wood cabinets with a sink and nothing else — no hob, no oven, no fridge in the photos, and almost no counter.
+
  • Attractive, quiet, well-maintained neighbourhood with landscaped paths and a paved garden; near the mall and public transport
  • ₪2,900 includes arnona, water and internet — a clean, predictable all-in
  • Clean and freshly kept; immediate entry
  • Karmiel train to TLV (~2h); Hazorea ~50 min drive
–
  • Kitchen has no hob, no oven and barely any counter — a sink and cabinets only; fails the #1 requirement outright
  • Effectively unfurnished: a bare mattress on a frame and a coffee table; the ad's own list is 'bed, table, fridge, sideboard'
  • ₪2,900 is expensive for what is delivered, given the same money buys 1c0f26ba or 80d16bfd with real kitchens and real furniture
  • Rooms read bare and beige; nothing inviting about spending two months in it
  • One workspace at best; no desk, no table
  • Agency listing (Shefa Nechasim)
?
  • Only worth revisiting if the landlord will install a hob/oven and furnish properly for a short let — unlikely at this price
  • If revisited, confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov 2026 availability and ask what 'שולחן' and 'מזנון' actually means in practice

Ramat Yishai, HaErez — perfect location and dates, ₪1570, but it is a 15 sqm single room SKIP
Ramat Yishai · שאר העיר · ₪1,570/mo rent · 1 rooms · 15 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪2,280/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪1,570 + arnona ₪60 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'לא כולל חשבונות (כ-60 שקל ארנונה בחודש)' — arnona ~₪60/mo stated; other bills excluded.
car recommendedconfirm short-termvery small
Painful, because everything except the flat itself is right: Ramat Yishai is the closest town in this cluster to Kibbutz Hazorea (~10 min), the Yokneam–Kfar Yehoshua station is a short bus 22 ride away, arnona is a token ₪60/mo, the ad says entry 1.9 — exactly their September start — and it's ₪1570. But the unit is 15 square metres. One room containing a bed, a wardrobe, a small kitchenette with a cluttered ~1.5 m counter and no oven or hob, plus a shower room. Two people working from home in 15 sqm for two months is not viable, and the brief says to filter out the really-small.
+
  • Closest of any listing to Kibbutz Hazorea — roughly 10 minutes away
  • Entry stated as 1.9 — matches the September start exactly
  • ₪1570 + ~₪60/mo arnona — by far the cheapest here
  • Renovated, with a separate entrance, no neighbours, and only 4 steps up
  • Central in the village: walkable to a shopping centre and public transport; Yokneam–Kfar Yehoshua station reachable via bus 22, 1–2 trains/hour
  • Ramat Yishai is a calm village — the quiet criterion is easily met
–
  • 15 SQM TOTAL — a single room for two people who both work from home all day. This is the whole story
  • Kitchenette only: ~1.5 m of cluttered counter, no oven, no hob — fails the #1 criterion
  • Furniture is fridge + wardrobe only per the ad
  • No workspace for even one person, let alone two
  • Bills not included (only the ₪60 arnona is quoted)
?
  • Skip on size — but Ramat Yishai as a TOWN deserves more search effort: it's the best-placed town in this cluster for Hazorea and it's calm. Worth widening the Ramat Yishai / Yokneam search to 2–3 room flats

Renovated Hadar basement, but no stove/oven and a tiny sink-counter SKIP
Haifa · ₪3,910/mo rent · 3 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,560/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,910 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen fails their #1 need: photos show a full-size fridge/freezer and a real sink, but NO oven and NO stovetop at all, and only a single base cabinet mostly taken up by the sink — no genuine prep space or second surface. Since a couple who cooks most meals said only ONE missing appliance is negotiable, having both absent plus minimal counter is effectively a token kitchenette. It's a renovated but by the host's own words "low ceiling and dim lighting" basement, which is poor for two people on laptops all day and only offers one small dining table to work at. The saving grace is location — Chaim Weizmann St 5 in central Hadar HaCarmel, a short walk to Talpiot Market (fresh produce and a vegan food shop) — plus it's furnished, in budget, and available short-term.
+
  • Strong fresh-food location: ~5-10 min walk to Talpiot Market (fresh produce + a vegan food shop) and Hadar shops - ideal for a vegan/GF couple cooking daily
  • Fully furnished and move-in ready (blue sofas, raised-platform double bed, wardrobes, dining table, washer + dryer); they only need to bring a desk
  • Full-size fridge/freezer and a real sink with a good pull-down faucet; renovated finishes (subway tile, wood-look floors, recessed lighting)
  • Within budget (~4,936... approx 3,936/mo), has a bomb shelter, and fully available with a 1-night minimum across the Sept-Nov window
–
  • Kitchen fails their top criterion: NO oven and NO stovetop at all, and only a tiny sink-counter with no real prep area or second surface - they'd have to buy both a hotplate and a countertop oven with nowhere to put them
  • Basement unit - by the host's own description 'low ceilings and dim lighting' - meaning poor natural light for two people working on laptops all day and taking video calls
  • Only one small dining table as a work surface; no separate second workspace for two remote workers
  • Hadar is a dense, busy neighborhood and Haifa is steeply hilly; with no car the train station is a ~1.5-2 km downhill walk (a steep climb back), so they'd lean on buses
?
  • Ask the host whether a stovetop and/or oven can be added - but the sink counter is too small to host both plus any prep, so this may not be solvable
  • Verify quietness of Weizmann St and the specific unit (a basement may buffer street noise, though Hadar itself is lively)
  • Short-term looks fine (listed 1-night min, all 61 days open in window) but re-confirm a ~2-month Sept-Nov booking and the price with the host
28
Givat Amos studio, 30 m² floor 12, ₪2,900 all-in — token kitchenette, cramped for two remote workers SKIP
Nesher · גבעת עמוס · ₪2,900/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 30.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪3,550/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
The kitchen is only a token studio kitchenette: a short peninsula counter, one upper cabinet, a countertop appliance and a fridge, with no visible stovetop or oven — a fail for a couple who cooks most meals. It is a small (30 m²) lived-in student studio on the 12th floor with a bed, one sofa and a single desk, so two people working from laptops would be cramped and short a second workspace. The upside is all bills included (electricity, water, arnona, internet) and a one-minute walk to the Technion's Nesher gate. Photos 05–14 are other apartments (scraper contamination) — the real unit is photos 00–04.
+
  • All bills included (electricity, water, arnona, building, internet) — simple budgeting
  • One-minute walk to the Technion (Nesher gate); high, airy, quiet floor per the ad
  • Furnished and move-in ready
–
  • Token kitchenette — no visible stovetop or oven; fails the #1 good-kitchen priority
  • Only ~30 m² studio with a single desk — cramped for two people working from home
  • Lived-in student setup; one small bathroom
  • ₪2,900 is steep for a 30 m² studio; framed as annual
  • Photo set is mostly contamination from other ads
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month furnished short-term let is possible (ad is annual-only)
  • Only worth it if a real cooking setup (stove/oven) can be verified — photos suggest none
  • Ask whether a second work surface can be added for two laptops

'Right, Wingate' Airbnb — ₪3,899 for a bare basement unit with a single-burner hotplate and a phone-screenshot listing SKIP
Haifa · ₪3,899/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,899/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪3,899 + arnona n/a · Airbnb monthly booking — utilities included in the rate by platform convention · missing arnona not stated
The worst value in the cluster. An Airbnb garden/basement unit near Bnei Zion hospital at ₪3,899/mo. The first 'photo' is literally a screenshot of the host's phone photo gallery. Inside: a bare mattress on a pallet, a folding table with plastic chairs, fluorescent panel lighting, and a kitchen consisting of a ~1.2m grey counter with a sink, a microwave on a wire shelf, and a single-burner portable hotplate. The wooden deck does have a genuine Haifa Bay view.
+
  • Real panoramic Haifa Bay view from the wooden deck
  • Airbnb monthly booking — utilities included, 2-month stays are the native use case, no lease or guarantor
  • Central, adjacent to Bnei Zion hospital, with public transport and a neighbourhood grocery right there per the listing
  • Private outdoor deck with planters
–
  • Kitchen is a single-burner portable hotplate, a microwave and ~1.2m of counter shared with the sink — no oven, essentially no prep space. Fails the #1 criterion
  • ₪3,899/mo for a bare basement unit — appalling value next to the ₪3,070 Talpiot Airbnb or the ₪3,700 boutique flat
  • Bare mattress on a pallet, folding table, plastic chairs, fluorescent panel lighting — uninviting and dim
  • The listing's lead photo is a screenshot of the host's phone gallery; the whole listing is careless
  • The unit is below deck level with the door as the main light source
  • Steep external wooden stairs are the only access
?
  • Not worth pursuing
28
Haim Ozer, Petah Tikva — hotel-pretty 40sqm studio with an IKEA mini-sink, no hob, no oven, no counter SKIP
Petah Tikva · חיים עוזר · ₪3,300/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 40.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-16
≥₪3,950/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,300 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A nicely styled 40sqm ground-floor studio: new laminate floors, recessed lighting, a proper double bed, a TV console, a small bistro table and two chairs, and a smart little shower room. But the entire kitchen is a freestanding IKEA-style metal rack holding a bar-fridge, a microwave on a lower shelf, and a tiny steel sink with roughly 40cm of steel beside it. No stovetop, no oven, no cabinets, no counter. This is exactly the token kitchenette the brief says to fail. Note that photos 9 and 10 belong to the homeless 'similar ads' carousel, not this unit.
+
  • ₪3,300 rent — all-in ~₪3,950 even with the estimate, leaving real headroom
  • Genuinely bright, clean and nicely finished — the most attractive interior in the batch
  • Double bed, safe room, storage, renovated, AC in the room; walkable central PT with the shuk and the light rail nearby
  • 40sqm with a safe room and a storage room is decent for a studio
–
  • Kitchen is a hard fail: an IKEA mini-unit with a bar-fridge, a microwave and a doll-sized sink. No hob, no oven, and essentially zero prep surface — two people cooking vegan/GF daily is impossible here
  • Single room only — no separation, no second workspace; the console/bistro table is the only surface
  • Ground floor on a central street — check light and noise
  • Arnona listed as 0, i.e. not stated — the ₪650 estimate is doing all the work
  • Entry 15/7/2026 — the landlord wants a tenant now, not in September
  • Fitted out as a short-stay/hotel-style unit for one person, not a home for two
?
  • Only reconsider if the landlord would install a real hob and counter, which the whole fit-out argues against
  • Photos 9–10 in the set are homeless carousel padding from other ads — a false-dedup risk

Rishon Tel Hai 30 sqm, ₪4,000 incl. water+arnona — bright and new, but no hob, no oven, and badly overpriced for the size SKIP
Rishon LeZion · ₪4,000/mo rent · 2 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,650/mo all-inincl. arnona · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'המחיר כולל מים וארנונה' — water and arnona included; electricity extra, amount not stated.
confirm short-term
Freshly renovated and genuinely bright — white walls, a black sofa, new flooring, a bed by a big window. But the 'kitchen' is a wooden counter with a small sink, a mini-fridge, and a countertop toaster oven and microwave sitting on top of it: no hob, no oven, and the appliances eat most of the already-short counter. At ₪4,000 for 30 sqm, plus electricity, it is both a kitchen failure and over budget.
+
  • Bright, clean, freshly renovated — good light and a big window
  • Price includes water and arnona
  • Sofa, bed, wardrobe and AC included; central Rishon location
–
  • Kitchen fails the non-negotiable: no hob, no oven, and the short counter is largely consumed by a microwave and a toaster oven

  • 30 sqm for ₪4,000 is poor value in this cluster — the Shikunei HaMizrah studio is the same size, better equipped, and ₪1,000 cheaper
  • One open room: no separate workspace and only a low coffee table to work at
  • Agency listing with hard-sell copy ('מהרו לפני שהדירה תחטף')
?
  • Only reconsider if the rent drops substantially AND a hob is installed — both are unlikely
  • Directly outclassed by a9b1a3a1605eb594 at ₪3,000

Afula, Holland 9 — token kitchenette with essentially no counter; fails the top criterion outright SKIP
Afula · מרכז העיר · ₪2,300/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪2,950/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,300 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A 2-room first-floor unit in the heart of Afula at ₪2300. The kitchen decides it and it decides it fast: a ~1.2 m base unit with a sink and a wooden splashback, plus a separate low counter holding a microwave next to the fridge. No hob, no oven, and virtually no free prep surface — the definition of the token kitchenette the brief says to reject. The rest is bare white walls, a mattress on a bed frame and a brown sofa, with a plain white bathroom. The ad is almost entirely RE/MAX boilerplate, and one of the three photos is just the agency logo.
+
  • ₪2300 is cheap; central Afula location ('בלב ליבה של העיר') — walkable, 825 to TLV ~1h45
  • Clean, freshly painted, bright white; AC visible
  • ~20–25 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea
–
  • Kitchen is a token kitchenette: ~1.2 m unit dominated by the sink, no hob, no oven, essentially zero prep space — an outright fail on the #1 non-negotiable
  • Nearly bare: a mattress and a sofa; no dining table, no desk, no real furniture
  • Only 3 photos and one of them is the agency logo — no living room, no kitchen wide shot
  • Ad is pure agency boilerplate aimed at buyers, with one line about the actual flat
  • Central Afula — quiet not verified
  • No stated utilities; ₪2300 + est. ₪650 ≈ ₪2950 all-in
?
  • Skip — the kitchen cannot be fixed by a portable hob because there is nowhere to put one or to prep on

Tel Aviv · יד אליהו · ₪4,500/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪5,150/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
no photos
50sqm 2.5-room at Emek Ayalon 3 (Yad Eliyahu), rented by the owner 'for several months' ahead of pinui-binui demolition — so the short-term flexibility is genuine. Everything else is weak for this couple: photos show a tired flat that is essentially unfurnished (one bed, one worn sofa, empty rooms), and the kitchen — though it has a decent counter run — shows no stove, no oven and no fridge. At ₪4,500 + arnona + bills it lands ≈ ₪5,000-5,100 all-in, clearly over the ₪4,500 cap, for a place that would still need furniture and appliances. HaHagana rail is ~1.2km; the Ayalon highway ~250m west adds background hum.
+
  • Genuinely short-term by design (owner renting until pinui-binui) — no landlord persuasion needed
  • 2.5 rooms / 50sqm — space for two workspaces in principle
  • HaHagana train ~15 min walk; good bus corridors (La Guardia); central-ish location
  • Enclosed balcony; pets allowed; AC + boiler
–
  • All-in ≈ ₪5,000-5,100 (4,500 + ~₪200 est. arnona for Yad Eliyahu 50sqm + ~₪400 bills) — over budget; estimates, nothing stated in ad
  • Essentially unfurnished for their needs: no table, no desks, no visible fridge/washing machine; 'furnished' box not even ticked
  • Kitchen shows counters but NO cooktop/oven/fridge — would need equipping for 2 months
  • Tired pre-demolition condition throughout; grimy enclosed balcony
  • Ayalon highway ~250m — background noise likely with windows open; occasional Menora arena event crowds
  • Same one-month-notice risk profile as other pre-demolition lets — end date must be pinned down
?
  • Only worth a call if price drops sharply (owner has zero long-term alternative) — then: confirm Sept-Nov window is safe from the pinui-binui timeline in writing
  • What appliances/furniture (fridge, stove, washing machine) would the owner install?
  • Actual arnona and va'ad amounts

  • Price includes arnona and cable
  • A desk and office chair are visible in one photo
  • Neve Hof is a pleasant area near the beach; immediate occupancy
  • Furnished per the ad ('מרוהטת קומפלט')
–
  • OVER THE CAP: ₪4,000 + electricity → ~₪4,650 all-in on the fallback estimate
  • Photos are blurry, low-res video stills — condition and light cannot be judged, and what is visible looks dim
  • No hob or oven visible; the counter run is modest (~1.8m)
  • Neve Hof is in far-west Rishon, a long way from either train station
  • Poor value versus the ₪3,000 Shikunei HaMizrah studio
?
  • Ruled out on price; the photo quality makes it unverifiable anyway
26
A 40sqm wood-clad shed in Florentin at 4,200 all-in — inside the cap, but effectively unfurnished, no cooktop, and it will bake in September SKIP
Tel Aviv · פלורנטין · ₪4,200/mo rent · 1 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,200/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪4,200 + arnona n/a · Ad text: '4200 כולל חשבונות' — 4,200 stated as including bills. Whether arnona is inside that figure is not stated and needs confirming. · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A ground-level unit on Maon St in Florentin, explicitly 'a unit, not a building' — the photos show a standalone structure clad and roofed in timber, with a pitched wood ceiling and small windows. It is essentially empty: a single armchair, a shelving unit, a fridge and a white kitchen run with a sink. There is no bed, no table, no stovetop and no oven. At 4,200 stated as including bills it does sit inside the cap, but there is nothing left over and almost nothing here for a couple who own no furniture.
+
  • 4,200 stated as 'including bills' — sits inside the 4,500 all-in cap without relying on estimates
  • 40sqm is a reasonable footprint, and the kitchen run does have a ~2m stretch of usable counter
  • Florentin location: ~6-8 min walk to the Red Line Elifelet station, Shuk Levinsky walkable for vegan/GF groceries — strong for Roy's weekly TLV interests
  • Private ground-level unit rather than a flat in a block: no shared stairwell, no neighbours overhead
  • AC, security bars and a steel door stated; bathroom is clean with a modern glass shower
–
  • Effectively unfurnished: the ad lists only a wardrobe, an armchair and a fridge. No bed, no table, no desk, no proper sofa — the couple own only a desk, so they'd be furnishing a whole flat for two months
  • No stovetop and no oven anywhere in the photos or the ad — and no evidence a cooktop is even plumbed/wired in
  • It's a timber-clad, timber-roofed shed structure: an uninsulated wood roof in a Tel Aviv September is an oven, and one split AC will fight it all day
  • Dark and uninviting — heavy brown wood ceiling and walls, small windows, low light in every photo. This fails the 'nice place to BE' test hard
  • Nothing left over from the budget: 4,200 of a 4,500 cap for a near-empty shed
  • Ground-level unit in a Florentin backyard — privacy and security worth questioning
  • Agency listing (Concord Nechasim)
?
  • Confirm whether 'כולל חשבונות' includes arnona as well as electricity/water
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short_term unknown; ad says immediate entry)
  • If pursued at all, ask what furniture would actually be there in September and whether a cooktop can be installed

Har Nof 12 sqm 'vacation room' at ₪2,500 — sink-only kitchenette, dark and dated; fails the kitchen gate outright SKIP
Jerusalem · הר נוף · ₪2,500/mo rent · 1 rooms · 12 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪3,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-termvery small
Tiny one-room unit (12 sqm) on HaKablan St in Har Nof, a strongly Haredi neighborhood on Jerusalem's far-western edge (well inside the Green Line), marketed as a daily/weekly/monthly holiday room (₪2,500/mo). Photos show a dated, dim space: a sink + small tiled counter with a mini-fridge (מקררון) and no stovetop or oven, a cramped shower room, sofa bed plus two joined singles, and one small table with plastic chairs. No cookable kitchen, no workspace for two, and not a pleasant place to spend two hot months — the low price can't rescue it. Transit is also weak: no light rail nearby, ~20-25 min bus to Yitzhak Navon station before the 35-40 min train to Tel Aviv.
+
  • Very cheap: ₪2,500/mo with explicit monthly pricing — big budget headroom
  • Immediate entry; AC, boiler, elevator and parking listed
  • Quiet residential street in a low-traffic neighborhood
–
  • Kitchen fail: sink + small counter only, mini-fridge, no stovetop and no oven — cannot cook most meals
  • 12 sqm for two remote workers: one small table, no real workspace
  • Dark, dated, shabby finish in photos — uninviting for a long indoor stay
  • Har Nof is a strongly Haredi enclave far from the center — full Shabbat closure, awkward fit for a secular couple
  • No light rail; ~20-25 min bus to Navon station, then 35-40 min train — weakest commute of the cluster
?
  • If pursued anyway: confirm the ~2-month Sept–Nov window (monthly rate is listed but availability isn't)
  • Ask whether ₪2,500 includes arnona and utilities — not stated in the ad

A first-floor unit on HaShdera in Moshav Tzofit: big bedroom plus a living room and, in the ad's own word, a 'מטבחון' — a kitchenette, not a kitchen. There are zero photos, so nothing can be judged on the couple's actual priorities. At ₪4,000 plus the ₪650 estimated bills it lands at ~₪4,650 all-in, over the hard cap. Transport is better than expected for a moshav — the HaShdera/HaKeshet bus stop is a 2-minute walk with lines to Kfar Saba and beyond, and Hod HaSharon–Sokolov station is ~19 minutes on foot — but that doesn't rescue it.
+
  • Genuinely quiet and pastoral setting per the ad ('מקום שקט ופסטוראלי')
  • Less car-dependent than a moshav suggests: HaShdera/HaKeshet bus stop ~2 min walk (lines 10, 29, 32, 142, 149) and Hod HaSharon–Sokolov station ~19 min walk
  • 2 rooms — a big bedroom plus a living room
  • Immediate entry
–
  • ZERO photos — kitchen, condition, furniture, light and space are all completely unverifiable, which is disqualifying for a couple whose top two criteria are the kitchen and the feel of the place
  • All-in ~₪4,650 (₪4,000 + ₪650 estimated bills) breaks the ₪4,500 cap. NOTE: the overage rests entirely on the ESTIMATE — the ad states no utility figures
  • The ad itself says 'מטבחון' — a kitchenette, which is exactly the failure mode to avoid
  • No mention of furnishing at all — may well be unfurnished
  • Tzofit is mid-way to neither Gedera nor Kibbutz Hazorea, and Tel Aviv needs a connection
  • ₪4,000 for a moshav kitchenette unit when ₪3,000 Netanya flats with real kitchens exist
?
  • Only worth a call if the landlord sends photos showing a real kitchen AND drops the price enough to clear the cap
25

A 45 sqm unit near Kanyon HaZahav with a separate entrance. It is furnished (bed, wardrobe, sofa, dining table) and has a small white kitchen with a sink, a microwave and what looks like a small hob, but the counter is short and the space reads as a long, dim, tiled corridor with very little natural light. At ₪3,900 before arnona and bills the all-in exceeds ₪4,550.
+

  • Separate entrance; 5 min walk to Kanyon HaZahav for shopping
  • 45 sqm is a reasonable size; Rishon is ~25 min to Gedera
–
  • OVER THE CAP: ₪3,900 + arnona (not stated) + bills ≈ ₪4,550+ all-in
  • Dim and uninviting — a long tiled corridor layout with almost no visible natural light
  • Small kitchen with a short counter; no oven visible
  • Neve Yam is in west Rishon, far from either train station
  • Poor value against a9b1a3a1605eb594 at ₪3,000

Token kitchenette, single cramped room — skip SKIP
Haifa · ₪2,365/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪3,015/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,365 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
Photos show a shrink-wrapped portable hotplate, no oven, no real stovetop, essentially no counter — unworkable for a couple cooking most meals. It's also one ~10-12 m² room with a single chair, so two people can't both work. Great groceries (Talpiot shuk) and transport, but the core cook/live/work needs fail.
+
  • Very cheap (₪2,365)
  • Talpiot Market + vegan shop at the door
  • Metronit stop outside

24
Token kitchenette: 1 hotplate, no oven, no prep space SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,434/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪5,084/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,434 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen is a token kitchenette and the dealbreaker here: a single portable electric hotplate, a small microwave (no real oven), a mini-fridge and a small sink, with almost no prep surface — just the narrow stone slab wrapped around the sink. For a couple cooking most of their vegan + gluten-free meals, there's essentially nowhere to work and no room to even add a portable oven. The studio itself is otherwise a genuine plus: a clean, newly renovated industrial-loft space, fully furnished, within budget at ₪4,434, with good short-term availability. But it fails the #1 criterion, and it sits on HaGibborim — a busy main artery in the weaker eastern-Haifa Halisa/Tel Amal cluster — with no walkable train and only a cramped, shared open-plan work setup for two.
+
  • Fully furnished, clean, newly renovated industrial-loft studio — livable for 2 months bringing only a desk
  • Within budget at ₪4,434 (≤ ₪4,500)
  • Separate entrance with garden view; garden-facing door may buffer some street noise
  • Strong short-term signal: all 61 days open, long-stay minimum (up to 21 nights), description says 'suitable for any period'
  • Some cooking is possible if they adapt (hotplate, microwave, kettle, mini-fridge, sink)
  • Reasonable base for visiting Kibbutz Hazorea; buses on the main road link to downtown, train and Metronit
–
  • Token kitchenette fails the #1 criterion: single portable hotplate, NO oven (only a small microwave), and almost no prep counter
  • Counter is just the sink surround — no second surface and nowhere to add a portable oven for two people cooking daily
  • Weaker/lower-income eastern-Haifa neighborhood (Halisa/Tel Amal cluster), described locally as low social resilience
  • On HaGibborim, a main transport artery — likely traffic noise, working against the 'quiet' priority
  • Open-plan studio: no separation for two simultaneous video calls; only one small table as a work spot
  • No walkable train station; car-free trips to Tel Aviv mean a bus downhill plus train
?
  • Short-term availability looks good (61/61 days, 'suitable for any period') but confirm the specific ~2-month block within Sept–Nov 2026
  • Ask whether the countertop appliance is a microwave or a small oven, and whether a larger portable oven or second burner could fit given the tiny counter
  • Verify real street-noise level given HaGibborim traffic — does the garden-facing separate entrance actually insulate the unit?
  • Check walkable groceries and any vegan/GF options; specialty shopping likely requires Hadar or central Haifa

Newly renovated, clean and white, on a sought-after central Rehovot street with only two steps at the entrance. But the fit-out is bare — a grey sofa, a mattress on a pallet base, a white wardrobe — and the kitchen is a small kitchenette: a sink, roughly a metre of counter, and a fridge. No hob, no oven, nowhere to prep. That is a fail on the couple's first requirement, and at ₪3,850 plus water and electricity the all-in sits right at the cap.
+
  • Freshly renovated, clean and white; ground floor with only two steps

  • Very bare: a mattress on a pallet, no dining table, no desk, no workspace
