  • OVER THE CAP: ₪4,300 + arnona (not stated) + bills → ~₪4,950 all-in. Not close
  • Visibly worn and cluttered — old terrazzo floors, marked walls, one room piled with belongings, a bare-bulb fixture
  • Ramat Eliyahu is one of Rishon's weaker neighbourhoods
  • Not an inviting place to spend two months indoors
?
  • Price alone rules it out; the condition confirms it
22
Tired, dated 4,300 Florentin studio with a reed-screened balcony onto a light well — over the cap and shabby with it SKIP
Tel Aviv · פלורנטין · ₪4,300/mo rent · 1 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,950/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,300 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A lived-in studio in Florentin at 4,300/mo with 'partial furnishing' — wardrobe, bed, fridge. The photos show a dated, worn unit: scuffed walls, old beige floor tiles, a dark inner corridor, an aging bathroom with a plastic shower screen, and a bare mattress on a base. The kitchen is the one bright spot — a genuinely long granite counter run with plenty of prep space — but no stovetop or oven is visible. The 'sun balcony' is a tiny recess screened with a reed mat, looking straight at the neighbours' AC units. At 4,300 plus arnona plus bills it is comfortably over the all-in cap.
+
  • The kitchen has a long granite counter — real prep space, the best surface-length in the batch after 940dcf256bfc69de
  • Florentin: ~6-8 min walk to Red Line Elifelet, Shuk Levinsky walkable for vegan/GF groceries
  • There is a balcony, however small, and windows on more than one side
  • Bed, wardrobe and fridge included
–
  • OVER THE ALL-IN CAP: 4,300 rent + arnona + bills ≈ 4,950 — nearly 500 over, and 4,300 alone leaves only 200 of headroom
  • No stovetop and no oven visible in the kitchen photos despite the good counter
  • Shabby and dated: scuffed and patched walls, worn tiles, an old bathroom with a plastic screen, bare mattress, a gloomy internal corridor. This fails the 'bright, clean, inviting' priority
  • 'Partial furnishing' is accurate — wardrobe, bed, fridge and nothing else. No sofa, no table for two laptops
  • The 'sun balcony' is a ~1.5m recess behind a reed screen facing the neighbours' AC condensers and washing lines — not usable outdoor space
  • Florentin nightlife noise
  • Agency listing, and most of the ad text is the agent's self-promotion rather than facts about the flat
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short_term unknown) — but the price alone rules it out
22
Stunning hotel-standard Caesarea studio with resort amenities — but a bar-sink kitchenette, annual-only pricing, and over the cap SKIP
Caesarea · נאות גולף · ₪4,200/mo rent · 1 rooms · 25 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,850/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,200 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · Ad: 'המחיר כולל דמי ניהול' — management fees included; no arnona or utility figures stated. · missing arnona not stated
car recommendedconfirm short-term
The most beautiful listing in the cluster by a distance: a designer studio in the Neot Golf compound, finished to hotel standard, with a lovely bed, a view over greenery to the sea, 24/7 security, heated semi-Olympic pools, gyms, saunas and tennis. And it's the wrong apartment for this couple. The kitchen photo shows a bar kitchenette — a sink taking most of a narrow counter, an under-counter fridge, no oven and no stovetop anywhere. The ad also states the price is valid for an ANNUAL let, and at ₪4,200 + ₪650 estimated bills it's ~₪4,850 all-in, over the cap.
+
  • Genuinely beautiful, immaculate, hotel-standard finish — bright, calm and inviting to be in
  • Fully and completely furnished ('אבזור וריהוט מלא לחלוטין')
  • Resort amenities: 24/7 security, heated semi-Olympic pools, private gyms, saunas, squash and tennis courts
  • Price stated to include management fees ('המחיר כולל דמי ניהול')
  • Green outlook to the sea from the bed and a small café table by the window
–
  • The kitchen is a bar kitchenette: a sink consuming a narrow counter, an under-counter fridge, no oven, no stovetop, no prep space. An outright fail on the #1 non-negotiable for a couple cooking vegan + GF daily
  • All-in ~₪4,850 (₪4,200 + ₪650 estimated bills) breaks the ₪4,500 cap. NOTE: the overage rests on the ESTIMATE, though management fees are stated as included
  • Ad explicitly says the price applies to an ANNUAL rental ('המחיר תקף להשכרה שנתית') — a 2-month let would likely cost more, if offered at all
  • Studio: one room, one small café table, no second work spot and no closable room
  • Caesarea is genuinely car-dependent — no train, sparse buses, and Tel Aviv is a real trek
  • No walkable supermarket or shuk in the Neot Golf compound
  • Furthest of all from both Gedera and Kibbutz Hazorea family
?
  • Not pursuable as-is; would need a real kitchen, a 2-month price under the cap, and a car in the budget

Aparthotel micro-studio on Menachem Begin: zero kitchen, huge junction outside — wrong product for a cooking couple SKIP
Tel Aviv · לב תל אביב, לב העיר צפון · ₪3,900/mo rent · 1 rooms · 25 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪4,550/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
25sqm 'studio apartments' operation at Menachem Begin 19 (hotel-style corridor with numbered doors, lobby plants, generic room shots — some look staged/rendered). Furnishing is hotel-room grade: bed, 55" TV, dresser, bar table, fridge, microwave — and NO cooking facilities at all: no stovetop, no oven, no kitchen counter. That's an outright fail on the #1 criterion. The window photo shows the massive Begin/Levinsky-area junction below: heavy traffic noise on one of TLV's busiest arteries. Transit is admittedly superb (Allenby Red Line ~5 min, Carlebach ~8, HaHagana rail ~15) and 'short and long periods' are welcomed, but this is a crash-pad product, not a home to cook and work in for two months.
+
  • Explicitly rents 'short and long periods' — zero availability friction
  • Outstanding transit: Red Line Allenby ~400m, Carlebach ~650m; buses everywhere; ~15 min walk to HaHagana rail
  • Elevator, entry-control, tenant laundry room, security room; renovated interiors
  • Walkable to Rothschild/Levinsky market area
–
  • No kitchen whatsoever (fridge + microwave only) — automatic fail for a couple cooking vegan+GF most meals
  • Directly over a giant multi-lane Begin junction — serious traffic noise for people home all day
  • 25sqm hotel-room layout: one bar table, no real dual-workspace potential
  • Some photos appear staged/renders — actual unit may differ; aparthotel operations often add fees
  • Old central bus station vicinity to the south is seedy at night
?
  • Skip unless everything else falls through AND they accept microwave-only eating — not their profile
  • If contacted anyway: actual unit photos, total monthly incl. bills/fees, which floor/side (courtyard-facing units would be quieter)

A 55 sqm 2-room flat in a nice central Rishon spot — quiet, sought-after street, walkable to the midrahov, shops, restaurants and a big park. But the numbers kill it: ₪4,000 plus a stated ₪325/mo arnona plus bills gives an all-in near ₪4,975, well over the cap.   • 55 sqm with a big living room, 2 balconies, 3 exposures — spacious and light
  • Freshly painted; a mamad and bars; a desk-able dining table in the living room

  • Kitchen shows only a cabinet run with a dishwasher on the counter — no oven or hob visible, and the ad lists only 'ארון מטבח תקין' and a dishwasher

?

Kiryat Nordau 3-room with a real kitchen — but essentially empty, and ₪4,520 before a single bill SKIP
Netanya · קרית נורדאו · ₪4,300/mo rent · 3 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪5,170/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,300 + arnona ₪220 + est. utilities ₪650 · Yad2 field: ארנונה לחודשיים 440 → ₪220/mo. Rent ₪4,300 + arnona ₪220 = ₪4,520 all-in before utilities — over the cap on stated figures alone.
confirm short-term
Frustrating: this is the one listing with an unambiguously real kitchen — a long counter run with proper prep space and a freestanding gas range with an oven. Everything else fails. The ad says 'מרוהטת חלקית כולל מקרר ותנור אפיה' — partially furnished meaning a fridge and an oven — and the photos confirm it: room after room is completely bare, no bed, no sofa, no table. For a couple who own nothing but a desk, that's disqualifying. And ₪4,300 rent plus the stated ₪220/mo arnona is already ₪4,520 — over the cap before any utility bill at all.
+
  • A genuinely real kitchen: long counter run with proper prep space, a freestanding gas range with a full oven — the best cooking setup in the cluster
  • 3 rooms and a spacious, bright living area with large windows
  • Well-kept building on a wide, quiet street per the ad, with easy access out of the city
  • Kiryat Nordau is a calm residential Netanya neighbourhood
  • Bathroom is clean and modern with a bathtub
–
  • Over the cap on stated figures alone: ₪4,300 rent + ₪220/mo stated arnona = ₪4,520 before any electricity, water or vaad — this does NOT rest on an estimate
  • Essentially unfurnished — 'partially furnished' means a fridge and an oven, and the photos show empty rooms with no bed, no sofa, no table. The couple own no furniture
  • No work surface, no seating, nothing to make it a place to be — they'd be furnishing a flat for 2 months
  • The one kitchen photo shows a lived-in, cluttered kitchen while every other room is bare, so what actually stays is unclear
  • Netanya train station is not walkable from Kiryat Nordau
?
  • Not pursuable: over the cap on stated numbers and unfurnished. Only relevant if rent dropped substantially AND it came furnished

Tel Aviv · ₪3,900/mo rent · 2 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,675/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,900 + arnona ₪125 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A 2.5-room with a private yard on Israel Guri Blvd in Neve Sharet, far north-east Tel Aviv beyond the Yarkon. The agent copy calls it 'pleasant and well-organised'; the photos say otherwise — the kitchen is a run of scuffed old white cabinets with a portable CAMPIT camping gas burner sitting on the counter, no oven and no built-in hob, the bed is a mattress on a raw wooden pallet, and the bathroom is a dated tiled cubicle. All-in also breaks the cap.
+
  • Private outdoor yard with a pergola, and an office chair already on site
  • Arnona is stated and low (₪250 per two months = ₪125/mo)
  • 2.5 rooms is more space on paper than most of this cluster
  • Quiet residential neighbourhood away from through-traffic
–
  • Kitchen fails the #1 criterion: no oven, no built-in stovetop — just a portable camping gas burner on a worn counter (photo 04)
  • Genuinely shabby, not merely dated: mattress on a pallet, tired terrazzo, scuffed cabinet doors, dolphin-curtain bathroom — the opposite of the bright inviting place they want to spend their days in
  • All-in over cap: ₪3,900 + ₪125 arnona + ~₪650 est. bills ≈ ₪4,675
  • Neve Sharet is far NE Tel Aviv with no train or light rail nearby — bus-dependent for Roy's weekly Tel Aviv commitments, which kills this cluster's one big advantage
  • Agency listing (RE/MAX Infinity, a Petah Tikva office) — broker fee likely, and the copy is generic boilerplate that doesn't match the photos
  • Listed since April 2026 — sitting unrented for ~3 months is itself a signal
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (Yad2 regular rental, short_term unknown)
  • Not worth pursuing unless they'd accept ~₪3,200 and install a real hob — the location alone doesn't justify it

Generic 'assorted studios' ad above a busy Allenby-corner café — no specific unit, token kitchenettes, and it clears the cap SKIP
Tel Aviv · לב תל אביב, לב העיר צפון · ₪3,900/mo rent · 1 rooms · 35 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,650/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,900 + arnona ₪100 + est. utilities ₪650 · Yad2 structured field: ארנונה לחודשיים: 200 (=100/mo). Ad states no bills figure; rent itself is vague ('מחירים סביב 4000').
confirm short-term
This is not one apartment: the ad offers 'a variety of studio apartments' at 'prices around 4,000' in an old character building at the Levontin/HaHashmal corner of Allenby, above a well-known café. The photo set is a grab-bag of different units — some bright with a bed and a table, some bare with a mattress — so there's no way to know what's actually on offer. Every kitchen shown is a token kitchenette: a 1m sink cabinet with a two-ring hotplate perched on it, no oven, effectively no prep space. Combined with a rent of 3,900-4,000 plus 100/mo stated arnona plus bills, it lands over the cap.
+
  • Prime central location: Levontin/HaHashmal, minutes' walk to the Red Line Allenby station, Rothschild, and Shuk Levinsky/Carmel for vegan/GF groceries
  • Some of the units shown are genuinely bright with high ceilings and good windows
  • Agent sends video walkthroughs by WhatsApp, so units could be vetted before travel
  • Building has real character and a leafy street outlook in one of the photos
–
  • OVER THE ALL-IN CAP: ~3,900-4,000 rent + 100 stated arnona + estimated bills ≈ 4,650. (The overage rests on the 650 estimate, but even generous bills for two with AC would clear 4,500.)
  • The kitchens are exactly the failure case: a sink cabinet with a hotplate on the worktop, no oven, essentially no prep surface. This couple cooks most meals — it's a non-starter
  • Not a real listing — 'assorted studios, prices around 4,000' means the specific unit, price and condition are all unknown; the photos mix at least four different apartments
  • Directly above a busy café on the Allenby corner, in the bar belt. Loud downstairs, loud outside, and the ad's own phrase for the area is 'מתחמם' (heating up)
  • The exterior photo shows a visibly derelict building with unglazed window openings
  • Requires checks, a guarantor and a month's deposit — hostile to a 2-month term
  • Agency listing (Homerun) — same agency as 773eede360697307
?
  • If revisited, demand one specific unit with a specific price, a video, and confirmation of what cooking appliances it has
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short_term unknown)

no photos
The ad states outright that it suits 'a Litvish or Hasidic couple' and it sits in Chazon Ovadia / RBS Gimmel-2, a strictly haredi enclave — the landlord is filtering for a tenant this couple is not. There are no photos, so the kitchen is unverifiable beyond the text (upgraded cabinets, upper cabinets, an electric hob — no oven). RBS Gimmel is also several kilometres from Beit Shemesh station with no walkable connection.
+
  • Ad describes it as well-invested, clean, airy and bright with two large windows
  • Furnished per the text: table and 4 chairs, washer, dryer, fridge, electric hob, bed, 5-door wardrobe
  • Separate bedroom, plus a laundry room
–
  • 'מתאים לזוג ליטאי או חסידי' — the landlord is explicitly seeking a haredi couple; RBS Gimmel-2 is a strictly haredi neighbourhood where this couple would be a poor fit
  • NO PHOTOS — the kitchen, light and condition are unverifiable
  • No oven (electric hob only) and no counter information
  • RBS Gimmel-2 is ~4–5 km from Beit Shemesh train station with no walkable link — bus-dependent or car-dependent for the TLV run
  • ₪3,850 + bills = ₪4,500 exactly at the cap before arnona (which is not stated) — realistically over
  • Vegan/GF grocery options in RBS-G are limited
  • Furthest from the Tel Aviv axis of anything in this cluster
?
  • In scope (Beit Shemesh is inside the Green Line), but ruled out on tenant fit, price and transit
  • Only worth revisiting if nothing else survives and the landlord confirms they would rent to a secular couple
18
Karmiel attic unit: ₪2,500 all-in for a cramped, dark loft whose kitchen is a hotplate on a wooden trestle SKIP
Karmiel · ₪2,500/mo rent · 1.5 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪2,500/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪2,500 · Ad: 'כולל אינטרנט, מים, ארנונה וועד בית' — internet, water, arnona and building fees included in ₪2,500. Electricity not mentioned.
car recommendedborder areaconfirm short-term
A ₪2,500 attic unit including internet, water, arnona and building fees, near the college. The photos are decisive: sloping wood-clad ceilings, cramped dark rooms lit by a bare bulb and a blue LED, mismatched worn furniture (a sagging sofa, a leopard-print throw, a heavy dark coffee table), and a bedroom where a single mattress sits under the eaves. The 'kitchen' is a wooden trestle table holding a microwave and a two-ring hotplate, next to a tiny sink cabinet covered in floral contact paper. No oven, no counter, nowhere to prep.
+
  • ₪2,500 includes internet, water, arnona and building fees — a clean all-in
  • Furnished (of a sort) including electrical appliances; near the college
  • Karmiel: train to TLV (~2h); Hazorea ~50 min drive
–
  • The kitchen is a hotplate and a microwave on a wooden trestle table — no oven, no counter, no prep space whatsoever; a categorical fail
  • Cramped attic with sloping ceilings; dark, low, and claustrophobic in every photo
  • Shabby, worn, mismatched furniture; the single worst 'place to be' of the 16
  • Attic + September in the Galilee = a heat trap regardless of AC
  • Single mattress under the eaves; no space for two people to work
  • Agency listing with the agent's phone number burned into every image
?
  • No follow-up warranted — this fails the kitchen requirement and the 'nice place to be' requirement simultaneously
15
Windowless basement studio in Rishon — nonstarter SKIP
Rishon LeZion · קרית חתני פרס נובל · ₪4,000/mo rent · 1 rooms · 46 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪4,650/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · המחיר כולל אינטרנט ויס. למעט חשמל · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
New renovated 46sqm basement studio explicitly 'without a window'. Bright is a core need; this fails outright despite decent equipment.
+
  • Well equipped (washer, dryer, TV, oven)
  • 4000 incl internet and Yes TV
–
  • Basement, explicitly no window — fails the brightness requirement
  • Poor setting for two people home all day
  • Stale June availability
?
  • Skip — no ask needed; fails a hard criterion

Renovated Golan house with a huge garden — beautiful, and completely off their map SKIP
קצרין · ₪4,500/mo rent · 3 rooms · 325 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪5,150/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
no photos
3-room ground house in Katzrin with new furniture and a giant garden at ₪4,500. The Golan is 2.5h+ from Tel Aviv — wrong side of the country for both families and any TA access.
+
  • house + huge garden + private parking
  • new furniture, renovated
  • quiet
–
  • geography disqualifies: TA/Gedera routine access impossible, Hazorea ~1h15
  • rent at cap before bills; a big house costs more to run
  • private landlord insists on phone-only details
?
  • confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability with landlord
  • verify kitchen (no photos reviewed)
15
Rehovot Weizmann 1-room, ₪1,900 — a token kitchenette in a dim basement room, advertised for live-in workers SKIP
Rehovot · א' / צפון מזרח העיר · ₪1,900/mo rent · 1 rooms · 24 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪2,550/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪1,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-termvery small
The cheapest listing here, and the reason cheap is not automatically good. The single photo (from May 2024) shows a low-ceilinged, dim semi-basement room with a bed, a plastic garden table, a drying rack and a token kitchenette — a tiny counter with a microwave. The ad states in English that 'the apartment is for nannies or foreign workers', which is both a fit problem and a signal about the standard. Chaim Weizmann is Rehovot's main artery.
+
  • ₪1,900 — all-in ~₪2,550, the cheapest option by a wide margin
  • Furnished with a bed, wardrobe, fridge and washing machine
  • Rehovot is close to Gedera and has a train station to TLV
–
  • Token kitchenette with almost no counter — a clear fail on the #1 non-negotiable
  • Dim, low-ceilinged semi-basement room; not a place to spend two months indoors
  • Ad explicitly targets 'nannies or foreign workers' — the landlord is not offering this as a couple's home
  • Chaim Weizmann is Rehovot's main road: noisy
  • The only photo dates from May 2024 — the listing is almost certainly stale
  • One room, no workspace, no seating
?
  • Ruled out on the kitchen and the condition; low price does not compensate
15
2,100 in HaTikva — cheap, but a grubby, semi-basement-feeling unit with peeling walls, no cooktop and a single mattress SKIP
Tel Aviv · התקוה, בית יעקב, נווה צה"ל · ₪2,100/mo rent · 1.5 rooms · 20 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪2,750/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,100 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-termvery small
A 1.5-room unit at Abbas 33 in the HaTikva quarter, at 2,100/mo. The price is the only thing going for it. The photos show bare grey-green walls with paint and plaster peeling off, exposed cables and pipework, a dim windowless-feeling interior lit by a wall vent grille rather than a window, a filthy old fridge, a single mattress on a wooden base, and a dark concrete passage to the entrance. The 'new kitchen' the ad advertises is a 1.5m sink cabinet with a granite top jammed into what looks like a hallway/wet area — no stovetop, no oven, and it is already dirty in the listing photos.
+
  • Very cheap: 2,100/mo would leave roughly 1,700/mo of budget after bills — enough for a car and a co-working desk
  • The sink unit does have ~1.5m of granite counter
  • Ad claims a quiet building and a corner unit
  • Shuk HaTikva is walkable and is genuinely good, cheap produce for a vegan/GF household
  • Inside Tel Aviv municipal limits
–
  • Genuinely dilapidated: peeling paint and plaster on multiple walls, exposed wiring, unfinished concrete surfaces, visible grime in the ad's own photos. This is the opposite of 'bright, clean, inviting'
  • Dark. There is no real window in any interior photo — the only opening is a small wall vent grille. For two people working from home through Sept-Oct this is grim
  • No stovetop, no oven, and the 'new kitchen' is a sink cabinet in a passage — the token-kitchenette FAIL case
  • A single mattress on a wooden base is the only furniture besides a filthy old fridge. Nothing else: no table, no chair, no wardrobe, no sofa
  • 1.5 rooms and cramped; the photos read as a partitioned ground/semi-basement unit rather than a flat
  • HaTikva quarter, and the immediate approach in the photos is a narrow concrete alley — worth being blunt about how it would feel at night
  • No light rail; bus-only to central Tel Aviv
  • Only an old wall AC unit visible, in poor condition
  • Ad text is four words long — the landlord has told us essentially nothing
?
  • Nothing worth confirming — the condition rules it out regardless of the answers
15
Tel Giborim, Holon — ₪2,700 shabby room+yard with a fridge-and-microwave 'kitchen' and no bed SKIP
Holon · תל גיבורים · ₪2,700/mo rent · 1 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪3,400/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,700 + arnona ₪50 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A cheap one-room unit with a small yard off an alley in Tel Giborim. The photos show a black leather sofa, a coffee table, a fridge standing in a corridor, a washer/dryer in a bare utility space, and a yard under a rusting corrugated roof with peeling walls and a stained floor. The ad itself lists the kitchen as a fridge and a microwave, the furniture list contains no bed, and it says only 'partially furnished'.
+
  • Very cheap — ₪2,700 + ₪50 water leaves large budget headroom
  • Private yard and a washer/dryer, which is rare at this price
  • Tel Giborim has good bus links to Tel Aviv and is close to Holon Junction
–
  • No kitchen worth the name: fridge + microwave only, no stovetop, no oven, no counter visible in any photo — a hard fail on the #1 criterion
  • No bed listed anywhere in the ad's furniture list; 'מרוהטת חלקית' (partially furnished)
  • Shabby and uninviting throughout — bare walls, exposed wiring, corrugated roofing, stained floors
  • One room for two people working from home; AC only in the 'salon'
  • Alley/rear-unit access; ground-level with a yard likely means limited light and privacy
?
  • Nothing worth pursuing — no advantage here survives the kitchen and the condition
14
Room in a 6-way student share, kitchen not even pictured — skip SKIP
Herzliya · ₪4,157/mo rent · 6 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,807/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,157 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen — this couple's #1 criterion — is completely unassessable: not one of the 10 photos shows it, because this is a single private room in a 6-room shared apartment marketed for "students or young professionals," so the kitchen is a communal one shared across up to five other rooms. A couple who cook most of their vegan + gluten-free food need dedicated, cookable counter space they control; a strangers' shared student kitchen (GF cross-contamination, competing for the stove, no reservable prep space) is the opposite of that. The room itself is clean, bright and furnished, and one room even has a large desk with an office chair (good for one remote worker), and the location near Herzliya station is excellent for Tel Aviv — but the shared-flat format also collides head-on with their need for quiet. Not a fit.
+
  • Central-east Herzliya, ~900m (12-min walk) from Herzliya railway station — 11-min train to Tel Aviv Center every ~15 min, plus frequent buses (501/502/47/48/247); no car needed
  • Rooms are clean, bright, freshly furnished (double beds, wardrobes, AC in every room); overall condition is good
  • One room has a large desk + office chair — a real workspace for one person
  • Price ₪4,157/mo is under the ₪4,500 budget (but this is per-room in a share, not a whole apartment)
  • Airbnb window shows all 61 days available with short min-nights, so a ~2-month Sept–Nov stay fits the calendar
–
  • Kitchen — the top criterion — is not shown in any photo and is communal, shared across a 6-room flat; impossible to verify oven, stovetop, or any usable prep space, and a shared student kitchen is unworkable for cooking most of your meals
  • Gluten-free cooking in a strangers' shared kitchen means constant cross-contamination risk with no dedicated surfaces
  • It's a private room in a shared apartment for students/young professionals — not a self-contained home for a couple
  • A 6-room share is inherently noisy and social — directly conflicts with their value of quiet
  • Two remote workers would be sharing one room and one desk, plus competing with roommates for common space
?
  • Availability: listed as available for the full 61-day window with short minimum nights, so short-term is fine — but confirm a couple (2 people) can book one room
  • If ever tempted, you'd need to see actual photos of the shared kitchen and learn how many roommates share it before this could even be evaluated
  • The whole premise (private room in a student share) is a mismatch for a self-catering couple who value quiet — better to look at self-contained one-bedroom units
12
Kogel 26, Holon — spacious 3-room but completely UNFURNISHED and 1970s-dated; over cap anyway SKIP
Holon · גרין ועם · ₪3,700/mo rent · 3 rooms · 75 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
~₪4,740/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,700 + arnona ₪390 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
A genuinely roomy 75sqm 3-room on Kogel Blvd with a big south/north-facing living room, but every photo shows an empty shell: no bed, no sofa, no table, no appliances. The kitchen is original 1970s — olive-green cabinets and a decent marble counter run, but no oven, no stovetop, no fridge. Kogel is also the axis of the Green Line light-rail works (photo 2 shows the torn-up ground across the street), so 2026 means construction noise, not a usable train.
+
  • Genuinely spacious for the price — ~75sqm, 3 rooms, would easily give two people separate work spots
  • Bright dual-aspect living room (south + north), 3 AC units, public shelter attached to the building
  • Green/Am is a quiet, established central-Holon neighborhood with buses and shops close by
–
  • NOT FURNISHED — totally empty. Unusable for a 2-month stay; they own no bed or furniture
  • Kitchen has no oven, no stovetop and no fridge; cabinetry is original 1970s
  • All-in ~₪4,740 (₪3,700 + ₪390 stated arnona + ₪650 est. bills) — over the ₪4,500 cap
  • Kogel Blvd is a major artery and the Green Line light-rail construction corridor — noise and roadworks through 2026; the line itself won't open in time to be useful
  • Broker listing with 'immediate entry' framing — aimed at a 12-month unfurnished tenant, not a 2-month sublet
?
  • Only worth a second look if the landlord would furnish it, which is very unlikely at this price
10
Sha'ar HaYam luxury sea-view 4 rooms — the ₪800 figure is a nightly rate, i.e. ~₪24,000/mo SKIP
Rishon LeZion · שער הים · ₪800/mo rent · 4 rooms · 120 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪800/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪800 + arnona n/a · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A genuinely spectacular short-term luxury rental: a 4-room designer apartment in the Sha'ar HaYam tower with frontal sea views from every window, a full built-in kitchen with an oven and a large counter, and complete high-end furnishing. The scraped price of ₪800 is not a monthly rent — the ad is a short-term holiday let ('להשכרה לטווח קצר', 'אווירה של חופשה יוקרתית בכל יום מחדש') and ₪800 is almost certainly the nightly rate, which annualises to roughly ₪24,000/mo. Far outside budget.
+
  • Explicitly a short-term let — the one listing here with no availability question
  • Full designer kitchen with a built-in oven, hob and generous counter
  • Completely furnished and equipped; frontal sea views; beachfront Rishon
–
  • The ₪800 price is a per-night short-term rate, not a monthly rent — the real cost is on the order of ₪24,000/mo, roughly 5x the budget
  • Luxury holiday-let pricing is structurally incompatible with a 2-month ₪4,500 budget
  • Beachfront west Rishon is far from either train station
?
  • Worth a single call ONLY to confirm the pricing basis — on the off-chance ₪800 is a typo or a weekly rate, but this is very unlikely
  • Possible duplicate of c60835619e3e20b8 ('0m away, similar price', same Sha'ar HaYam project) and of the 95a29a88f8969499 sublet — neither candidate has photos in this cluster to compare against, so no duplicate_of is set. Both are skips regardless

Ha-Yad, Neve Remez, Holon — one photo of a gate, pure broker boilerplate, and over the cap SKIP
Holon · נווה רמז · ₪4,300/mo rent · 2 rooms · 40 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,950/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,300 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A ₪4,300 2-room 'housing unit' with exactly one photo — a black metal gate on the street — and a description that is 100% realtor self-promotion about the virtues of using a broker, containing literally zero information about the apartment. Nothing about furnishing, kitchen, size or condition. It is also over budget before a single bill is counted.
+
  • Neve Remez is a reasonable, quiet central-Holon area
–
  • All-in ~₪4,950 (₪4,300 + ₪650 est. bills, arnona not stated on top) — clearly over the ₪4,500 cap
  • One photo, and it shows a gate — no interior, no kitchen, no furniture, nothing
  • Description is agency boilerplate with no property information at all
  • No evidence it's furnished
?
  • Not worth requesting photos — even a perfect apartment is over the cap at this rent

4,200 'luxury suite' with one marketing collage, a mini-fridge and no kitchen — over the cap SKIP
Bat Yam · הגבול רוטשילד · ₪4,200/mo rent · 1 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-16
≥₪4,850/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,200 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
A single collage image is the entire photo set: a floorplan plus thumbnails of a small studio 'suite 3' with a mural wall, sofa, bed and a desk. The collage's own caption lists what's included — shower and toilet, closet, sofa, internet, light fittings, bed, work desk, and a SMALL FRIDGE — and conspicuously does not mention a kitchen; none is visible in any thumbnail. At 4,200 rent plus estimated bills the all-in is ~4,850, over the hard 4,500 cap, for a place that appears to have no cooking facilities at all.
+
  • Fully furnished including a work desk; internet included per the collage
  • HaGvul–Rothschild area, near the Rothschild Red Line stop
  • Design-conscious and renovated
–
  • No kitchen visible or listed anywhere — the included-items caption tops out at a small fridge. Total fail on the #1 requirement
  • All-in ~4,850 (4,200 + est. 650) exceeds the 4,500 cap
  • One collage image only — no honest photo of the actual unit
  • Small single-room studio, one work desk, no second workspace
?
  • If ever revisited: demand real photos of a kitchen and a like-for-like price with bills stated
7
One-person studio, microwave-only kitchenette — skip SKIP
Tel Aviv · ₪4,281/mo rent · 2 rooms · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,931/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,281 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen is a token kitchenette and fails the #1 criterion outright: a tiny square sink with only a ~40cm ledge beside it, a mini-fridge underneath, a microwave and an electric kettle — no stovetop and no oven in any of the 7 photos, and no counter/prep space to even add a portable one. On top of that the listing is out of scope: the description explicitly says "Suitable for ONE guest only - a folding couch opens to a single bed," so it cannot house a couple for two months. There is one wall-mounted fold-down table with a single folding chair — no way for two people to work remotely. It is clean, central (near the Carmel Market), ground-floor and within budget, but none of that rescues it.
+
  • Central Tel Aviv location (near Carmel Market/Nachalat Binyamin) — walkable to the shuk for fresh vegan & gluten-free produce and to frequent buses
  • Within budget at ~₪3,942/mo
  • Clean, bright, well-organized; ground-floor with its own entrance
  • Short stays accepted (min 3 nights) and fully available across the window
–
  • Explicitly "suitable for ONE guest only" — single folding bed; cannot house a couple for two months (out of scope)
  • Token kitchenette: NO stovetop, NO oven — only a microwave and kettle — and essentially no counter/prep space, so cooking most of their own food is impossible; no room to add a portable hob either
  • No workspace for two: a single wall fold-down table with one folding chair
  • Kitchen sink/prep ledge sits right against the tiny wet-room bathroom
  • Central Carmel Market area is busy and touristy — not quiet
?
  • This is a one-person unit — only worth a message if the host has a larger apartment; otherwise skip
  • Short-term availability is fine (min 3 nights, 61/61 days open), so that is not the blocker
